### Shijin13 — 2012-05-02T16:15:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20650>

so with this hellish detox/cold or what ever I've got going on... yesterday I did 2 CT sessions in bed laying on top of ice packs, and w/2 on my chest/tummy by the end of each session the constriction in my bronchial tubes/lungs started to ease and I began to hack up lungs full of crap! CT helps w/expectoration of crap from LUNGS!!! now that I'm able to cough up crap I threw out a rib w/all the coughing... but at least I can breath now!!! btwn this, the sinus and eye drainage... I'm a freaking mess... at least now my voice is starting to come back...

in addition to the loosing of the crap in my chest - I was actually kinda move off into a psuedo sleep - not really sleep but very restful. Felt tons better - and actually was able to get dinner on the table for the family last night!

### Pamela — 2012-05-02T22:07:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20792>

I've stepped up my next gradient to go for 1 hour soaks 4 times a week in about 55 degree water. Getting in and adapted has been much easier this past week, which is week 5 for me. I really feel ready for my soak when the day comes, I can feel it in my legs. They feel kind of swollen and then I do the soak and they feel good again. I'm working on getting my hormones fixed, will be doing blood this week for Dzugan program. Weight is staying steady while I eat a good keto paleolithic diet and enjoy all my fats. My next plan of action is to get the water a little colder, will probably have to go buy a bag of ice each day for that.

### nonchalant — 2012-05-03T00:29:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20842>

Today I was able to keep my feet in the 55F water for 45 minutes with no problem. That is an improvement. I tried it in 50F water, but 30 minutes was enough of that. So I guess they are finally adapting. I used to just put my heels in, then half the foot, then everything but the toes, now the entire foot.  Easy peasy.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-03T00:45:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20846>

> **Pamela;20865 wrote:** I've stepped up my next gradient to go for 1 hour soaks 4 times a week in about 55 degree water. Getting in and adapted has been much easier this past week, which is week 5 for me. I really feel ready for my soak when the day comes, I can feel it in my legs. They feel kind of swollen and then I do the soak and they feel good again. I'm working on getting my hormones fixed, will be doing blood this week for Dzugan program. Weight is staying steady while I eat a good keto paleolithic diet and enjoy all my fats. My next plan of action is to get the water a little colder, will probably have to go buy a bag of ice each day for that.

My heart beats faster when I prepare my cold bath in the morning.  I would fill the tub when I finish eating my BAB, check the water temperature, walk in the tub and let my feet feel it, then go away for 10 minutes - surf the internet, take my vitamins, fish oil, etc, drink my iced water... then wash my face in cold water, then go in the tub. This "ritual" is kinda like foreplay  that prepares me to greet the cold water with grace.

I don't multitask in the tub, I focus on the bath and make the most out of it. My arms and legs are always moving to circulate the water - it feels great when water moves against my body, almost feel like being massaged. Sometimes I use a washcloth to bring water onto my chest which doesn't always get under the water, or use the washcloth to massage my arms and legs - I figure as long as I'm in the tub let's do some scrubbing  And I bring water to my face too. It feels great to cool down my hot face with cold water. At the end I take a quick cold shower to wash off and let water drop on my face. Wonderful.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-05-03T01:52:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20857>

### Birdy — 2012-05-03T01:57:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20858>

### kcorcoran@comcast.net — 2012-05-03T02:52:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-20865>

I got back into the tub for CT today, 30 minutes 59F going in 63F water coming out. Didn't feel too bad once acclimated, layed back up to neck, swished water over torso. I found it easier to get in and stand, cup water over my legs before sitting, and then pour water over back and shoulders before laying down. Skin was mildly pink, so I should have stayed in longer, but I was bored. stapped the ice sheets around my back belly for ~20 min.

Not thinking my problem is incontinence as I never have to pee at night. Heck, after I go at 6 am when I rise, I usually don't pee again until around 10:30 or so. Still thinking it is estrogen related or something else. I remember flying to Hawaii 5 years ago and when I finally got to swim in the ocean, I couldn't stop peeing all the fluid that I retained from flying and this was a couple of days later. I filled right back up on the way home and my calves were so tight with fluid build up that they hurt. It was pretty bad.

Since this is a CT'ing thread, how do I go past the 30 min. mark in 53 degree water. I am not making forward progress in regard to time. I know I have reaped bennies from it but wonder if I could stay in longer if that would help me. I get cold and can tell you when 30 minutes is up.

### ealachan — 2012-05-04T21:38:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21407>

> **ColleenM;20762 wrote:** so I wonder what the reason is if you DON'T need to pee cause I rarely have that reaction.

someone may have already answered this, but - I don't think that's necessarily a bad sign. As I understand it, the reason cold makes us need to pee is because our brains sense cold and instantly start going into survival mode, pulling blood away from the limbs to try and protect the core. This in turn raises blood pressure. The brain then wants to jettison extra fluid to help keep the blood pressure from getting too high, so it signals the bladder to empty...et voila, desperate need to pee. SO...with all that taken into consideration, it could be that you're just cold-adapted enough that your brain doesn't freak the hell out when you get cold anymore, or possibly you're someone who has low-ish blood pressure to begin with and so the cold reaction doesn't necessarily send your blood pressure up enough to trigger the need to shed excess fluid...?

Maybe? Possibly? That could all very well be complete baloney...just in the interest of full disclosure...

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-04T22:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21420>

I started freezing liter bottles of water and using them to cool the water before I get in, and it worked fairly well, but is a lot of effort to fill them, take to the basement freezer, then haul up two flights to the bathtub! ### Guest — 2012-05-05T00:46:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21446>

I have been practicing face dunks, icing and cold showers, everyday now for about 4 weeks. However, I went to see my chiro today and he checked me out, stepped back, and asked me how I was. I told him I haven't had any problems with my back since I saw him last. He said, well, I can't find anything to adjust. We then talked about CT and I told him that when I turned around in the shower to do my back it felt MUCH colder then the front--way colder. He said, that maybe, the reason it felt colder on my back was that it's actually helping with chronic inflammation. Why else would I not need an adjustment--first time ever in 20 years. Here I didn't think I was progressing and felt I paled in my efforts compared to many here.

Trying to encourage him to read about CT. Very good reason to keep going so we can help others.

Will, definitely, try a cold bath this weekend.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-05T22:56:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21685>

> **Lyndra;21551 wrote:** Colder is definitely easier for me. Two days ago I used tap water - around 60 degrees - and shivered and really worked on "mental toughness" in order to stay for 30 minutes. Yesterday I put in ice from the icemaker and got it down to around 57 and it was easier. Today I added a 10 pound bag plus the icemaker ice and got the water down to 52 (what it was when I first started CT). It was so much easier - no mental agony and very little shivering. This may be be my incentive to clean out the garage so we can buy a freezer and I can make my own ice instead of buying it.

I had the same experience. Lately, when I go back into the tub from my morning CT at night, I've been able to stay in forever... do we get adapted at a certain temperature??

### Lyndra — 2012-05-06T05:10:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21727>

> **Souldanzer;21761 wrote:** I had the same experience. Lately, when I go back into the tub from my morning CT at night, I've been able to stay in forever... do we get adapted at a certain temperature??

I think I have adapted to a certain temp. Today I put in 4 10-pound bags of ice and got the water down to 40. I could only tolerate keeping my hands & feet in for 7 minutes but the rest of my body was fine for 30. I felt good when I got out but after about 10 minutes, I started shivering like mad - so bad I could barely hold my coffee mug! That crazy shaking kept up for more than an hour. I haven't had that reaction since my very early days of CT so I think my body is used to 50-55 degrees. At least now I know what much colder is like.

### Destiny — 2012-05-06T13:51:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21751>

> **Souldanzer;21761 wrote:** I had the same experience. Lately, when I go back into the tub from my morning CT at night, I've been able to stay in forever... do we get adapted at a certain temperature??

I was a person who used to love a really hot bath so it took me a month to get into 50's. I never shiver, I hate the idea of shivering. I don't go below 53F and only recently started submersing my back and rib cage. Is shivering required?

### BeingVenus — 2012-05-06T22:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21815>

Took a dip at Easton's beach in Newport today. The water temp was 54 or so according to the water report. I was meaning to go in with the polar bear club but they were all the way on the end of the beach, went in and then quickly jumped out. I hung out for about 40 minutes. It was a lot easier than an ice bath. I unfortunately attracted some

attention on the beach from people wearing windbreakers and long pants asking me how I didn't get hypothermia. Hahahaha but it was fun.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-05-06T23:27:23+0300

Just a cold shower this morning, and an hour of spot-icing this evening ...

### indigogirl — 2012-05-06T23:44:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21824>

Water temp in bath today was 60 degrees with 20 lbs of ice...usually 62 degrees. This was the first time I lay down with my head against the back of the tub and my hands and arms submerged almost up to my armpits (upper stomach and chest not submerged). I was much colder than usual with my arms and hands submerged. This true for others? Why??

### Lyndra — 2012-05-07T03:12:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21868>

Today was the first time I have done a morning session - 30 minutes at 52 degrees (courtesy of 20# of ice) just before my normal lunchtime. I warmed up quickly and then went outside to do yard work where I was a mad woman. I have never gone to town like that before - I felt like I had the energy of 3 people. It took about 20 minutes for me to wind down to normal pace. I kept at it for almost 2 hours and then came in and had a moderate lunch. Never before have I had an energy burst like this after CT - it was awesome.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-07T05:15:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21886>

> **Souldanzer;21947 wrote:** I wish.

I'm no medical professional, but I'd guess it is lingering effects from the concussion. Sleep is the brain's way of healing. Recovery can be variable - you're good for a while, then suddenly need more sleep.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-07T05:38:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21888>

> **Lyndra;21963 wrote:** I'm no medical professional, but I'd guess it is lingering effects from the concussion. Sleep is the brain's way of healing. Recovery can be variable - you're good for a while, then suddenly need more sleep.

I would totally blame it on this... except that this was happening before I ever hit my head!

I get 10+hrs a night plus whatever napping I happen to do during the day... today around 3hrs but usual is more like 1-2hrs. I think my body has just decided to need some serious healing time ... that's what we do while we sleep, right? TBI certainly isn't helping the situation.

I keep figuring I'll just sleep it off. Eventually it will stop.

### Guest — 2012-05-07T14:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21905>

Absolutely ...we heal when we sleep. I welcome sleep for that reason. I also take naps and they can be anywhere from 1 hr to 2 1/2 but sometimes I only get 7 hrs sleep at night. I take my b complex and b 12 right before I go to sleep because my homeopathic doc says that is when they work. I just started to do that again and it's helping me feel more rested during the day.

Do you feel you are progressing physically even though you are still sleeping a lot? I know I do. The last thing you need is to worry. Go with it and if you feel you need to see someone then go for it. But for heaven sake, stop the worry, as it does no good.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-07T18:22:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21938>

> **Souldanzer;21965 wrote:** I would totally blame it on this... except that this was happening before I ever hit my head!
>
>
>
> I get 10+hrs a night plus whatever napping I happen to do during the day... today around 3hrs but usual is more like 1-2hrs. I think my body has just decided to need some serious healing time ... that's what we do while we sleep, right? TBI certainly isn't helping the situation.
>
>
>
> I keep figuring I'll just sleep it off. Eventually it will stop.

How long before the injury was your pattern like that? Seems clear that your body needs the sleep, for whatever reason. If you aren't already, you might want to create a log to capture the details about your sleep (duration, quality, etc.) as well as lifestyle (stress, diet, supplements, meds, etc.) so that you can recognize any changes in patterns and try to correlate. I've been doing that for about 6 months, trying to figure out why I can't sleep more than an hour or two at a time (no luck for me, but that's another story).

### MartiD — 2012-05-07T18:25:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21941>

I've got the weirdest things going on a work right now. Our office was a bit stuffy from being closed up all weekend with the air conditioning up but it was comfortable to me. My boss, who sweats profusely all the time normally freezes us out. He turned on the air as soon as he walked in and even though I can feel the air getting colder around me, especially my hands, I have an intense heat feeling on back and legs. I am assuming my body is reacting because it thinks it is going to be exposed to cold water or something. So bizarre!

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-07T18:38:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21944>

> **Suhenna;21982 wrote:**
>
>
>
> Do you feel you are progressing physically even though you are still sleeping a lot? I know I do. The last thing you need is to worry. Go with it and if you feel you need to see someone then go for it. But for heaven sake, stop the worry, as it does no good.

I have lost track of the progress. My TBI has turned bad and all I can judge is my head is pounding terribly or my head is pounding. I know I felt like I was starting to get better pre TBI. I've had some obvious improvements in skin health the last couple of weeks.

I have seen at least two docs about this. The first took a brief look at my medical history, told me I must have a \*terrible\* life, then sent me home with anti-depressants and a referral to the "let's dig out everything bad in your life that ever happened" people.

The second is much better... room for hope and improvement. He diagnosed me with Adrenal Fatigue, gave me some tips on supplements and basically told me I need to sleep it off and stay away from caffeine.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-07T18:42:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21946>

> **MamaGrok;21996 wrote:**
>
> Souldanzer, I'm sleeping like crazy, too, have been on and off for several years. Apoptosis happens while we sleep, right? That's what I keep telling myself, or it would make me crazy.

I have done an unreasonable amount of sleeping since November. I went to Europe in December and basically slept for 3 weeks straight (in the very quiet, calm, cold house of my parents).

Do you think the sleeping is only due to adrenal issues? Or have you noticed any other things that are going on, possibly? I'm thinking thyroid, since due to my ED it got "uncoupled", but even there all I can really do is be patient right now.

CT puts me out. I get out of the tub, I have 20-30min to get myself settled before I can't keep my eyes open any longer.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-07T19:00:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21951>

> **Lyndra;22015 wrote:** How long before the injury was your pattern like that? Seems clear that your body needs the sleep, for whatever reason. If you aren't already, you might want to create a log to capture the details about your sleep (duration, quality, etc.) as well as lifestyle (stress, diet, supplements, meds, etc.) so that you can recognize any changes in patterns and try to correlate. I've been doing that for about 6 months, trying to figure out why I can't sleep more than an hour or two at a time (no luck for me, but that's another story).

I've been sleeping more than "normal" people for months... but it was interspersed with sleeping difficulties at times. I was still sleeping a lot but at crazy times. Figured those out now I just sleep, deep and heavy. I really like this sleep. It's awesome sleep. I've been thinking of starting some sort of record about stuff, so this might be a good time. That's how I figured out why I couldn't sleep sometimes.

Sorry to hear you've got the opposite issue going on. I had a very difficult time with not sleeping through the night. Made me feel awful. Have you tried stopping all your supplements for a few days? For me it was a combo of hormones, allergies and CT.

I don't know if I'm worried about it, really. I think I just need to sleep it off. But it's taking a toll on my "other" life. Not that there is that much left of that either, since TBI.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-07T19:14:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21959>

> I don't know if I'm worried about it, really. I think I just need to sleep it off. But it's taking a toll on my "other" life. Not that there is that much left of that either, since TBI.

Thanks for the ideas about stopping all the supplements/etc. I think my problem right now is inadequate/unbalanced estrogen but am not sure. After battling raging hot flashes for so long, I'm really reluctant to stop the estrogen. I want to get re-tested soon so I think I will just forge ahead so we can a true assessment of what is happening now and then can adjust based on results. If I start switching things up now, I fear it will muddy the waters too much. I'm just hoping CT won't throw the results.

Recovery from TBI is not going to be linear. You'll go forward and then backwards and forwards again but not in a predictable way. Coupled with your pre-TBI history, it may be tough to sort out what is causing the symptoms. Do you have confidence in this new doc you're seeing? Seems like you could use helpful guidance (not the idiot, pardon me for saying so) you saw first.

Lol, I didn't want to say it... she's actually a nice person, just has no idea. We talked about dogs for 30 min when I came in for a follow up (insurance... didn't want to and she knew). She totally made up the "complete physical" notes in my chart that day

I'm seeing a holistic and paleo doc now, that's a good start. And he will actually listen. If I want something (labs) looks into getting it covered for me. I like that I don't have to be a PIA to get what I need.

Hope you get things figured out soon... it was the fillers that were messing with me with my supplements. Dr K commented on another post that you can stop CT for about a week and then test to get a better result.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-07T20:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21982>

That's what I plan to do - stop CT and then test. But I find I am at least psychologically addicted to CT. I have been doing it almost 9 weeks now and don't want to interrupt progress. But if Dr. K says do it, I will give it a whirl. He was so right last week when I asked about getting a cortisone shot for my thumb arthritis. He said look to my diet. I thought I had a great, clean diet, and then remembered that the day before the pain flared I had a piece of "healthy vegan" cake to celebrate a friend's birthday. I've been wheat-free for quite a while so I attribute my pain to the gluten. Why is it we have to do stupid things before we learn not to do it anymore? As the saying goes, experience is what you get after you need it. Duh.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-07T21:33:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-21998>

I'm addicted, too  Dr K should have warned us about this one

### Lyndra — 2012-05-07T21:53:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22008>

> **MartiD;22081 wrote:** Ok, I'll ask here and see if I get a real answer. Our local pool opens Memorial Day weekend and should be nice and cold for first 3 weeks or so (I'm very excited!) Well, given how often Dr. K cites Michael Phelps' swimming in a cold pool, I don't see why it would be a problem. You might find that getting your head in the water takes some getting used to, though.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-07T22:10:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22018>

You lose more heat when exercising in cold water so you need to be cold adapted to do this. I think you'd be fine. Just get out if you get too cold or start to feel faint etc.

### Birdy — 2012-05-08T02:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22062>

You might want to start by just sitting in the stairway until you are more acclimated to the cold. But, I agree with Dr. K on this, start CT now. As I have read about it, the point of CT-ing is to lower your skin temp. Dunking your head or moving around alot can lead to reduced core temps, which you don't want.

### kathylu — 2012-05-08T17:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22207>

Great success yesterday! My temp was 98 before the ice bath. That's a miracle in itself because I am hypothyroid and my body temp runs low, usually in the 96.5 range. Ok, after the bath my temp was 99.1 ! And 30 minutes later my temp was still 98.9. Uncoupling proteins anyone?

Looking forward to radiating heat like the sun!!

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-08T17:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22210>

Woohoo!!! Congrats!!

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-05-08T18:26:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22250>

30 minutes at 55F today. Had that weird non-shivering shivery feeling, so I'm hoping that's a good sign, and my warm-up time afterwards hasn't been too bad, either. Oddly, though, my cold shower this morning was awful - it felt so much colder than usual for some strange reason.

Will have to start taking my temperature before, during and after the baths.

### MartiD — 2012-05-08T18:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22253>

> **Birdy;22139 wrote:** You might want to start by just sitting in the stairway until you are more acclimated to the cold. But, I agree with Dr. K on this, start CT now. As I have read about it, the point of CT-ing is to lower your skin temp. Dunking your head or moving around alot can lead to reduced core temps, which you don't want.

I've been CT'ing since Mid-February once if not twice a day so I feel like I am as close to adapted as I can be with this much time in. I was more worried about injury to muscles when they are cold since we always hear you should have your muscles warmed up and stretched to avoid pulls and tears.

### QuantumRadical — 2012-05-08T18:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22259>

> **MartiD;22332 wrote:** I've been CT'ing since Mid-February once if not twice a day so I feel like I am as close to adapted as I can be with this much time in. I was more worried about injury to muscles when they are cold since we always hear you should have your muscles warmed up and stretched to avoid pulls and tears.

Warmed up and stretched is not consistent with the demands of nature. Those who need to act right now (fight or flight) don't have time to warm up and stretch. If you were to be naturally prone to pulls and tears in a flight or fight situation, you would be at best an endangered species, or extinct via some predators food chain. Of course, those that are not naturally active or healthy may be more prone because of a non-natural muscle/tendon/bone composition. YMMV. Actually, I think Jack is implying that if we don't get healthy like nature intended, we may very well go extinct.

### shilohman — 2012-05-08T20:20:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22302>

> Originally Posted by MartiD
>
> I've been CT'ing since Mid-February once if not twice a day so I feel like I am as close to adapted as I can be with this much time in. I was more worried about injury to muscles when they are cold since we always hear you should have your muscles warmed up and stretched to avoid pulls and tears.

If your muscles are ketogenic, then they are ready to function in an instance. If your muscles burn carbs for fuel, then you need to warm up to get the blood flowing with the right amount of oxygen, otherwise you can injure your muscles.

### jayjha1@gmail.com — 2012-05-08T20:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22310>

I bought the deep bath plug - so I filled the tub up more, dissolved about 12lbs of ice and held a 20 lb ice pack on my chest

yikes - started shaking towards the end of All Along The Watchtower (the first song in my 51 minute CT playlist - normally I'm into song 4 for this to happen)

held strong - need to keep my feet out though - even with the wool sox there are my Achilles heel

### PaleoDentist — 2012-05-09T04:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22456>

I skipped CT yesterday due to getting home at 1am and straight to bed.

Did my 30 min at 57F CT today. I generally immerse up to my chest , arms out of the water as I read. today I submerged my arms for the last 2 min. it was tough. I guess my arms are not cold adapted! now I am shivering. very interesting turn of events. as I have not shivered in weeks. I think I will continue with arm immersion at the end of my baths increasing time incrementally.

### ealachan — 2012-05-10T03:34:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22718>

CT: 25 minutes in 53-degree water. Skin was 82 deg going in, 60 coming out. Oral temp going in was 98.0, coming out was 98.6. Goosebumps the whole time, started to get shivery at about the 20 minute mark. Continually amazed at how relatively easy it now is for me to haul my fat ass up out of the tub. I know I haven't gotten significantly lighter in a while, so I must have gotten stronger. Without actually doing anything. COOL!

### freesia — 2012-05-10T03:50:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22726>

I want to jump in here with you guys. \*splash\*

I've been following this thread and it's been my inspiration!

I'd been doing lukewarm showers right down to cold then into the bath with ice but my last few times my temperature dropped so I've dialed it back a little.

I got a nice temperature rise today in response to a 20 minute bath @ 58. I kept the temp to 59 or lower by adding plenty of ice. I did wear neoprene socks for the first 10 mins, and a woolly hat.

Shivered only lightly, but for several hours.

Happy to see others are getting nice temperature rises too!! (Temp drop is horrible.)

### ginastarke — 2012-05-10T21:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-22969>

Cold-Adapted? no

CT Baths or Icing or Both? Both but mostly Icing

Duration?bath 45 min icing 60+ minutes, usually 90

Temperature? bath 60 degrees , lowest I could get it

When do you CT? about 30 minutes after dinner

How long have you been doing CT? Experimenting 3 weeks with the ice packs , first CT bath last night

I love the ice packs. I'm incredibly relaxed within minutes and I generally keep them on until they're not cold anymore. I usually have one on my stomach and two I either put on my thighs or upper arms for my "bingo wings" held on by ace bandages

Tried my first CT bath last night -YEEEEEEIKES! I made it 45 minutes but was cold for hours afterwards- you know how when you're so cold the muscles in your back tighten up? I'm sure I'll get used to it, but hey I lost a pound!

### ginastarke — 2012-05-11T05:03:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23089>

I don't mind shivering and being cold if it means those fat cells are dying!

My only wildlife scare was flying down a hill on my bicycle on the way to work and nearly hitting a raccoon. from his face we were both thinking the same thing; "Oh, CRAP!" both of us got out of each other's way safely.

I think the wildlife is the only thing I'm going to miss with my new job, which is closer to the center of town. I could swear I saw a feral cat and owl co-operating on the hunt together.

### freesia — 2012-05-11T05:06:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23091>

I got my bathwater down to 55 before getting in today.

Stayed there for 25 minutes, added a bit more ice around the 15 minute mark and the water was up to 58 by the time I got out. I had a temperature rise again so I'm pleased about that.

### Dali Dula — 2012-05-11T05:29:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23095>

All the native water here has warmed close to 60. I have been doing full immersion in >50 for almost two months. Today i set up a cast iron claw foot tub I've had for twenty years out by the stream in the backyard. I filled it up with 51 deg water from my deep well. I let the water run while I'm in it and it stays at 51. Perfect. This is where I will be every morning until the pond gets cold in the fall. The tub faces the rising sun, overlooks a babbling brook and is surrounded by bird filled trees. Oh yeah, it gets better everyday.

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-11T07:21:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23108>

With 8 bug bites on my feet and ankles I decided to do some foot CT. Have done face dunks at 40 deg no sweat, but full body so far has been no lower than 64. Got a foot bath to 43 and plopped my feet in. Weird!!!! Shock of course... Then in felt...Goood!!! Then my bones started to ache kinda. But not in a bad way? I could do it for a few min at a time til the sensations were getting too freaky and if take a break.

Feet were cold for hours but just to the touch- my skin - they 'felt' warm and comfy to me but if you touched them they were Icy. Hard to describe but I know y'all will get what I mean.

Now to get the rest of me that cold! I'll rely on the pacific ocean tho as only my legs and butt fit in my tiny tub!!!

FYI- leptin rx since 2/21/12..... And down 36 lbs since then (including my vaca last week where I ate right but indulged in quite a bit of malbec - and still lost 6 lbs that week!! But no more wine til I lose the next 70 lbs!!!). Hooray for leptin rx and CT!!

And thanks to all who post here to inspire me to get in the tub. Stay frosty my friends!

### Birdy — 2012-05-11T20:11:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23221>

This week I have upped my time in the CT tub to 45 minutes. The temp varies, depending on the time of day. Lately the nights have been cold, so the am water is in the 60's. I can't fully immerse in my current tub, but will be able to get to the beach in a few days. As things warm up, cold showers are becoming only a memory. The water is cold in the morning, but very warm in the afternoons. Despite the warmth, my CT spot is getting better and better every day. I have drained the saucer that harbored the mosquitoes, trimmed some branches hanging over the water too much, and try to CT before the sun warms things up too much and the bees visit nearby flowering bushes. The next time I empty the tub, I am planning to take a ladder to the tree and trim out lots of dead wood. I have a "CT basket" so I can take my books, radio, etc. with me.

### Jane — 2012-05-12T00:29:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23269>

I did my first CT bath last night. I got in our big claw-foot tub and then turned on the water and let it fill up slowly. It was only 62 F and only up to my waist, but much more comfortable than the cold showers at 64 F! I didn't shiver until I rolled over on my stomach and immersed my upper body and arms, but I managed to stay in for an hour. I've also been icing my belly laying by the pool on the lounge. The only problem was that I did it too late in the p.m. 9 and I was wide awake until 1:30 a.m. Next time I'm adding ice.

### AKMan — 2012-05-12T01:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23290>

Did a 30 minute, 35 degree soak today. First in a month. Had NO problems getting in or adjusting. Felt ggggggreat!

Since I had to go into town right away and didn't want to drive with the shivers, I followed the dip with a hot shower. Felt wonderful and no shivering.

I'm pondering the effects of the hot shower on CT. In a normal session for me at that temp, I would start to shiver about 10 minutes after I got out and it would last an hour or more and several digits would go 'white and numb'. With the hot shower, it completely negated the effects of the after-drop and I was normal immediately. Since all the pathways were activated (cold sensors, vaso-constriction, UCP-1, BAT, NST, etc...) You'd think it would be an overall win. I'm thinking the only thing warming up quickly 'cost' me was a few hundred or less calories I would have used shivering. Hmmmm

### AKMan — 2012-05-12T01:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23294>

PS. You guys GOTTA TRY water in the 30's...

### freesia — 2012-05-13T03:53:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23573>

> **Jane;23351 wrote:** I did my first CT bath last night. I got in our big claw-foot tub and then turned on the water and let it fill up slowly. It was only 62 F and only up to my waist, but much more comfortable than the cold showers at 64 F! I didn't shiver until I rolled over on my stomach and immersed my upper body and arms, but I managed to stay in for an hour. I've also been icing my belly laying by the pool on the lounge. The only problem was that I did it too late in the p.m. 9 and I was wide awake until 1:30 a.m. Next time I'm adding ice.

Hi Jane, nice to see you over here.

I've found baths _much_ less intense than showers too. So far the only drawback to my baths is that I don't get the shoulders/upper back area nearly as cold as I do in a shower. I'm thinking of alternating baths and showers....or getting a deeper bath perhaps?

I've done two more baths @ 57. After the second one recovery was difficult again (shivering after 5 hours), so I "resorted" to a warm bath to get comfortable because I had to go out in the evening. Next week I'm going to try timing my baths so that I can take a good brisk walk on the beach with my dog after...and see if that helps with the warm up. I know the dog won't be complaining either way...

### PaleoDentist — 2012-05-13T07:29:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23612>

sat in 57F water today. 30 min. it felt easy for the first time. I think its time to lower the temp!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-13T17:28:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23665>

Been taking a break because going to get tests this week. A little scary! I hope I can jump right back in!! It's been cold here the last few days (in the 40's at some points, rainy) and I noticed, when outside, I felt cold!! Are you guys getting to the point where you are more comfortable in general cold things?

### LinD — 2012-05-13T18:24:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23683>

You mean like going into the Costco refrigerated section (about 42 or so degrees) in a short-sleeved shirt without hurrying up? Why, yes!

Sprint Samsung Epic

### TerrierMom — 2012-05-13T18:38:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23686>

When taking our dog for a walk in the very early a.m., I pass all sorts of people bundled up in their jackets and sweatshirts.... I smile good morning at them as I walk by in my work slacks and springtime chiffony short sleeved blouse. Hee!

At lunchtime the other day at work, one of the guys mentioned he thought I'd want a coat when we went out, it was a little chilly.... Then we sat outside at lunch....they sat in the sun and shivered with every breeze and I sat in the shade all comfy. Yeah I admit I felt kinda smug!!

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-13T22:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23746>

50F river, 55F weather, pouring rain.

FELT LIKE AN AMAZONIAN.

(Only 10m because I had to run, but it got rid of my can't-sleep headache & foul mood. I was totally warm when I got out of the water and was standing in the cold downpour feeling superhuman!)

### freesia — 2012-05-13T23:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23749>

I want my CT to be like MamaGrok's when it grows up!

I had a very respectable resting temperature last night so I decided to do a cold bath around 8pm. 20 minutes at 57F - coldest I could get it and keep it. A positive development (I think) is that I had no ache in my legs after I got in.

I had no trouble getting to sleep by 10.30pm. (Shame about the 3am waking!)

After my previous bath (2 days ago) I woke twice in the night. Once with strong pain like I haven't felt before in my right knee, then later the same thing in my left knee. Curious stuff! Detox? Then this time I get in and the general leg ache is no longer there.

I _love_ days that feel like progress.

### Melinda — 2012-05-14T00:42:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23757>

The last two days I've tried a different routine for CT. We've added a pre dawn stroll through the neighborhood with skimpy tees and shorts. It's been in the high 50's with no breeze. My skin gets chilled. Got home, put on my swim suit and stepped into the pool. It was 67 and the water felt warm, warmer than 57! It was easy to get in immediately and I stayed in 15 min. Totally felt like I activated the cold receptors in the skin. Felt absolutely wonderful. In the future, I'll use pre skin chilling as a tool to get into cold water.

### primat — 2012-05-14T12:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23862>

### maggie — 2012-05-14T15:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23871>

Maybe this is an Ask Jack question, but I'll put it out here for now see if anyone has had anything similar.

I was in a car accident almost exactly two years ago, left arm broken at the wrist, and lots of deep bruising at shoulder and upper arm and across chest from seat belt.

Recently did CT in (for me) colder than usual water and for longer than usual. Submerged up to ear lobes. Afterwards, got some shooting pains at the fracture site, and my right shoulder blossomed out in my typical detox patch--raised, red, hot and itchy. But my left shoulder--the injured side-- has been excruciatingly painful to the point where I can't raise my arm, and I feel like I'm going to puke if I make a sudden movement. I haven't injured myself recently as far as I know.

I put off taking pain meds because the nsaids are so bad for you, but I broke down last night because I can't do anything and a realtor is supposed to come look at my house Wednesday preparatory to putting it on the market, so I have to get movin'

P.S. What is the least worst pain med?

Thanks.

### Melinda — 2012-05-14T16:09:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23876>

> **primat;23944 wrote:** Well, technically 67 is warmer than 57

I was trying to be funny. The thing is sometimes a temperature that I think I "know" feels really cold. I didn't know I could pre-chill for an easier entry. I just got out of the pool, beautiful sunrise, waiting for the coffee. I'm a little itchy, same as yesterday. I see that as a good sign.

Hope everyone has a great CT today.

### Birdy — 2012-05-14T19:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23928>

Jack mentioned somewhere that he had pain at former surgical/injury sites when doing CT. I have been doing deeper CT this week, and have noticed what seems like a higher estrogen level and also a deep pain in my foot as soon as I wake up. It is so painful I have trouble walking in the morning. Then the pain goes away without a trace after a few minutes. This is the same site as a relatively recent injury. I twisted my foot and it was sore for a bit at the time, but never like this. I think this is so weird.

### maggie — 2012-05-14T19:44:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23939>

Birdy, thanks for letting me know about your foot. How do you identify a higher estrogen level?

I remember Jack mentioned that his meniscus surgery site hurt when CT-ing, and I've also had some pain at old surgical sites, but nothing like this. Yours sounds more like what I'm feeling--deep and debilitating. I put some arnica gel on it today, and it seems to be helping. Also, Art Ayers, on his Cooling Inflammation site, mentioned calming down an inflamed tendon just by leaving some Dr. Bronners peppermint soap on it--so I bought some of that today. Anyway it is better now.

thanks again.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-14T21:54:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-23980>

Cold bath started at 57F, down to 55F. Followed by a cold shower. 45 minutes total. I'm definitely somewhat adapted, since 57F felt like "normal" to me. For a moment I forgot I was in the "cold tub". 55F was less boring  If I had time I would have tried 54F. Well, there's always tomorrow. When I showered I let the water drop on my back - didn't feel cold at all, I used just the cold tap water (52F) and hardly felt a thing. So I guess that's the "numbness"? Or my back just got used to the cold water. A good thing I guess.

I went to the beach today, and it was pretty cold and calm. Unfortunately, it also made the beach undesirable for others, so I cut my swim short after everyone else left. Once the beach was darker, everyone wanted to leave. Next time I will have to go earlier.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-15T16:01:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24191>

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-05-15T20:40:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24312>

35 minutes at 50F today. Oral temperature before was 36.0C, during 36.8C and afterwards 36.5C. An hour after the bath, my oral temperature was 35.7C, which was not so good! Warming up time was very long today, probably because of the break I had from CTing.

### janagram — 2012-05-15T22:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24355>

Spot CTed for the first time last night. Put ice on belly for about 15 min., then on each side of my back by the armpit for 15. (trying to get some BAT)

That was a first...then later...I took a hot bath. Hope it didn't undo the effects. Not clear on whether hot baths are now taboo...anyone?

Oh, and slept well for a change!

### bigknitwit — 2012-05-16T02:40:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24438>

New to this thread (and the board). Cted twice today, water at 50-55ish degrees - 30 minutes in the morning after breakfast and 30 minutes at 7pm. The morning Ct session took way, way longer to warm up. This evenings felt colder while I was in it (light shivering almost right from the beginning, which doesn't usually happen for me until the last 10 minutes). However, no deep afterdrop intense shivering upon getting out. In fact, I'm not shivering almost at all, which is amazing me!! I noticed this with my last evening CT session as well.

I had 10 cryopacs in the tub with me. It still only brings the temp down about 5ish degrees. How does one get the really really cold temps I here about?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-16T03:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24468>

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-16T18:55:17+0300

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-05-16T20:49:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24657>

30 minutes at 50F today. Oral temperature taken before 36.6C, during 37.3C and after 36.8C. For the first time since I started CT I felt that a furnace was starting to fire up after about 5 minutes in the bath. Sadly, the furnace sputtered to a halt after about 15 minutes ... and I had to force myself to complete the 30 min. soak. But warm-up time was quicker, too.

Also, it's been quite cold here and I have no heating on in the house AND my Aga's been turned off, so the warmest room in the house is currently a balmy 10C/50F - to my surprise I am not feeling the need to light fires or turn on heaters (although I may light a fire this evening just to take the chill off the sitting room), and whilst I'm not just in a tee shirt, I am only wearing a tee shirt and a cardigan. Things are obviously improving!

### freesia — 2012-05-17T00:37:30+0300

...30 mins at 55F. Comfortable warm up with the dog sitting on my feet

While I was in the tub a hail storm hit...it was deafening on the roof. Then the room was lit up with lightenening flashes for a few minutes. Wished I was outside in it!

Best CT yet! But I know I can go lower...longer...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-17T05:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24789>

Back to CT today! Met with a new doc, so I was taking a week off prior to tests/draws. Wooot.

I totally didn't want to get in, but here I am! submerged up to the ladies. Water is 55 tonight out of the tap. Going with that as it's late and I want to be able to sleep - and I didn't feel like going to get ice.

So, no noticible difference in CT in a weeks time off. I was starting to shiver at 25 mins at this temp. Think that will be about the same.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-17T17:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24903>

I'm doing 55F today too. 45 minutes. Didn't shiver much when I was submerged (up to my neck) but started to shiver after I got out, as always. Showered at the end. It was cool to feel the cold water dropping on my face. I always notice how clear my mind is when I'm in the cold bath. A fabulous way to start my day!

### Optimalbound — 2012-05-17T21:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-24978>

Just finished my usual 45 minutes bathtub CT session. My tap water is 48Â°F so I am not using any ice yet.

For my first eight or ten sessions, it took me several minutes to slowly lower myself into the water, but lately I am having no problems submerging myself almost as quickly as if the water had been body temperature. Very happy about that.

What is even more promising, is that I've started to notice some BAT action, I think. Nearly three decades ago, I spent a year in the military, and that winter was quite a harsh one, with temperatures dropping below 15Â°F for long periods. For the first couple of weeks, I was always cold and miserable. Then one day, I started noticing that whenever I went outdoors, within minutes this quite wonderful warmth would start radiating from between my shoulder blades. It was like I had a furnace in there! After a couple of months of this, I noticed that my abs were the most ripped that they have ever been, before or since. I didn't see the connection between these events until I read Dr. Kruse's blog.

So now, after little more than a dozen CT sessions, I have started noticing that my upper back is the first area to feel warm afterwards, and also warmer than usual when I am outdoors on cold days. I'm pretty excited about this, and hoping that these are early signs of my BAT making a comeback.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-05-18T02:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25029>

35 minutes in the Atlantic ocean, 51deg. Same spot, the Cove, and one of the same fishermen from last time was up on the jetty! "hey its the mermaid" LOL Played with 2 kids, 9 and 7, whose dad was letting them splash in the waves. The cove is very safe, in the summer chock full of families with little kids (shoot this is where \*I\* used to go as a little kid!)

We did handstands, bodysurfed, looked for starfish on the jetty wall, collected seaweed and mussels. I was up to my neck for most of it (sitting in the water, we weren't in very far) The tide was starting to come in, and all of a sudden we all started to chatter. In from 4:30 to just after 5pm

There is that Satchel Paige saying "how old would you be if you didn't know how old you were" I usually think I'm 23 or 25. This afternoon I felt like I was 12. so much FUN.

Old guy came down from the jetty, asked me how was the water, and why did I do that? I told him I'm trying to stay young

### MartiD — 2012-05-18T20:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25245>

I always take a 20-30 min bath after BAB before work in the mornings. I have done this almost daily since early March. I have not had major detox symptoms other than sinus drainage that I might have had anyway from allergies. I have lots of benefits from CT!

This morning after about 15 minutes at 55 degrees, I had a flush of nausia come over me. I thought I was going to get sick but never did. I was weak after I got out but recovered within about 15 minutes. I have also had some unusual heartburn/acid reflux the last week. I haven't had anything like that since I began losing weight a year ago. I have read and re-read the Leaky Gut info and really don't believe that I have a severe gut issues...I've never had any IBS, diarrhea/constipation, heartburn (other than during pregnancy), stomach cramps, etc. I assume when I was 55 lbs heavier and eating a SAD diet my gut was not great and has improved but I don't believe I have a leaky gut issue so not sure about what happened today or with the acid reflux.

### Pamela — 2012-05-18T20:12:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25256>

Today I decided to go buy some ice so bought 2 7lb bags which didn't do all that much more than using all the ice from my freezer. next time I will see if I can find larger bags of ice for less money. The water got down to 56 and I stayed in about 45 minutes. I took my temperature an hour after coming out and boy it was low at 96.2. Not sure what this means so have to go back and read.

What I do know is the cold baths make me feel better and I look forward to them especially now that it is not freezing outside. That was rough.

### neaderthal — 2012-05-20T07:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25658>

> **Krusing\_tptimal\_in\_CA;25532 wrote:** I wanted to "yay" everyone's CT today but did t want to clog the thread.....but YAY for all of you and keep posting, it inspires people!!
>

> Cheapest ice in my neighborhood - Sam's Club

Krusing, what is the cost for the Sam's Club ice? I don't have a Sam's Club nearby only Costco; however, I might have to check Walmart. I've been managing with freezing water in my empty tupperware storage containers as well as keeping several pitchers of cold water in the refrig that I add to the tub. I was able to get our So CA water temp down to 40 degrees a couple of days ago. I'm reluctant to spend $ on ice since I need it for food--obviously, one of the reasons my freezer is empty, & I can fit the tupperware containers in it

### MartiD — 2012-05-20T17:44:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25700>

> **MartiD;25327 wrote:** I always take a 20-30 min bath after BAB before work in the mornings. I have done this almost daily since early March. I have not had major detox symptoms other than sinus drainage that I might have had anyway from allergies. I have lots of benefits from CT!
>
>
>
> This morning after about 15 minutes at 55 degrees, I had a flush of nausia come over me. I thought I was going to get sick but never did. I was weak after I got out but recovered within about 15 minutes. I have also had some unusual heartburn/acid reflux the last week. I haven't had anything like that since I began losing weight a year ago. I have read and re-read the Leaky Gut info and really don't believe that I have a severe gut issues...I've never had any IBS, diarrhea/constipation, heartburn (other than during pregnancy), stomach cramps, etc. I assume when I was 55 lbs heavier and eating a SAD diet my gut was not great and has improved but I don't believe I have a leaky gut issue so not sure about what happened today or with the acid reflux.

It occurred to me the other day that I recently added MCT to my coffee in morning to replace CO. I wonder of that could also be causing my stomach issues. I had a bad episode yesterday after eating a handful of pistachios and felt nauseous for several hours.

### freesia — 2012-05-21T00:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25841>

(I really thought my 8oz steaks at sunrise would be the culprit of any indigestion, and that never even looked like happening once!)

I'm getting my water to 55F with the addition of about 5lbs of ice and adding a further 5lbs ten minutes in. Bathtime is up to 25 minutes and recovery is taking a couple of hours.

### indigogirl — 2012-05-21T02:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-25851>

Haven't CT'ed in a couple of weeks.

Tonight 30 minutes at 58 degrees.

My feet are still tingling 1/2 hour out of the bath!

I usually CT at 62 degrees. At 58 degrees my toes hurt and my soles were numb. I kept trying to envision my feet wrapped in a wool blanket near a roaring fire in an attempt to stay comfortable.

4 degrees made a big difference in my comfort level.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-21T03:31:15+0300

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-21T04:38:38+0300

25m (longest yet) in what was probably 55-60 degrees. FELT GREAT! No shivering.

I also took my 7, 5, & 2yos and told them I'd give them each a dollar if they got in. They all loved it, and the oldest two kept going in slowly for the entire time, till they got about chest deep and stayed. I gave them $3.

I plan to eat a little fruits, squash, and tubers in the summer and fall, but will stay pretty low carb most days, since i still have about 20 pounds of fat to get rid of. ### freesia — 2012-05-29T00:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28086>

I have a DIY ice set up too. I use 6 - 8 big yogurt pottles (500 and 850mls) and after the ice is half melted the water is generally 54 - 55F. It puts pressure on Freezer Real Estate but I'm grateful not to have to go out to buy ice.

When you say 50 is easier than, say 58. Do you mean it's easier to get into? Easier to stay comfortable in for longer? Is there much difference to the warm up?

50 (lower if possible) is where I'm headed and with Winter only officially a few days away I know I'll get there, but I'm intrigued to know what I can look forward to with "easier".

I took another 30 minute bath @ 55 last night. It was harder than the previous night because I was still suffering from the "slammed" feeling induced by my last bath.

Has anyone needed to pace their baths to cope better with detox?

I wish I knew what I was detoxing specifically so I could pick just the right thing to help! Tonight I might throw a bunch of activated charcoal at it and see if I wake feeling any less freight-train-run-over-like.

I'm impressed by some of the times here 45 mins....an hour...

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-29T00:51:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28088>

I never felt the lower the temperature the easier, but I've only gone to 53F the coldest. Well comparing with 70F, 53F wasn't easier or harder since I slowly lowered the temperature of my cold bath while increasing my tolerance of coldness. I actually like the feeling of coldness... I guess I may not feel as excited if I felt "numbness" instead. I don't know, I'll just have to find out for myself... when the fall arrives here in a few months.

I do find I have more detox when I CT longer, or colder. I have a lot to do at school/work so I don't want to handle heavy detox... so I'm taking it slow. Just cold bath in 56F, no ice for me for now. When the fall arrives I should be so cold adapted that I hope colder bath won't lead to much detox by then.

### Kiera2 — 2012-05-29T04:13:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28114>

I'm back into baths again, after 4 weeks break. I am so pleased to discover the tap water is now only 10C. So no more buying ice! And surprised that I could get in and lay back, no problem. No shivering. Of course I felt really cold, but I didn't mind; it was soothing.

After 30 minutes CTing the water had warmed to only 11.9C or 53.5F, and my skin was nice and pink. A few minutes after getting out, the shivering started, and continued for 20 minutes. And my feet and my bum hurt! That's a new experience.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-29T04:54:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28127>

> **Cavemam;28180 wrote:** Went for a swim at union reservoir!
>
>
>
> 64 degrees. No idea how long in - cant swim with my phone.
>
>
>
> Feelin accomplished.

Nice job!!! I'm a little surprised/disappointed it's that warm already, though.

### Inger — 2012-05-29T13:52:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28153>

Was a little scary today.

I had one small glass of malbec with my lunch (steak tartar) and right after I went to the river for CT. 25 minutes at 55 degrees F. Most days I do just around 5 minutes but twice or more / day.. today I stayed a bit longer. I shivered the last minutes, but it was actually quite nice and I was not feeling that cold. Then I went up, and layed in the sun to warm up.. and then I all of a sudden felt so strange.. like a little dizzy.. like I could not get so well oxygen, even if I was breathing normally. It felt like something was not right with my heart really. I headed to the shower and took my longest, hot shower ever. Uh. It helped. Now, after the shower, I feel quite normal again. Huh I got scared. I will never ever CT after drinking wine anymore! That must be no good for your heart. Jack said not to do it.. now I know..

Inger

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-29T17:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28171>

That's really scary Inger! Glad you're feeling OK now. Thanks for sharing...

### Inger — 2012-05-29T19:24:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28202>

### kathylu — 2012-05-29T19:45:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28208>

New experience for me...I didn't want to get out of the bath yesterday! I usually do 60 minutes at 55-58 degrees, having a hard time getting any colder since tap water is 68 degrees now. I sat in with water up to my breasts for 30 minutes and then laid back to get my upper back in the water for 30 minutes. It was really pleasant! So, I stayed in for another 15 minutes. Then a cold shower that actually felt hot! I hope this continues, since adaptation has seemed slow for me.

### Birdy — 2012-05-30T03:13:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28289>

today I did the tub after a long break. I really missed it! The tap water with ice bottles felt colder than usual. I realize that in order to get the coldest water, I'll have to refill the tub every day with cold water, it heats up too much for the next day. I have enough places that need watering, it is just that some days I don't like pails of water to be my LHT choice.

### youthfuliz — 2012-05-30T04:42:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28312>

Did 2 CT bath today. 20 minutes each, 56F. Hope my feet warm up quickly for bed.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-30T06:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28319>

No CT for me today - have to do some overnight medical testing and didn't want to mess it up by shivering halfway through the whole thing. Hopefully I'll still get a good night's sleep.

### janagram — 2012-05-30T09:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28328>

After a long break I had a great time in an Olympic size pool on the lovely grounds of a spa in No Cal! It was very cold but dont know how cold.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-05-30T18:23:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28368>

30 mins at 55F today. Oral temperature before was 36.6C, during 37.1C and afterwards 37.0C. Small changes, but at least the temperature rise is there. Warm-up time is not good today, for some reason - lots of that beneath the skin shivering!

### quelsen — 2012-05-30T18:51:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28376>

I notice that i stoped shivering about 2 weeks ago. specifically after my 2 one hour 32F sessions.

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-30T19:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28378>

I think I must try the deep CT... don't like the shivering part  I don't shiver anymore if I use tap water, only if I use ice to get it down to 50 degrees.

### Lyndra — 2012-05-30T20:16:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28385>

> **freesia;28185 wrote:**
>
> When you say 50 is easier than, say 58. Do you mean it's easier to get into? Easier to stay comfortable in for longer? Is there much difference to the warm up?

I don't find it any easier to get into a colder bath - it's always a bit of a shock - but I don't shiver as much at say 47-50 as I do at 55-60. Plus, I feel much more comfortable at the lower temperature and feel like I can stay in longer without constantly wonder if it's time yet to get out. I have only done one bath at about 42 and it was fine on my body but I couldn't keep my feet or my fingers in very long. One of my goals is to help the arthritis in my thumbs so I need to use water that's at the "just right" temp for me.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-05-30T22:55:33+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28483>

30 minutes cold bath at 58F... tap water is warming up. Followed with a cold shower... feeling good now.  I warmed up within 3 minutes I think.

### kathylu — 2012-05-31T04:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28485>

### Kiera2 — 2012-05-31T04:34:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28490>

The bathroom was 10.1C and after 30 minutes in the bath, the water warmed up to 12.1C. Very pink/red skin, but I didn't shiver at all, and not shivering 15 minutes after getting out. But my feet hurt again.

### kathylu — 2012-05-31T05:31:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28505>

> **Anna K.;28599 wrote:** How long do you stay in 50-55 degrees?
>
> Most people shiver at this temp.
>
> Also, where do you live? may be you are already cold adapted!
>
> Lucky you!!!

I stay in for an hour daily, sometimes a bit longer. I live near Washington DC, so it doesn't get very cold here. It takes me a long time to warm up, but no shivering. I do notice that I am starting to like the baths...I really dreaded them at first. Started CT on 3/30/12. It's getting harder to get the temp below 55 degrees because the tap water is pretty warm, around 68 degrees.

### kathylu — 2012-05-31T05:33:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28507>

My temp today was 98 degrees getting into the bath. When I got out an hour later, temp had risen to 99.4. So I guess something is happening...

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-05-31T18:45:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28630>

30 minutes at 54F today (still normal cold tapwater - no added ice!). Oral temperature before 36.5C, during 37.2C, after 37.0C Recovery time was quick and easy today with, for the first time ever, no shivering at all! Maybe, just maybe, I'm becoming cold adapted? I do know that my husband is working from home today and has lit a fire in our office - he's struggling with teeshirt, shirt and jumper, and I'm too hot in a teeshirt! Always used to be the other way around.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-05-31T20:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28660>

> **Anna K.;28743 wrote:** Nice! way to go! can't wait till I stop shivering...

Bath temp last night was a too warm 67F (my apt is set to 65F ish). I had a lefotver 1 gallon tub from my Nutiva CO, filled it with water and wiggled it into my freezer to turn to ice. Dumped that into that bath last night and it brought the temp down to 64F. When I got out after an hour it was back up to 67F so I guess that is a good sign. Had really crappy unrestful sleep though. I think my CT was too close to bed time. So today I have perpetual goosebumps. I don't really feel cold, it is almost like I'm a little sensitive to the cold or something because every time I move another wave of goosebumps. It is very strange, totally new to me. Anyone else have this strange sensation bascially all day after CT?

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-01T04:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28803>

Was kinda lazy today. TOM is approaching. Just had a quick cold shower to cool myself down... it's summer here.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-06-01T10:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28823>

Our hot water has packed up - so CT for the whole family until it gets fixed  Having said which, my shower this morning (which was, of necessity, cold as usual) was horrible. I wonder whether it was because the choice was taken away? Will have a bath later, but had the weird sensation of feeling really hot after breakfast this morning, and still feel as though I am radiating heat. I'm taking it that this is a good sign!

### Kiera2 — 2012-06-01T10:20:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28824>

I did 35 minutes @ 10-11C again. Was quite shivery going in; like, OMG this is cold! ^%$#ing cold!! By the time my belly was covered I had stopped shivering, and it wasn't too bad. My feet didn't hurt, and no shivering afterward, but I cheated and warmed up in the winter sunshine.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-06-01T13:37:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28828>

So, 35 minutes at 53.2F this morning (bath). Oral temperature before 36.4C, during 37.0C and after 36.8C. For the first time ever, I recorded the temperature of the bath when I got out, and it was 60.3F! It's clear that something is beginning to happen. Whilst I am not shivering in a classic sense after this bath, I am getting involuntary, slight subcutaneous tremors. Very weird indeed as I don't actually feel that cold!

### nonchalant — 2012-06-01T18:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28877>

Hi Oliviascotland. I know what you mean with the slight tremors. You can talk normally, and most people would not notice that you are shivering at all. Yes, you don't feel cold, but the tremors last, on and off, for about an hour. Neat, eh?

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-06-01T18:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28886>

It is rather, isn't Nonchalant! I'm constantly amazed at the changes CTing brings about, and it never seems to be the same two days running. But I am hoping that this is beginning to be a sign that I'm starting to cold adapt a bit.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-28955>

### janagram — 2012-06-02T03:33:58+0300

### freesia — 2012-06-02T22:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29095>

### Optimalbound — 2012-06-02T23:17:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29110>

Picture from my CT on Wednesday. I stayed for half of this week at a spa hotel with its own little beach and pier by a fjord with snow capped mountains in the background (just outside the frame). Sea temperature about 13Â°C (56Â°F), I'm guessing. My non cold adapted friend gave up after a few minutes, while I stayed in for 35 minutes and only got out because I needed to get dressed for dinner (seafood, obviously).

For the past two months, as I have gradually become adapted, my CT sessions have gone from only just bearable to comfortable to downright addictive. The ocean swims are my favorites, by far. I had no idea I could do this!

Funny thing is, none of my collegues who were watching from dry land asked how I was able to do it, or why I did it. All they said was "you're crazy" and "you're one step away from the asylum". Ah, those neolithic thoughts...

The hotel also had several indoor and outdoor pools, including a 9Â°C ice pool. Would make a nice venue for a CT convention..

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/29210=223-IMG_9925.jpg)

(Thanks to Abbas for the rubbish phone pic )

### Optimalbound — 2012-06-03T20:03:46+0300

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-04T03:19:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29292>

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-04T17:43:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29367>

Yesterday evening I had a "bad" CT session. It was barely 25 minutes at 54 degrees. I shivered almost the whole time, and it took me forever to warm up afterwards, like udring the first week of CTing. Just the day prior, I did 25 minutes at 51 degrees, and barely shivered during, or after. It was as if my BAT wasn't working yesterday lol!! I know it's there, because my post-bath warm=up period has dramatically decreased since starting this 8ish weeks ago. I am wondering if eating too many carbs hinders the BAT heating process. I ate about a cup of blackberries with my dinner meal (4:30), plus the carbs in the kale and salad.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-06-04T18:49:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29377>

Haven't been able to CT today (it's difficult to explain to builders!) other than my usual cold shower, and probably won't be able to tomorrow, either :-( Hopefully Wednesday will be less busy, as well as Friday ...

### freesia — 2012-06-05T00:08:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29430>

> **bigknitwit;29467 wrote:** Yesterday evening I had a "bad" CT session. It was barely 25 minutes at 54 degrees. I shivered almost the whole time, and it took me forever to warm up afterwards, like udring the first week of CTing. Just the day prior, I did 25 minutes at 51 degrees, and barely shivered during, or after. It was as if my BAT wasn't working yesterday lol!! I know it's there, because my post-bath warm=up period has dramatically decreased since starting this 8ish weeks ago. I am wondering if eating too many carbs hinders the BAT heating process. I ate about a cup of blackberries with my dinner meal (4:30), plus the carbs in the kale and salad.

I haven't personally noticed a connection between my less well tolerated sessions and carbs, or anything I eat, yet. I typically eat approx 15g carbs and 1 - 2 tablespoons of fat with my evening meal and try to do CT within 1 - 2 hours. (I feel pretty sure I'd suffer more if I went short on fat, but I guess I haven't really tested the carb thing.)

What does definitely affect my tolerance is how "hung over" I am with detox symptoms from my previous session. At the moment I'm needing to limit my cold baths to every second day to help cope with detox.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-06T00:37:07+0300

### Kiera2 — 2012-06-06T00:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29628>

I woke up yesterday feeling like crap. It took me a while to realise it was the 'almost headache when about to get my period' kind of crap. I haven't had a period since early March and I felt truly dreadful. So I jumped in the bath, thinking it could only help. I stayed 40 minutes at 11C, and the headache had eased off a bit. But after I got out, I got cold; really cold. My toenails turned blue, and I shivered and shivered, and felt worse and worse. I finally went to bed after lunch and shivered for an hour, even after turning on the electric blanket! What the hell was that about? I had to get up after another hour, and the headache continued until it was time to cook dinner. And no period. Feeling better today, though still a bit 'blah'.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-06-06T04:03:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29663>

### freesia — 2012-06-07T00:15:27+0300

### ehanner@gmail.com — 2012-06-07T00:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29800>

I'm doing straight cold showers for CT. My cold well water is at 55F and I stay in for 10 or more minutes, moving the shower head around to cover everything but the top of my head. The only place I get a cold freeze sensation where it hurts is doing the forehead (trigeminal) area. Everywhere else I don't feel the cold water at all. My belly temp is normally 90F before CT and 62-70F after depending on how long I stay in. Even though I am only doing showers for 10 minutes, I was adapted when I swam in Lake Michigan a couple weeks ago water temp 49F. I stayed in 20 minutes without any trouble at all. Pretty amazing actually. So from my perspective, it wasn't hard to become adapted to cold. I do CT most every day like this.

### Optimalbound — 2012-06-07T10:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29865>

Did my CT in the North Sea yesterday (on this beach [http://norwayrecommended.com/Content/Managed/Image/orrestrand-just-6e0f5ec48eb9.JPG](http://http://norwayrecommended.com/Content/Managed/Image/orrestrand-just-6e0f5ec48eb9.JPG))

Up to my neck for 35 min in ~54F. Quite a bit of swells. Started to shiver a bit and lose feeling in hands and feet at that point, but no real discomfort. Felt great.

Funny thing is, I had forgotten to eat breakfast and decided ad hoc to make it a fasting day. Wasn't hungry for lunch either. Had a couple of spoonfuls of CO before the CT and that CO was all I ate, yesterday. This morning I had my usual BAB despite still not being very hungry.

CT really drives my hunger to the ground.

### Optimalbound — 2012-06-08T00:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-29991>

> **Anna K.;29983 wrote:** by adding salt, salty water has a lower freezing point. The higher the concentration of salt, the lower the freezing point drops.
>
> [http://www.worsleyschool.net/science/files/saltandfreezing/ofwater.html](http://http://www.worsleyschool.net/science/files/saltandfreezing/ofwater.html)

Thanks, Anna! That's useful to know, although I don't think I'm quite ready for deep CT, yet.

### Qodesh — 2012-06-08T17:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30141>
