### Nymue — 2012-06-10T16:18:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30402>

> **Qodesh;30243 wrote:** 30 minutes at 50 is my level for now. Nice and toasty IN, shiver starts about 10 mins out, lasting for about 20 mins.
>
> I'm in S. Florida and planning a trip to Seattle in July. I plan on Puget Sound swimming with a nice sunny warm-up.

Hey Qodesh, I'm in S. FL too. I've been getting my water down into the upper 40s lately, which I'm thrilled about. Just wondering what your method is for getting it down to 50?

I use big tupperware containers that I keep in the freezer, as well as 4 very full gallon ziploc bags of frozen water, a big pitcher of cold water that stays in the back of the fridge, which I then fill up with ice from the icemaker and dump that in. That would get me into the mid to low 50s.

Then I bought 4 flexible ice sheets on Amazon, with the intention of using them to spot CT. However, I've instead been putting them in the tub as it fills up and that drops the temp a few degrees more.

It's definitely an involved process! I envy those that can just turn on the tap, let the tub fill, and get in! I would LOVE to be able to do a croak soak, but it's just not possible here!!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-10T16:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30404>

Last night I got back to spot CT on my carotids (I have a pituitary cyst that I am looking to obliterate) and it always causes intense pain for the first 10 minutes or so, Eventually I can keep the ice on them but oh how I scream and carry on...LOL...anyone have any advice on that??

### Nymue — 2012-06-11T00:08:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30447>

30 mins at 46Â°. This was my easiest CT so far. I had been debating over feet in or feet out, because they take SO long to warm up afterwards, and I felt like I was able to stay in longer with them out.

My internal debate is over - feet in. It was much easier, especially as I was able to relax my body and not keep myself balanced while propping just my feet on the edges of the tub.

I also think that the dips are more effective (for me, anyway), with feet in. I experienced a lot more cold diuresis this time.

It took about an hour to fully warm up afterwards, but I've found that keeping myself busy helps. So I CT on an empty stomach, and then cook something when I get out (chopping can be tough when you're shivering, though!).

I still haven't found any increased tolerance to heat. It's 93Â° outside today and as humid as pea soup, and I was sweating bullets. Still going to push forward! Today's CT felt AWESOME!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-11T02:34:03+0300

### Qodesh — 2012-06-11T18:49:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30538>

@Nymue:

Sorry it took so long to reply, I typically don't computer on the weekends.

I have a rubbermaid 100 gal tub, but I only fill it about 50 gals+. I also have a chest freezer full of tupperware ice. I have a little wire cart that I roll the ice to the tub with. Yep, I'd love to have an aquarium chiller or something of that sort. I use light wool socks to help with the feet now that I'm at 50 degrees. I plan to keep it here until it's comfy, then drop down to mid 40's for awhile.

I eat a lot of fat (fatty beef/bacon/coco-crack) and a bitter melon capsule, before I "chill". It seems to help me stay nice and toasty in the tub. I"m finding I could stay in longer than the 30 mins, but I do get board. I will push the time a bit as my warm up speeds up.

I'm going to Seattle in July and a swim in 50 degree Puget Sound is on the list!

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-06-11T20:25:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30560>

20 mins at 55F today - was short only because I found it really hard for some reason. Oral temp. beforehand was 36.6C, during 36.8C and after 36.9C. Recovery time was very quick, with no shivering, just a feeling of a furnace being turned on after about 10 minutes.

### Cowboy — 2012-06-12T01:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30620>

I have been CTing since April with cold showers, cold packs, and cold water therapy circulating pads. Today I did my first big tub immersion. Got a 150 gal Rubbermaid tank at a farm supply, filled it with water from the tap, and climbed in. Water temp was 52 degrees ( living in the Rockies drinking water that is snow melt), I lasted 45 minutes.

Had to leave my feet and arms out at first, but gradually was able to submerge all but my toes and my hands and head. Got out and was just about ready to shiver. Probably could have stayed longer but I was getting pretty cold. Now 5 hours later I am still a bit on the cool side, but feel pretty good. Guess the ice packs and cold therapy pads did get me aclimated. Will try it again tomorrow.

Now need to get a pool filter system and some sort of anti bacterial (chlorine or bromide) treatment that is normally used in hot tubs. Our water should stay in the 50s for the next month at least and we are still getting temps at night in the forties. Most of the summer it hits lows of 60 at night.

### johnnyb — 2012-06-13T07:11:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30851>

Odd CT today. Only 22 minutes in the Pacific. I was getting occasional shivers. Maybe from the recovering sunburn. On the upshot I feel great better then after any other CT session. Drip dry in the setting sun as the clouds slowly came in. Life is good.

### Qodesh — 2012-06-13T16:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-30889>

water temp was 50 going in and 47 with ice still floating by the end of my 30 mins.

It's strange, but at 47 degrees, I got to the point where it didn't hurt anymore. No prickle feeling, just rosey red and sort of numb. I could've stayed longer, but wanted to check my warm-up times before I extend the length.

Warm-up was quick. This is my new temp goal to shoot for.

### Birdy — 2012-06-14T03:49:58+0300

I have been away from CT and finally decided that I absolutely needed it today. Tub was filled with bottles of ice and a huge jug of cold water plus tapwater, so colder than usual.

### kathylu — 2012-06-14T17:53:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-31129>

Struggling to get my tub under 55 degrees. Adding 50 lbs of ice frozen in 1 qt bottles. I let the bath sit for 30 minutes before I get in, that seems to be the coldest point. Starting from tap water at 68 degrees. I am so jealous of you folk who still have 50 degree water!

Yes, the colder I can get the temp, the more comfortable it is and the longer I like to stay in. I usually get out at 60 minutes though, because of time constraints...have other things to do too!

### Optimalbound — 2012-06-15T22:58:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-31406>

Just came back from swimming in a fjord for 40 minutes. My longest ocean CT so far. Really lovely. Funny thing is, I was guesstimating that the temperature was about 16Â°C, so I was quite surprised when I activated my wristband dive computer after 30 minutes and it measured a mere 12Â°C (54Â°F).

Only mild shivering afterwards and nice and pink even after a warm shower.

On a previous ocean beach CT swim, I swam past a couple of teenage surfers who were waiting for the next good wave. They were fully clad in neoprene, with only their faces exposed. One of them caught sight of me as I swam past, and I heard him say to the other one: "Dude, that guy behind you really OWNS us!"

### kathylu — 2012-06-18T14:47:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-31908>

CT has been good for the last two days. I still feel a little anxiety going in "oh no, I have to do this again...", but once I'm in it's nice! I'm using sixty lbs of ice now and it's still hard to get the bath cold enough (56 last night). I'm dreading how difficult it will be to get cold water once the temps are in the upper 90's outside.

One small benefit is that I am doing bicep curls and upright rows with a 30 lb bag of ice in each hand...my arms are starting to look good! LHT you know.

### Stonecold — 2012-06-20T01:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-32281>

Today's CT was awesome! Big breakfast followed by 10 minute or so cold shower. Blasted the A/C all the way to work (one hour.) After work bought a 300 gallon stock tank. Blasted the A/C on the way home. Wore my ice vest while I waited for new stock tank to fill (almost two hours I'm on a well.) Did 15 minutes or so in stock tank while Labrador drank and tried to figure out how to get in. I guess I'll have to put some blocks next to the tank so he can climb in too. Cold shower after to rinse off and air dried under ceiling fan. I feel great!

And thank goodness for spell check or you guys would think that I am a moron.  .... well you may anyway. But I feel incredible so I don't care.

### freesia — 2012-06-20T06:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-32351>

I caught a virus that came home from school with my youngest child so I missed a few days.

Gratified to find that getting right back in is no more difficult for the break.

45 mins at 54F. Detox symptoms have reduced significantly with the addition of DIM, calcium d glucarate and i3c...I'm actually able to do consecutive days now. Yay!!!

ETA: Now that detox symptoms have lessened I have noticed that my aerobic capacity has improved significantly from where I started 10 weeks ago. I also have a bit of muscle going on! I didn't have weight to lose but my waist has got smaller...like it was before kids.

I've had ME/CFS since one of the outbreaks in the 1980's and fitness, body composition...(and everything really) has been on a slow decline.

In my world increased aerobic capacity is HUGE!

### freesia — 2012-06-20T10:03:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-32358>

I also need to report  - it's only taken ten weeks, but - my temperature is jumping up in anticipation of taking a cold bath!

The last 3 times it's jumped 1 - 1 1/2 degrees C as I'm running the bath and putting the ice in.

Pretty rapt about that...I don't really get cold in there any more. Time to go lower I know.

### Cowboy — 2012-06-21T17:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-32704>

Been full imersion CTing for the last couple of weeks. Added another session yesterday evening, and took about half the night to warm up. Ended up putting a blanket and the bed to get warm and my wife wouldn't touch me as I was too cold. The water was only 58 degrees but for some reason the evening CT seems to me to feel colder thanthe early morning. Also find that after 30 minutes in the morning it takes me a good 30 minutes to regain good keyboarding coordination. I can still type but slowly till I warm up.

### freesia — 2012-06-22T23:59:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33034>

40 minutes at 53F last night with big chunks of ice left in the bath when I'd finished. The modest drop in water temp was enough to promote another round of sinus draining. Not uncomfortable, but intriguing...

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-23T01:46:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33066>

40 mins at 49F. Since my tap water is now 85F, I fiind that making huge blocks of ice from large plastic containers is best, as they continue to cool down the water as they melt. And they sound like little icebergs hitting the side of the tub, which makes me smile.

I made 5 playlists, each 40 mins. One for each day of the week (I'm doing 5x/week). I close my eyes and wait for the playlist to get through.

Since my tub isn't deep enough for full immersion, I do about half the time on my back, then I turn over and prop up on my elbows to get the front of my body. I find I get more of my body covered this way, plus, it really gives my skin the pink-ness all over.

### freesia — 2012-06-23T05:43:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33114>

Pam - I might just try the turning over trick too. My tub is on the shallow side.

I was wondering about using my old coconut oil pails to make ice. They're 4 litres - I guess they'd freeze overnight?...(One way to find out!)

Getting your water down to 49 from 85 is quite an achievement! Obviously those icebergs are effective.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-25T00:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33369>

I took two weeks' break, and am back to CT today. 20 minutes bath followed by a shower... 62F. Felt like the most natural thing to do.

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-25T03:48:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33387>

> **freesia;33245 wrote:** Pam - I might just try the turning over trick too. My tub is on the shallow side.
>
> I was wondering about using my old coconut oil pails to make ice. They're 4 litres - I guess they'd freeze overnight?...(One way to find out!)
>
> Getting your water down to 49 from 85 is quite an achievement! Obviously those icebergs are effective.

I've had to become creative. I use plastic cups (the throwaway kind) to make large ice "cubes". I do use coconut oil pails. I also bought some large Glad freezer plastic containers (they say "BPA Free"; specially made for the freezer). It takes 24 Hrs. in my freezer to get them all rock solid. I haul them downstairs to my tub (I have a 3-story home; wouldn't you know it, the kitchen is on the 2nd floor but both bathrooms are on floors 1 & 3!). I use a digital thermometer, which I keep checking every 10 mins or so.

The neat thing is that as the large "icebergs" melt, they continue to cool the water. So when I get in, it's around 57. By the time I'm out, it's 52.

The only way for me to get it lower than 52F is to empty out my icemaker, which I also do sometimes if I feel I won't get the water cool enough.

The turning-over thing is something I can't take credit for. I read it on one of the threads here; I'm sorry I can't remember who. But it's an awesome way to get the front part of your body (yes, the girls!) fully immersed.

did a 2nd CT session today in my new 100gal tank... hit it right around 10am or so... the water wasn't incredibly cold (Used as much Ice as we had made + Ice packs... but w/the wind and the overcast skys I had goose bumps going for 1/2 the cT.. loving the big tub got to figure out how to drain the damn thing... for cleaning... as I have no fancy set up! ### freesia — 2012-06-25T23:35:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33534>

I've done baths 6 days of the last 7 and the past 2 days the water has been 52F getting in and 55F getting out 45 minutes later. Warm up is easy.

I feel I'm really getting somewhere now....and the every day approach (made possible by the addition of DIM and ca d-glucarate) has snowballed progress.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-27T01:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33798>

50 minutes in 62F bath. I used ice to cool down the tap water from 63F, then with me and 11 pounds of ice in the tub the temperature stayed at 62F for 50 minutes. At the end I discovered that cold tap water can feel warm!!

### vkiernan — 2012-06-27T03:03:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33826>

I have to say it was the pits and the worst one since starting. I didn't want to do it in the first place but I did it anyway. The first 5 minutes or so I shivered and I never do that. Then, I could only go for 20 minutes total. I usually do at least 35 or so. My finger even froze and it wasn't even in the water. WTH???? Don't know what is up but I am not liking it. I stopped my HC completely today so maybe that is part of it. Oh well, tomorrow I get another chance.

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-27T18:00:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-33950>

I posted something similar on this thread a few pages back, and someone (I can't remember who, unfortunately) said they felt that way when they were overdoing it with CTing. Now, when it gets that way, I take a day off and then the following day is always better.

That said, I always struggle to get to the 25 minute mark. It seems it's all I can do to get to 30, and beyond - ultra challenging. Somedays I'll do 20 minutes 3 times though.

### jmathisen — 2012-06-28T05:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34107>

I bought some 1 gallon storage containers and freeze them (5), then bust the ice up with a sledgehammer (not as tough as it looks, the ice cracks from getting it out of the container, a gentle tap breaks it down). More surface area the better. Most of my baths are around 40 degrees, but for some reason last night, when i got out, doing the same thing I always do at the same time of day, I shivered for at least an hour. I mean a teeth rattling, butt-shaking all over shiver. Core temp was fine, down like .3 degrees from when I got in. Not sure why, as it's been going just fine for weeks. But be that as it may...

Cheap metal muffin tins make nice sized ice cubes for baths as well.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-30T01:27:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34475>

ice does not do well in 107 degree heat........but I did it.

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-30T04:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34518>

What's working for me is making large ice blocks with plastic containers, as they continue to cool the water the entire time I'm in. When I got in tonight, it was 57F; when I was done, it was 51F.

I've set myself a goal of 5x/week @ 50-53F for 40 mins. I took the last 2 days off and today, I got back in to make sure I got my 5 x in this week. I had not only really missed it, but I found it was easier. Easier to stay in, easier to walk around naked afterward, easier to avoid putting on a heavy robe and just let myself cool off naturally.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-30T16:44:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34585>

50 minutes in bath + shower. Used 12 pounds ice made with plastic bottles. Water temperature between 62F and 60F. Glad that I felt a bit cold!

### freesia — 2012-07-01T06:04:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34750>

I did an hour last night - 52 getting in and 53 getting out. (The biggest iceblocks melt slowly over the hour and keep it nice and cold.)

I had serious shivering in my big leg muscles 1/4 hour after getting out and my feet felt cold all night. Today my feet feel too hot

More evidence of progress:

Yesterday it was 46 outside with a cool wind which felt fresh off the Antarctic! Waiting to cross a road I noticed most people around me were huddled in their coats and scarves and I was comfortable in a light jumper and lightweight jacket. When I took my child's hand to cross the road he mentioned how nice and warm my hand was! I've _never_ been the one with warm hands!!

Coming back to the car later we turned straight into the cold wind. I felt it hit my face...not unpleasant at all...and I swear I felt my body temperature rise just like when I'm getting into a cold bath!

Another thing, a biggie for me - a hallmark symptom of my illness is post exertional malaise. So anyway it seems I can suddenly climb a pretty steep hill like I did on Friday and have zero negative after effect in my muscles! Climbing the same hill in early April I was stiff and sore for several days afterwards.

Loving this progress.

### WestmorelandGrape — 2012-07-02T07:21:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34961>

Yesterday we went to the beach for the day. I went swimming in Lake Superior for two hours. It was delicious! I was the only one there swimming, few were wading, most were staying out of the water. I've never been able to swim in Lake Superior unless it was August of a warm summer. I've always been able to wade, but never swim in Superior. I got goosebumps, but never shivered. I guess because I was moving around a bit playing in the waves. I'm loving CT!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-02T16:24:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-34998>

CT has been limited to ice packs or wallowing in the pool since the storms have wiped out the Ice Supplies in the DC metro area. more evidence I. NEED. AN. ICE. MACHINE!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-02T16:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-35003>

Yesterday: 30min at 44 degrees, 60min at 55 degrees, then spot CT front of trunk for ~2-3hrs (lost track) with a couple huge trash bags full of ice. I slept well though a little short. Fell asleep at 9pm the moment my head hit the pillow. Woke up at 4am but stayed in bed til 6:30ish. HR was down to 49 (good) and I could barely feel my heart thump this morning when I woke.

### freesia — 2012-07-05T03:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-35567>

I finally got my water temperature down to 50F....by defrosting our small freezer and dumping a bucket extra of ice in. Now I have more room in that freezer for icebricks. Never thought it would take so much ice to bring 56 tapwater down to 50!

I'm already worrying about next summer...

Spent 70 minutes in there. Temp was 52F on exit.

I never get cold until a bit after I get out. Then I get a temp drop and really intense **yawning** (anyone else get this?), and my feet turn ice cold for a bit.

> Symptoms of cortisol-is-in-excess-of-T3 (same as T3-is-too-low-compared-to-cortisol) are temporary excess cortisol causing ACTH suppression, causing too low preg, causing drowsiness and / or the yawns - try reducing preg.

Fascinating.... as I get the same and need to sleep or else 20-30 min after CT. I think CT can induce this effect if your HPA/thyroid isn't up to speed.

### NWgirl — 2012-07-08T00:38:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-36142>

Took my 4th bath last night in 55 degrees... Luckily our well water comes out this cold even though the weather is starting to get warm here. But I think I need to work on getting it a little colder. I have done up to 35 minutes (full body) but last night I could only do 25. From the middle of my back to shoulders are so sensitive that is the most difficult area. But now it only takes me a few minutes to sit down. Slept HARD last night wrapped in a blanket UNDER the covers. Having detox symptoms... Some behavioral issues plus back of neck and head is aching. Plus my feet have a hard time warming up after but the are fine by morning. I really want the detox symptoms to go away I feel kind of crappy and don't want to do anything. I hate that because I'm not getting anything done around here.

### freesia — 2012-07-11T04:04:49+0300

Love the pics Lauren and Shijin - thanks for sharing

I CT'd myself to a screeching halt after a couple of extra cold (for me) baths @ 48F. Every time I go lower I get a round of sinus clearing, then deeper detox. I was hit with symptoms of oxidative stress...probably more toxins than my system can handle right now. Extra lipo glutathione is helping and I added curcumin today. Pretty sure I have fast phase 1 detox and slow phase 2 (genetics still pending) so continuing on best I can now after a couple of days off.

### HCB — 2012-07-11T04:57:59+0300

Had my third today in 50 degrees. I had some little dots on my forehead after that are gone now. I placed a bag of ice on my belly and put two more in the tub after I acclimated. ### Shijin13 — 2012-07-17T18:05:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-38729>

It's been close to a week since my last deep CT in the tub. I'm not sure if I dropped the temp or what but I've had goose bumps the whole time and haven't fully submerged yet. I needed this. I'm going to try to hang on for another 10-15 mins. Got to make some more ice bricks to toss in here so I can try to hit this 1st thing tomorrow morning. I want to be fully cold adapted come winter - though lets be honest winter doesn't occur in VA any more. Heck even the alligators are moving north it's so warm here in the winter.

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-17T18:27:08+0300

holy shiver and shake! I haven't shivered like this in FOREVER!!!!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-17T20:07:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-38785>

> **quelsen;38949 wrote:** Really, what made you shiver

not really sure... While I wanted to CT - when I got in it was hard... used 96lbs of ice to drop my 100gal stock tank to ~50 or so. couldn't get my shoulders/neck in the water to save my life, had goose bumps the whole time... got out I was ok (of course it was 1130 and the outside temp was close to 100!) went inside the house which was 76degrees w/all the over head fans on and started shivering like a mad woman! I guess every now and then its a good thing to push the boundaries.... 30min later I went to the local outdoor pool and puddled back and forth for 30mins... felt great

### Cowboy — 2012-07-18T01:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-38860>

Dumped the warm water in the stock tank, and refilled with 64 degree water from my in ground cistern. Then added all the ice I could freeze in the last couple of days. Ended up with a temp of 57 degrees. It was cold and I only managed 30 minutes, but it did feel good. I am still a bit uncoordinated with my keyboarding from being cold. I was right at the shiver and shake point.

Must have used close to 80 pounds of ice. Should be able to do another tomorrow before the water gets too warm to cool off with a reasonable amount of ice.

There appears to be a big difference between 57 degrees and 70 degrees.

### Optimalbound — 2012-07-19T01:04:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-39177>

Had my longest ocean swim so far, yesterday. 45 minutes in 16Â°C (60Â°F). Probably about half a mile. Got stung by jellyfish all over my torso and left leg, got dive bombed by terns nesting on a little island that I swam too close to, and nearly got run over by someone driving his boat without watching where he was going. Fairly uneventful, otherwise.  No shivering afterwards at all. It felt like I could have gone on for hours. No hunger afterwards and great sleep.

I'm still quite amazed that such exposure to cold is now not only tolerable, but actually very pleasant. I even miss the 12Â°C (54Â°F) sea temperatures that were here just a few weeks ago.

I live on a peninsula with sandy beaches to the west, deep fjords and an archipelago to the east and several lakes in between, so there's plenty of choice. But rising sea temperatures is making me look for other options, and I discovered that a local health spa has a cold pool that they claim is kept at a balmy 6Â°C (43Â°F). Might just have to try that one of these days...

### Dextery — 2012-07-19T03:42:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-39257>

Am spending the summer in Southern Colorado in a RV Park. Have a river flowing slowly through the park. I found a deep hole and am able to submerge up to my shoulders and the best thing is the water at 7am is 50-52 degrees. In the afternoon, the water warms up to 65 degrees from the sun radiation. I stay in for about 45 minutes before the numbness and shivering take over. I am the nutjob of the RV Park.

But this sure beats bathtub CT. I imagine that in Sept Oct the water will be down to 40 degrees and by then I should be fully adapted to handle the 40 degrees.

### Cowboy — 2012-07-24T07:11:18+0300

### nonchalant — 2012-07-24T15:12:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-40652>

I'm back into CT despite the heat!!

Well, sorta. Yesterday I figured I could do the face dunks, anyway. I was missing my CT baths. 4 trays of ice cooled down a sink of our 90F tap water just fine.

I dunked in the morning and it was so refreshing. (I had stopped the ice baths in early May because of the rising water temps.) Last night I face dunked again, and about 2 minutes later, I generated a heat wave! I got really warm and had to turn on the fan. Wow, the body remembers! This is so cool!!

### Optimalbound — 2012-07-24T21:54:30+0300

I'm ocean swimming at least five days a week and hoping for six this week. It feels divine. So happy to live near the Pacific Ocean!

### Jude — 2012-07-29T11:24:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41771>

A question for all you C.T. folk about using and the effects of compression garments.Today was mu first time.

Was surprised how easy they made my entrance into our pool-generally takes me

a minute or two to get in,especially with strong winds -but not this time.

Have had a 2 week break.

The pool was 60F- been 57-58 -it's middle of winter here and has warmed up the last few days!

It's the first time I've actually been fully submerged and walked around mostly. Yah

Stayed in 35mins-I really didn't feel cold but was trembling lots the last 5mins.

That's why I got out, didn't feel cold out with the compression stuff on and my skin wasn't cherry pink- ?

Somehow I feel like I've had a cheat CT with this garment-but I like it !

My best is 65mins at 58 and usually have no problem with 50mins in swimmers and bare feet with skin turning pink.

Anyone else use them? My family kindly got them for me!

I think you need to use them with spot CT, not necessarily in the pool. What I have found is you need them for spot CT because it hold the cold pack on your skin better; otherwise you it just kind of sits there and is spotty. When you are in the pool, the cold water surrounds you equally and you should get cherry color without a compression garment. That is how I roll though. Others may be diff. Pretty sure Jack doesn't use them in the water.

### PrimalPam — 2012-07-29T18:01:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41797>

I never did purchase a compression garment. I started to just get in the water slowly (I do baths, since I don't have a pool) and found that once my entire body was in, it was actually quite pleasant. I still keep my fingers & toes out of the water, but these days, my feet are starting to even go in. I do 5 times a week, 40 mins, at 50-52 degrees F.

For me, it's just the initial cold that's difficult, but the second (literally, the second) my entire body is submerged, I'm at peace.

I was so afraid of frostbite, numbness, etc., but keeping the temps steady with a thermometer has alleviated all those fears. My toes & fingers get numb about 15 mins after I'm out, and stay numb for about an hour. I'm used to it now, as I believe it's that the blood in my body is rushing to warm up my core, so it's taken away from extremeties.

I also don't immediately try to warm up anymore. I spot dry with a towel, and walk around the house naked, enjoying seeing all the pink skin. I don't even put clothes on for about an hour, where I just wear short shorts and a cami-top.

### Optimalbound — 2012-07-29T22:23:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41853>

> **Nonchalant;41027 wrote:** How fun!! Please take some pics, if you can. You will be the poster child of this website!

Made another attempt today just for you, Nonchalant.

Unfortunately, visibility was poor because of waves and lots of seaweed. The only oyster I found was dead.

Sea temp is now up to 62F, so not exactly deep CT today..

Lots of bladderwrack (_fucus vesiculosus_) in the area, though. Never thought to eat these before, but not that they are officially epi-paleo approved, I of course gathered a few handfuls so I could try them out. Not very inspiring in raw condition. A little bit salty, and the texture is a bit like under-cooked tagliatelle. Not much taste, to be honest. OK to nibble on, though. I think I'll have to look for recipes ..

Sorry about the boring pic, Nonchalant, but this bladderwrack is all I could manage today:

### Jude — 2012-07-30T04:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41897>

> **vkiernan;42008 wrote:** I think you need to use them with spot CT, not necessarily in the pool. What I have found is you need them for spot CT because it hold the cold pack on your skin better; otherwise you it just kind of sits there and is spotty. When you are in the pool, the cold water surrounds you equally and you should get cherry color without a compression garment. That is how I roll though. Others may be diff. Pretty sure Jack doesn't use them in the water.

Thanks vkiernan, Just felt like a cheat and you've confirmed it for me! Certainly a different experience.

### Jude — 2012-07-30T04:49:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41898>

> **PrimalPam;42030 wrote:** I never did purchase a compression garment. I started to just get in the water slowly (I do baths, since I don't have a pool) and found that once my entire body was in, it was actually quite pleasant. I still keep my fingers & toes out of the water, but these days, my feet are starting to even go in. I do 5 times a week, 40 mins, at 50-52 degrees F.
>
>
>
> For me, it's just the initial cold that's difficult, but the second (literally, the second) my entire body is submerged, I'm at peace.
>
>
>
> I was so afraid of frostbite, numbness, etc., but keeping the temps steady with a thermometer has alleviated all those fears. My toes & fingers get numb about 15 mins after I'm out, and stay numb for about an hour. I'm used to it now, as I believe it's that the blood in my body is rushing to warm up my core, so it's taken away from extremeties.
>
>
>
> I also don't immediately try to warm up anymore. I spot dry with a towel, and walk around the house naked, enjoying seeing all the pink skin. I don't even put clothes on for about an hour, where I just wear short shorts and a cami-top.

Thanks Pam, Just proves that I'm still a sook! I think I'll just go back to plan A- was coping well and I liked the pink skin thing...

Just have to find another use for the compression stuff.

### Jude — 2012-07-30T04:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41899>

> **Optimalbound;39358 wrote:** Had my longest ocean swim so far, yesterday. 45 minutes in 16Â°C (60Â°F). Probably about half a mile. Got stung by jellyfish all over my torso and left leg, got dive bombed by terns nesting on a little island that I swam too close to, and nearly got run over by someone driving his boat without watching where he was going. Fairly uneventful, otherwise.  No shivering afterwards at all. It felt like I could have gone on for hours. No hunger afterwards and great sleep.
>
>
>
> I'm still quite amazed that such exposure to cold is now not only tolerable, but actually very pleasant. I even miss the 12Â°C (54Â°F) sea temperatures that were here just a few weeks ago.
>
>
>
> I live on a peninsula with sandy beaches to the west, deep fjords and an archipelago to the east and several lakes in between, so there's plenty of choice. But rising sea temperatures is making me look for other options, and I discovered that a local health spa has a cold pool that they claim is kept at a balmy 6Â°C (43Â°F). Might just have to try that one of these days...

Optimalbound, read most of this thread last night!-and your pictures are great.

One question-do you ever get sharks there? Sounds a bit dangerous with jelly fish and diving terns and boat drivers?

My pool has no inherent dangers- has nice rural views of sheep, emus, kangaroos, plenty of birdlife and farmland, 90kms from anywhere.

### Optimalbound — 2012-07-30T11:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41917>

> **Jude;42134 wrote:** Optimalbound, read most of this thread last night!-and your pictures are great.
>
> One question-do you ever get sharks there? Sounds a bit dangerous with jelly fish and diving terns and boat drivers?
>
> My pool has no inherent dangers- has nice rural views of sheep, emus, kangaroos, plenty of birdlife and farmland, 90kms from anywhere.

Thanks for your concern, Jude! Not to worry; this is all very safe. While there are some species of shark here, most are small and all are harmless to humans. The jelly fish are also harmless. They only sting a bit, some of them. The terns' attack are only mock attacks unless you get really close. I normally swim close to shore, so boats are not an issue. I think I've learned my lesson and will not cross open streches of water again..

Your pool and surroundings sound wonderfully relaxing!

### nonchalant — 2012-07-30T17:04:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41937>

Optimalbound, great sea veggie pic! Nice to gather the fresh stuff, instead of settling for dried bladderwrack out of a package.

I was wondering about your encounter with the jellyfish. I bet your cold adaptation lessened the pain of that encounter. I was stung by a plant a few weeks ago. That pain usually lasts for an hour, but the pain was turned off after 5-10 minutes.

### Optimalbound — 2012-07-30T19:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41950>

That could well be, Nonchalant. I am not qualified to say if it is the cold adaption, the O3/O6 balance or some other factor, but something has definitely changed. I don't notice getting stung while in the cold(ish) water, and several times I've congratulated myself for avoiding the jellyfish, but then when I get back on dry land and warm up a bit, I start noticing that I did get stung after all. In previous years, it used to be quite painful, but now it only smarts a bit, and it goes away within the hour.

The jellyfish in question is [The lion's mane jellyfish (_Cyanea capillata)_](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lion's_mane_jellyfish)_, by the way. I sometimes also see Cyanea lamarckii_ later in the season.

I haven't eaten anything at all since I ate that handful of bladderwrack 24 hours ago, and I am still not hungry. Weird. Too early to tell if it's the bladderwrack that has this effect, but it is kind of interesting nonetheless.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-31T01:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-41990>

> **Optimalbound;42192 wrote:**
>
> The jellyfish in question is [The lion's mane jellyfish (_Cyanea capillata)_](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lion's_mane_jellyfish)_, by the way. I sometimes also see Cyanea lamarckii_ later in the season.

Interesting jellyfish. The lion's mane can get huge! The blue jellyfish looks lovely. On the Texas coast, we see a small white jellyfish of some sort, and Physalia physalis, the Portugese Man O' War. It has a nice iridescent color.

### quelsen — 2012-07-31T16:01:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-42041>

After "servicing" the icemaker by replacing all the insulation with armatex and replacing the overload capacitor and insulating the water intake line to lower the water intake temp it is running much better, however the cycle time seems a bit long.

hopefully i will have enough ice for a bath this week

### Qodesh — 2012-07-31T17:09:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-42047>

I've insulated my 100 gal. stock tub and have a water drip line going through the top of my chest freezer. The outflow from the freezer drain hole balances out the water level through the drain hole in the bottom of the stock tub.

whoa... does that make sense? lol

right now I'm maintaining 50 degrees in 90 degree South Florida summer. This will be great when the days cool off in winter (down to the 70's) but for now, I''m randomly stopping the drip flow while the freezer gets a better chance to chill the water down even more.

My new love is 42 degrees! It's amazingly easier!

### Qodesh — 2012-07-31T18:03:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-42059>

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/42309=373-ct2.jpg)

I'll try to remember to take a picture tonight. For now, here's a drawing.

The drip from the small immersible pump in the tub will be turned on or off when the water in the freezer is cold enough.

The water level hose from drain to drain keeps the temp and water level even through displacement of the drip speed.

The filter is Epsom salts and a flow 100 gal aquarium filter with carbon.

The tank is insulated with 2" thick styrofoam board glued together with spray foam insulation. (I know, not exactly environmentally friendly)

### Malkavian — 2012-07-31T21:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-42095>

I'm no frequent poster, but I am slowly exploring the CT for myself. I've done three cold water immersions in the bath tub for as cold as the faucet can deliver, which is about 11 degrees celsius (when I get in the water, my body warms the water up to 14-15 degrees during the duration I'm in the tub).

During my first immersion, I was shivering like crazy from the beginning. I stayed in for about 20 minutes.

The other day (and the CT session before that I didn't start shiver until after perhaps 20 minutes. What I noticed the other day though, and this is an open question to all the males reading this: I experienced a very uncomfortable feeling in the scrotum. Not pain, just...I don't know a "warning" from my body that this isn't good... it had me a little nervous...

Have you guys experienced this uncomfortable feeling when doing cold water immersion?

I stayed in for about 40-45 minutes last session and the session before that (# 2 and #3 of my three in total).

/Malkavian

### nonchalant — 2012-08-13T18:33:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-44701>

OK, I'm starting my Autumn today. A month earlier than I had planned. Last night I filled the bathtub about a third full, and let it cool overnight to room temp. This morning I put in every ice cube I had, plus some ice packs and a frozen water bottle. I noticed that I started sweating as I popped the ice out of the trays. Guess my body is ready for this! I was able to drop the water temp by 10 degrees, to 68F. I was able to get goose bumps, so that's good anyway.

My chest freezer is full of neglected grass-fed beef, lamb, and bison, plus conventional meat, which is all covered up now by various frozen sea delicacies. I'm going to have to cook some roasts and whatever to make room for bags of ice. Guess I can toss out those chicken feet. Never told DH I bought those.

### Cowboy — 2012-08-14T01:51:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-44788>

We have finally had a break in our heat wave, and I am now able to get my water temps (without ice) to 68-69 degrees. Days are now in the low 90's rather than the high 90's and nights are in the low 60's and high 50's rather than the high 60's. Anyhow a couple of the hot days I was getting temps in my water of 79 without ice. I do have ice frozen but it takes 48 hours to really freeze an adequete amount to get intothe mid to low 60s.

I have noticed that the 60 are easier to tolerate than the 70's and I am hoping the low 60's will be even better. I have standardized on about 25 minutes once a day. Maybe with less yard work I will be able to manage some 2 a daysessions.

### nonchalant — 2012-08-14T17:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-44898>

Hi Cowboy. Yes, lower temps seem to activate something in the skin/nerves that in turn blocks the sensation. I went down to 60F this morning with the help of a bag of ice. Barely any goose bumps today. I'll have to make room somehow for a second bag. But I did notice my ankles were a bit stiff and slow to move afterwords. Good sign.

### PrimalPam — 2012-08-14T17:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-44906>

I find that my sweet spot is actually even lower, around 50-52F. It's easier to take than in the 60's. When the water temp is higher, I find I shiver and can't wait for my time to be up. But in the very low 50's, I close my eyes, listen to my music, and done.

Unfortunately, my tap is coming out in the mid to high 80's now, so it takes a ton of ice. My freezer is now about 70% dedicated to the large blocks of ice I make from my plastic containers.

### quelsen — 2012-08-14T19:48:06+0300

The ice maker is back in business... Full time CT here I come

### Qodesh — 2012-08-15T17:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45024>

I love that temp. Although I can imagine it might be tough in the wind.

### quelsen — 2012-08-15T21:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45062>

good and chilled but not yet cold.

### Rosekelly — 2012-08-16T06:06:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45159>

I'm really enjoying my summer ocean swims, even though the water temperature has risen to probably around 60, a little too high for perfect CT, but I sure love it. I saw an otter again today. He looked at me, and I looked at him. It's probably the same otter I keep seeing, because the way he looks at me, it's like he recognizes me now. Oh, you again! Hello!

Today the water felt especially cold, which made me happy. I swam and warmed up. It makes me so darn happy to be in the ocean.

### Adria — 2012-08-16T15:26:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45186>

Rosekelly I love reading about you and the otter.. sounds so wonderful to swim in the ocean !!

### NWgirl — 2012-08-16T17:50:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45206>

My CT yesterday was amazing. We're at the Oregon coast renting a house and I went to the beach of the bay that is essentially filled with ocean water, it is so close (Tillamook bay). The water temp kept changing, but I got my skin temp down to 49. I must have stayed in half hour to 45 min. the 3 teen girls (two are mine) went with me and they went in too but they didn't stay in long and were impressed that I did.

At one point an old fashioned steam engine went by and we waved. Near the end I settled into a hole in the sand in the water that was shaped like an arm chair, faced the sun and watched a pelican hunt for dinner.

Best CT ever!

### Cowboy — 2012-08-16T20:54:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45236>

Temps are still dropping in my neck of the woods with yesterdays water temp 68 degrees, which lowered to 65 degrees with ice and this morning with no ice ,the water temp was 61, which warmed up a couple of degrees with me in it. It will continue to drop and cool off and I am hoping to ride the drop down into the 40s and then move the tank into the garage. Not the classiest setting, but it will work. I was also wondering about using an aquarium heater to keep it warm so it can stay outside longer.

### MartiD — 2012-08-19T04:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-45657>

With dear hubbys help bagging up ice all week I was able to get bath water around 53 degrees this morning. It was difficult to get in but once I was settled it felt great. I managed to stay in about 30 minutes before life (aka kids needing breakfast) interrupted but since I haven't had water that cold for months 30 minutes was good. I felt pretty chilled afterwards but got dressed and made a run to the farmers market where I felt very awake and energetic and really just happy. I felt good and it was a beautiful mild day.

It will probably be several more days before we have 6 gallon bags filled back up but it will be worth it when it's ready!

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-08-21T18:52:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-46060>

This morning I took an extra long cold shower - 20 minutes. From bed staight to the cold. And man, all morning I feel like I'm flying. I've never taken speed before - but I imagine this is how it feels.

### omlh@todmi.com — 2012-08-22T18:09:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-46323>

Managed a nice long (20 minute) shower on the coldest setting - our tap water is in the mid-fifties at the moment, so it's not optimal, but not bad! Looking forward to being able to resume baths once the children are back at school and I'm not spending my whole time running them around the place!

### jayjha1@gmail.com — 2012-08-23T23:21:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-46597>

I think I threw down my 36th cryo bath today

### freesia — 2012-08-24T05:57:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-46664>

I'm CT'ing in mid 40's water for approx 45 - 50 minutes. I managed an hour a couple of times but lately it's been getting tougher around the 40 minute mark and I've been cutting it short rather than suffer.

Warm-up is sweet - inside an hour. My muscles feel permanently energized...as if some aspect of my muscle fitness is exceeding what I'm conditioned to.....hmm not sure if that makes sense.

### Birdy — 2012-09-14T23:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-51689>

cooler temps mean more ct for me. I went to 2x per week and I'll be able to visit the ocean a few times this month hopefully. I'll be at an inlet, but might venture out into open water depending on how the waves are.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-09-15T19:47:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-51829>

I now get to use a spring-fed lake for my CT (for 2 weeks now). I went swimming again today with nice cooler air temps (but sunny). Water feels pretty 'warm' to me. The air gives a nice chill when coming out. There are trees already turning colour in some places. Wow! Fall is here! :-D

I like the Fall. I wonder if based on what Jack says about cellular signalling throughout different seasons from an evolutionary perspective means that people might see more cases of more inflammation at this time of year even though they have been following epi-paleo. I know I saw some places where someone was concerned about lab #'s being a bit skewed compared to their June readings. So I guess it's just something to expect (within reason) during this specific time of year. Hence why CT would be best at this time of the year, to help our naturally occuring cellular inflammation. As Jack says...chemically reduced.

This year Fall for me is much better, but I can notice a bit of a difference from even a few weeks ago... so I will continue with good circadian cycles, epi-paleo and CT. "Chemically reduced"

### PrimalPam — 2012-09-15T21:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-51849>

I've been 4-5 x/week for 3 months now, and the funniest thing is, if I skip more than 2 days in a row I feel odd. Like, I miss the feeling of being in an ice cold tub for 45 minutes! But I actually do! This week is "only" 4 times a week for me, and it feels like I'm missing something really important.

I love the ice baths. Never in a million trillion years would I have thought that when I started. I now love the feeling afterwards too, of being ice cold for an hour or so. It's like...I don't feel normal unless my skin is really pink.

### Cowboy — 2012-09-18T17:06:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-52348>

### nonchalant — 2012-09-22T00:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-53311>

When I'm sitting in the tub, I sometimes don't want to get my back wet/cold. Especially if I've gotten the temp down to a new low. My upper back seems to be the most sensitive to cold.

But I've found that I just need to get my face and hands wet with a washcloth, and then I'm able to get the rest of myself wet, and finally I'm able to lay down in the cold water. I often reapply the cold water to my face a few times, until I just change my mind, and decide that the cold water is no longer scary any more. After I lay down, I wonder what the big deal was. It's easy.

### MsYo — 2012-09-27T08:02:15+0300

### Cowboy — 2012-09-30T00:09:59+0300

No frost yet, only temps in to the high 40s.

### Cowboy — 2012-10-04T00:52:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-56130>

One thing about living in the rockies, is the quick change in temperature. On Monday it was in the high 70s and Tuesday was about the same. Right now it is 57 degrees at 3:40 PM. My tank went from mid 60s to 57. Just got out after 25 minutes. It felt cold and my keyboarding is slow. Managed to keep my feet in and submerged up to my neck. The infared thermometer registered 57 on my skin, after 25 minutes. Tonight is supposed to be in the high 20s and I am figuring the temp of the tank will be in the 40s by tomorrow. Might need an aquarium heater. Was hoping the temps would just drop slowly this year like they sometimes do. Not this year. I am going to leave my filter running and hope that nothing freezes.

### Cowboy — 2012-10-06T18:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-56601>

After a couple of sub 30 degree nights yesterday my tank was 47 degrees at 4:00 PM. I lasted 20 minutes. It felt cold and took a couple of hours to warm up. Should be in the 40s again today, and that will be three out of four days of CT below 60 degrees. It might be getting easier, but I am not sure.

The rest of the world must think we are crazy. My wife sure does.

I hope to see some big result soon. I was paleo/LC/ZC for the last 1.5 years, started the LR last fall but I had already lost 50 pounds. I miss a BAB once in a while, I get 8 hours of sleep a night, I refrain from snacks except for an occaisonal slip up, I have hours between each meal, don't eat before bed time, sleep in a dark room,(my wife complains), and follow as much of the guidlines as is humanly possible for me. I want Jack's results. I don't know if I can abslolutely follow the perfect guidline each and every day. Paleo, low carb, CT, sleep in the dark, no snacks, etc etc., but I am giving it a try.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-12T14:35:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-57730>

I have been swimming daily in a pond of decreasing temp. I do a crawl (stroke, stroke, breathe right, stroke, stroke, breathe left) I alternate laps with breast stroke fully submerging head between strokes. I experience differing sensations.My skin feels one way, my face (trigeminal nerve) has it's own sensation and my head ( top and rear) have a different more intense almost painful sensation. With this activity face and head are submerged at least 50% of the time. I guess my question is why the pain in the head? The pain diminishes instantly as head is brought out of water. Maybe O3/O6 ratio?

### Inger — 2012-10-14T12:45:03+0300

### nonchalant — 2012-10-16T02:55:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-58463>

On our vacation last week, I went swimming in the Gulf of Mexico. What a fabulous experience! It was cool water, and it felt so good! I'd lick my lips and taste the saltwater on my face. The salty-smelling waves came every 5 to 10 seconds, lifting me and challenging me. The gulls were calling, and I'd see pelicans occasionally, grabbing at small fish in the foamy surf. Sandpipers darted before the waves, finding some tasty morsels hidden in the seaweed that rolled up on shore. An almost constant roar filled my ears. My toes delighted in the feeling of the sand. I'd have to watch the waves, to make sure they didn't take me by surprise.

It was great during the day, and very nice at sunset. We camped on the beach with a small fire, and the next morning was a revelation. Standing in the waves, feeling the coolness, motion and immensity of the water, and watching the sun rise over the waves and amongst the cloud formations was a spiritual experience. I couldn't stop smiling and laughing. Life is so good.

BTDT. I've found if I'm cold ahead of time, I wimp out. But I can turn up the heat for just a few minutes, and my attitude changes.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-11-07T18:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-62576>

I swim at the lake with temps around or below freezing now. The water temp is a little warmer than that but only a few degrees.

I truly love this exercise! People are so shocked and think I'm either crazy and/or brave.

It really does become addicting. I find it's no fun to do it at home in a tub (I don't have a tub any more anyway since moving). I prefer to do it outdoors somewhere like the lake. Now, I am probably not needing it as much as some others who are still very broken, so it might be different for me now. Last February I did all the tub stuff etc. with SPECIFIC therapy in mind. Now in November (well just simply haven't stopped going to the lake since September), I find it is a different criteria. It feels like it's more of a sport or something, for enhancement and/or maintenance. It's something I want to continue for the rest of my life.

For me the protocols on here started out as purely prescriptive, but now I feel they are 'part of my life and part of who I am'.

### nate.reik@yahoo.com — 2012-11-08T16:51:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-62884>

2nd CT session yesterday. First one was Monday. 61 degrees in the tub for 1/2 hr yesterday. 63 degrees Monday.

Did CT a few times early in the year....time to get more serious.

Tap water is 57. Should be able to ride the falling water temps down I hope. Kinda thinking of a goal of "easily" doing the Polar Plunge here in Milwaukee. (Jumping into Lake Michigan's 33-34 degree water on January 1st)

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-09T00:43:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-63020>

Back in the tub. Water is 42.5 degrees. Out side temp is 53 degrees w/19% humidity (yea!) and a few point of 11 I've been in for almost 10 min and I am numb. Haven't gotten in to where the girls are covered though. I need to get some full coverage compression gear

### Dali Dula — 2012-11-09T16:13:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-63115>

Got back in the pond yesterday. 40 deg. I can stay in but I can barely swim. Swimming becomes really hard below 40.

### ealachan — 2012-11-11T17:19:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-63608>

Just got out of the tub. Cold as \*\*\*\*. Here is my data:

oral temp before: 98.1

beg water temp: 54.5

beg skin temp: 88

28 min in, oral temp was 98.7

duration: 35 min

ending oral temp: 99.2

water temp at end: 55

skin temp: 58

oral temp 5 min post: 98.8

Something new and different, possibly because of the slightly colder water: my feet and lower legs went numb about 8-10 min into my soak. Not NUMB like, couldn't feel them, but...numb. Hard to describe. It was sort of like everything got really tight, but not like muscle-cramp tight. Like wearing compression hose kind of tight, if that makes sense. It was odd. It went away a little bit when I started moving my legs around in the water. Also, my lower legs felt really tired when I first got out of the tub, wobbly, like I'd just climbed a steep hill.

I found it interesting that while the water is technically only 2 degrees colder than the last time I did CT, it felt so! much! colder! when I first got in. Not stabby-cold, but just "OMFG THIS SUUUUUCKS" cold for a few minutes, then I was ok. There was a lot of F-word yelling this morning, since no one was home...lol The poor dog sat there in the bathroom doorway looking at me like "bitch, you crazy." and I was somewhat inclined to agree.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-12T01:01:43+0200

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-15T16:44:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-64483>

> **ealachan;66477 wrote:** We've had two nights with temps in the low 20's since the last time I CT'd. I forsee tonight's session being brutal...lol

you know... IME the lower the temp the easier it is b/c you get numb much faster... DH hopped in the stock tank this am - it was 40deg. he lasted 11mins. Which is great for him... he started CTing regularly when I started up again last week... he's been hitting it ever 2-3days... initially he could only make it 5mins... so he's getting longer... his biggest complaint is that he goes from numb to shivering in a heart beat. since going epi-paleo in July, he has very little bodyfat (I can almost see his 8pack) where as me... I get that initial rush of cold, cold, cold... oh now I'm numb and tingly - then I roll w/it. I'm also not shivering when I get out at the lower temps, where as at the higher temps (above 50) I do shiver....

### ealachan — 2012-11-15T17:08:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-64490>

I agree - I have an easier time the colder the water is, but I find it's more of a mental block for me to get in when I know it's even colder than usual. The last time I CT'ed was actually the first time I really got numb / tingly, and it kind of freaked me out a little! That's going to take a bit of getting used to for me.

My hubs has lost a ton of body fat too, but I haven't been able to get him to get cold yet. He, of all people, really needs it because he's T2D, but he's the BIGGEST sally when it comes to getting cold. He totally can't take it, and he has no interest in developing a tolerance, unfortunately. I've at least gotten him to not crank the thermostat up every time he walks by, now...that's a step in the right direction, I guess. It bums me out, because I know CT would really help him a lot, but I have to balance that with the whole "you can lead a horse to water but you can't make him drink" thing. I'm trying to lead by example, but I think he mostly just thinks I'm crazy.

### Cowboy — 2012-11-19T07:11:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-65279>

Missed the last two weeks of CT due to some business travel, a hunting trip and a few days of inch thick ice on the tank. We now have a warm spell and the tank is not freezing with the pumpfilter running. Did twenty minutes at 41 degrees. Thought I wouldn't make it past 2 minutes but gradually it felt ok. Have had a sore hip from hunting then sitting in a truck for 900 miles. It seems like the CT got rid of the last little stiffness. Another week of mild weather for Wyoming. then it might be time to move the tank into the garage.

### Cowboy — 2012-11-27T12:18:35+0200

### Birdy — 2012-11-29T12:03:31+0200

### nonchalant — 2012-11-29T14:35:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-65852>

I just got an infrared thermometer for the bath, and was surprised.Â  My deep-fat thermometer is off by 5F.Â  Guess my floating aquarium thermometer was right after all.Â  So I've been taking baths that are 45F to 50F for quite some time.Â  LOL.

Glad it wasn't the other way around.Â 

Â 

Also, I've been a little hesitant to CT lately, when I've been walking around with cold fingers and toes.Â  But I've found that once I get in, it's the same as before.Â  I still respond well to the cold.Â  Guess I just needed a little nudge.

I do like the fact that I can tolerate cold fingers and toes now.Â  It used to be that I was just miserable when that would happen.

### Inger — 2012-11-30T05:45:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-65922>

It is nice to read about what you all do for CT..! You are so brave..

I go in the river when I am already cold before, too Nonchalant! I do not care, I just tell to myself, don't be a chicken..! Sure it needs a bit more courage but it is just fine.

It is snow storm here today. Huh...

My CT is as before quite minor.. I am taking it slow.. I just go into the river, submerge my body and head quickly three times (I think 3 is a nice number..lol) and stay in for about 2-3 minutes max. The water is 35,6 F.

But in my appartement I have in between 53-60 degrees F and I am not freezing.. (in my bedroom much colder.. but I do have a duvet as a blanket and it is warm..only my nose almost freeze off huh) I like this! It is amazing how fast one get used to lower temps. It is totally amazing. I think it happends just in hours! At work I get sooo hot. I usually try to get a cold shower in.. I just tell my coworkers I'll go for a "smoke" as they do all the time.. and then I run upstairs and take a icecold quick shower. OMG how great one feels after! For sure they all know what I am doing..lol (they know I do not smoke) and they use to joke about it..lol

### endless — 2012-11-30T12:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-65967>

I have been staying at 50F baths but today I jumped in without looking after filling the tub with straight cold. It was 44F....I really noticed the difference and only stayed in 10 min, up to my mid-abdomen in front, bra strap area in back. Goal for tomorrow to submerge up to my neck for the same amount of time.

### nonchalant — 2012-11-30T12:22:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-65968>

### Cowboy — 2012-12-01T13:15:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66032>

Three days in a row of 41-45 degrees and twenty minutes each time.Â  I am very cold for at least an hour afterwards.Â  Is any one dunking their head at these temps?Â  I have not been and am not sure I want toÂ  unless there is a really good over riding reason.Â  No way to see what Dr. K says as I am not a high enough member.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T13:32:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66035>

Mornin' Cowboy. Â My water temp (indoors) has dropped to 49. Â I manage 15 minutes sitting and 30 lying on my back. Â NO head dunking because when my head gets cold I freeze up immediately. Â I don't think you need to dunk your head. Â Go back and read the CT protocol blog as it should have the answers there. Â Oh and I started shivering yesterday about 20 minutes in and was cold for about 3 hours after even with running around town doing errands.

### Inger — 2012-12-01T14:04:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66037>

I am often submerging head and all.. but that is mainly because I love to rinse my hair in the river water, it makes it so soft. I wash my hair inside before going for the dip. I ususally submerge 3 times but just down and up. Now the water has ice on the top.. I needed to crush the ice to be able to take a dip today. So from today I have to dip in a hole in the ice..
I have not noticed anything bad from dunking my head.. but I am staying only around 2 minutes so that might be why? I guess if you want to do it, increase it gradually and do it carefully. Dip shortly at the end of your bath if you stay longer. I think when used to it it is nothing bad with dunking your head too!

I do think it feels very refreshing to dunk my head too, for sure!

### Dali Dula — 2012-12-01T21:00:04+0200

### Cowboy — 2012-12-02T17:05:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66162>

Today it is 60 degrees in the rockies (Wyoming)Â  Amazing weather for December 2.Â  Just finished 20 minutes in the 45 degree water.Â  No head dunking but I am still pretty cold.Â  Two nights in a row I have slept from approx 11 PM to 5:30 AM straight through.Â  No up to use the facilities at all.Â  Feelling good but too cold to type much.Â  CT really does seem to help me with aches and pains from getting old or being non optimized.Â  Still have to leave my toes out of the water but I am up to my neck and up to my wrist. Today was very windy and it did make it harder.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T18:39:18+0200

Â Started shivering at the 20 minute mark. Â Took about 2 hours to warm back up. Skin temp when I got out was 59 but my oral temp had actually gone up a degree which is something new.

### Cowboy — 2012-12-03T19:29:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66266>

Coldest yet for air and water.Â  Air 33, water 40.Â  Lasted 20 minutes.Â Â Â  Don't want to move the tub indoors, but it has to happen pretty soon.Â  Sleep continues along a nice path with 6-7 hours without s single waking moment.Â  I keep the room as dark as possible but a bit of moonlight does filter in.

### diane — 2012-12-03T21:29:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66270>

Whoo! I just got in the tub after a couple of months (have been icing), and wow, it's much colder out of the tap! Was so cold, it was painful on my feet, but they got used to it after a few minutes. I stayed in 45 minutes - but that counts the filling up time. I'm still not jumping into it - more like the gradual fill. On the plus side, there are parts that almost feel "warm" in the cold water - legs, belly - probably because of being out in the cold and icing.Â 

I tend to put it off because the only time I get time to do it is at night, and have had a couple of nights shivering for a while. But the heck with it - I'll get past it!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-04T19:13:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66333>

Diane, once I decided to Â just do it all the angst has disappeared. Â Our water is around 47Â° right out of the tap. Â I put on my socks, step into the water and SIT. Â No easing, no whining, just SIT. Â My legs are getting used to it and hopefully I'll be able to ditch the socks soon. Â 

I was easing back onto my back the last few days and it was awful. Â So this morning, gritted my teeth and just DID it. Â Much better. Â Now I'm even putting my arms down into the water (I've been hugging myself which has to stop). Â Not too bad so far.

So is everyone CT-ing daily or every other day or what? Â I'm going every other day with cold showers between.

### longrob — 2012-12-04T19:34:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66337>

My first post...but I've been reading since last December.Â  Followed JanSz to theÂ monster MDA Leptin Reset thread by Kymma and have been reading JK's blog posts ever since.Â  So far, I am up to CT7, plus some of the BG series.Â Lots to read. Â I am totally on board.Â 

I feel like I know some of you already.Â  I'm male, 58 yo, 6 ft tallÂ and 165lbs now living inÂ north MS.Â  Started face dunks, icing my abs, and have been riding my pool water down from 65 to 52 degrees for the last few months and stay in 30 minutes. I kneel down in the water withÂ my head, upper back,Â shoulders, and arms out on the deck.Â  I always wear socks,Â gloves, and a knit hat.Â  Just started submersing up to my chinÂ for the last 5 minutes, but my hands are still out.Â  I wish I had done this initially, because it is such a shock to my back and arms.Â  I plan to start taking my temperature before and after CT to see what is happening there.Â  Â Â 

You brave ones motivate me to go deeperÂ and I am enjoying the pain relief in my lower back and kneesÂ and also the decreased hunger.Â  I am glad, as the water got colder, myÂ shivering stopped during CT, but I still visibly shake for about 30 minutes after.Â  I envision you guys backspacing typos for your latestÂ forum posts as you shake and warm up, lol.

### Optimalbound — 2012-12-06T18:45:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66595>

My tap water is now down to 7 Â°C (45 Â°F) so today I had my coldest bathtub CT session yet. I was quite surprised to find, like others, that 45 Â°F was actually easier to lower myself into than 50 Â°F. Didn't have a skin thermometer ready, so I only did 25 minutes, to be on the safe side. My skin has never been this red from CT before! Looked almost like a freshly boiled lobster. Hands and feet lasted only 10 minutes at this temperature so I kept them out of the water for the most part.

Â 

This morning, the air temp was -8 Â°C (17 Â°F)Â as I drove to work. I got a few stares and comments from my coworkers as I walked the couple of hundred yards from the parking lot to work, wearing just a shirt while the others were clad in wool sweaters, hats and down jackets. Little did they know that I had been driving with the A/C blasting away at maximum cold, so the outside actually felt rather warm when I stepped out of the car.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Â 

In related news, I've somehow managed to inspire two friends to start doing CT. They looked at me, listened to me a bit, read a few selected bits from Jack's blog and concluded that "This looks real solid. I'm gonna do this". To my utter amazement, they then went from zero to pretty hard core once-a-day full submersion CT in less than a week (blissfully ignoring my advice to follow the Rx and take it a bit slowly to begin with). They both had better starting points than me and they've seen quite impressive BF% reduction and athletic performance improvement in just a few short weeks. Needless to say, they're completely stoked about their sudden progress after several years of not making much headway. I'll see if I can get the numbers if anyone's interested.

### kathylu — 2012-12-07T09:48:08+0200

### Optimalbound — 2012-12-07T14:41:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66707>

F. is in his late 40's and has been overweight for years despite trying "everything".Â He measured his bodyfat using a fitness scale (yes, I know those things aren't entirely reliable), and went from 26% to 20% BF during the first 12 days of CT. Granted, those figures are possibly a bit optimistic, but there is no doubt that the trend is very real. He also noticed very quickly that the vertical line between his abs and obliques reappeared after having been awol for many years. His overall weight hasn't changed much, so far.

A. is in his mid 40's and very fit and physically active, but struggled to lose that last bit of belly fat and love handles. He had also reached a plateau in his running and in his weightlifting. Now, after just a couple of months of cold water submersion CT 2-4 days a week, he has blasted through both those two plateaus as well as having clearly better muscle definition. He also reports an increase in calmness and concentration.Â 

None of them are totally epi-paleo, but A, especially, has lately made considerable progress on that front, also.

Â 

I keep being amazed how some people embrace change, absorb new knowledge quickly and run with it as far as it will take them, while others totally resist it. The other day I suggested to a relative who is constantly suffering from bloating and joints pain that she try eliminating grains and dairy for a month, just to see if it would make a difference. That idea was simply impossible for her to even consider. So sad.

I am so exited already and my sisters too BTW..

Â 

Thanks, Inger!

Believe it or not but F. is actually from very near the equator in South America, and he's the one who really went pedal with CT with a determination that left me speechless. Wow, just WOW. And now that he's seeing and feelng such progress, he is even more determined. Which is brilliant for A. and me as well, as it puts pressure on us to keep up. Â 

I've been freediving after oysters a couple of times. As luck would have it, when I finally managed to pick half a dozen, it turned out that there was a warning out not to eat any mussels due to dangerous levels of toxic algae in the area. That particular week was the only time during the whole year when this was a problem. Argh. So, unfortunately, the only safe thing for me to do was to throw the oysters back in. But I know where they live now...Â  I've picked scallops too, but that requires neolithic scuba gear, as they are usually much deeper down.

Stavanger is in my neck of the woods, as it happens. Drop me a PM if you want to join me for a dip in the cold North Sea or to compare notes over a serving of whale sashimi! I know I'll probably upset a few people here, but I have to tell you that sashimi whale really is the most surprisingly delicious thing! I had absolutely no idea until I tried it for the first time just a few weeks ago.

### mccune9@hotmail.co.uk — 2012-12-08T08:13:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66789>

Long time reader, first time poster.

Been CTing on and off for about a year, all using a shower as it's all that I have access to. Might have the chance to do some proper cold immersion when i'm back home over christmas.

Am getting into a regular routine now of AM and PM exposures, as well as wearing as little clothing as possible whilst working in the cold.

I don't know how well adapted I am, but definitely get the feeling of my skin burning up upon stepping out of the shower.

I've found that having my stopwatch set for 90 seconds is a good way to motivate myself as I can start by thinking i'll stay in for 90 seconds on the coldest setting and that's all I have to do. By the time that is over I don't feel so bad and then work in further 90 second blocks. Usually i'll do half turns every 90 seconds, with 30 seconds directly on my face when facing the shower head (at which point it's starting to go numb)

Â 

I can't imagine being able to submerge myself in a bath tub!
