### mccune9@hotmail.co.uk — 2012-12-09T09:25:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66920>

Dali Dula, my name is a bit of a play on words, as it refers more to my recovery from anorexia.

2 years ago I weighed <43kg at a height of 6ft 2" , and I now weigh around 90kg, much stronger and healthier.

I have managed one ring muscle up a long time ago, but i've not had access to a bar with sufficient headroom to practice them, and I don't have anywhere to hang my rings right now.

Â 

Nonchalent, Anyone can manage 90 seconds if they know there's the option to quit immediately afterwards, but suddenly that discomfort subsides and there's no reason to. Last night I did 7 and a half minutes on the coldest setting and could quite happily have stayed in longer but decided i'd like to increase by 90 seconds every evening rather than jumping ahead one evening and finding it harder (or more boring) to continue increasing. It was much more enjoyable with some music on, I didn't sing.... much!

### nonchalant — 2012-12-09T15:28:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66943>

My CT was AWESOME today!

I tried going down lower, and yes, it is good!

I got in when it was 39.5F, and it felt about the same as getting into the lower 50's.Â  I sat with legs immersed for 10 minutes and noticed my skin got very pink, like when I started CT earlier this year.Â  I layed down on my back, and it did seem easier than doing that in warmer water.Â  The temp at that time was 43F.Â Â  After another 10 minutes I turned over onto my tummy, which was also noticeably easier than doing so in warmer temps.Â  When I got out, total time 35 min, the temp was a balmy 48.5F.Â  After I was out about 5 minutes I started trembling a bit, and I noticed that my hands and feet were moving a bit slow, which was to be expected.Â  But I don't seem to be trembling a lot.Â  I'll see how warmup goes.

If you have been doing the 50-55F thing for a while already, it might be a good thing to try the lower temps you have been considering.Â  Thanks everyone who went before me and posted about it!

### Cowboy — 2012-12-09T19:27:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66957>

Well the low temps finally hit.Â  Last night was 5 degrees.Â  Right now it is 10 degrees.Â  I went out this afternoon and found 2 inches of ice on topÂ of my tank, time to move it indoors.Â  Got the ice broken upÂ  and the pumpÂ  was running by the inlet and outlets were frozen.Â  Got the drain going and the tub is now empty of all but some ice and sitting in a 50 degree garage.Â  I guess I should have taken care of this a coule of days ago.Â  Ended up with a broken filter inlet tube, so no filtration for a while.Â  I do not sleep as well with out a session in the cold water.Â  Still sleeping pretty good, but usually wake once a night.Â  With steadyÂ CT it seems IÂ  have a better chance of 6-7 hours of uninterupted sleep.

### furface — 2012-12-09T23:13:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66975>

Just had my first semi official bout of mini CT just now.

I just began my Leptin Rx last Monday, and after most of my regular morning showers, I'd turn the water completely cold, and stand there for about 2-3 minutes, just to get my body used to colder water. Even with that miniscule amount of time, I found it very refreshing, and invigorating.

Anyway, while watching the Sunday Night Football game tonight between Detroit, and Green Bay, in the snow, I thought I'd take a bigger leap. Nothing major mind you...I don't have a skin thermometer, haven't done any face dunking etc, but I've always been a go for the gusto type of guy.

So about a minute before halftime, which was about half an hour after I finished my dinner,Â  I drank a large glass of ice water, stepped into the shower, had a luke cool "regular" shower for about a minute, then turned the water completely cold, and just stood there.

I let the spray hit my upper chest, upper back, rear shoulders, carotid artery, stomach, and basically stood in each position for about 2 minutes before turning around to get as much of my body covered as possible. I even let the water hit me on the neck while facing sideways, just to get as much "coverage" as possible. Altogether I stood in the cold shower for 15 minutes.

I realize it's no big deal, and nothing compared to what you all do, but it's a start, and again, it felt so invigorating.

Just thought I'd share my first semi official mini CT experience. I might try face dunking tomorrow...if I can find a container big enough to hold cold water, lots of ice, and my big, FAT, head!!

### furface — 2012-12-09T23:56:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-66981>

Aww thanks, Caroline, and Anne...that means a lot to me.

I'm actually finding a lot of what we need to become adapted to...BAB (although I've always been a fairly BIG eater), very limited carbs, no snacks between meals (well that one is a bit harder getting used to, as is consuming all of the extra fat), cold adaption, a lot less trying than I actually thought, or envisioned it would be.Â  Not saying that it's "easy" mind you, but I am determined to change my life, lose the weight, and reinvent myself.

I don't know if I'll adapt, or even want to adapt to every single "rule", such as eating raw meat...I don't even like sushi, but for the most part, I am committed to taking this as far as I can. I realize it's a marathon, and not a sprint, and I'm taking it one day at a time.

I really appreciate, and value all of the support this board has given me, and hope I don't let anyone down.

I'm not sure if I'd actually completely fit while sitting in my bathtub, but one of these days, I just might try and see...even before I get a skin thermometer. No one's saying that I have to stay in for 45 minutes the first time, but it may help me get adapted a bit faster.

Another thing I have noticed since starting my Leptin Rx, being a type 2 diabetic, my blood glucose reading after meals, has never once been higher than 7.5 or 135 in U.S. units. (the allowable range after meals is 7-10, or 126.1 - 180.1 in U.S. units) so my blood glucose levels have been excellent IMHO.

Anyhoo, I apologize for the ramble, but I'm really liking the way this all is making me feel.

Thanks again everyone for your support, and encouragement.

Good luck to all of you on your journeys, and continued success!

### furface — 2012-12-12T14:56:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67241>

Just finished my morning face dunking session. Only my second FD session, but loving it so far.

Â 

Again only 6 dunks in 43Â° ice water. First dunk lasted 21 seconds, then the next five in order were, 32, 30, 32, 32, 32.

So overall better than yesterday, and I'm glad I was able to get up over 30 seconds for the majority of them. I find that if I don't take a really deep breath before I dunk, but instead just take a few good, slow, normal breaths...perhaps a bit longer than normal, then after the third one or so, calmly hold my breath, I can hold it longer than if I do take a really deep breath before dunking.

In any case, I really like this, as it sure is invigorating, and refreshing.

Â 

Will have my cold shower CT later on tonight.

### Martin — 2012-12-14T08:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67484>

Hi guys, I'm really getting into my CTing and decided to come join you all over here.

Whenever I mention this protocol to anyone they look at me like I'm nuts.Â 

Well, yes, but...

Anyway, as a thin person, it has been tough to confront the 55 - 60 water temps.Â 

In my consult, it was suggested that I could do a higher water temp due to my low body fat and lousy thyroid labs.Â So I've been doing mine at about 75 - 77 for the past few months. Anything lower and I shiver uncontrollably and it's tough to confront doing the next one.Â 

To help get inspired, I re-read the CT series and that always gets me pumped up to plunge in. I put on earbuds, play a podcast, and concentrate on learning rather than how cold my a$$ is. Works.

Reading about the Ancient Pathway is inspiring me to go colder. My well water comes out at 58 degrees and I should videotape my skinny butt jumping into a tub of what would beÂ to me, in essence, liquid nitrogen. Then I could post it here! On second thought, it may scar both me and any newbies permanently. Then again... it would be so funny!

### furface — 2012-12-14T13:16:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67500>

Man it is amazing what self confidence, and determination can help you accomplish. All while keeping things in perspective of course.

On Tuesday of this week I had my first ever sessions of face dunking in ice water with temperatures fluctuating between 43Â° and 46Â°. Not the coldest mind you, but not a reminder of Hawaii or the Caribbean either.

Anyway, of my 6 dunks, the longest I was able to hold my breath for was 28 seconds, which I did 5 times. Not bad I thought for someone rather obese. But I was determined to increase that time.

So this morning, on only my fourth day of FD, I again did 6 dunks in the same temperature ice water, and of those 6 dunks, my times were, 33 seconds for the first two, then for the final four dunks, I had a time of 40 seconds each!!

Again, I realize that this is no big deal for most, if not all of you, but for me, I feel so incredible! I honestly never thought I'd be able to hold my breath for 30 seconds, let alone 35, or 40.

I just want to thank Dr. Kruse, and everyone else on the forum who has given me so much help, support, and encouragement. And most of all to a very special someone whose belief in me, and never ending support and encouragement has helped me strive to try and become the best I can be, and to not stop until I get there! Thank you, Lindsey for all you've done, and all you do for me!

### freesia — 2012-12-14T14:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67514>

My kidsÂ watch old episodes of the Muppets on my notebookÂ while they're inÂ their cold baths. 27 minutes fly by! They looked forward to it because it's the Muppets....now they look forward to it for how they feel afterwards. And the Muppets.

### nonchalant — 2012-12-15T22:14:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67618>

Well this was a first.Â  I think I froze my callouses yesterday!Â  After my 30 minute bath in water that started out as 40F, everything seemed normal.Â  An hour later I was walking around and I noticed the soles of my feet felt funny.Â  I looked and my soles were whitish.Â  They felt ok to my hand, slightly cool like the rest of me.Â  The arches of my feet were fine, however, as were the tops of my feet/toes.

I didn't do anything, and later on they looked normal.Â  Today they are fine too.

### mccune9@hotmail.co.uk — 2012-12-16T05:54:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67626>

### nonchalant — 2012-12-16T10:33:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67630>

Muscle Up, I think when the skin senses warmth from your clothes (or the bed), the non-shivering thermogenesis shuts off, and shivering has to take up the slack.Â  You don't really have to be active, you just have to be cool.Â  I have stopped some fairly intense shivering by turning on a fan and removing covers/clothing.Â  It's counter-intuitive, but that's what Jack excels in...

Oh, and oxytocin stops shivering.Â  But then you start shivering again when the fun ends...

Â 

Hey I'm just doing my best to do what I can. I may not fully embrace every single aspect of everything, but I am willing to stepÂ  out of my comfort zone to get the results I desire.

Anything worthwhile is worth fighting for, and making a few...or more than a few sacrifices for. I've lost 22 lbs. since Dec. 1st, and I feel great!

Who knows, my typical New Year's resolution might actually be accurate this year, instead of the usual pile of B.S. (the same ol' "I'm going to lose weight, get into shape, blah blah blah" that a lot of people tend to make.)

### caroline — 2012-12-17T15:17:42+0200

Guess what? The other day I stupidly forgot my portmonnaie at home and I could not pay for the train to work. But the entire trip the conductor never showed up, and the trip takes almost one hour! He always comes. I was just too happy because if there is a controlleur and one has no ticket it gets really expensive! That was kind of magic too...

What is weird too.. I never felt so content about where I am and what I do. It is just amazing!? It is like i have gotten really good in enjoing the very moment without even trying to.. I just want to be just where I am. I sometimes think if there is somethong wrong with me..lol

### caroline — 2012-12-17T17:12:29+0200

I had an appointment with my Doctor yesterday. He is the loveliest man. It doesn't mean that everything is perfect - It definitely is not. There are some very sad things that I wish I could resolve.

and we understand/love our path?

### furface — 2012-12-17T21:44:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67765>

Thanks so much, Caroline, and Inger! So much appreciated for everyone's support and encouragement!

Â 

Kudos to both of you as well on your tremendous progress! It is amazing how much of a snowball effect this all has! You start off taking baby steps, then little by little you increase what you do, you add another few ingredients to the Leptin Rx/Epi-Paleo/CT recipe, and before you know it, you are well on your way.

Â 

Actually Caroline, I said I never liked Sushi. LOL. So I'm still putting Steak Tartare/Raw Meat on the back burner for now. I did like Lomi Lomi salmon, but that's when it was all mixed with the onions and tomatoes. haha.

I did have some braised GF beef short ribs the other day that I cooked pretty rare (I think I could still hear Bossy mooing) and it was very good. I did reheat the part closest to the bone for a minute or so as I wasn't even able to chew it. But otherwise, it was good. So as I mentioned before, even if I don't ever go into a butcher shop and order a steak, and begin to munch on it in the store, I do like my beef cooked medium rare, if not rare, so that's good enough for me.

Â 

As far as the CT showers go, Inger...hey whatever a person can do is better than nothing. It all adds up. I find the hardest thing to do is stand there all that time, so what I do, is sing Christmas Carols. LOL I'll sing a carol, like say, The 12 days of Christmas while facing forward and letting the cold water hit my carotid artery, upper chest, and sternum, then turn around and let the water hit my neck, shoulders, and upper back, while singing the same song. That way I'm getting the same amount of cold water hitting my front, and back. Then I turn, and sing another song, and repeat the process.

I love how the cold water "hardens" the skin a bit for a few seconds and makes it feel not frozen of course, but sort of not completely thawed out.

As far as the face dunks go, I can't believe how much I love those! So cold, refreshing, and invigorating!

You are so right as far as energy levels go, Inger! Even on 6 hours sleep, I feel so awake and rested. And my energy levels are through the roof. It's almost as if I'm "high" on those old ECA type fat burners...without the dangerous side effects, and drugs.

Keep it up everyone!

### furface — 2012-12-17T23:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67777>

Just finished today's CT session.

Had quite an upset stomach this morning after my BAB, so I held off on my morning face dunking sessions, so it was just a cold shower today, which I usually have after dinner.

So at halftime during the MNF game, I turned the cold water on in the shower, and stood there and sang Christmas carols for 40 minutes (good thing the walls are soundproof!

) then returned to watch the rest of the game.

Feeling great!

### Inger — 2012-12-18T02:35:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67783>

Furface, guess what! I do the same..! I sing too.. and so loud! I love it. When I lived with my husband still and I was taking cold showers there because I had no river, I always sang in the shower, it made it so much easier! I sometimes sing sitting in the river too.

I am playing I am a singer/star lol I do it all very emotional..lol nobody listens anyways (I am very carful about that!).

Â 

I think it is great how you eat your steak medium to raw! Baby steps. And with all this CT maybe you need to be less "radical" with your foods.. I have it easier going very strict with foods and a little less CT maybe. I do keep it at 55-58 degree F in my appartement and much colder (close to freezing) in my bedroom. I have a quite thick cover though, only my head gets cold! But I hope it does something good for this damaged brain..lol

We are all different, and have to do what works the best for us in given situations.

The results count!

### caroline — 2012-12-18T02:36:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67784>

I only take cold showers too - I am going to start singing Christmas Carols too - what a great idea! Thanks furface! It is so hard to stand there for any length of time without losing the plot!

### furface — 2012-12-18T09:52:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67802>

LOL! Thanks Inger, and Caroline!

Â 

That's awesome, Inger! Hey people sing in the shower all the time. haha. Not only that, but it sure makes the time go by faster. I also sing both the American, and Canadian national anthems as well. (Canadian in both French and English lol) I don't speak French, but I've watched so many Montreal Canadiens hockey games that I know it in French...well half french/half English, off by heart).

Not only does it make the time go by faster, if it's a fast paced song, like Jingle Bells, or even the 12 days of Christmas going down from the twelfth day, and you speed up as you're going, it even makes you build up some musical rhythm so you move around a bit.

Hey whatever works. And like you said, Inger...it's the results that count.

I sleep with my bedroom open as my landlord, who lives upstairs, cranks the heat so it's hotter than the equator, and I can't control it. But it is comfy with the window open. I'll usually sleep with no covers for the first hour or two then cover up as it starts to get a bit nippy.

Can't wait to have my BAB then face dunk time before I shower (regular shower) and go meet my dad.

Have a great day!

Thanks, Diane! Very much appreciated. I'm feeling awesome, and it just makes me want to continue on this path.

### furface — 2012-12-19T00:05:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67910>

Time really does fly when you're having fun!

Â 

So after a day off from face dunking, I had my usual 6 sessions this morning, which lasted, 45, 45, 50, 50, 60, & 60 seconds respectively! This was at about 6:30 A.M. Great way to start the day! (After my BAB!)

Â 

Then tonight after my dinner, it was cold shower time! So I'm singing away, thinking of all the Christmas carols I know off by heart, the U.S. and Canadian national Anthems...plus God Bless America, and a few other songs, and I'm thinking I've only been in here about half an hour.

Suddenly my phone rings, and I figure it's probably my dad, so I get out and answer in case it's an emergency, check the time, and to my surprise, I was in the shower for 45 minutes! Damn! LOL! I could have gone back in, but it's not the same. Still altogether, not a bad day of CT!

Â 

As I said, time really does fly when you're having fun!

### furface — 2012-12-19T05:24:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-67923>

> **caroline said**

I just loved him - he was so one directional - the only thing that mattered was winning! Â He once made the team take a train to their next game and made them read Â "The Power of Positive Thinking by Dale Carnegie.

Oh yeah, I remember those "Good ol' days!" Punch Imlach was a great coach! That was when they played for the love of the game, not the love of the money!

I was always a "Habs" fan! One time when I was living in Hamilton with some former friends, my ex-friend and I drove to Montreal one night, paid a scalper $50 each for two tickets to see a Toronto-Montreal game at the fabled Forum. What an experience! I swear you could actually feel the ghosts of the old Montreal Canadien legends. And to see all those banner hanging from the rafters. I still get chills just thinking about that night. Toronto won the game 5-3, but I didn't care. I actually got to see a game live at the Forum!

### furface — 2012-12-21T01:00:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68107>

It was a bit of a change of pace CT day for me today.

First off, instead of doing my usual morning face dunking sessions where I dunk for 45-60 seconds each time with 1.5-2 minutes rest in between, I decided to do quick bursts instead with shorter rest periods. So I dunked for 20 seconds, rested for 30 seconds then repeated. Did that 3 times, then increased my dunks to 30 seconds each also with only 30 seconds rest in between. Just for a change.

As far as my nightly cold shower, I was out all day walking around doing some last minute Christmas shopping, and my feet were just killing me. So my shower only lasted 25 minutes as it was too painful to even stand in the shower. But that's better than nothing.

Hopefully I'll have a longer session tomorrow.

### freesia — 2012-12-23T18:49:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68261>

Summer CT'ing is still a happening thing here! HopeÂ we can keep going right through January at least

I doubled the ice delivery order so I can keep doing 40's baths 3 - 4Â x per week. I'm paying $8 per week and delivery is free.

As well as delivered ice I fill the freezer with "upcycled" icy water inÂ 24 850ml plastic containers after my bath. All that fetching, filling and walking back and forwards to the freezerÂ is aÂ good warm up exercise!
We've been using a probiotic (an effective microorganisms product I use for general cleaning and washing)Â in the water so it doesn't go icky, and we take showers first. It seems OK to re use enough water to make all those ice blocks 3 times anyway.

The kids needÂ 8 or 9Â of thoseÂ big ice "bricks"Â to get their bath water to around 58F. Then while I'm settling the kidsÂ into bed after their bathsÂ my husband dumps all the rest of the ice in the bath, and I get in when it's anything from 42 - 47F ...so long as it's still got enough ice to carry me through an hour at the same temp.

The air temperature is too warm for optimal CT but IÂ know it's worth doing. I feel SO great afterwards.Â We all sleepÂ incredibly well afterwards too. The kids seem keen to continue and as long as they want to I'll support it. Active BAT is their birthright anyway!

My husband is trained in Buteyko breathing and he got completely off asthma meds 12 years ago. He's _always_ maintained it might help me with increased oxygenation to the tissues but I could never manage even getting started on it. It was just too difficult and I felt like my lungs would burst! Well it's early days but I can do it now. I reckon I might climb Mt Everest yet! There's a plan!

### furface — 2012-12-23T19:52:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68263>

It's been a good weekend so far for me as far as CT goes.

First off, on Friday, after a rather anemic 25 minute cold shower on Thursday, I kicked it up a few notches and increased my time on Friday to one hour and three minutes!

Then on Saturday I decided to try and videotape my face dunking sessions for my friend in Texas with my cellphone video camera...and had 3 dunks of one minute each, two at 50 seconds, and one at 37 seconds. It's rather awkward trying to hold your breath in ice water while holding one hand above your head while holding the phone video camera at the same time. Takes away quite a bit of your concentration, and energy.

But overall, so far a pretty good weekend.

Not sure if I'll have a cold shower tonight as well though.

### MamaGrok — 2012-12-23T23:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68275>

I haven't been able to post lately. Â I'll keep this short in case it doesn't work.

I get horrible shivering after CT. Â HORRIBLE. Â I did CT a lot last spring, skipped the summer, went again this fall. Â It was no problem getting back into the 55F water, and I even took it to 51 and got pink skin for the first time. Â In the water, I am never cold. Â  I get out after half an hour, and then 5 minutes later, I AM FREEZING. Â This happened a few times last spring, but is killing me now. Â My temp goes down to 95.0 from its normal hypothyroid 97.0.

I've been hypo a long time, but have never had the common experience of being cold all the time. Â I am only cold after CT!

I can't take the 30 - 60 minutes of freezing to death after CT anymore unless I know there is a light at the end of a fairly short tunnel. Â I'm not seeing any health changes from it lately, either.

Spur me on, please!! Â Tell me about your afterchill experiences, and how long it took to get over it. Â

I get horrible shivering after CT. Â HORRIBLE. Â I did CT a lot last spring, skipped the summer, went again this fall. Â It was no problem getting back into the 55F water, and I even took it to 51 and got pink skin for the first time. Â In the water, I am never cold. Â  I get out after half an hour, and then 5 minutes later, I AM FREEZING. Â This happened a few times last spring, but is killing me now. Â My temp goes down to 95.0 from its normal hypothyroid 97.0.

Â 

I've been hypo a long time, but have never had the common experience of being cold all the time. Â I am only cold after CT!

I can't take the 30 - 60 minutes of freezing to death after CT anymore unless I know there is a light at the end of a fairly short tunnel. Â I'm not seeing any health changes from it lately, either.

Â 

Spur me on, please!! Â Tell me about your afterchill experiences, and how long it took to get over it. Â 

Hi MamaGrok - you are missed around here! Sounds like an "ask Jack" question maybe? It will be hard to keep at it if it persists.

Well I decided to have a cold shower CT session tonight after all. I guess I was just in a festive mood to sing some Christmas Carols...which I did for 70 minutes.

So all in all a pretty decent last three days of Cold Thermogenesis.

MG, have you tried to take way shorter baths and more often? Like every morning and evening a dip or a icecold short shower, just a few minutes or less? I am sure it will get better little by little, even if it takes months and years!

### MamaGrok — 2012-12-24T10:36:32+0200

Shorter is a great idea, except it takes a Â tremendous amount of effort to get my bath to 51 degrees (I do have the river, but it takes even more effort to get there in the daylight with dh to spot), and I want to maximize the effort I guess! Â It's just crazy, b/c I'm totally comfortable in the water for 30 minutes, but the misery is afterwards.

I don't know what the "6-12" refers to. Â Help?

Thanks, all! Â I WANT to do this! Â I even installed a chest freezer in my bedroom to make it easier! Â But I don't want to be doing the equivalent of chronic cardio (too much too frequent too intense stressor), either.

I had the same scary result when I would take temp orally after CT. Try rectal. I have had almost no shivering lately, but have reduced my time in cold. I also think Dr K says more frequent shorter sessions may be more beneficial. I would play with duration and possibly find a sweet spot that doesn't make you shiver severely.

### freesia — 2012-12-24T19:15:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68336>

Mamagrok, it must be individual, depending on hormone status I guess,Â but after a bit of trial and errorÂ I found I just couldn't handle warm up well if my temp was was lower than 97.4 before I got in the bath. I resorted to warm up assistance a few times early on and I believe though maybe itÂ slowed down my adaptationÂ it didÂ me over a hump.
Anyway, I've been CTing a solid 9 months nowÂ and my temperature has come up to 98.5 (after many, many years of low body temp!)Â so overall my strategy seems to have paid off.Â 

Otherwise, yep maybe shorter sessions more frequently. I think Dr Kruse recommends that approach in some cases. It wasn't always practical for me to do it that way though.

### MamaGrok — 2012-12-24T20:05:04+0200

### furface — 2012-12-25T13:57:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68382>

First off, MERRY CHRISTMAS everyone!! Hope you all have a wonderful day!!

After taking the day off from any type of CT yesterday, I didn't want to skip two days in a row, so I decided on an early face dunking session, in which I discovered one important factor...if you don't give your full attention to what you're doing, you cannot expect to perform to the best of your ability.

During my first two dunks, my mind was clearly on other things, and therefore my times were sub standard of what I now expect from myself. Only 30, and 32 seconds respectively. So I cleared my head of all distractions, took a few deep breaths, and focused all my energy, and concentration on the task at hand. After all, if I can't dedicate 5-10 minutes to my health in an otherwise hectic day, then there is something seriously wrong, and I need to reassess this whole thing.

So I gave my full attention to the ice water in the bucket, and my next 4 dunks were significantly more focused than the previous two. The times for the final four were, 45, 50, 60 and 70 seconds each respectively! I think that is exactly what I needed as I felt so refreshed, invigorated, and energized, as if a surge of energy shot through my body!

Showed me just how much a person's mindset plays a key role in whatever they are doing.

If you only put in a half-assed effort, you'll only get half-assed results!

### freesia — 2012-12-25T16:20:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68404>

Warm up assistance....not optimal for the fast route.
Anyway, I used warm baths several times early on. ...Once after shivering for nine hours. My only regret is thatÂ I waited nine hours!Â I usedÂ a hot water bottle a few times too. Mostly I managed without, but sometimes it was too hard.

What you're describing sounds like whatÂ I've heard peopleÂ call "after drop". This is probably unleasant enough for anyone who goes into their CT with a decent body temperature, but if it's running low to start with the after drop maybe (?) affects you worse. That was my impression after taking my temperature more regularly.Â 

I have seen cold adaptation (for heart health improvement purposes) described with a warm up bath afterwards. They only adapted to 57F but biomarkers improved...despite the warm up assistance. I know this is not CT as Dr Kruse lays out in his protocol but my point is that even aÂ gentler route gets some results.

Anne.

### MamaGrok — 2012-12-26T17:28:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68448>

> **AKMan said**
> Did a 30 minute, 35 degree soak today. First in a month. Had NO problems getting in or adjusting. Felt ggggggreat!
>
> Since I had to go into town right away and didn't want to drive with the shivers, I followed the dip with a hot shower. Felt wonderful and no shivering.
>
> I'm pondering the effects of the hot shower on CT. In a normal session for me at that temp, I would start to shiver about 10 minutes after I got out and it would last an hour or more and several digits would go 'white and numb'. With the hot shower, it completely negated the effects of the after-drop and I was normal immediately. Since all the pathways were activated (cold sensors, vaso-constriction, UCP-1, BAT, NST, etc...) You'd think it would be an overall win. I'm thinking the only thing warming up quickly 'cost' me was a few hundred or less calories I would have used shivering. Hmmmm

AKMan, have you learned anything about the answer to this question yet?

### MamaGrok — 2012-12-26T17:56:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68451>

Okay, interesting study:Â http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/1447067 Â It seems to say that shivering is more effective than exercise in warming core temp after a serious drop, and a warm bath \*slows\* core warming! Â That seems to be counter to Anne & AKMan's experience! Â Perhaps because they were slightly cold adapted at the time? Â I don't want to cop out with that easy an explanation, though...

It's possible I would have done better holding off on a warm bath, but on a couple of occasions it definitely offered me relief, and didn't seem to cause a worsening of the "warm up crisis" at the time.Â It did make me sleepy!

### furface — 2012-12-27T01:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68470>

Had a great CT cold shower tonight! Longest session so far...71 minutes. Probably could have gone longer, but I was tired of singing. LOL

Chest and back both a nice, rosie pink/red colour. Can't wait to jump into bed, lay without covers by the open window, then bundle up nice and cozy/toasty.

### MamaGrok — 2012-12-27T14:40:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68505>

I did Perplexus in my 52F bath yesterday, and it helped to keep my mind off the cold! Â Usually, I don't have any trouble with the cold, but yesterday, I was having a very hard time, and couldn't ever get my trunk in the water, just legs & belly. Â 

I warmed up with a 95F bath, which kept me from shivering misery. Â I could feel a low, underlying shiver in my legs, but no misery. Â I was chilly, though, and longed for the water to be much warmer. Â My temp had dropped 1 degree by time I got in the warm bath, and it dropped no more after that, so I think that's a success! Â But over 30m in the warm bath, it only went up only .1 degree, even though I was much more comfortable. Â I'm guessing the warm water told my body it didn't need to do the warming itself?

But when I got out of the bath, I was FREEZING! as always after getting out of warm water into cool air, and shivering like crazy till I got in the bed 5 minutes later, then BAM, stopped shivering and my temp was back to normal.

All very interesting to me!

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-27T15:16:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68514>

Today in the pond I ran into some serious ice buildup....banged my shin up before I realized how bad it was...It wasn't my plan to continue to get in the pond once things got really cold. But, I was inspired by a consult with Dr. K .earlier in the month. We talked about the difference in outside spring water and inside baths and how the electron energy of the outside water recharges you really quickly . Now that we're getting much colder temps and more snow, I may be looking for an ice auger because I'm not going to stop. Â Mind you I'm no where near able to stay in for great lengths of time...but I love being in there right up until my feet or hands tell me to get out.

Happy Winter!

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-27T16:06:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68519>

Hey Anne...I do feel really grateful that I have our pond right out our front door. I'm not CTing every day...it's been every other day for me this month and while I'm really happy that I can talk about ENJOYING being in 36-38degree water, I know I'm not cold adapted by a long shot. My thyroid and adrenals have a ways to go..things are getting better all the time though because I start to really get revved up thinking about going into the pond after a day away from it. I usually make sure I've just done all the barn chores so I'm warm and then I'm able to go in whatever the weather. We're going to get around 16 to 18 inches of snow here today, so maybe I'll try some rolling in the snow tomorrow.

It was kind of funny today...one the town's trucks came up our road with the snow plow just as I was pounding the ice with my shovel...they turn around at our house so I know the driver got a good look...too bad I couldn't see his expression when I waved at him...but I'm happy to provide a new topic for discussion Â down at the town store!

today's CT was spent on the Mountain hitting the steeps and deeps... it was awesome.Â  came home took a shower - then DH and I sat down in the snowbank outside my parents kitchen window... I was only able to lie on my back for about a minute... tomorrow I shall lay on my front!

### furface — 2012-12-28T02:35:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68559>

Had an off day of CT today. My cold shower only lasted 58 minutes. I had fully intended to surpass an hour once again, but for some reason during my session, I just wasn't "feeling" it, so I cut it short. Of course had I known how close to being in the shower for an hour, I would have somehow managed to stay in at least two more minutes. But oh well...maybe next time! Still better than nothing I suppose.

I read somewhere online in a few articles actually that heavier/obese people actually don't have as much B.A.T. as leaner people do, but hopefully this is still having a positive effect on my bodyfat as it is. I know I'm feeling a lot more energized from both the cold showers, and the face dunking, so at least in that respect I'm receiving benefits from it. Of course I've just started about 3 weeks ago, so I think it's still too early to tell if there'll be any long term, positive effects on me.

### Inger — 2012-12-28T06:15:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68565>

I love reading about peoples CT! Wow nice pictures Seahorse!!! Looks like my hole..lol The path to the river behind the house looks so beautiful now!

I try to post a pic but it is a struggle... looks like it want work.

I use crocs now into the water, makes my toes freeze a little less. I do very short dips too Seahorse my toes and fingers cannot take more than a minute or so! And I always keep my hands out from the water most part.

Furface you are crazy..lol one hour in a cold shower! Wow.

Gretchen to roll in the snow is so cool! I do that sometimes too, it is really cold, colder than the coldest water for sure! One day I took a "shower" in the snow on my balcony, it was this very light and soft new snow and I used it to wash my body. It was so refreshing!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-28T07:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68568>

WOW, Inger, that looks so beautiful. It makes me want to pack my bags and move back into the wilderness, where I was the happiest ever. No civilization, no people, no phone, no electric light, water just a spring outside and pure, raw and unadulterated nature. The cooking place just an old wooden stove with the chopping block right next to it. The furniture just an old burned out oak tree for the clothes, the heating a sheet of metal, wrapped to make an open fireplace with some rivets in it to keep it together and some pipes to let out the smoke, self made chairs and an occasional visit from some stinking skunks.

At night, you could see the moon and the stars and deer gently moving around the "house" which was justÂ  a tipi like structure with clear plastic wrapped around it with half a meter of snow in the winter.Â  Only the spring and some nearby creek to bathe in. I was surely cold adapted then.

We only went down into the valley, when we needed food to stock up and then went back up into the mountain. We were the crazy Germans who lived out somewhere and nobody knew exactly where. When we needed some money my husband drove down into the valley, picked out a house he liked and did a watercolor painting of it. Soon enough somebody came out of the house to look what this guy did there and always they loved the picture so much that they bought it. Funny thing was that in the painting, the house looked exactly as it was, but he painted it with such detail, catching the sun and the shades, the cat that was lying in a hole in the stack of firewood that the people suddenly saw it with his eyes and realized how beautiful it was.

Your picture brings back fond memories. CTing in my backyard in a cold whirlpool has its charm too, but it is just not the same.

### AdrianaDr. Radler-Pohl — 2012-12-28T07:30:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68570>

... such a beautiful place ... ! ... I also feel the longing to live in the wilderness. I was always the happiest when we would camp somewhere and do things as simple as possible ... sigh!!

### AdrianaDr. Radler-Pohl — 2012-12-28T07:34:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68571>

.... here in Germany the winter is nowhere to be seen .... was the warmest winter since they started charting the weather ... so for me is now a short weightlifting training and then shower - CT ...

### furface — 2012-12-28T11:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68584>

LOL, thanks, Inger! I find that after the first 5 seconds or so, I don't even feel the "cold" of the water, other than when I turn to direct the water on either my upper chest/carotid artery, or my upper back/back of the neck. It's just that initial blast that sort of shocks you for a few seconds, then I just get used to it. Most of the time the only reason I get out is because my feet are too sore from standing that long, and/or I can't think of any more songs to sing...or I'm just tired of singing. LOL.

It does look very beautiful where you live. A true winter wonderland. Must be very peaceful.

### Inger — 2012-12-28T11:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68586>

What is so magic with the wild? Me too always felt best there.. I like luxury too and beautiful houses and stuff but nothing comes close to wild nature and the peace in there. Without any comfort and stuff. Nothing. It is the very best place to me to regenerate and heal.

Charlotte I cannot believe you lived like that.. huh! Must have been quite a experience... maybe why you have gotten so wise?

@ Anne, too sad you have no winter this year. Then I am happy to be here.. I missed Germany the last days quite a lot IDK why.. friends I had there and such. I really loved to live in Hamburg, but then.. now I feel just happy to live in this winterland.

@ Furface, I bet your voice is beginning to sound like a proffesional singer with all this exersice..!

LOL! I don't know about that. I think I did hear some coyotes trying to blend in with their howling the other night. Maybe they think I sound like the roadrunner...or one of their off key off spring.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-28T14:14:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68597>

Coyotes, I remember those too. The howling was incredible. After they got their kill they sat around the dead animal waiting for the death rigidity to wear off so they could have their feast and while waiting they howled. Maybe thats how you sound, furface.

They scared the shits out of me, espcecially when they were close by.I wanted to burry my left over chicken really deep deep, so they couldn\`t smell it. They didn't, but the skunks did and tried to get into the tipi. One managed one day to dig under the plastic. We woke up as it was grunting in the "kitchen" trying to get into the shelf, where I had stored the chicken. We both got up, my husband taking a stick trying to manage it out of the house. We were stark naked, so we put a sheet of plastic before us in case he would let loose. He did and after that we had to move out of the house for a week, the smell was so bad. After that, no more food inside.

Well, Inger, I do not know about wise, actually a lot of the time I feel rather stupid and I still get myself into a lot of trouble. I don't mind though. These days I feel I am just babbling along a lot, but I love how everybody else's story makes me remember mine and I love sharing them. This blog is such a great conglomeration of exceptional people who share the same goals and work hard to achieve. I really like that and the harder someone works on himself the more I am driven to do better. That is almost heaven. Working with people day in and day out who only do it for the money and that only because they have to, but without any passion or will for self-improvement is very tedious at times.

Maybe I need to watch Dr. Kruse more closely to find out, how he keeps so many people on their toes.

### furface — 2012-12-28T15:42:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68609>

Those must have been some rather heart pounding experiences, Charlotte. Not only the coyotes, but also the skunks. I can only imagine how your house smelled. We get quite a few dead skunks on the highways here, and the smell is atrocious...to have one spray inside a house must be fifty times worse. Good thing you had the sheets of plastic, and weren't directly hit.

It actually must sound so incredible to hear coyotes, or wolves howling in the wild...esp during winter months, and if you'd ever see them, and their breath in the cold air...must be a spectacular sight to witness. Nature at it's purest form.

I myself think that one reason why this whole journey is so rewarding, and invigorating...no matter which aspect, is because the rewards are much greater than any monetary reward one could ever achieve, accomplish, or gain. Self improvement, and just the way it allows us to feel about ourselves, even so early on in the scheme of things, is so much more rewarding than any token, substance reward could ever be. To know that what we're doing, or trying to accomplish, even on a day to day level is helping us become healthier, and happier, really does help a person stay on course...at least it does for me.

Also seeing, and reading about others' accomplishments, and successes is so inspirational, and motivational, it pushes me to not only keep going, but to try harder.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-28T16:35:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68612>

yes, furface, yes, exactly. I used to think that self-improvement was only something you do on the inside, like observing what you think, how you react, how disciplined one is, etc. Now I have come to realize it is not just that. It is on the outside also if you want to think of your body as outside. How can I be clear in my mind, if my brain is foggy? How can I feel good, when my stomach is bulging from the bloating, how can I be lightfooted when my ankles are swollen? And what else?

Now I find that the better my body is in shape and functioning the lighter everything else is. Life is becoming real easy. And OMG, give it what it needs and it heals so fast. At least with me.

So, please, everybody here, please stick with me, bring out the best in me, so I do not have to make my biggest fear come true which is now, that some day I get so cocky, that I ditch it because I think I do not need it anymore. I call that self-boykott.Â  I tend to think that success has to come the hard way and I cannot believe sometimes that it is so easy. Maybe it is my German upbringing. One of my parents saying used to be: just wait until you grow up. It made me not to want to. Or: life is hard. So I tried everything to make it easy and thereby making it harder.

I am on course now, thanks to all these wonderful people here, who show me by example, what really hard work on oneself means. And even though that when I fall I eventually get up every time, but the getting up is not always easy. I really do want to spare myself this time. So keep the standard high, Dr. Kruse, so I never have the feeling to quite live up to it, just to keep me on my toes.

### furface — 2012-12-28T17:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68618>

Very well put, Charlotte!

Being a virtual puppy around here compared to all of the "veterans" lol, so sometimes I feel a bit out of place in saying some things, or giving my opinion about certain aspects of all of this. So I just try and relay from my own experiences...as limited as they are, but knowing how lousy I used to feel in comparison to how I feel now, even after even less than one month along on this whole journey, hopefully does help me to understand, or relay the feeling to those who are just starting out themselves, or even on the fence about partaking in this lifestyle. One thing I will say is that it is actually easier to not only start, but to continue, and delve deeper into, than one would actually think it would be...esp things like CT.

Two months ago if someone were to tell me that not only would I be dunking my face in ice water, or taking cold water showers on a regular basis, even showers that last over an hour, but even loving it, and looking forward to doing it on an almost daily basis,Â  I would literally have called them crazy (with a few expletives included), and sent for the guys in the white suits with the straight jackets! But it's surprising at what one is capable of if they not only put their mind to it, but don't stick to, or conform to standardized way of thinking. i.e. "A cold water shower, or an ice bath? I'll freeze to death!! I'll catch pneumonia!! etc" But if you've never tried it, how do you know? (Still doesn't mean I'll try eating raw meat though. LOL! I'm doing this at MY pace...haha.)

I also tend to think that memory has to play a key factor in all of this, and in keeping us on course. All we have to do is remember how we USED to feel...for some of us that only requires a very short term memory, or some such as myself, still have some of those negative feelings as we're no where close to feeling how we'll eventuallyÂ  hopefully feel, in order to stay on track. I know I never want to feel like crap again. I just want to feel the best that I can feel, while still remaining happy, and enjoying myself. If it becomes a "chore" that's when it's no longer fun, or enjoyable to do, and that's when I'd say, "screw it!"

Life shouldn't be a chore, or it shouldn't be a TASK to become healthy and happy, it should be something we WANT to do, not something we feel FORCED to do. If I never reach my "optimal" self because I refuse to partake in certain aspects, so be it. That's fine with me. I'll still know, and feel that I'm so much healthier and happier at whatever "level" I eventually reach than I would ever have been had I not even begun this journey. It's great to strive to be the best that we can be, and that's what I believe we are all doing, as long as we maintain perspective, and remember that NOBODY is perfect, and IMHO no one could ever be "perfect", and to even think that it is possible to be, puts false expectations on one's self. So as I said, I believe we should strive to be the best we could possibly be, but IMHO there is no such thing as "perfection".

I apologize if I'm speaking out of turn.

### SeaHorse — 2012-12-28T18:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68623>

Furface...great thoughts...I agree about perfection. Somebody very wise said that perfection is the kiss of death for creativity. If you fear failure you'll never be comfortable heading into uncharted waters. The great thing about regaining our health is that all these beliefs that have shaped us in the past can be examined and either thrown away or kept. We have the freedom to think creatively and try things on to see if they serve us. I just find myself doing a lot of laughing at the reactions I get around CT or what I'm eating. We don't know where we may be in a year but we're going forward with a spirit of exploration and adventure.

Â Don't knock the raw meat too much Furface, you never know what's in store!!

I find myself snacking on raw meat before I cook it...and believe me that was not me six months ago. We are constantly inventing ourselves from the cells up, and I agree that our memory of how and who we used to be can serve a useful purpose, but I don't dwell on it too much either. I used to think that happiness was something that was a gift from the outside...I don't believe that anymore..we are the creators of our own happiness and it is so tied to our well being and ability to see within.

### furface — 2012-12-28T19:38:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68626>

Thanks SeaHorse, much appreciated. Some great thoughts of yours as well! I agree that we shouldn't dwell too much on the past, as that could lead to falling into bad habits, or a familiar "comfort" zone, even though it might have been a bad place, but people for some reason are unwilling to change a lot of the time...esp if that change may be difficult to adhere to, or seemingly "painful" or awkward to initiate.

I also think that people are always looking to find the easy way out, or the easiest, fastest, simplest way to do things, and we have to remember that this is a MARATHON, and not a SPRINT...even though Dr. Kruse advocates Sprinting versus running a Marathon as far as exercise is concerned. Kind of a Dr. Kruse OxyMoron isn't it if you think about it. LOL.

As far as the raw meat goes, please don't misunderstand, I'm not knocking it by any means. I just have no desire to try it. At least not now, and not in the near future as far as I can tell. I love my beef cooked medium-rare, as IMHO the spices you add, and the slight cooking of the outer portion at least, adds to the flavour. Esp if you have a cut such as ribeye, and the juices all flow into the onions or mushrooms also in the pan. At least this way I'm eating plenty of it, where otherwise I most likely wouldn't be.

I am also one who does care what other people think, and if I'm ever having dinner at a restaurant...esp with a S.O. or potential S.O., I don't want her to look at me as if I've completely lost it, and order a steak and tell them to bring it to me while Bossy is still mooing.

I know I shouldn't care what other people think, but at times I do.

"If you only put in a half-assed effort, you will only get half-assed results!" Jason aka Furface.

### furface — 2012-12-29T01:52:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68638>

Just finished a double whammy of CT!

Usually I'll do either face dunking in the morning, or take a cold shower at night, but today I felt enthusiastic, so after dinner, I did my first ever nighttime face dunking sessions of 45, 50, 60, 70, 60, and 60 seconds respectively. Then I dried my face and hair, relaxed a few minutes, then drank two glasses of ice water, and stepped into the shower, and began to sing while enjoying a nice, refreshing cold shower which lasted 80 minutes!

The face dunking beforehand actually made the cold water of the shower not seem as cold. Maybe I'm onto something. LOL.

So all in all, a great night of Cold Thermogenesis.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-29T06:20:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68640>

yes, furface, I agree, the only limitation we have is our own thinking. So to me the process goes like that. Think about your goal and how you want to achieve it, create your personal plan and then become determined. Just recently I thought, what is determination? It is made of two words:

1\. deter

2\. mind

So to me it means that once your mind is made up, deter mind and become determined. In action that means do not let your mind deter you from your goal by making excuses, but use the brain to improve and continue.

One other thought. If you have not yet considered raw meat yet, start with carpaccio. Every good restaurant, at least in Germany, serves it and it has become very popular. It is really tasty and that way you can ease into it. And since it is popular, even a date would not mind. And besides that, would you want to associate with someone long-term who is not tolerant of your eating habits? That way you can find out easy and early if there are things you have in common and if it is only tolerance.

My youngest son told me not long ago an interesting thing about his girl friend. He said: you know Mom, I do not know if I love her, but I feel so comfortable and at ease with her. That made me really happy because this is exactly what a great relationship is made of. To feel comfortable, accepted and at ease in each others company. I am glad he found someone he can be relaxed with and takes him for who he is. A great gift.

### furface — 2012-12-29T12:49:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68668>

True words of wisdom, Charlotte! I guess one could say that the only person who could stop...or DETER a person from reaching their goals, is themselves if they let themselves, or anyone else sabotage their efforts. If you keep a mindset that you are going to succeed, and not let anyone, or anything...including one's self stop them, then you are guaranteed to be successful.

As far as raw meat is concerned, I wish people would just let that issue go. I may eventually try it, I may not. I myself doubt that it's going to make a hugely significant difference to my end result, so if I'm happy doing what I am doing, and making progress that makes me comfortable, then as far as I'm concerned, I'm happy. People have been healthy for hundreds of years eating their meat cooked, even medium-rare. We still don't know how raw meat will effect our bodies inside down the road, and it's been proven that eating ground beef on the rare side has caused health issues. I'm not trying to start anything, and as I've said, have no problems with anyone who wishes to eat raw meat...more power to them, but why can't people just please respect my feelings, or wishes on the matter, and let me enjoy me meat the way I like it? Quit trying to force me to do something that I am not currently comfortable with, and currently have no intention or desire to try.

### diane — 2012-12-29T13:44:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68673>

> **furface said**
> As far as raw meat is concerned, I wish people would just let that issue go. I may eventually try it, I may not. I myself doubt that it's going to make a hugely significant difference to my end result, so if I'm happy doing what I am doing, and making progress that makes me comfortable, then as far as I'm concerned, I'm happy. People have been healthy for hundreds of years eating their meat cooked, even medium-rare. We still don't know how raw meat will effect our bodies inside down the road, and it's been proven that eating ground beef on the rare side has caused health issues. I'm not trying to start anything, and as I've said, have no problems with anyone who wishes to eat raw meat...more power to them, but why can't people just please respect my feelings, or wishes on the matter, and let me enjoy me meat the way I like it? Quit trying to force me to do something that I am not currently comfortable with, and currently have no intention or desire to try.

Even though my first time was a bit squeamish (although I love sushi, it was the beef that freaked me out a bit. Of course, I was grossed out when I started to feed my dogs raw and now it's no big deal - so I knew I would overcome LOL). But I love how people are pushing others to do just a bit more. That to me is what this forum is all about!Â

### furface — 2012-12-29T14:02:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68675>

I understand, and appreciate that, Diane, but I'm the type of person if someone keeps "pushing" me to do something, or "nagging" me about something, it will just piss me off, and I'll say "screw it!" to the whole thing. I had a former roommate whom I thought was a friend, but all she did was order me around, and that really turned me off, so now anytime it seems as if anyone is "ordering" me to do something, or even telling me what to do, it just irritates me, and pisses me off eventually.

I'm feeling great right now, and yes, perhaps if I started eating raw meat, I might feel a bit better, or even quite a bit, but I'm happy with the way I'm feeling for the most part now, so please, respect that, and drop this whole eating raw meat discussion. I might try it in the future, but at my own pace if I do. Grass-fed meat is expensive as it is, and I'm on a fixed income. I don't feel like shelling out $15 - $25 for one steak, then trying a few bites raw, only to most likely puke it up esp if it's my first time. I can't afford to do that.

I'm sorry if I sound rude, that is purely not my intention. I just have to do this whole thing at my own pace, and if I partake in every single aspect, or just the majority of them, then that's up to me. I don't drink, don't smoke, don't party, so I get a lot of pleasure from my meals...whether or not that's frowned upon, I really don't care...food should be enjoyed...even if it's healthy food. So IMHO a nice, juicy GF ribeye steak, cooked medium-rare with all the spices, and mushrooms, and onions etc is going to taste delicious, and much juicier, and tastier than if it was a round steak, or lesser cut. But I feel if I were to eat it raw, it wouldn't taste any better, it would just be raw beef, and I want to enjoy and savour my meals whenever I can. I think I deserve SOME pleasures in life. This whole journey should be fun...not a chore.

As far as making a change in people's attitude, and spirit goes, I myself think it's a result of the whole enchilada...not just one particular aspect. Yes I'm sure it plays a role, but as I've said, if I can be 85-90% healthier, happier, more energetic, less brain fog, etc etc because of what I am currently doing, then that's good enough for me. It's 85-90% healthier, happier, more energetic, less brain fog, etc etc than I was before all of this.

So please, everyone, once and for all, let the raw meat situation/discussion, as far as it concerns me, GO. Please.

### Charlotte — 2012-12-29T14:57:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68679>

Of course Furface, it was never my intention to push you into anything. I couldn't, even if I tried as the choice is entirely your own. Let's say I was trying to entice you. But of course the subject is closed.

And I definitely do not wish to bring your good mood down and above all, not your input into this forum as it is appreciated and enjoyed.

I am heading off tomorrow morning on my annual Sylvester party at a beautiful lake in the south-east of Germany, where I will be enjoying the fireworks from my friends window, from which one has a great view around the lake. The whole lake reflects the firework and it is some sight to behold.

So I wish you a great Sylvester too and a very healthy and happy New Year.

### kathylu — 2012-12-31T16:22:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68852>

I make it a policy to not do things I don't enjoy. So, eat your food the way you enjoy! Makes perfect sense to me. I have always been a bit adventurous with food, so I was pleasantly surprised to find that I actually like raw protein (both meat and fish). I suspect it might have something to do with seasonings though...steak tartare has a lot of flavorings in it, sushi gets dipped in tamari and wasabi. I only eat raw occasionally though, for a change. I probably wouldn't like it all the time. So, please don't feel pressured to do anything. I don't think anyone meant it that way.

### furface — 2012-12-31T16:43:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68855>

Â

But I agree with you as far as not doing things that you don't enjoy. That makes sense.

### furface — 2013-01-01T00:24:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68884>

Had a bit of an off day as far as CT is concerned.Â  Had a morning session of face dunking with 3 dunks @ 50 seconds each, and 3 more at 60 seconds each, then tonight I really couldn't get "into" it, so my cold shower only lasted 51 minutes. Hope to do better next year!

I also want to see my average time per dunk, and per shower each month, so since I began my cold showers on Dec. 9th, and face dunking on Dec. 11th, for the 66 face dunks I've done, I averaged 42 seconds per dunk, and for the 17 cold showers I've taken, my average time was 41 minutes per shower.

As I said above, I hope to do much better in the new year!

Happy New Year everyone!! \*blows party favour...throws confetti\*

### endless — 2013-01-01T01:51:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68886>

Happy New Year, everyone!

Seahorse, how long do you stay in your swimming hole? I am only asking because I am about to attempt the same type of CT spot and wondering what to expect!

Inger, your path is like the idyllic winter CT wonderland trail!

Charlotte, I have lived like you are describing, also, as unusual as that is in this day and age! No electricity, no plumbing, and we were snowed in in the winters. Wood heat for heat and cooking, kerosene and candles for light, and CBC radio for entertainment! We had an old log building we renovated into a sauna, and once a week we would fire it up and all go sweat and wash, which included a dip in the icy stream.Â 

As far as the coyotes, they are STILL a pain in my ass, trying to eat my kitties and dog

. I had to buy wolf urine on the internet and head out on my snowshoes into the woods to mark my territory!

Not much to add about my own CT....I was hoping to join a 'polar bear swim' tomorrow, it's the one time of year when regular folks brave the cold water! Depends on work....hopefully I can swing it! I'll show them how it's done!

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-01T15:19:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68923>

Endless...I'm only able to stay in between 2-5 minutes depending on the day and my feet. I'm actually thinking that I'll get some neoprene socks to allow me to stay in a bit longer. The water this morning was 35 degrees and the air was minus 1 with the windchill. Holly Smokes! I wore a pair of old Bog boots that filled with water and kept my feet a little insulated so I didn't have white toes when I got out. Ingar recently said she was wearing crocs...anything to help the toes! I'm trying not to get dressed right away, but let my skin warm up slowly...it seems to be working in that I didn't feel as cold half an hour later. Still got the nice tingling feeling though.

I should probably add in some indoor longer baths to my routine, but for me these really intense sessions have made a lot of difference in how I'm handling winter. No depression, more energy (mitochondria issues for sure) and a sense of excitement about the winter that has to do with not fearing the cold.

Hope you have a great time with your outdoor CT!

### furface — 2013-01-02T01:02:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68958>

I wanted to start the New Year off right as far as CT sessions go, so I've just finished a 67 minute cold water shower. Maybe this is an omen that things will add up for me...since 6+7 add up to 13!!

Not only for the year 2013, but also since my name is Jason, and Jason Voorhees is the star of the Friday the 13th Movies...which I love! A double bonus whammy!!

### caroline — 2013-01-02T02:22:36+0200

### Inger — 2013-01-02T03:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68980>

That is why I aim for not letting food take too big a place in my life. If all your pleasure is about food, you will get in serious troubles when trying to heal. It feels you have to give up your whole life.. because t was.. just.. food.

What have helped me huge is to find other ways to pleasure. Like creating things, the nature, friends, love.. whatever but not food. I try to look at food as medicine. Sure I enjoy my food too, it slowly changes so that you enjoy the weirdest foods like plain beef fat or something I could not have imagined I could enjoy that much. It changes. We are so able to change it is amazing.
To me it is like a decision I had to make.. to keep pleasing my palate or to start thinking clear and not putting anything in my body that damages it. The way of pleasing my palate lead me into so much suffering it was no way worth it!
A few seconds of good taste.. and ongoing bad mood.. lousy feelings.. anxiety.. it was just not worth it.

I am still not perfect yet, but I know perfectly well why I do not feel the best at times. And because I know that, and I know why, it gives a amazing sense of power and freedom. I can adjust my own well being, I can cheat if I want and take the consequences but I never feel lost and in despair because I know when I tighten my diet again I feel just amazing again. I love how easy it is. We are allowed to feel a bit bad too at times, but then there are no one to blame for it just my self.
And if someone want the healing to take a bit longer, it is perfectly fine to be less strict.

Because there are many factors at play, and for some there are also social or financial reasons why they choose not the most optimal stuff. We are human and can take just that much individually. So no need to stress.. because if we start stressing it means I guess we are just not ready for the next step. It has to come naturally, not forced because someone forces us to do a thing. It have to come from our heart and own will for sure.

### caroline — 2013-01-02T04:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68981>

Yes - that is what has changed for me too Inger. Food doesn't have power over me any more. I can choose to have anything I want and then suffer the consequences and then get back on track. On New Year's day I was feeling sorry for myself and had some Baileys - actually - a lot of Baileys. Huge mistake - but I knew what I was doing and chose to do it and suffer later. Part and parcel of being human. We for sure aren't perfect - probably makes us much more interesting!

### furface — 2013-01-02T04:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68983>

Good points, Inger.

Hey I love photography, but again, most of all my issues boil down to financial strain. The equipment I want cost a lot of money. Sure I could "settle" for something a lot less expensive, but then I wouldn't be happy, or satisfied, which would then cause me further anxiety and stress. I know I shouldn't let material things dictate how I feel, or what I do etc. but that's just how I am. Something as precise and competitive as nature photography requires a certain level of equipment in order to compete in the field. That may not be how things "should" be, but unfortunately it's the way they are.

As far as friends are concerned, the few I have seem to be too busy with their own lives to have time for me.
As far as love is concerned, well there is someone, she gives me my inspiration, motivation, encouragement, etc, but she lives in Texas, I live in Vancouver, because of my parents health situation, I can't move to Texas, and besides...she likes me "AS A FRIEND!" (The six most painful words a man can ever hear from a woman..."I LIKE/LOVE YOU AS A FRIEND!" lol) I mean it's great, and I also love her as a friend, but also as more. But que sera sera.

I guess I am willing to sacrifice being optimal, or "perfect" (if there is such a thing, which I don't believe there is, nor would I want to be) in favour of not restricting myself completely as far as the foods I'm willing to give up, or ingest. I am willing to give up a lot of foods that I enjoy, and already have. (And I'm not just talking about the usual "bad" foods like pizza, ice cream etc.) I'm talking about what were considered"good" foods such as tomatoes, fruit, yogurt etc.

Basically my diet has consisted of Basa, Tilapia, Salmon, clams, grass-fed extra lean gr. beef (the store was out of grass-fed regular beef, and I find the ex lean way too dry), grass-fed lamb, bacon, eggs, lobster, broccoli, kale, baby bok choy, cucumbers, pickles, sauerkraut, liverwurst, dark dark chocolate, coconut oil, coconut manna, grass-fed beef short ribs, gf beef tallow, avocados, heavy whipping cream, grass-fed butter, prawns/shrimp, celery, mushrooms, and radishes. With the occasional piece of cheese, a few blackberries, and a few unsalted, raw, macadamia nuts. I just wish I could afford to keep most of those items in my house all of the time. haha

I think perhaps I just need to see some more positive results like I did after the first couple of weeks I was on the Leptin Reset. Hitting a plateau sucks!

Anyway, time to hit the sack, and get some shut eye!

### Dali Dula — 2013-01-02T10:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68995>

Of course you may call me Jason.
I just wish that the weight would start coming off again. After losing 20 lbs during the first 2 weeks or so, I've only lost 8 since then, and I've even been eating a lot more seafood, and even some clams. WTF is going on? I'm beginning to get frustrated!

Yo furface, throw away your scale. I know you probably paid money for it. Give it to somebody for safekeeping if you can't part with it. Actually I would throw it out the window cuz that would be fun. As you progress on this path your weight loss will slow and believe it or not that is a good thing. Your weight loss is slowing because your body is now starting to build muscle and bone. You may even gain slightly at times. I experienced this. I got heavier as I got thinner. Trading fat for muscle. Watch your body comp and how your clothes fit, not the scale.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-02T10:35:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-68998>

Endless said: "I think that wool socks would work, too. When we did our river dip the other day one of the ladies was wearing wool socks (no shoes) into the river, and then she wore them on the walk back to the house, on snow. I went barefoot and had a few toes aching by the time I got back. I'm definitely trying wool next time!"

Socks certainly make a difference, but I've found since the ice really started building up that my toes need a bit more help.

Furface....I've found that the progression of becoming optimal isn't linear, and that losing weight isn't the only marker of success. You may be lowering your inflammation without always losing weight every week. Your hormones may be changing in a good way and your adrenals and thyroid may be repairing too (if that is what is needed).
This fall I had a big plateau and a bit of a crash, not because I did anything different, but because the change in the light has such an impact on me...Getting to know myself at this level of understanding helps keep the spirits up when we frustrated.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-02T14:17:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-69029>

I do not feel like I need to give up anything that I used to love. I always baked my own cakes for example, because I always liked them better than the ones you can buy. So I get into the kitchen and get creative. My latest invention is a German Chocolate-Mocca torte which is out of this world good tasting and completely in line with what I am supposed to eat. So the cake is eggs, almond flour, stevia some spices and the egg whites folded under and off into the oven. Makes a dense, but wonderful smelling cake (I cannot eat it myself as I am still on hCG but so I have been told and besides, being on 500 kcal a day gets you real creative about food). Then I make a frosting from butter with some stevia again and some dark melted chocolate and some coffee into it. Then fold in some Mascarpone. Cut the cake in 3 segments and put the cream inside and then on the outside and decorate.
All it is is protein and lots of fat. Soon I can eat a piece and I will love it and the good news is that I can freeze it and take out a piece occasionally and have a feast.
I know that for me, being able to make this a lifelong eating habit, a cake or a torte just has to be part of it. Adriana is visiting with her family on the weekend and that is exactly what they are getting as a treat. Yeah!!! So happy!

As for CT I also wear my sons skiing hat, my warmest socks over my hands. I do have the feet under water though. I can manage to stay in for 30 minutes, distracting myself by watching the birds over my head, trying to meditate (at which I am terrible) or watching myself shiver. Then I need to run around the house like mad which makes me a very efficient housewife. My feet regularily get numb, but no sores or anything. Just once in the beginning I had a little sore on one of my toes, which was itching. It took a while to heal. But since then I am good.
Pretty soon I will need to use the icepick to cut a hole in my pool.
