The ice is going down on our pond these days...we've even had some rain instead of snow. The other day I got in the pond, it was sunny and way above freezing and once I got out I just lay on the snow and did some sunbathing....very pleasant! Of course this week the temps are going down again and we'll be back to snow.

Anne, I have had the same experience as you with CT...if I change anything that gives me less electrons I can feel it in how my body responds to the CT.As I said somewhere before...we walk a very narrow path with these protocols to recover health. At the same time, some big changes like not feeling depressed ALL winter have been constant...that makes it all worthwhile and keeps me so motivated with the food, light and CT.

Dr. Kruse told me on a consult that staying keto in the summer is a really good way to keep the good progress going...not so important if you are optimal as you have more wiggle room.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-14T00:24:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-80424>

The one time I accidentally went below freezing, I stayed in for less time than usual, only 30 minutes. But that was because I was nervous! I had no ill effects, though. It was about the same as being in 40F water.

If you are used to the low 50's, you should try the 40's for a while, instead of going straight down to the 30's.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-30T04:41:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-82244>

For the past couple weeks I CT'd in a freezing cold river that is about a 20 minute drive up into the mountains. I was so amazed at the difference between my little spring-fed CT hole, which is probably 45-50 degrees (need to bring a thermometer up there to test the temp; I bet it stays stable all year round), and the river which was still somewhat ice covered, in the shade, with little icebergs floating down river. After CT in the cold, cold river I ended up with what I am calling bruise-like pain on the back of my arms, my lats, and parts of my back. It hurts, but it also feels good in that I'm sore and I earned it sort of way.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/conchas-march-jpg.67/)

Monday, I caught a cold, and due to time constraints and not feeling great, I missed my CT sessions for most of the week. Today, I still didn't feel great, but I decided to hike the 1.5 miles up to my little spring-fed hole. The last time I was there I discovered these little water beetles that bite. Ouch! So today, I decided to wear compression shorts to prevent the bites. (I think they were just looking for food and my legs were nothing more than a substrate.) It was a warm day, disturbingly warm. It should still be snow-covered on the ground and difficult to get to. But, currents are starting to grow leaves, Valerian was in bloom, there is no snow at all. I sank down in the hole and settled in. I realized that I'm probably the only person in my little town who is crazy enough to hike up a canyon and sit in cold water while recovering from a cold. The longer I sat in the water, the better I felt. Because I really had no time constraint today, I ended up staying in the water for over 45 minutes. I did start to shiver, which is the first time I've shivered in that water. After about 20 minutes is when I start feeling really energized. That's when I think the grounding plus the spring water really starts taking effect. I've had very interesting experiences the last several times I've been there in that I start feeling like I'm being spoken to by the the place, the water.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/h20-canyon-march-jpg.68/)

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/spring-shadow-jpg.69/)

Today it was no different. When I got out, I decided to walk barefoot back to my car.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/2013-03-01_13-39-34_3-jpg.70/)

The terrain is varied and includes brambles, lots of sharp gravel, some sand, burned out flood plain material, some soft and moist dirt, and lots more painful volcanic gravel covered in pine needles. When I got back to my car my nose was definitely not as painful and stuffy as it had been walking up. My head was super clear and energized. My skin and body felt energized, although I was colder than I had been hiking up, which felt hot (55-degrees F outside). Overall, I just felt better. Also, there had been a patch of sun-warmed, super-soft silty sand that I warmed my feet in half way back, and that was pure bliss.

### caroline — 2013-03-30T05:50:52+0200

### Inger — 2013-03-30T06:58:29+0200

Oh wow.. that looks like a magic river! I love those stones and all! Wow you do so long in there (and others here too..!)! I still do only a minute or a few in my ice hole. I get red as a tomato, the water is very cold we still have -10 degrees C in the night here and maybe 0 degrees C in the day and lots of snow. But maybe it is enough for me because I do feel great and I am happy with my body comp too. Maybe that is why I do no longer. If I do 5 minutes I do shiver after huh.

I can feel you when you say you get spoken to by the water and the place.. I can totally feel you. I often get that feeling when outside, I feel like I am one with everything beautiful around me and it sure is magic.. time stays still somehow.. it is pure bliss!
I CTed too when I had 38,6 degree C fever a month ago or so.. I dipped 3 times that day in my hole, just shortly. And the next day I was healthy.  It really felt not bad at all, I have to say, exactly like you describe! It is sad.. we have learned to fear the cold when we are sick. Weird, as it looks like it helps instead!? Question everything..lol

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-31T00:54:37+0200

I turned very pink and my arms felt like they were falling asleep. During the MovNat workshop we visited a natural hot spring near an icy river and we alternated between the hot and the cold many times. I didn't experience that bruise like pain during that session, which is probably attributed to the warming up after the very cold. I really enjoyed that total thermogenesis experience. Hot, cold, hot, cold, hot.

### Nick — 2013-04-08T09:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-83332>

I have been CT'ing for over a year now and have started really ramping it up as we are going into winter in Australia. I jump in an ice bath 2-4 times a week for around an hour.

Last weekend I went camping with a bunch of friends and parents from my child's school. It was cold, around 10F in the mid afternoon then dropped around another 10 degrees into the night. It was also drizzling with rain. I started off in shorts and T-shirt and thought that when I got too cold I would throw a jumper on. About this time the CT kicked in big time, while everyone else had three to four layers of clothes on and were really feeling the temperature, I never got very cold, even though I was pretty damp from helping set up a tent in the rain. The night was a bit eventful, [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6670-Tooth-Dislodged-Additional-measures](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6670-Tooth-Dislodged-Additional-measures) but I was quite astonished by how I never needed that jumper even at 11pm after 7 hours in these conditions! I could feel heat generating from me under my T-shirt. Its one thing doing it an ice bath but it is totally exhilarating seeing your body respond in real world conditions in a way that was impossible a year ago.

It was also drizzling with rain. I started off in shorts and T-shirt and thought that when I got too cold I would throw a jumper on. About this time the CT kicked in big time, while everyone else had three to four layers of clothes on and were really feeling the temperature, I never got very cold, even though I was pretty damp from helping set up a tent in the rain. The night was a bit eventful, [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6670-Tooth-Dislodged-Additional-measures](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6670-Tooth-Dislodged-Additional-measures) but I was quite astonished by how I never needed that jumper even at 11pm after 7 hours in these conditions! I could feel heat generating from me under my T-shirt. Its one thing doing it an ice bath but it is totally exhilarating seeing your body respond in real world conditions in a way that was impossible a year ago.

Great to hear of someone else noting such progress in outdoors situations, via the icebath path. I know I wouldn't have got in the sea without getting cold-fit in my bath first.
I really love my new, improved "normal". I've worn sundresses most of the time, three and a half out of the last four seasons.
It's magic when you're hit with cold conditions and you respond by heating up. I remember this happening for the first time when I was hit by a freezing Southerly last Winter. Even my ungloved hands stayed toasty warm - so unlike anything I'd experienced before. Funny how I get such a kick out of something that's built into our design and my long ago ancestors would have taken for granted.
It even happens when I open my deep freeze and lean into get things out LOL.
I'm looking forward to Winter.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-04-09T05:32:25+0300

### freesia — 2013-04-09T07:53:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-83442>

Hi caroline, the only thyroid labs I was able to get - well even my doctor (GP) admitted they were next to useless. I guess at least he was honest?!  Not a hope of getting any RT3 measured...unless I wasn't looking hard enough.

I had a low body temperature since adolescence, and increasing poor tolerance to cold over the past decade. Fingernails would go blue with any sharp drop in temperature and I could take a day or two to recover. I had a very low basal temp up until mid last year. That's all I know! Sorry I can't be more helpful!!

I found in my early CT days (a whole year ago now!) that regular short sharp bursts of cold - remember you need to aim to get as much skin as possible to between 50 and 55 F - are what kept me moving forward most steadily. And I just did whatever I needed to do in between to stay comfortable - but lengthened out the cold time gradually, while getting the water consistently colder.
I did try and tough it out more like some of the fitter, sporty types were doing at the time - going out in the cold straight from the bath etc - but after a few times shivering for hours (and hours) I realised I was risking 1/ making myself unwell and 2/ turning myself off CT! Now I go out in the wind afterwards and get a kick out of how warm I feel.

Like you I was kicking off blankets all night...hard to know whether thats just down to adaptation or some (related?) hormonal shifts?

Do you have a low body temp Caroline?

### caroline — 2013-04-09T08:05:07+0300

Anne - I used to have a low body temp but I haven't checked for a long time ... I might have a check tomorrow morning.
My doc justs orders Ft3 and rT 3 and I pay $40.00 for the rT3. Have you checked health scope? They have a functional medicine section. Actually there is another lab that does functional testing - if you go online to the complimentary compounding pharmacy - it is the one they use ... Can't remember the name.

### Nick — 2013-04-09T09:52:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-83445>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=83333)
>
> Hi Nick - what a great story! It sounds like you are very cold adapted and loving every second ...where are you in Oz?

Hi Caroline, I am in Brisbane. We went camping at Queen Mary Falls, over the dividing range up in the mountains. Dont know that I am very cold adapted, when I hear Jacks experiences I am in awe and know I am an amateur! After about four hours in the wet and cold I started getting a few shivers every ten minutes or so, but it never got to be more than that and I never felt uncomfortable. I was very surprised.

I love doing CT, and have a system where I freeze 28kg of ice in 1 litre containers which I can turn over every day if I get the opportunity. In summer this will get bath down to 10c (50f). This winter I am riding it down temperature wise to see how well I go by keeping on adding the same 28kg of ice. I know last winter I only needed 16kg of ice to get down to 10c. The tap water is now starting to get a fair bit colder than summer, I just jumped out a few minutes ago after an hour and there was still a fair bit of unmelted ice left in the bath. I also throw in several handfuls of epsom salts for good measure.

### caroline — 2013-04-09T10:14:48+0300

I want to go in the ocean every day all winter - I don't know how cold the ocean will get tho. When I started going last year it was around 55 degrees but I do't really remember when that was - probably June or July.
Keep up the great work!

### nonchalant — 2013-04-16T00:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-84175>

OK, now I know the meaning of life.

Well, let's just say I feel marvelous. I CT'd in a bath that started out at 36F for 50 minutes, then got dressed and went outside to sit in the sun with a good book and my bare feet on the concrete. And a large bowl of good water. I must say the sun was really hot today, but it felt SO GOOD! And the sun water was like drinking life itself. I found myself not wanting to put the bowl down, but one must inhale eventually, no?

### caroline — 2013-04-16T02:33:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-84189>

### SeaHorse — 2013-04-16T03:37:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-84204>

The ice is slowly moving off the pond this last week. I've been away and when I came back there was no real sign of my friendly hole in the ice. It kind of made me sad, but I'm really looking forward to being able to swim and the warmer air temps means CT is feeling balmy. Plus the lack of snow is making grounding so much easier....I can wear my mocs everywhere now. And I suppose I should mention that while I was away I got to CT on the other side of the big pond ( in Portugal!) The water was cold and the sun and sand were warm...I soaked up the southerly electrons like a sponge...it was paradise and so exotic to the senses after a New England winter. We went to a fish market that was honestly the size of a walmart....full of individual fish stands selling amazing stuff. I pointed at a lot of things, brought them back to our rented house and ate them all! Portugal is in many ways still straddling the line between a "developed" and undeveloped economy. Village life continues as it has for many generations but they are in big trouble financially in the EU and are having to be bailed out like Greece. For me, I spent my time noticing how in many places the streets are still cobblestones (good for grounding). People shop for local seasonal fish or meat every day in the village or town markets. Sitting in the sun is a sanctioned pastime. The sea is never too far away and (like Newfoundland) cod is considered the national dish. It was so easy to have an optimal experience there...I would go back in a heartbeat....

### freesia — 2013-04-29T04:29:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85077>

Hi Wurst, I remember way back when lotsa people started CT some mentioned their fat composition changing..becoming squishy or something..prior to disappearing!! So perhaps what you're noticing is the start of something desirable!?
My body comp change was less interesting, more subtle I guess. I just lost a little around the waist and suddenly found I had defined muscles in my arms and legs. I really wasn't looking for it so it was a pleasant surprise!

Still ice bathing here every 2 days. Haven't cancelled the ice deliveries I used to manage CT all the way through summer, so I'm getting some really good cold baths now the tap water is cooler!
Coming into my second winter now.  I love CT. I actually think it's the first time my body has responded with appropriate endogenous chemicals - and seriously beneficial ones - in decades. Can't see myself giving it up.

### nonchalant — 2013-04-30T16:03:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85187>

I absolutely love my new CT protocol. I put a large bowl of water out on the ground in the noon sun. I then deep CT for an hour with epsom salts and a couple drops of iodine, then I go outside and warmup while earthing in the sun for 2 hours, and drinking my sunwater. I still am a bit amazed that I don't get a sunburn.

A few weeks ago I was doing the same thing, except to add more sun time before the bath, to warm up enough to want to CT. Lately it has been warm enough so that I skip the pre-tanning. I really like this protocol. If I have any hesitation getting my upper back into the icewater, I just think of the sunshine waiting for me outside. It's the next best thing to CT'ing in real seawater.

### kleinb87 — 2013-05-02T00:11:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85260>

Hi guys! I have become inventive with CT and photoelectric effect lately and thought I would share for those who do not have pools, the ocean, or large tubs for ice baths. In the winter, I was out laying in the snow. Spring has sprung and it's 90F and sunny today in Indianapolis. Here's what CT currently looks like for me. Mornings are cold showers. Afternoon is laying outside absorbing photoelectric effect with 44 lbs of ice on top of me on my deck. (Yes, i do have neighbors, and I dont care what they think of me.) It's definitely helping. Inflammation has gone down and my clothes are beginning to fit differently.

For all of you saying I don't know how I can do it because of x,y,z, here's motivation to go outside of the box and use your creativity.

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-02T02:44:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85267>

That's getting the job done with style! I love the image of your sun/ice bathing!

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-05-02T03:12:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85272>

I think I'm done with my little spring-fed pool until winter. I'm not usually one to freak out about bugs, in general, but it's become impossible to sit in there without getting the total heebie-jeebies! Once it started getting warmer, these little aquatic beetles started swimming around me constantly. They bite! Ouch! Yesterday, I managed to shake my legs the whole time trying to keep them away. Did you know spiders walk on water? I had three spiders jump down over my head and walk across the water right where I was sitting. Yipes! Once out of the water, I enjoyed the warmth of the sun and noticed some interesting iridescent blue crystals in the water. They are called sanadine. I discovered that there are hundreds (!) of snails in the little pool, and apparently they like to make their little shells out of the sanadine crystals. Finally, there were a whole bunch of insect nematodes in the water. I think I'll let the life in the pool enjoy their spring and summer without me. It actually kind of bums me out because I've become so fond of my weekly routine. But I feel like I need to wear a compression suit just to prevent the creepy-crawlies. Oh, and yesterday I finally saw the owl that I've heard on so many occasions, but never seen. It flew down over where I was sitting and up into a huge tree, one of the few that survived the two fires that have raged across the area. So cool. I think it's back to the tub with ice for me now.

### caroline — 2013-05-02T03:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85273>

klein.bobbi ..... I really admire your spirit and intelligence and the ability to just get the job done! Great lesson for all of us....... in spite of our available resources, time commitments, location etc. etc. ...there is always a way!

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-02T19:04:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85319>

Chrysanth...that made me laugh. Our pond is getting kind of lively too. I'm wading through clouds of mucky organic matter to get to the deeper water and there are massive numbers of salamanders doing their spring mating everywhere, plus bullfrog tadpoles, little skating bugs and every now and then a trout will leap out of the water and put on a show. Once the black flies are out I'll be sprinting out and back while on land and making a wild splashy racket while in the water to clear away the bugs that float and keep the black flies off my head. A new movnat exercise for summer? We've hit some high temps this week for this time of year and it feels great to get really hot and then dive into the still cold water.

### agatha — 2013-05-02T23:20:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85342>

Ha, your pond stories made me laugh.

I'm just getting back into CT after losing focus in the autumn. This evening was my first time in the sea this year here in Sussex in the UK. I did go in in leggings and a t shirt and a hat which attenuated the cold a little but I'm still very pleased to be back in the lovely ocean. I'd forgotten just how wonderful both the sea and the cold are. It was just magical to float and watch the colours of the sun going down and the soft light reflecting on the water. Air drying is undoubtedly better than towelling off. And now I can feel a wave of delicious sleepiness coming over me... blissful.

### kleinb87 — 2013-05-06T03:45:45+0300

I'll take spending $8 for ice as my form of spa treatment. Hopefully in next few weeks I'll be at the beach. Currently having another ice session indoors watching the clouds come in.

Agatha: I agree on the magical feelings of the cold. I have found that this is one of the best times for me to find an answer on one of my projects.

### Dali Dula — 2013-05-06T14:39:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-85510>

I traveled to Long Island for work the last two weeks. Went swimming a few times on the south shore. 45 deg water temp and pounding surf. Great stuff. I will be there again May 13 through 17 if anyone is interested in a swim meet up in the Long Beach area early evening 6 pm. Good fresh fish there also.

### Cowboy — 2013-05-16T06:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-86190>

Our weather went from a 24 inch snowstorm to 86 degrees. Got the tub out of the garage and outside sitting in the aspen grove. Only one problem, by the time I took my first cold soak the water had warmed up to 70 degrees from the high 40s to high 50s it had been the last few weeks in the garage.. 70 degrees is very easy. Hope it will go back to cool nights so that I gets a couple of months in of 60 degree soaks without ice.

This year I need to find a cooler. Ice is too much trouble and too much work. I am hoping to find a reasonable aquarium chiller that will keep the 150 gallon tank in the high 50's.

### Inger — 2013-05-26T11:08:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-86886>

I want ocean CT too... so jealous Optimalbound and Audrey!

My CT looks like this right now;

In the morning face dunks, 4-10 times. Then I go into the river.. and stay there up to my navel for a while... until my feet feels warm.. and then I swim 3 rounds, each takes 1,5 minutes.. so it is really not much! But it is making me feel great The water is so warm now, 57 degree F. Often I go for a second short swim if I feel to hot in the sun or so. It is not much I know but it is every day. I have started to put a ice pack under my neck / head when going to sleep too, it is so warm here now I need some cooling at night!

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-26T16:26:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-86887>

so yesterday - we went to the local outdoor pool! the weather hasn't been that warm and the high yesterday was in the mid 60's... we hopped into the pool.. and lets just say it was quite "REFRESHING"! the kids actually started getting too cold... so we had to get out.. but we still stayed there an laid out on the pool deck ground, and maximizing the photoelectric effect...

we're going to go again today - and we're going to try do some lap swimming... in that brillantly cold water..

### nonchalant — 2013-05-27T17:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-86943>

I finally got my toes in the sand the other day. Sheer bliss. Couldn't stay long, but I made the most of it. Not CT, because the water was only cool, but the waves were hypnotic. And we went beach combing. It is angel wing shell season! I found large ones longer than my hand, all the way down to tiny ones that were difficult to pick up without breaking.

This website says angel wings get up to 5 inches, but I now know they get larger.
[http://oceanica.cofc.edu/shellguide/shells/ANGELWING.htm](http://oceanica.cofc.edu/shellguide/shells/ANGELWING.htm)

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-31T23:37:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87223>

After 10 days of travelling (a few cold showers in sulfur water...yuck!) I was so ready to get back in the pond...it's been cold and rainy here which cooled the water again. Today and yesterday it was in the high 80s and I just kept going in the pond, lying in the sun, doing chores, going in the pond, lying in the sun, for the whole day. It was like being on vacation at home...so grateful I could just spend a few days recouping from the travel. I'm still sharing the pond with a few pairs of Canada Geese and the bullfrogs are really getting going in the evenings.

### Cowboy — 2013-06-01T20:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87264>

Our outdoor highs went from the 80s to the 50's with lows at nights in the 40s. Water in my tank is between 48 and 53 degrees depending upon the day or the time of day. Now we are back to a cool normal for June with temps in the 70s for highs and 50s for low. The last few days my immersion temps have been 48 (felt very cold) with cloudy skies, to 53 with sunny skies, a bit easier to handle. Joints feel good, sleep would be great if my wife did not have a bad allergy year starting off, with coughing and nose blowing repeatedly through the night.

### salamander — 2013-06-01T23:12:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87273>

I went into an icey mountain creek in the Great Smokies three weeks ago. I had a ten minute swim in one deep pool, underwater and moving to the bottom. Then an hour later after hiking up the trail, went in for another dip. Stood still and estimated maybe 15 minutes. Creek was moving and it was hard to stay still. Got out and realized shivering was uncontrollable and I got close to perhaps a cardiac infarcation or something. Pretty scary and made no sense at the time becuase I have been doing this for longer than that for months. But then I realized, the MOVING WATER must work like wind chill. I believe it pushed me further than I realized I was going in quicker time. Anyway, I pulled through it, took 3 days to get my energy back. Took 15 minutes of pacing back and forth to get my heart/shivering back to normal. lol.

### SeaHorse — 2013-06-02T02:15:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87283>

Salamander....that sounds pretty frightening..I'd say the moving water and doing two periods in the water certainly made the difference. Glad you recovered!

### Optimalbound — 2013-06-02T22:19:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87350>

My 4th ocean beach CT in 5 days. 11 degrees C (52 F) for 40 minutes. Air temp 13'C and a pretty strong breeze.
Swimming in the surf, enjoying the feeling of being submerged in the cold ocean, as well as presumably soaking up some electrons and photons. Still can't quite get my head around how easy it is now, and how awesome it makes me feel.

After 40 minutes, I stopped mainly because of boredom, but I am glad I did! No shivering while in the sea, but once I got back in the car, I shivered enough to make me see double...  I actually switched on the heated seats for the first time in probably 15 months.

Beach CT is quickly becoming my favorite kind of CT. I've found this excellent little beach where the parking lot is only 150 meters from the sea. So easy to get in and out, and no risk of stepping on anything sharp. Only problem is that the ocean is quickly warming up now. I need to step up my game and make the most of these low temps while I can!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-06-02T23:13:31+0300

### Albert83BCN — 2013-06-03T15:09:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87371>

Yesterday I went to the beach for the first time this year. When I was a child I used to spend a lot of time in water but once I got older I changed preferences to just lay at the sand and sunbath. Now being into the CT/EMF experiment I thought that of course it was a perfect time to walk barefood on sand and cold adapt a little bit even if the water was still only 17ÂºC not 10 as rx'd. One interesting thing I noticed, I've always been reluctant to enter water when in the beach as I'm very sensitive to cold. But yesterday I had not much of a problem, I adapted fast to the feeling. I thought It could have something to do with this last 3 weeks or so that I've been trying to cold adapt with cold showers and ice aplication. Looking forward to get to the beach again

### Optimalbound — 2013-06-05T22:55:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-87577>

40 minutes in the ocean again, today. Probably about 12 ÂºC / 53 ÂºF or slightly lower. (Punishing myself for having a pint of beer yesterday for, like, the third time in half a year..) I was thinking of IvanDrago's comment that "even if CT was good for absolutely nothing, it would still be an awesome thing to do".

Did a small biohack today. Had my usual BAB at 7am, but I had run out of eggs so it was a bit smaller than usual; probably <40g of protein. Broke the normal procedure and did not eat anything before the CT session at 5:20pm other than a spoonful of CO in my green tea. So I was quite hungry as I started swimming. When I got back on dry land 40 minutes later, the hunger was completely gone. I could feel that I had not eaten for a while, but there was simply no hunger at all. Pretty much as expected, but quite awesome and fascinating nonetheless.

Hi Inger,
Yeah, I'm trying to up my game a little bit over here.. The last four months have been quite frustrating with some time-consuming responsibilities that just had to be taken care of. During that time, I didn't get to do much CT or to be as careful as I'd like with light cycles and such. My progress came to a halt, and I am starting to wonder if also all the wifi in my new place of work may have contributed to that.
But now that I am yet again free to do CT as much as I like, I am going to ramp it up significantly and see where it takes me. Still have bodyfat to lose and labs to fix..

Oh, and I forgot to mention: About 45 minutes after CT, I happened to look at myself in the mirror and was shocked by how dark my skin was, all over. Has anyone else noticed something similar? I recently started supplementing with kelp, and wonder if the iodine might have played a role in this. Iodine also seems to have made me more tolerant of long CT sessions, but I could very well be wrong. (Time to start re-reading JK's blog, I guess... )

Interesting!
Funny thing is, I looked "normal" again, the very next morning.

Back at the ocean beach again, today. Sea temp 11 ˚C (52 ˚F), air temp 13 ˚C. Stayed in for 35 minutes, varying between swimming around and just standing on the bottom. Didn't get to the beach until 8:30 pm, but sunset is at 10:43 pm tonight (I'm at 59˚ N), so the sun was still up and it was quite lovely on my totally deserted little beach.

There didn't seem to be much difference in post CT shivering between 45 minutes yesterday and 35 minutes today, so I'm tempted to go for 55 minutes tomorrow and see what happens. Boredom more than cold seems to be the main hurdle, and I wish I had a CT buddy to keep me company.. Will try to convince my reasonably cold-adapted exercise buddy to join me in the ocean the day after tomorrow.

### PrimalPam — 2013-06-16T19:10:05+0300

### CTforlife — 2013-06-16T19:26:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-88181>

Great CT today. I am using my local river to ground + CT at the same time! What sucks is the water temp around 8 am is only 54-56 degrees F. In the middle of the day it heats up to 60-64 degrees F. I can't wait for winter. CT is doing something for me I never thought possible. When I get out of the water I have, and I mean HAVE TO, flex as hard as I can. I feel the urge to move every muscle in my damn body and constrict it like its going to pop. CT makes me feel alive, especially outdoors.

This summer the water is eventually going to be too warm for desirable effects, but as it cools down again heading towards the winter solstice I made a promise to myself to CT every day so I can slowly adapt to the freezing temperatures coming this winter. We are talking tons of snow and ice on the ground, it will be intense. Wim Hof your not the only one about to amazing things. Also, CT is helping me with my hip pain (if your curious head to my CT thread) and I am hoping it will help me do what most people think is impossible. I just love eating a ton of salmon in the morning, walking 2 miles to my local river and CT'ing along as I can, my days are so productive I can't believe it. I don't work out, yet my muscles are growing (extra T and GH ?). That is most likely due to me being an estrogen sink whole because I don't have a gallbladder and ate a SAD for so long. Now that its clearing I am becoming the person I never thought I could be...

### CTforlife — 2013-06-16T19:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-88184>

I stay in the water for an average of an hour, but I've been known to go as far as three. I don't swim due to strong currents, and I prefer to start my day relaxed while I watch nature in all of her beauty. When school rolls around again (and its gonna get cold), I will cut how much morning CT I do because I will be very busy. What bums me out is I am definitely still on the path to optimal, but the water is warming up too fast. I love grounding with CT combined, I feel a surge of current through my body, which I never really felt CT'ing in a tub... I am going to have to start buying ice again to CT during the summers, and using the river during the colder parts of the year. I try to CT every single day, but sometimes I go on morning walks with my family, who are also on their own path to optimal. I would say I average a minimum of 5-6 days a week.

Reporting in from deepest, darkest, hibernation (Sthn hemisphere)...

The way my body's responding to cold doesn't stop amazing me.
It's midwinter here. All windows in the house are open (we use no heating duing the day). I'm in a tee shirt, and even my hands are radiating heat. The air is cold, not freezing, and every now and then I get a slight, pleasant, tingly under the skin shiver...which I take it is my Winter thermostat.
I CT'd consistently through last Winter (and summer) but things feel even more dialed in now. I don't even think about the cold.
Took a leap of faith last year. Best move ever. Ever.

Taking 40F baths 30 - 50 minutes every day. Lights out when the sun goes down hence no time online!

Best to All,
freesia x

### MeghanK — 2013-08-04T17:42:39+0300

### nicld — 2013-08-04T18:13:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-91495>

I will be traveling for work next week and so going to try and at least get in a 20 minute session each day.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-04T21:32:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-91507>

I definitely try to stay as still as possible at this point

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=91493)
>

### freesia — 2013-08-04T23:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-91510>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=91493)
>
> For those that CT in the tub... do you just sit completely still? Read a book? What do you do for 20-30 minutes... what is the time you try to stay in for?

Hi, I read on my kindle, listen to a podcast or watch a video on my laptop. We hardwired an internet outlet close by so I don't need to resort to wifi.
Reading wasn't an option for me in the first couple of months because the baths were quite strenuous with my health condition, but now that I can I love it. CTing and doing something else productive at the same time feels like a 1 + 1 = 5 kind of deal.
I stay in 35 - 45 mins...depends on other demands.

PS Should have mentioned I move around too...just gently...no splashing!

### PrimalPam — 2013-08-05T03:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-91525>

45 minutes, and I lay completely still. I do half time on my back and half flipped over, so my entire body gets the cold. 3 times a week minimum, but strive for 5 times.

I created playlists that are about 45 minutes long, so sometimes I'll just listen to one. Lately, I've been watching T.V. shows on my Netflix app on my Ipad (I know, EMT nightmare and conflicts with benefits of CT).

I hang around the house for about 25-30 minutes after, as I warm up, then I go out to my yard in a tank top & shorts and lay on the ground, soaking up earth and sun. Got a bunch of bites the other day, but totally worth it.

### kleinb87 — 2013-09-05T16:41:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-94884>

I just got back from 3.5 weeks in Paris and Lisbon. I was luckily enough to be able to CT in the Atlantic Ocean almost every day that I was in Lisbon for 2 weeks. The effect was amazing. My body started to instantly become optimal. Inflammation decreased, weight was shedding and body composition was coming back. My family and friends just kept saying you must have dieted so much when you were away. All I could say was that it was the ocean. Oh, and I even ate gluten..GASP as an n=1 one day to see how my gut would react. I have avoided carbs and gluten like the plague for over 2 years as my stomach would instantly look like it was pregnant and become super distended. The gluten had no effect with all of the negative ions from the beach and the environment was much better out in the middle of nowhere on a beach, without wireless and only a few people with electronics.

This speaks wonders to how nature is the cure and our altered field really does affect us in more ways than we come to accept. This just reiterated the fact that I must move ASAP from the midwest, and this altered field is hard to overcome with all of the ice baths.

### Danco3636 — 2013-09-09T00:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-95283>

Here was the post

_**I've always enjoyed getting cold - do any of you feel the same or do you purely do it for health reasons? I really enjoy shivering. For this reason I got an old chest freezer, lined it with rubber pond liner, filled it with water, and run it on a timer for 5 hours a night to keep the water at 0C. I love lying in it for an hour or two then enjoy the shivering afterwards aswell. I also feel a lot more full of energy for about 3 days afterwards. Then I realised I was losing weight and looked this site up. I guess being cold burns a lot of energy - I read something elsewhere about 400 calories an hour from being "cold" - but I think that was referring to feeling chilly (eg going for a run in shorts and tshirt at 0C, which I don't call cold at all), not shivering hard, so maybe more calories for that? In winter I go swimming outdoors, the exertion of swimming plus the cold burns a lot of calories. But it's 22C here now which is not very good. The water in a mountain lake nearby is 14C! That's not cold :sad:**_

### Nick — 2013-09-24T13:06:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-95943>

Been hammering out lots of CT lately. Even though we are going back into Spring in Oz I have been doing CT 5-7 times week for the last three weeks. One stage I used party ice (speed issue that time) instead of my usual 28kg of block ice I make each night and the water seemed pretty cold. I measured it and it was between 4C or 39F with about 15cm of ice floating on the surface. I thought what the heck I would give it a go as I knew I had done some colder than usual CT's during winter when the base temperature tap water was much colder. It was pretty much the same experience as when it was 10c 50F except my toes became too painful to leave in after 10 mins but I left as much of the rest of my body as i could in the bath and lasted the usual 50 minutes without any real difference. My shoulders, head and knees stick out and everything else was the usual cherry pink colour.

One thing I have noticed with a lot of CT, bed early on the Magnetico and up at daybreak is that have been having some awesome dreams! Really pleasant, detailed, sprawling dreams that seem to go for ages. Not sure what that means but I have been dreaming heaps of very relaxing dreams that are easy to remember in detail the next day. Jack mentioned it in one of his webinars but I forget the significance.

Anyway will keep on CT'ing as right now it is an easy thing to fit into my family life thankfully.

### bacon lover — 2013-09-24T15:12:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-95947>

Since adding magnet sleep pad have had the same increased dreams long duration and I seem to be much more aware of them. Wonder what that's about?

### Inger — 2013-09-25T22:01:19+0300

Nick you are hardcore!

I did my second bathtub CT, water was 14 degree C and I stayed in 25 minutes, that is when I started to freeze. Last time I did batthub CT I had 15,5 degree C and at 15 minutes I started to shiver. That was fast progress in just 2 baths! Cool. I am curious if it is only because I had a ton of coconutoil today and fats..lol IDK but the ice baths (ok... not really cold yet lol) feels so great. I kind of miss it already a few hours later. Weird.

It is getting colder in my tent too. There have been nights with +5 degree C. I do love it! I am not sure how I will manage to sleep in a room anymore huh.

### nicld — 2013-09-26T03:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96150>

I am going to try and do CT 4 days a week now. 2X during the week for about 40 minutes and then on the weekends for as long as I can stay in. I just cannot do more at night since I cannot get in the tub till 7 (yes, my Neolithic life gets in the way) and right now, rewarming can be rather rough and causes issue with falling asleep. Trying to find the balance and right now, sleep is more important. I did take a warmer shower after my 40 minutes last night and it did help. My only issue is that I need to bundle up when going to bed to get warm enough to sleep but then 2 hours later I get too warm and wake up and can't get back to sleep.

We will see how it goes when it gets colder out. I am so hoping that I will be better able to handle the cold this winter. Plus, might to a round of HCG starting next week. If I do, I will definitely keep doing CT but it is still up in the air. I will see what the doc says on Monday.

Nick, I am with you on the toes. They just do not stay in the water. If the feet get cold, I am done and have to get them rewarmed ASAP.

### freesia — 2013-09-26T08:36:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96175>

Hi...oh dear...yes I worried about expiring in the tub back when I used to induce a mild hypothermia in the name of adapting!
Sounds like you need a handy man!! First to - when he's not around to assist you personally  - put some kind of strap above the bath to help you pull yourself up. Nothing too industrial looking or it might be a blow to the ego....but something **strong** nonetheless.
Do you have wired internet? If so, an outlet near the bathroom would help cut down on (wireless) emf. Another way, maybe, is to see if you can preload an episode of something then turn the wireless off and just use your ipad as a player. It used to be easy to do this with sites like Dailymotion but I'm not sure about others.

Water - \*phew\*. I am trying to learn more about hydrogen bonding networks    before I do anything radical. My water has been a long time under the ground and it feels hydrating. I also drink quite a bit of imported mineral water. This is the water I have been drinking while I made my gains so far so I will happily take a bit longer to figure out a solution on that front. I need things to sink in a bit. ### Nick — 2013-09-26T10:30:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96178>

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=96159)
>
> Nick do you re-freeze icy bathwater or freeze from scratch each night?

I use fresh crap tap water each time. Basically I have 28kg (61lb's) of of ice I throw in my bath in 1 litre (approx 1/5gallon) containers. After the containers have been floating around the water for a few minutes I harvest the plastic lids and containers. I then fill them up with tap water again and back in the freezer while the bath water drops to 10c (50F)in around 10-15 minutes. I can turn over ice every 24 hours this way which is terrific. I have a 3yo and 6yo that also use the bath during the day so I am happy to use fresh crap water.

I then throw in 2 cups of Eposom salts and slide in. The first few seconds until my chest is as submerged as it can be is a bit of a 'suck in the air' moment but the instant I am in the water properly I completely relax and move onto reading. At this point life is good, even to the end where I start to get a few small shivers. There is always ice left at the end of the bath, in summer I need to use more ice to keep the temp down. Interestingly when I first started CT, before I starting using the Magnetico and lots of F free water, I used to get much colder and start to shiver a lot at the 30-40min mark and be shivering violently at the 60min mark.

I start work early and finish at 3pm, spend 3.5 hours with the family then it is bath and bed time. I find that right now in my life this works easily and simply, a great balance between work, family and health for fitting in CT.

I think I was distracted by the benefits of the Magnetico but I have found out that for me, together, they are a magic combo. I plan keep on doing both as often as I can into the future.

### freesia — 2013-09-26T11:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96183>

Hi Nick, your ice production line is similar to mine in terms of amount and the size of the "bergs"!
We have untreated (regularly tested) artesian water as our town supply so it's not nasty, but the stuff I have delivered to supplement what I can make at home is made from someone else's less optimal town supply! I should probably get a bigger chest freezer.. I'm not giving up CT anytime soon either.

Good to be at the able to relax and read stage! I don't mind admitting I had a difficult first few months with CT but in the scheme of things that hardly registers now.
Lucky you having a magnetico! I've been reading about night-time unidirectional magnet therapy (approx. 25 gauss field from under the bed) that was promoted a bit in the 1980's and 1990's. It looked promising but achieved mixed results on it's own. It probably didn't have a chance of reaching it's potential without other measures in place...even less of a chance today of course.
Magnets are next on my list. Bigger freezer under consideration...

### nonchalant — 2013-09-26T16:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96207>

> [Nick said:](/goto/post?id=95943)
>
> I measured it and it was between 4C or 39F with about 15cm of ice floating on the surface. I thought what the heck I would give it a go as I knew I had done some colder than usual CT's during winter when the base temperature tap water was much colder. My shoulders, head and knees stick out and everything else was the usual cherry pink colour.

Nick, I think that once you finally get adapted to 10c/50F, you already have the mechanism in place that turns off the pain receptors. So after that you can quickly go colder and colder, and it all seems to feel even easier than 10c/50F to most of your body (possibly because the pain is shut off better). My feet are finally getting used to the colder temps, (I can keep them in longer and I don't get any freezing on the calouses) but my hands are still pretty shocked at the cold.  Guess I need to start keeping them in longer so they will adapt.

### sjoshua — 2013-09-27T00:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96229>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=95283)
>
> Here was the post
>
> _**I've always enjoyed getting cold - do any of you feel the same or do you purely do it for health reasons? I really enjoy shivering. For this reason I got an old chest freezer, lined it with rubber pond liner, filled it with water, and run it on a timer for 5 hours a night to keep the water at 0C. I love lying in it for an hour or two then enjoy the shivering afterwards aswell. I also feel a lot more full of energy for about 3 days afterwards. Then I realised I was losing weight and looked this site up. I guess being cold burns a lot of energy - I read something elsewhere about 400 calories an hour from being "cold" - but I think that was referring to feeling chilly (eg going for a run in shorts and tshirt at 0C, which I don't call cold at all), not shivering hard, so maybe more calories for that? In winter I go swimming outdoors, the exertion of swimming plus the cold burns a lot of calories. But it's 22C here now which is not very good. The water in a mountain lake nearby is 14C! That's not cold :sad:**_

This is very smrt. I may make this into a fall project... time to start watching craigslist for another deep freeze!

I'd probably want to improve the design slightly past the timer though. By adding a water temp sensor to the system, you could have the freezer regulated to a specific, adjustable range (think, a similar sensor to a hot water heater, which activate the heating element when the temp drops below a certain threshold, this would be that system just in reverse.. activate the cooling system at an upwards temp threshold). This would alleviate having to adjust for seasonal differences in ambient temps. That may be overkill for most people, especially if you live somewhere that has a basement. Something like this would probably be preferential for me though, since this would likely live in my garage.

Also, water conservation could be improved by using farm grade H2O2 (hydrogen peroxide, ~30%) every couple of days, so that the water could be recycled for a longer period of time while mitigating bacteria growth. I'm sure the cooler temps will help this too... as well as only getting into the "cold bath" after already having showered to avoid bringing excess bacteria in. My initial "dosage" starting point for H2O2 would be 1ml/L of water. It would likely need to be done every 2-3 days, maybe more/less depending on frequency of baths taken. I bet this could extend the water life out to a few weeks. Again, this may just be a concern for those of us living area where water is more sparce.

I'm sure the cooler temps will help this too... as well as only getting into the "cold bath" after already having showered to avoid bringing excess bacteria in. My initial "dosage" starting point for H2O2 would be 1ml/L of water. It would likely need to be done every 2-3 days, maybe more/less depending on frequency of baths taken. I bet this could extend the water life out to a few weeks. Again, this may just be a concern for those of us living area where water is more sparce.

So far I have been pretty lucky.... We have an extra bathroom that has an extra bathtub.... I keep that full of water with blocks of ice. I have buckets in my remote freezer (where I store my seafood, beef, liver, butter, bacon etc. that I get on sale) and I just throw in blocks when ever the temperature creeps up. I keep it around 45-55 F. I also add some Epson salt and magnesium chloride..... This way I always have a cold tub to jump into...... when the water looks a little murky I just drain and refill....

> I use fresh crap tap water each time. Basically I have 28kg (61lb's) of of ice I throw in my bath in 1 litre (approx 1/5gallon) containers. After the containers have been floating around the water for a few minutes I harvest the plastic lids and containers. I then fill them up with tap water again and back in the freezer while the bath water drops to 10c (50F)in around 10-15 minutes. I can turn over ice every 24 hours this way which is terrific. I have a 3yo and 6yo that also use the bath during the day so I am happy to use fresh crap water.
>
> I then throw in 2 cups of Eposom salts and slide in. The first few seconds until my chest is as submerged as it can be is a bit of a 'suck in the air' moment but the instant I am in the water properly I completely relax and move onto reading. At this point life is good, even to the end where I start to get a few small shivers. There is always ice left at the end of the bath, in summer I need to use more ice to keep the temp down. Interestingly when I first started CT, before I starting using the Magnetico and lots of F free water, I used to get much colder and start to shiver a lot at the 30-40min mark and be shivering violently at the 60min mark.
>
> I start work early and finish at 3pm, spend 3.5 hours with the family then it is bath and bed time. I find that right now in my life this works easily and simply, a great balance between work, family and health for fitting in CT.

Nick - can you get epsom salts at a reasonable price? I pay over $5.00 for a small bag and the instructions are to use the whole bag for one bath! yikes!

### sjoshua — 2013-09-27T02:08:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96244>

>
> Nick - can you get epsom salts at a reasonable price? I pay over $5.00 for a small bag and the instructions are to use the whole bag for one bath! yikes!

$1/LB:

### Hemming — 2013-09-27T10:18:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96265>

I have a question for everyone. Do you also find that when it gets colder or you do more CT that your appetite for fat goes up and carbs down? Autumn is coming here in Denmark and I've just noticed that swimming, keeping a cool apartment and sleeping in a <15C room has changed my dietary habits. I guess its somewhat of the same as Navy SEALs and polar explorers have reported where they start craving fat.

### caroline — 2013-09-27T11:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96277>

### sjoshua — 2013-10-01T20:31:45+0300

### sjoshua — 2013-10-01T20:59:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96625>

Regarding my pursuit for a chest freezer/CT bath, I have ordered a pool liner to put on the inside, and found this temp sensor which should do the trick for keeping my tub at the temps I want: [http://www.amazon.com/dp/B00CM6ELBY/ref=pe\_385040\_30332200\_pe\_309540\_26725410\_item](http://www.amazon.com/dp/B00CM6ELBY/ref=pe_385040_30332200_pe_309540_26725410_item) It looks like it'll take a little wiring, but I'm not worried about that

The only thing I haven't figured out yet is how to attach the pool liner to the chest freezer without causing a loss in the ability to seal at the top. I'm thinking I may even use magnets for this, since they'd adhere to the internal walls of the freezer due to the metal construction... would this even serve dual purpose of reducing the field around me while CTing at the same time??  I bet a couple of those Harbor Freight monster magnets would do the trick. If I set it up like this, does anyone know what I should try to avoid regarding the magnetic poles? Should I assure all of the same polarity face me (or, face in rather), and if so is there a preference to the N/S pole?

I've got a couple of potential freezers on craigslist that I am looking into as well... seems like a decent sized chest freezer (~14 gal) is $100-150 around here on the used market.

### Hemming — 2013-10-04T22:28:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96884>

I have noticed that my heart rate has increased for most of the day after I've been exposed to both colder water and air temperatures as fall is coming. Is this a normal sign of adaption or could it be a bad sign of something else? I can definitely tolerate lower temperatures now so is this my body's way of staying warmer?

### nicld — 2013-10-05T00:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96894>

Good question.

It seems, for me, that my internal furnace is kicking on now that i have been doing CT for a couple of months but I have not noticed the higher heart rate. I so hope that it means that I can handle the cold better too.

### Hemming — 2013-10-05T14:00:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-96943>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=96894)
>
> Good question.
>
> It seems, for me, that my internal furnace is kicking on now that i have been doing CT for a couple of months but I have not noticed the higher heart rate. I so hope that it means that I can handle the cold better too.

I definitely feel warmer and I've noticed that I've started to sweat a little just from being in the office at work. If it gets above 22C its almost uncomfortable for me.
Its not a huge difference but its higher. There might be other factors as my heart rate has been quite low, during sleep it could go below 40 and just sitting below 50.

My own thinking is that my heart rate has increased because I feel warmer and that my body has to get rid of the excess heat by pumping more blood to the surface of the skin - just like when you're in a very hot environment.

### oliveview — 2013-10-10T16:24:33+0300

I guess maybe b/c I don't get the numb sensation....

### Optimalbound — 2013-11-10T21:44:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100344>

Have any of you noticed a change in your perception of time, after CT?

I had the weirdest sensation while driving home from the beach after my North Sea CT session today. It felt like everything around me was moving at a slightly faster pace than normal. I kept checking my speedometer because I thought I (and everyone else) were speeding, but I was not. Also, g-force in corners felt too low for what the visual cues suggested. A totally surreal experience. The effect gradually wore off after maybe 10-15 minutes.

While I am (very, very vaguely) aware of how quantum effects might cause something like this, but I cannot quite get my head around how it could be noticable on this scale. What's going on?

### freesia — 2013-11-11T00:08:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100354>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=100346)
>
> I cannot have my hands and feet in the water at all. Even with keeping them out the feet get so cold that I have to rewarm them of walking hurts. Maybe someday I can get them in.

I've never got my feet in under 50 F (neoprene or wool socks for me) but what I found about a year in, is my feet were re-warming really fast. I just went a whole Winter without footwear...except in the bath.
If I go out of course I wear something, but I usually end up taking them off.
I have sheepskin slippers, possum fur inners - merino possum socks...a whole bunch of "essentials"  I bought just before I started CT and they have been made firmly redundant!
I think for some people the feet can take a while to catch up, but with consistency they will eventually. In the meantime do what you need to do to keep yourself reasonably comfortable.

### SeaHorse — 2013-11-11T00:46:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100356>

What I do when the water is really cold in the pond is wear a pair of wool socks and felt slippers the whole time. I keep mitts on my hands and then I don't get the white blanched look on my fingers and toes when I get out. The water is about 40 degrees now, so I'm wearing socks...not quite time for the slippers yet. I just rinse them out and they dry off in the shower until I use them again.

### nicld — 2013-11-11T16:43:03+0200

Ok, so the blonde in me is coming out and I should know this as one of my degrees is in Apparel manufacturing but just not clicking. So, some here wear wool socks in the tub? From what I remember wool does have great wicking properties but one they get soaked they trap the water by the skin so no different then putting the bare feet in the tub.

For Christmas I am going to ask for some shearling slippers with no sole, just fleece and leather. I cannot get outside anymore for grounding with just leather slippers. Feet got too cold yesterday. I HATE it when the feet get so cold...and so does the hubby.

### Inger — 2013-11-12T09:03:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100488>

I do CT with socks too in the winter... have not started using them yet. I should! When I use socks and gloves and hat I can stay longer for sure. I just dip and swim for a minute or so these days. Water is between +1-+5 degree C now. I often warm my toes shortly in the shower, just the toes, right after. Because if my toes are cold for long they suffer and get not pretty. So take care of your toes

I have been thinking of making me some felted wool "socks" to walk outside with. I have this wool jacket that shrunk it the washing machine.. so weird  so it is very tiny now but would do nicely as socks in the cold

### nonchalant — 2013-11-12T17:19:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100510>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=100488)
>
> I do CT with socks too in the winter... have not started using them yet. I should! When I use socks and gloves and hat I can stay longer for sure. I just dip and swim for a minute or so these days. Water is between +1-+5 degree C now. I often warm my toes shortly in the shower, just the toes, right after. Because if my toes are cold for long they suffer and get not pretty. So take care of your toes
>
> I have been thinking of making me some felted wool "socks" to walk outside with. I have this wool jacket that shrunk it the washing machine.. so weird  so it is very tiny now but would do nicely as socks in the cold

I've been walking around outside, marveling at how my feet can be chilled, and my toes can be cold, but I don't feel the all-consuming cold sensation from them that I used to feel. I don't remember this from last Autumn, but then I realized that I didn't walk around outside much then. Yes, I was CTing, and Jack had posted a picture of his feet on grass, trying to make a point, but I remember that my feet were in no condition to walk outside barefoot for long. I was still recovering from about 15 years of plantar faciitis. Thank you, Jack, for teaching me how to provide the energy and raw materials to heal my feet. I am really enjoying being able to walk outside barefoot, shower barefoot, and pretty much stay barefoot for an entire day.

### nicld — 2013-11-13T03:15:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100586>

Wow, got my water really really cold, about 42F. Only stayed in for about 30 minutes due to the hormone boot camp and good thing. I ordered some 100% wool socks today since the DH could not find any in the store and I don't think that the ones we have are 100% wool.

### Danco3636 — 2013-11-13T03:43:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100587>

Man I just can't hang with that cold **yet.**. Today I did a swim for 15 in 55F water and was cold as hell afterwards. Usually I can stay in without moving for 20 min or so but I think the swimming brought a different element.... Mild cold temp I can hang pretty good.... morning walks in 40F mornings with shorts only.... sometimes gloves and a hat with the dog is easy for an hour or more..... I still get cold to the core when I do Deep CT. I do like short cold plunges though.... Those heat me up pretty good..... I do like the fact that I heat up at night when going to bed so the wife wants to cuddle me to warm up.... bonus. I have a ways to go to get where I see others are posting.....

### yewwei.tan — 2013-11-13T04:05:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100591>

Did about 15 minutes in 12C/53F water in the tub today, while cradling another block of ice on my chest (knees and head sticking out, hands, feet, and rest of body submerged). I make 2 or 3 blocks of ice which are 3+L each in the freezer, and then just drop them in the tub, which drops the water temp by about 3C.

I personally find that I can stay in cold water indefinitely (or at least, up to a few hours), and like many here, find the 9-13C range the most comfortable / easiest. But once I get out, the shivering can get pretty intense. I've always been tempted to do things to warm up more quickly (exercise, hot shower, warm clothing), but have always resisted that. I do wonder though, if allowing yourself to naturally re-warm is crucial.

### Hemming — 2013-11-13T10:17:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-100608>

But once I get out, the shivering can get pretty intense. I've always been tempted to do things to warm up more quickly (exercise, hot shower, warm clothing), but have always resisted that. I do wonder though, if allowing yourself to naturally re-warm is crucial.

I also often have the feeling of being able to stay in the water for a long period but I dont want to push my luck as I swim in the ocean alone at 8-9C currently.

I think exercise (shivering is a form of exercise) is the natural way of rewarming whereas a hot shower or warm clothing is 'neolithic' way.

Swimming instead of just hanging out in the water really shortens your time. Our pond is about 39-41 degrees and when I go in (which has not been enough recently) I just kind of walk around slowly up to my neck or just float upright. I was swimming actively until a few weeks ago, but it felt a little scary, so now I'm less active and it's better.

Question to the lean guys: Will the shivering go away or is is normal when you have a low bodyfat percentage? I don't know when to finish my bath because I shiver all the time.

### Josh — 2013-11-26T02:26:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-101647>

So now I am trying a steeper gradient. I have a tub outside so the temp was 49 on Friday and 39 this morning. I have been going in for about 30 seconds twice per day up to my neck and then standing in the 30-40 degree air for a while afterwards without drying off. Each day I feel warmer and I have not sensed any difference as the temp has dropped. Today after the second immersion, my body turned red and I was warm for a while without shivering. This was pretty much the same as how I had been responding to the abdominal ice packs that I did for an hour or more for a few weeks with immersion in the tap water here which was in the 60's at the time. I had a 4 week break because I got a flulike episode and could not stand any cold during it.It is a big change since Friday. I like being outside, so I am subject to the local weather. This week it looks like 20's at night meaning the tub will be in the 30's. I figured I would add about 10-30 seconds per immersion until I lose the impulse to jump out. Last night I went to sleep without Melatonin for the first time in a year. Too soon to tell whether there is a connection. On I go...

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-12-02T21:51:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-102069>

Hi Everyone! I am finally getting back into regular CT after a warm summer with lots of sunshine.
I've missed my cold CT sessions, and I've missed this place! I hope you are all well, that all you Americans had a great Turkey Day, and are all on your pathways to full body, full soul wellness.
My CT sessions began again in earnest this past November when we spent a week and half at the cold Pacific. Brrr...! But we loved every second in the cold water at the beach. My kiddos were way into the cold water, too, and had no qualms about swimming for long stretches at a time.

For those of you that remember my tales of my little cold saoking hole, deep in a canyon where the owls fly and I keep a watchful eye out for the mountain lions that I know roam around, my cold soaking hole got completely destroyed this past summer after multiple days of heavy rains. (If you recall the scenes from Boulder, CO, we had the same storm system here in NM, and had equally as devastating rains and floods.) So, my canyon got completely rearranged. My little soaking spot had been a nice, private spot that was several hundred yards off a trail, and with little possibility for anyone to find it. It got filled in with boulders, and now the big holes are down low, near a pretty well-traveled trail. Thankfully, the biggest hole is now about 1.5 feet deeper, so you can actually soak all the way up to the neck! But for the sake of privacy, I may do some crafting higher up in the canyon so that I don't have to worry about passerbys, unless I plan to always bring a swimsuit, which is not my preferred choice.

Anyway, I just wanted to check in with this lovely community, say "Hi!", and get back into my regular grounding/CT/Sun soaking sessions.

I recently entered an artistic contest, which I didn't win, but which inspired me in a huge way, and we used this new pool as the backdrop. It was about 30 degrees outside, sunny, and with some snow, so I thought I'd share it with you all.

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-05T02:27:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-102360>

I'm back to chopping through ice with an axe every time I CT...it's my ritual warm up which puts me in the mood to sit in the pond up to my neck. I love hearing the ice chunks crackle and shatter when I throw them out onto the frozen surface with the rake. The ice was not as thick this time last year, I like it best now...fall CT was a nightmare and I just got really cold core temps.....now I get out and have a cold shower to keep things going. I'm not having the Reynauds stuff as long as I protect my feet. Today it was in the low 20's and the water was 32-33 degrees.....one of our barn cats kept me company today sitting out on the ice and drinking from my water hole...sweet.

> **I'm back to chopping through ice with an axe every time I CT.****..it's my ritual warm up which puts me in the mood to sit in the pond up to my neck. I love hearing the ice chunks crackle and shatter when I throw them out onto the frozen surface with the rake.** The ice was not as thick this time last year, I like it best now...fall CT was a nightmare and I just got really cold core temps.....now I get out and have a cold shower to keep things going. I'm not having the Reynauds stuff as long as I protect my feet. Today it was in the low 20's and the water was 32-33 degrees.....one of our barn cats kept me company today sitting out on the ice and drinking from my water hole...sweet.

Oh my goodness....I'm in awe.....Seahorse.......what with you hacking into ice and then sitting in an open air pond ..Chrysanthenum on the watch for mountain lions.....it's kind of unbelievable.......for the wuzzy warm adapted ! I really would need to visit a cold place to do what you're doing and I just wouldn't go anywhere near any mountain lion habitat...I'm sure they can swim!

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-05T14:51:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-102399>

For all that it sounds like I'm a polar bear...I'm really not. Some of you CT far longer than I do. There is something (called electrons) that makes the outdoor CT much more powerful for me, but I'm not in the water for more than five minutes so far when I'm outside. I do spend a lot of time outdoors everyday doing chores.
I still have a long way to go to be properly cold adapted and I'm understanding that this winter will get me further than last winter. CT is keeping my very low charge from dying altogether. It also makes winter a lot more fun.....will continue to work on hydration (just to give you an idea of the issue, by latest BUN/creatine ratio was 39!!!!!) hormones, pathogens and the biggie: emf mitigation)
Knowing that if I CT I won't have depression is one of the biggest blessings of my life and makes me love my pond!!

### nicld — 2013-12-15T00:26:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103435>

Did an hour bath CT today then got about 2 hours of outdoor CT and it was awesome.

It was actually not windy and was WARM today, well relative to what it has been, it was about 20 F. We have a bunch of little snow falls and the driveway was needing clearing. I love my DH but he sucks at getting the driveway really nice and clean so I got outside and blew a bunch of snow today. Just had some fleece pants on, my wool socks (cold feet are horrible) my boots (had to have on for safety), hat, gloves and a long sleeve compression shirt (protect the skin) and a fleece jacket. I took the fleece off and just was out there with a compression shirt on and it got a little chilly but was really nice. I checked the skin temp on my booty after I can in and it was about 57 F, not too bad.

Right now, I am feeling like I just got done with a CT bath since I have a little bit of the shivers.

### nicld — 2013-12-16T05:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103563>

So from the QA tonight I found that something I am doing during CT is actually good (thought is was not). I have a small bathroom with no heat in it. So when we remodeled and put a walk in tub in for the DD I made sure to add an over head heater fan. Past couple of weeks I have been turning it on about half way though CT since the bath water has been getting cold, cold cold (40-45F) it gets the bathroom so nice and warm. 2nd CT today I just turned it on right away and it was great. I felt I could have stayed in there for hours but needed to get out for the QA. So, now that I know that it is ok and good, I will be turning that heat on all the time.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-12-18T18:51:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103757>

I have CTd multiple times when sick and it has always helped me to recover in lightning speed. Recently, we were in CA and me and my two daughters all caught colds. We spent every day in the ocean, which was cold--about 58 to 60-degrees depending on where we were. It was cold enough to make my feet numb. We all recovered quickly, and the cold never turned into much, although when I first felt it come on I thought it was going to be a bear. I did the same thing last winter in my cold spring soaking hole, and I always recovered quickly.

### Inger — 2013-12-18T19:07:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103758>

It actually felt nice to dip in the ice water with that high fever... like my body craved the cold, seriously! I was pretty excited to do it as it was my first time doing CT when really sick. Kind of a Bio-hack to me. I was so glad I did! Now I know what to do if I would get sick again
