### Chrysanthemama — 2013-12-18T19:24:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103763>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=102437)
>
> CTing in the wild sure blows any depression away Seahorse.... but I feel deprived if I did not jump in the river once a day. There is something magic by being alone outside in the river. Submerged in the nature. I like that a lot. Different than doing indoor CT. I do even less than you, mostly only around 2 minutes. I sometimes am jealous of people having tubs and doing long baths being able to keep feet out but then... outdoor CT in a ice hole does have its magic!

It's amazing how much I've missed outdoor CT! Once the spring came along and the little spring became active with all sorts of critters, I just didn't have the drive and the motivation to get up there regularly. Now that winter has come, I've been heading up there 2x a week. It takes about 1.5 hours for me to get up there, soak for 20 to 30 minutes, and then hike back out, plus it's a bit of a drive over to the trailhead, so as much as I'd like to go more, it's just not really an option with two kids, work, and all that other stuff.

Gretchen and I talked about this last spring, but I am now certain that CTing has a lot to do with my will. I do not crave sweets or pastries or pastas or anything at all like that. I eat really well every day, but once I stopped CTing, I began craving microbrewed beer. This has always been my beverage of choice. I just don't like wine. So, as I started CTing less and less, I started indulging in beer more frequently. Not surprisingly, I gained weight over the summer as a result. Thankfully, I have started CTing again regularly, which includes the 2x a week in the outdoor spring, and 2x a week super cold showers for 10 minutes after my morning swims. I add in my own tub when I can, plus lots of outdoor cold time doing things I love like hiking, skiing, skating, sledding with the kids, etc. Almost instantly I've noticed that my desire to have beer is gone. Like completely. It's really weird, at least for me. I must figure out a way to CT regularly through the summer so I can tackle this issue once spring hits. But for now, I am enjoying that magical, beautiful, spiritual connection that I only seem to get while sitting in the spring water, in a canyon outside. Bliss!

### freesia — 2013-12-21T19:50:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103972>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=103619)
>
> Those of you who do CT regularly, what do you do if you happen to come down with a bad cold/fever/chills/flulike sx? Do you hold off on CT sessions til it passes? Or do you just keep jumping in no matter how cold you feel? Curious.

Hi Neil,
I really haven't been sick since I've been doing regular CT!! In the first couple of months I did get a cold with flu like symptoms (not flu though) and I skipped one day's CT then got in for a 30 minute session the next evening. My sense was CT sped up recovery. I felt completely well the next day.
If I felt dizzy, weak or had zero appetite (and was not adapted to fat burning) I would not CT personally but wait until I felt a bit stronger.
Just my perspective...maybe helpful if you're looking for a middle ground approach??

### freesia — 2013-12-21T20:02:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-103973>

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=103766)
>
> Freesia, I have nothing to add except to say...I'm jealous!!!!  And congrats....

Hi Hope, I've slept outside this past Spring too and had the same experience. My feet can be cold but not lose circulation. Amazing how adaptable we are with gentle consistency. I do wake up with the cooling before dawn - not uncomfortable though and on a bit of a 'high'. I'm not sure how optimal that is (the extra early waking) but I feel _great_ through the following day.
Sleeping outside with just a sheet was in late Summer by the way...in case you thought I'd turned into some kind of superwoman! . It was cold enough at night to mean dew settled though, and the surface temp of the wet ground numbed my skin somewhat.

Each person is an N=1 so how long it will take to fix stuff will vary greatly.

As for the skin, yes, perfectly normal. I put neoprene shoes on my feet and keep the feet out of the water but afterwards they still get really really cold and go white and numb. Also my skin is whitish in the tub but as soon as I get out it turns bright red and you can tell what parts did not get exposed to the cold water.

Nicole

### nonchalant — 2014-01-09T16:51:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-105167>

Hi healthman, yes the lobster look is normal after the bath.
As far as overcoming health problems, it varies widely. I noticed that I put on more muscle pretty quickly. I noticed some good hormone effects...I believe it was after a month or so. It helped my back so much, especially after I started eating more seafood and drinking clean water. But that process took over a year.

You can look in some of the CT threads for the various health issues CT helps. I believe it can help/cure some issues all by itself. Other problems need a combination therapy including sunshine, magnetism, water, and diet. And of course a good circadian rhythm and lack of artificial EMF. Dr. Kruse says CT can slow/stop the damage (like to the mitochondria) while you work on mitigating EMF and other health risks.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-15T17:25:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-105765>

Nicole, you might consider going lower in temp. 50F was the hardest temp for me to tolerate, though it looks like you're doing fine. At Gretchen's urging, I went down to 45F and was amazed at the difference. The colder water really seems to "turn off" the cold/pain sensation, and it is easier to submerge more than just the legs/tush.

### nicld — 2014-01-15T17:47:52+0200

### Inger — 2014-01-15T20:07:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-105778>

LOL about your hubby wanting to take a picture! You should have let him... and post it here lol ok that was not appropriate of me  but I think your hubby is so cool!

Today I went into the ice hole later, at 6 PM when it was already totally dark. Outside temp maybe -13 to -15 degree C so even if I had made a hole with an ax + sledgehammer when it was light outside it had frozen again! I needed only the sledgehammer now to crush the ice as it was not too thick. I went in up to my neck and sat there in the dark.. and the moon was shining on me. Very clear air and no wind. It was beautiful to sit there and look into the moon... I tell myself I am charging my batteries, and this will save me from all the crazy EMF and smart meters around ;-) I sometimes do my CT in darkness and I like that, it is different and very nice

### cantweight — 2014-01-15T21:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-105789>

Just spent the last half hour out side with the hubs taking turns chipping away at the ice in the trough. Its warmed up here...in the 40's and I was so set to hop in and CT thinking there was only a thin sheet of ice on the top....that sucker had to be 8 inches thick, the whole surface of the 150 gal tub! We couldnt even break it up! My sides hurt from laughing so hard as we did our best to bust through. Maybe when the kids get home from school I'll outfit them with safety goggles and hammers....will at least keep them busy for a while

### nicld — 2014-01-19T01:01:36+0200

I was filling my horses water tank today and getting some grounding and thought, oh I can't wait till I can take the extra water tank and do CT in it this spring until.......... ### Nick — 2014-01-29T09:11:15+0200

I have just returned from an amazing holiday in Canada and a little of the US. After leaving in 40 degree (110f) weather we landed in Vancouver in 2 degrees weather which was just fantastic. The CT worked better than expected, I was the only person on the first day I saw who was in a t-shirt. We spent a week and a half in Whistler where i learned to ski badly. One thing that surprised me was how cold the showers were. They must have only just above freezing point as they felt really cold. The heat that I generated after getting out of those was pretty cool. I wish I could have access to that quality and temperature of water all year round.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-02-13T04:44:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-109911>

I am hoping to build an in-ground, stone-based hot tub and cold tub set-up within the next year. This is my new dream. With all the discussion of hot/cold CT sessions I keep coming back to what it feels like to sit in hot springs during a serious snowfall in really cold weather. Hot on bottom, cold on top. If I can find a way to refrigerate the cold tub during the summer, we should be able to have cold on bottom and hot on top. It's like the perfect year-round solution! Plus the ability to do full body immersions in the cold tub for significant periods of time, and alternate between the two tubs, like at certain Japanese-style spas. I'm so over-the-top excited about this idea. I want it to be the perfect blend of completely grounded, with just the right combo of stones and minerals to enhance the grounding and electron experience. Of course I don't want to treat the water with chlorine or other hard chemicals, so we'll have to arrive at the perfect filtering system. Now to find the right engineers...

In the meantime, I've continued to do my in-canyon mineral-spring soaking regularly this winter. We are having a terrible winter here in the mountains of NM. While winter started off with a bang, it has fizzled out to nothing like I've ever seen before. Having lost my home to a wildfire due to extreme fire-danger conditions and drought in the year 2000, and then enduring another serious wildfire and evacuation in 2011, I can honestly say this is the worst winter I've ever seen, ever. In my entire 42 years here in NM. My backyard, which sits at 7500 ft above sea level, would normally have an average of 2 ft of snow in all the north-facing spots during an average year. This year, nothing. Nada. Zilch. Zero. My chickens seem to be happy about it. But I am not.

However, I've continued to do what I can for me, including lots of in canyon-hiking and soaking. Going up the higher mountains for river soaking. Getting in as much cold time in the outdoors as possible. Unless we decide to move to AK or BC, this is our home. I'm still dreaming about my in-ground hot/cold pools.

### Gracelind — 2014-02-13T05:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-109918>

Hi [Chrysanthemama](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?members/chrysanthemama.1795/),

May your hot and cold tubs dream come true this year. When you're finished it will be a work of art! Sounds ideal to be able to alternate tubs. Plus you can practice the Fournier effect in the hot tub.

You may have heard the Sierra faces the same lack of winter and scary threat of dangerously dry conditions next summer. In 2007 we were two blocks away from losing our house in a large wildfire, so I know all the anguish leading up to that point. We were lucky that time that the winds didn't blow the fire over the top of us. Several hundred people around us did lose their homes and full recovery will take many years. I can imagine all too well what it feels like to lose everything you treasure and have worked for so hard. I so sorry this happened to you and your family.

May we all be safe from wildfire this summer.

### Grmoline — 2014-02-17T12:11:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-110769>

Cold-Adapted? If not fully, then getting there. The thermostat's down in the house, and it no longer feels cold. When I walk outside these days most often I have to take off my jacket and get down to shirtsleeves (Londoners look at me like I'm nuts.... maybe I am.....). I feel tingly and warm after CT, and burn like a furnace the last part of the night.

CT Baths or Icing or Both? Cold showers, icing, and now adding in cold baths. Cold showers just because I take showers and just decided to stop turning on the hot water. Icing with packs to back and torso daily. Cold baths so far have been experimentation, but none of the versions have a direct plunge into a full tub. I shiver initially, then stop.

Duration? 1-1/2 to 2 hours with ice packing, up to 10 mins shower, up to 30 mins bath.

Temperature? Tap water is in the mid-40's, skin temp gets to 55 in baths, low 50's to high 40's with icing

When do you CT? At night, before bed (8pm-9pm) as well as earlier in the day, sometimes morning, sometimes late afternoon.

How long have you been doing CT? Started early Jan, so about 6 weeks, increasing in intensity and frequency.

I keep my hands out of the water and just my toes, and so far only submerge the lower half. I have Raynaud's, but it has got massively better even before the CT.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-02-18T20:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-111050>

Had the best CT session yesterday I've had in a while. We went to the closest lake near us, still an hour's drive away. Last year at this time, it was frozen solid. Yesterday, not a drop of ice in the water. What a difference a year and a terrible winter make! Jack, this was the same lake we were at last January! There is no blue ice at the moment.

But, in our effort to take advantage of the liquid lake, and the cold water temps coupled with a warmer-than-average day, my family and I ended up at the lake. Despite the holiday, we had the place to ourselves. My husband and I found a great south facing spot to settle-in on some great sandstone slabs, within cold water and receive abundant sun warming rays at the same time. The water was approximately 41 degrees. We both sat in the lake for about 45 minutes, while getting a tan at the same time. It was awesome! Afterwards we sat on the rocks and air-dried off, while the kids played in and out of the water the whole time. It was a perfect afternoon.

### Julie5 — 2014-02-22T02:02:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-111629>

Today I donned my compression shirt, ran cold tap water into our deep tub, and for the 3rd time this week took a cold bath. It was about 5 degrees colder than day before yesterday: 57 deg. I really felt the cold on my butt, and the shivering started almost immediately. It did calm down some. My intention was to read Ender's Game and stay in for 40 minutes, a 10 min jump from lasttime. But the phone rang just out of arm's reach. I should have just let it go, but....I didn't. I was in for 30 minutes. This time I am leaving the water in the tub and perhaps I will do it again tonight.

A couple of years ago we bought a Rubbermaid stock tank that is about 6 feet long and 4 ft. wide, 2 ft. high for the little kiddos to splash around in. I can see it being a great way to CT this spring, early summer before the water warms up.

### Danco3636 — 2014-05-05T01:04:18+0300

### nonchalant — 2014-05-20T00:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-127597>

We dug up a round, metal trough on our property a few weeks ago. DH has cleaned and painted it, and now I have my wonderful, outdoor CT pool. Last week I filled it with well water and enjoyed it. CTing outdoors in the sun is awesome!!! It wasn't nearly as cold as I get my bathtub, but it was cool enough to enjoy. My back relaxed and I was able to get an adjustment or two while underwater. Yes!!

I noticed hundreds of tiny bubbles forming on the hairs of my shoulders, and then noticed they were everywhere (except when I'd rub the skin.) Must be some quantum energy exchange going on...

Back in the city, CTing indoors in the tub, I looked for these tiny bubbles, and didn't see any. Lack of sunshine? Oh, must be the dielectric blocker in the tap water. Bummer.

### nonchalant — 2014-05-20T02:01:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-127608>

Dan, it is half in the sun and half in the shade. We filled it one day, and I enjoyed it for that day and the next. Unfortunately, it doesn't have a valve at the bottom, and the metal is very thick. DH siphons it out and refills it for another couple days. Probably have to buy ice for summer, but it is nice now. When we move to the country, I'll probably freeze lots of blocks of ice. This water is magic all by itself, though. Very enjoyable to shower, sponge bathe, and even wash dishes in it.

Even if nn EMF and pollution were to instantly disappear from my city, I'd still move, for the better water.

Watermelons?

### nicld — 2014-05-20T17:03:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-127678>

I used plastic milk jugs to and so far they have held up pretty good. I also use a smaller outdoor animal bucket to make ice disks. Can't fill it too full or even they will break. I busted the bottom of one of my large ones this winter as I was setting them outside and it was too full and froze solid.

I would suggest getting one of these. [http://www.amazon.com/Little-Giant1...397&sr=8-5&keywords=freeze+proof+horse+bucket](http://www.amazon.com/Little-Giant18-Quart-Bucket-DF18FB/dp/B0017GXKF8/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1400594397&sr=8-5&keywords=freeze+proof+horse+bucket)

### Nathan — 2014-06-03T04:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-129468>

I got this aquarium chiller for my tub and it works pretty well: [http://www.amazon.com/Hydroponic-Ch...3-spell&keywords=activeaqua+acquarium+chiller](http://www.amazon.com/Hydroponic-Chiller-Refrigeration-250-1200-110-120/dp/B004LQ7GH4/ref=sr_1_sc_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1401758422&sr=8-3-spell&keywords=activeaqua+acquarium+chiller)
It take about 2 hours to get my water to the low 50s and I've got it down to 42.

I was using disposable glad storage containers before to make my ice. They're really cheap and last a while before they break. I also have a plastic bucket from some sorbet that I had and that thing has been great and has lasted forever. I still make ice blocks for when I'm short on time and don't have 2 hours for the chiller to do it's job..

Randy

### PaulG — 2014-07-02T03:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-132938>

Getting cold numb feet for approx 1 hr afterwards is not something I enjoy but maybe its a good thing if circulation is poor.

I can't think of a safe way of cooling upper half in the pool and warming the lower. Being dangled by your legs is not an option

Maybe an outdoor tub or bath is a better option for you as you can better control placement of extremeties. Also you can do other things such as read or listen to podcasts which I find make the session go quicker and I learn things. I also hop straight into the shower afterwards which I know is not advised.

In the summer after CT I used to snuggle up against the nearest wall for the radiant heat lol. My wife said get a room. Don't forget you can also use showers to become semi cold adapted by titrating down from piping hot to warm to cool to cold. At the moment I am at piping hot inhaling chloroform gas so I am also in need of winter CT kickstart (up the ....)

### QuantumRadical — 2014-07-04T05:29:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-133109>

Just turned on the chiller and pump. Water temp in the bathtub started at 78 degrees F (it has been around 90 degrees outside here in Northern Virginia for several days in a row). Set the chiller to 45 degrees. Will take measurements of water temp every ten minutes with IR thermometer. I hope it takes less than two hours to cool to 45 degrees. We will see. I am using 3/4 inch tubing for intake and output including the pump. The chiller is rated to 4 degrees C/ 39.2 F. I love experiments. I will post pics of the setup if it works.

### QuantumRadical — 2014-07-04T05:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-133110>

4 degree F drop in 20 minutes. If that is a linear phenomena then each hour would be 12 degrees drop. I am looking for a 33 degree drop so approximately a bit less than three hours. That would be OK, not ideal. However, in the winter, the tap water is about 48 degrees. Run the bath and jump on in.  Last summer, it seemed that the high for the tap water was about what it is now. I never recorded anything above 78 degrees, but last summer seemed mild compared to this one, so far. w

### QuantumRadical — 2014-07-04T07:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-133115>

Only a 12 degree drop after two hours. I will let it run a couple of more hours, maybe more, to see if it can "git er dun."

### QuantumRadical — 2014-07-04T15:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-133152>

Well, I let the chiller run for about 6 hours and the chiller thermometer only got down to 48 degrees, but my IR gun said the water temp was 42 degrees. SWEET! The rate of temperature drop over night was about 1 degree every ten minutes. I turned it off earlier this morning. Three hours later, I got in! Temperature was 47 degrees. It felt great. Note: I have a really nice IR thermometer and I have calibrated it so I believe what it reads is more correct than what the chiller thermometer shows.

### Da-mo — 2014-07-05T17:38:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-133234>

Had a great session the other day and found my ideal scenario . . .

My swimming pool is right around 50F at the moment. If the sun is shining I can stay in it up to my jaw with the sun on my face and a bit of meditation for 20 minutes before I begin to shiver. During CT, its my lower arms, wrists and hands that suffer the most . . . not sure why, maybe because they get the least cold exposure. If I only submerge up to solar plexus and its sunny I can stay in indefinitely. After that I get inside and sunbathe inside the ranch slider. I've found that moving as little as possible while I rewarm in the sun almost eliminates any shivering afterwards. It seems like movement after CT transports cold blood on the surface deeper into the body and sets off the shivering.

Still a long way from the days of my youth where I would spend all summer holidays at the town pool - 6 hours a day, every day for 2 months, alternating between the cold pool and lying on the sun-baked concrete.

For me the ideal is wake to a sunny day- hot tub for 30mins - breakfast - CT - sunbathe.

Looking forward to when the days get longer and I can move the outdoor CT so that my day ends with HIIT or Tabatas, dinner, then CT.

I've also started using wood fires more often now and enjoying the ambience and heat energy from them.

Anyone else notice that sunburn became a thing of the past after starting CT?

I been slacking on the CT and really need to get my arse back in. When I am done, I will be going outside and laying in the afternoon sun to rewarm.

### cmegan — 2014-07-14T18:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-134319>

Hi Randy!

### QuantumRadical — 2014-07-14T19:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-134324>

> [cmegan said:](/goto/post?id=134319)
>
> Hi Randy!
>
> I've been following your thread waiting for your results of the chiller! Would you mind sharing the name and model? Thanks!

The chiller works splendidly although it does take a bit longer to cool down the water than I would like. It reduces the water temp about 6 degrees per hour. Starting temp now is 77 (tap water temp) F. The pump model is:
**Hydrofarm AAPW550 550-GPH Active Aqua Submersible Pump. ($39 US at Amazon)
The chiller model is:
**Hydroponic Chiller - Refrigeration Unit - 250-1200 Gal/Hr. - 1/2 HP - 4100 BTU - 360 Watt - 110-120 Volt - Use with 1/2 3/4 1 in. Tubing - Active Aqua AACH50 ($600 US at Amazon).

The chiller gets the water quite cold, less than 45 degrees.

Hope this helps.****

### Greenisland — 2014-07-23T18:17:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-135310>

My 6 year old daughter did CT with me last night. I never suggested it. I never told her anything about how it's "good for you". I just said, "Don't get in the bath, it's really cold!" And she crawled in. And stayed in for 40 minutes. No ice- 58 degree tap water up to her rib cage. I asked her several times if she wanted to get out but she said, "I like it." and "It's colder when I get out". Weird!!! How, why?? She must be in more in touch with her body and what it needs?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-07-23T19:47:04+0300

### Sandra — 2014-07-24T00:06:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-135323>

"a jacket is something you put on when your mother is cold" ...

... it's a joke in my son's joke-book but I now think it is true. I've stopped saying that exact thing to my kids as I'm believing they aren't actually cold.

I think I'm adapting too as my husband now puts a jacket on before I do - and in the 20 years I have known him, this winter (in OZ) is the first time that has ever happened. I jumped in our home pool yesterday for 45 minutes after a long walk with lots of hills - I was dreading the pain in my legs ... nothing today, no troubles walking, and no pain walking up stairs.

The water was only 17 deg C (which is 62 deg F) so still a way off 10-13 deg C, but getting closer. The funny thing is that when I dipped my foot in the water, it felt freezing to me, but only after a few more seconds there, it was totally fine ... so I inched in, bit by bit, until I got up to my neck. I wasn't cold when I got out (it was a nice sunny day) so layer in the sun for a while too. Might have to try it again. I wouldn't say that until now I have really tried CT properly. A couple of months ago I did it every day for a couple of weeks, but the pool at that time was low 20s so pretty warm! - and maybe some will say it's still not cold enough, but it's what I have to work with at the moment and seems to be making some difference for me.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-07-30T04:48:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-136022>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=135313)
>
> Kids know but the adult beliefs ruin them.......just like the need for veggies or wear a hat when you go out.......all BS.

Definitely. My kids have been swimming nearly daily since the end of May. In and out of seeing tons of kids with blue lips, shivering, refusing to swim when it's cloudy, etc., I have observed that my kids never get cold, rarely towel off, and could care less what the weather is like. We went to another lake this past Sunday, and a big summer squall came in and dropped the temp by 30 degrees. We continued to swim, and after the 5th or so session in the water, I noticed my youngest was shivering. It's the first time I've seen her shiver all summer, and she noticed it, too. On her own, she toweled off, and then laid down on the rocks for a while. She was ready to jump in and swim about 30 minutes later. Kids crack me up! But they are so worth watching when they are still following their innate instincts. They move right, and act based on what their bodies need, not on what some stodgy old tradition says.

experienced this the other day by my tiny lake...

2 moms with their kids come there for picninc and I got to follow them closely... very interesting
they had to have their hats on too... and t shirts and shorts, even in the water... but the kids wanted to be in the water all the time lol They could not swim, was around 3 yo

Weird is, they was so interested in me...lol I said nothing I just lied there in the sand and read with my feet in the water.... watching them from my eye corner... but they talked to me, and it all ended up like that, they wanted to sit tight by me, so tight they touched my bare skin... haha that was funny! I showed them how to build sand cakes, and I tried to learn them swim too (that was not easy...lol)

It was very funny! The sad thing was, these kids was clearly destroyed by adults, very sad. One was overweight and there was something very sad about them.... clearly had some mental issues.. i also could see their moms was very unbalanced.... and not healthy, poor kids. It always breaks my heart. Their moms did not play with them or even come close to the water, they just sat a bit far away, smoked, and shouted to the kids when they got in too deep
i cannot stand it very well at all to see kids suffer... it seriously hurts me more than anything

I was just thinking about the grounding/negative ion concepts. I really haven't done enough reading to really "get it" yet but it seems the grounding going on walking from carpet to tub wouldn't be nearly what it would be walking on sand and rocks into the vast ocean.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-23T20:04:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-138719>

First time wading into this thread .

I've been doing "Sea-T" early every morning for the past week or so. Drive to a nice northern beach, put on my neoprene scuba gear (just the short boots, the gloves, and a head cap that covers my ears) and immerse in shallow sea water up to my neck for about 30 minutes. Water temps now are between 60 and 65 degrees, I plan to continue this as long as it seems safe. It drops to just over 32 degrees in winter, sometimes lower.

I'm using tide tables to go when it is most placid. Still haven't figured out if that's low tide, high tide, or somewhere in between! At 7:30 in the morning it's usually just starting to get the high tide back in.

With the addition of the new gear, I am able to stay in and get cherry-red skin on my arms/legs/torso but still feel very warm. I also wear a flotation belt (loosely so my skin still gets cold)- I'm a good swimmer but the ocean makes me a bit nervous and I'm still learning the wave patterns, gives me some peace of mind that I'll always surface if a big one clunks me on the head.

Currently trying to read through this whole thread from the beginning!

### sean — 2014-08-24T06:02:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-138745>

I've been off my CT regimen since may, was living in NC water got too warm. I'm back in the bay area, CA with family, and got myself swimming today. first sunny day. was foggy / cloudy all week. Water temp feels like 62 so not cold by any standards, no shiver afterwards. 55 is my favorite temp. I'll take 62 over 48 though. I wanted to make this post because I did a 5 block run afterwards, 5 city blocks lol not miles. It's always hard to run after CT, now it's the first time I ever checked my heart rate, 165 bpm wow. So is it good to do? I take it I still have some vasoconstriction in my extremities after 62 degree swim, so this is why running is hard and the fast heart beat. BTW I'm in my early 20's not overweight but not an athlete by any means. Could do one 8 minute mile. I shouldn't be swimming in the ocean. Agree with the poster above - It's dangerous. one of my closer calls today.. but the ocean is 5 blocks away from me .. the bay not.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-25T19:11:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-138860>

40 minutes at 62 degrees in my backyard cold tub- couldn't get it any colder despite adding more ice. Feet and hands out of the water, neoprene boots/hat/gloves, reading Stranger in a Strange Land. Made sure to cover my neck to get my thyroid exposed to the cold. Got my roommate to try it for the first time, she has thyroid issues and was intrigued. She screamed when she got in but found it so relaxing that she stayed in for 20 minutes! Along with my other roommate I now have 75% of my household doing this.

NO shivering while in the water, but started shivering five minutes after I got out and it continued for ten minutes. I was walking around outside in minimal clothing, outside temp was in the 70's. After my skin temp went from 62 degrees to 90 degrees, I took a hot shower.

Despite my gigantic breakfast, which I estimate was 1000 calories, I am now hungry. Is there an estimate for how many calories we burn at different times and temperatures? I know it's different for everyone but I imagine there is a range.

### Winter Mermaid — 2014-08-27T05:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-138994>

There is a major difference between 62 degrees and 55 degrees . . . I think I gave myself very mild hypothermia today at 55 for 50 minutes. My thoughts slowed down and my muscles didn't work well when I got out, didn't warm up on my own very quickly. I wasn't shivering in the water so I thought I was fine.

After 15 minutes of sun and stomping my feet, then got into a hot shower and I was fine.

However, I am obviously not cold adapted enough to do 55 degrees. I think I will lowed it one degree a day, starting with 62 degrees again tomorrow.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-08-30T19:43:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-139401>

Paul asks on the CT 12 blog: Does CT help in summer? Paul cold works all the time because of its effects on matter in us. We are made of protein polymers. Magnetism acts to condense these protein polymers in us. This saves us energy. Anything that saves us energy makes us more healthy. Magnetism has a huge effects on electrons. Electrons control the phenotype of how chemistry works everywhere in the universe. This includes your cells. Magnetism acts like a giant invisible circuit board for the currents emitted from DHA. Magnetism is tightly coupled to cold temperatures naturally. Colder temperatures increase the strength of a magnets and their magnetic field. Why is that? Cold can have an effect on magnetism, because cold can have an effect of how fast or slow electrons move. This links the sun’s power to the magnetic field of the Earth no matter what season you are in Paul. It also links the Schumann resonance strength to the sun. When the sun is shining, the Schumann resonance is strongest. When the sun is shining the Earth’s magnetic field is weakest. This implies that the Schumann strength varies inversely with the magnetic field’s strength. At night time, the magnetic field is stronger on Earth than it is when the sun is out.

Here is my home-grown CT system, which I now have all my housemates using multiple times a day! This is the 64 gallon storage tub from Home Depot. You can see the giant ice cubes made from frozen metal bowls and the industrial size whisk for swirling water around the cubes without warming the water with my hands. The crowded furniture is to keep it from deforming from the water weight. Two big ice bowls gets it down into the 50's. On the left is the house scuba gear: hats, boots and gloves.

Today was my first day not using any of the scuba gear, and it went great. I can feel instant relaxation as soon as I adapt. Housemates are still using the gear and I will use it in the ocean.

Um, Wow! Beautiful, Josh!!

I'm patiently waiting for our weather to turn cold. I've been doing cold showers all summer, and lots of swimming with air drying. Lots of sun and movement. I'm beginning to crave my cold winter spring soaking.

Fall is just starting to ramp up, with leaves turning yellow, and we've had some nice cool evenings. We try to go in our short sleeves and shorts as long as comfortable, and I never encourage my kids to wear sweaters or jackets (although I always keep them on hand for when they want them). Until it starts to get really cold, which hopefully will happen sooner, rather than later, we just try to get in a ton of grounding, and enjoy our cool evenings. I wish I was near a cold ocean. I am working towards that goal. In the meantime, I'll eat some seafood, and enjoy our sun while it moves towards the solstice.

### Optimalbound — 2014-10-05T20:37:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-142907>

Here, up on the 59th parallel it has been an unusually warm summer, but now the ocean temperature is finally down to 14C (57F), so I had a nice, 40 min. long swim today. My first proper ocean CT since May. It felt very, very cold at first, so I was a bit worried that I had lost my cold adaption, but after a minute or two, it seemed like my uncoupling kicked into high gear, and I was perfectly comfortable. Found myself wishing for lower temperatures, in fact. Will happen soon enough.

@[nicld](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6689/) - I'm very impressed by your 3 hours @ 48F. Do you shiver? How do keep your mind occupied? I find that I can sort of meditate when the sea is calm, but not when I constantly get buffeted around by waves like today.

### Butters — 2014-10-29T10:17:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-145530>

2 Gelpacks sessions - 1h + 2h in the morning and the evening. Also outside CT with shirt and shorts at temperatures of 7-12°C. Not feeling very cold, some goosebumps occasionally and feeling fresh but no shivering.
The hardest part is always the first minutes going outside from a heated room. Thats the time that will mostly induce shivering. Thereafter it is getting warmer without shivering.

Anyone feels the same?
I would explain it with the disability to activate BAT fast enough to generate enough heat when the temperatures fall so fast. So it relys on shivering the first minutes and when BAT is activated and is able to heat the body (like an car engine warming first up before driving in winter) it switches to the BAT. This thinking goes synchron with the studies which found that BAT is activated after some time exposure - not short time exposure. With time the body eventually learns to heat faster with BAT. Jack mentioned in the podcast with Ben Greenfield that cold adapted humans can radiate heat within seconds of going in the cold.

So the hardest part for me is to go out from heated to cold air, so as going the other way - then I will feel to warm.

Where do you lay your gelpacks on the body. I lay them in triangle from shoulders to midchest and between the shoulder blades. Will this cover the big surface arteries?

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-11-26T00:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-148492>

Finally I am back to my outdoor spring. For whatever reason, I kept coming up with excuses to not actually soak. I realized that the predatory water beetles were becoming an obsession of mine, so I decided to don some compression shorts, wear a tankini top and just get on with it already. October was unusually warm, so aquatic life seemed to be burgeoning and not dying off during this usually cold month. Once November hit, it hit hard, with temps dropping from 65 to 10 degrees F overnight. So finally, finally, I've been back in and soaking with some regularity for the past couple weeks. Ahhh...it is so magical, the combination of grounding, CT, deep blue sky, red-tail hawks, the sound of flowing water, and the trees.

### SeaHorse — 2014-12-02T21:46:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-149032>

CT with a FROG

Hi everybody…just checking in to share my wonderful CT experience this afternoon. We've had lots of low temps and high temps over the past month and twice there has been 2 inches of ice on the pond which has then melted into the water…really shoots the temperature down quickly for this time of year. Today it was about 18 degrees in the air and 33 degrees in the water. The ice was thick again but not bonded enough to the warmer edges of the pond for me to walk on it…So I'm bashing my way out through the ice out to the spot where I sit down and who should come swimming by but a lovely green frog…very slow and kind of out of it. He didn't mind the ice crashing above his head and stayed where he was while I sat down next to him….good company. I think he needed to head back into the primordial ooze on the bottom.

A few days ago a water beetle swam by ….interesting to see the remnants of summer activity so late in the year.

I've been dipping every other day all fall and so far I've had NO signs of reynauds….this is getting better and better each year. Energy is way up too. No a-fib since early summer…..other things aren't going so well, but I figure if my energy is revving then my mitochondria have to be working better overall.

There's an app for that! CLapp Notify

I set up searches on it for items we are looking for. My search areas are Houston, Austin, Dallas, Beaumont, and San Antonio. Whenever something new is posted I get an immediate notification.

That's how I got the FIR sauna for $350 recently. I called the seller within 5 minutes of it being posted. He held it for me until I could arrange transportation.

### Josh — 2014-12-14T18:06:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-150079>

I have not been doing much formal CT this year and I imagine I am not cold adapted. However, this morning I went out as usual to get some sun in my speedo and I am sitting on my frost covered roof and my neighbor looks up at me and says "you are crazy". He is out working in long johns and such while I sit on my roof in a speedo reading the newest blog. It is damper than usual as we had a good storm and the droplets on the pines and bamboos glisten like diamonds....

### nonchalant — 2014-12-14T18:33:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-150082>

After I got my outdoor tank in the country, my indoor tub CT in the city stopped. I just didn't want the fluoride. The tank is too big to cool down much, so I just got mild CT for a few months. When our first cold spell came, the temp got down quite low, with a thin ice layer on the tank. I had no problem relaxing in the water, but eventually my feet got my attention. My toes got COLD and the soles of my feet got semi-frozen. I had to lift my toes (and hands) from the water. Guess I needed some adaption! All was well afterwards, of course, but it did surprise me.

I still find it interesting that the sensitive skin of my arches, and my skin elsewhere had zero issues with freezing water. Cell membranes are amazing things.

### nicld — 2014-12-14T19:57:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-150093>

I need to do more spot CT on my thighs specifically the upper most part of the inner thighs. . I noticed the other day that I have a distinct line on my inner thighs and for a while I could not understand why. Then it dawned on me that since my thighs touch in that area, they stay warmer and do not get as much cold exposure. When doing CT, I try to let them get more exposure but it does not appear to be enough. So will will try sticking an ice pack between my thighs when driving to and from work (about 2 hours). At least now I know that CT is really shrinking some of the fat deposits. Would love to have the deposits reduced in this area to make wearing dresses more comfortable and not have to wear undershorts with them.

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-16T16:21:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-150279>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=150093)
>
> I need to do more spot CT on my thighs specifically the upper most part of the inner thighs. . I noticed the other day that I have a distinct line on my inner thighs and for a while I could not understand why. Then it dawned on me that since my thighs touch in that area, they stay warmer and do not get as much cold exposure. When doing CT, I try to let them get more exposure but it does not appear to be enough. So will will try sticking an ice pack between my thighs when driving to and from work (about 2 hours). At least now I know that CT is really shrinking some of the fat deposits. Would love to have the deposits reduced in this area to make wearing dresses more comfortable and not have to wear undershorts with them.

I noticed the same thing, even after using icepacks to spot CT, and asked my massage therapist friend what she thought.... She suggested looking at a diagram of the body's musculature, and I can see a distinct pattern of body comp changes over certain muscles. My arms are toning faster over the deltoid muscle than the biceps, even though i've worked on getting the biceps area cold under water and with ice massage. On the legs from the sartorius outwards is much better than the inner thigh muscles. When I thought about it some more I decided maybe thats the way it's supposed to be ...... If I curl up in the foetal position, its mainly the muscles and skin areas that can protect me from heat loss by design that don't seem to be doing so well at fat loss even if I deliberately expose them to cold.

My waist is odd as well, even if I stretch out and get the water in the groove it still stays white while i'm in there and the left side groove is much deeper than the right, I'm not losing evenly. My stomach also now has a vertical groove at the bottom of the pudge up to the belly button where the fat is coming off each side in line with the diagram. Its like my body is either working on things in a particular order, or it gives up fat less easily in certain areas, not sure which.

### Inger — 2014-12-17T11:22:43+0200

I skinny dipped in the lake close to the boyfriends house yesterday... i went for a walk around the lake and i had not planned to dip but man i just could not resist in the end.
It then started to rain... it was maybe +4 degree C and i walked home and then the boyfriend called if he should pick me up as it rained... and i said nooo i am not made of sugar lol and then i came home and was wet and I loved to walk in the rain... and as i told him I had skinnydipped in the lake he thought i had lost my mind completely...haha he thinks i am totally crazy
he just do not understand my way of living at all.....  I do get that... and when he kissed me yesterday evening in front of the TV and I had my blue blockers on as always he said it was a bit like kissing a fly  because of those huge dark glasses.... hehe......

noo....hehehe not yet!   The boyfriend is so kind to always lit a candle  and if he has to put on the lights he tells me beforehand.... darling... put your head under the sheet now because i am putting on the poison blue light.....hehe... he teases me all the time
but sure i disappear under the sheet everytime he puts the light on.. and because he wants to see me he shuts the lights off pretty quick
To be honest I have to say i am happy that he does respect my wishes to have it dark pretty much  (maybe because it is saving energy and money....LOL)
lets me use candle lights in the evening, dimmed lights etc. he often makes fire in the fireplace too. nice

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-18T21:08:52+0200

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=150479)

I've been looking critically at my bed. I know I get quite warm on whatever side is against the bed. I tend to sleep on one side more than the other, and yes, that side is fatter also. I'm going to get a mattress pad that is cotton, not polyester. It would also help if I could do without the sheets!
>
> I've got that vertical grove below the belly button also.

I've noticed faster changes to body comp since I've submerged up to the neck, and not worried about shrinking the girls. I was reluctant at first because I was bottom heavy, and didn't want to look even more bottom heavy. Then i decided that my body would know what to do as long as the environmental signals werent scrambled, and my sleep is good, so if the water temp is under 5c I've submerged to the neck for 12 minutes max for signalling purposes. .....One interesting thing I noticed today was that the girls now look even sized. My left has been larger than the right for years, and its that side that the waist groove is deeper.

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-23T15:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-150851>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=150479)
>
> I tend to sleep on one side more than the other, and yes, that side is fatter also. I'm going to get a mattress pad that is cotton, not polyester. It would also help if I could do without the sheets!

Its the reverse for me, the thinner side is the warmer side at night. I've been wondering if the warmer side has been giving up heat to the cooler side .... or whether it is increased heart magnetism that is making my left hand side more energy efficient first. I've got summer covers on, but find sometimes when I wake that I have a leg stuck out of the bedclothes, almost as if my body is doing it automatically in my sleep to lose a bit of heat ....

I've just changed my CT a bit .... I'm curling up in the water as if I've fallen into freezing water ..... my hands are under the water but the wrists where the veins are close to the surface are pressed up tight against my legs . My feet are out because I can't get my shoulders under unless they are, but I'm curling up as much as I can. I was wondering if exposing the parts of my body it would want to protect from heat loss (like the inner thigh) is a good thing...... What if its encouraging me to maintain fat stores there as a survival mechanism.  Lets see what a change of signalling does......

### nicld — 2014-12-27T21:21:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151067>

Finally got some more snow. I could not seem to get my water cold enough (47F only). Now that I have snow to toss in it got it down to 43F today and felt so good. Got some weird stuff going on and really need to get some good 2 hours CT sessions in.

### Da-mo — 2014-12-29T10:28:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151122>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=150851)
>
> Its the reverse for me, the thinner side is the warmer side at night. I've been wondering if the warmer side has been giving up heat to the cooler side .... or whether it is increased heart magnetism that is making my left hand side more energy efficient first. >
> I've just changed my CT a bit .... I'm curling up in the water as if I've fallen into freezing water ..... my hands are under the water but the wrists where the veins are close to the surface are pressed up tight against my legs . My feet are out because I can't get my shoulders under unless they are, but I'm curling up as much as I can. I was wondering if exposing the parts of my body it would want to protect from heat loss (like the inner thigh) is a good thing...... What if its encouraging me to maintain fat stores there as a survival mechanism.  Lets see what a change of signalling does......

I recall Dr. K saying in a podcast that the most important parts to cool for CT were the femoral triangle and the upper clavicle. Think it was in this one [http://www.ephysiologix.com/living-the-optimized-life-episode-33-jack-kruse-cold-thermogenesis/](http://www.ephysiologix.com/living-the-optimized-life-episode-33-jack-kruse-cold-thermogenesis/)

I still have my hands and feet out when in the tub, but not gloved and socked. Seems doing it this way triggers less vasoconstriction for me. I like to try it the other way around one day - hands and feet in the cold only and the rest of me out. I wear the ice vest often and I think that has made my torso much more cold tolerant than the rest of me. I might try icing the various less tolerant parts individually to see if those areas respond in the same way.

I can tolerate the ice vest for as long as the ice in it lasts and it turns the skin on my torso the desired cherry red. I only tolerate the cold water (10-12degC, 50-55degF) for half as long even though it doesn't seem to turn my skin red like the vest does. Its that added exposure around the groin, thighs and clavicle/neck area that makes it feel way colder for me.

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-29T12:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151128>

I only tolerate the cold water (10-12degC, 50-55degF) for half as long even though it doesn't seem to turn my skin red like the vest does. Its that added exposure around the groin, thighs and clavicle/neck area that makes it feel way colder for me.

I had to break through the ice and wait til I could chip it off the sides of the bath to be able to lean back this morning. Water temp 0.4c. Got DH to spot as I haven't CT 'd at that temp before. He made me get out at 7 minutes, I can see how easy it would be to overdo it, it's nicer than at 10c, perhaps because I get numb quicker. Also clear blue skies ... Some photons ... I huddled up, the clavicle/neck area was submerged and unprotected, hopefully some good signalling, but didn't want to risk the inner thigh and armpits because of the low temp and risk of hypothermia. I've got a feeling that in my own n=1 that below 5c is a different ball game about intensity, duration and which areas to protect ....

7 minutes is the max i have been at freezing water temps..... no spotter either.. in the river. and that was a bit too much i recognized later... for me. ### Sue-UK — 2014-12-30T18:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151188>

Did a second session lunchtime yesterday, 10 minutes at 2.4C, then 9 minutes at 0.9C this morning after breaking the ice again, 20 minutes at 3.6 at lunchtime. I start going pink as soon as I get out at those temperatures, but don't really notice much pinkness whilst I'm in there.

Last night the temperature dropped generally but I woke up at 3 a.m and had to throw the bedclothes off to cool down before going back to sleep. Not like a hot flush, hoping its better reading of the energy status of the body kicking in..... It felt positive anyway.

### nicld — 2014-12-30T18:28:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151190>

I too only sleep with light jammies and a sheet or really light blanket on and the bedroom nice and cold or as my hubby puts it "cold as hell". 2 years ago I sometimes had 3 blankets on and the house at 72F.

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-31T10:44:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151222>

I've been working on a graph for my CT. Going upwards in degrees C, 0 to 10, and across the page minutes from 0 to 40 in 5 minute segments. I've then drawn a diagonal line from 0C/0 minutes up to 10C/40 minutes plus. At the lower end of the temp scale I've got a rough idea of how long I should stay in, based on my experience so far, so I won't need a spotter. I can add or subtract a minute or two based on the weather conditions, particularly wind chill  and when it's clear blue skies and sunshine .

### nicld — 2014-12-31T16:02:38+0200

### Optimalbound — 2014-12-31T17:47:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151245>

A chat with Jack last week reminded me that I had allowed myself to get lazy with my CTing, and gave me added motivation to end my several months of CT hiatus. So now I'm finally starting to get my act together and embracing the cold again!

Yesterday, Dec. 30th: Cold tub, 50 min. @ 46 ºF
Today, Dec. 31th: Ocean, 9 min. @ 42 ºF

Didn't bring neoprene gloves or boots today, so I chose to call it quits after only 9 minutes because I started to lose feeling in my fingers a bit, and since getting out of the water involved climbing a ladder, I decided to play it safe and quit early. 42 ºF is my coldest CT so far and I'm surprised at how easy it was to do, considering that I don't think I'm properly cold adapted at the moment. If not for my hands and feet, I'm pretty sure I would have been perfectly comfortable to stay in much longer. After the first 10 seconds it felt great!

### Sue-UK — 2014-12-31T18:21:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151249>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=151144)
>
> 7 minutes is the max i have been at freezing water temps..... no spotter either.. in the river. and that was a bit too much i recognized later... for me. I felt a bit weird and like my heart kind of freezed...uh. I do not have much body fat tho, so that might be a reason i cant do too long at those temps.. at least not without slowly increasing the time. My rain barrel have ice on the top of it these days. I break it and jump in nude for a minute or so. I love it. Always makes me feel so great after!

I read an online article ages ago about the adrenal glands, and it talked about adrenal related muscles. There are apparently 5 skeletal muscles related to adrenal gland function, (Leg muscles: sartorius, posterior tibialis, gastrocnemius, and soleus, inner thigh: gracilis). I’ve been wondering if having those muscles exposed for 7 minutes in the icy cold river would cause signals of thermal stress, whereas a minute or so in the rain barrel would be more of a positive thermal challenge signal. To get my torso under the water, my legs stick out of the short step bath I have, just the backs of my calves are dipping in the water, but I can only tolerate the legs and feet fully under very icy water for about 30 seconds which I do by sitting upright, putting my legs and feet under and counting, just before I get out. Its the most difficult bit of the session.

### SeaHorse — 2014-12-31T19:17:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151252>

What has been working for me in the pond this winter is 4 to 5 minutes at 33degrees…I'm wearing wool socks and neoprene booties or felt booties… I sit on the pond bottom which actually feels warm and soft on the back of my legs and the water is up to my neck. I keep my hands out, but try to have all my arms submerged. This feel great every other day….particularly with the sun out…then I feel I could stay in longer but I don't. I usually have a cold shower after and a few minutes in the tanning machine. Odd days I use cold baths only and some time in the steam sauna. I feel like I live at a spa, but none of my friends agree…it's all too hot or too cold for them. (The farmhouse we live in was upgraded in the 70s with a hot tub and sauna. I bought a tanning machine a year and a half ago and turned the hot tub into a cold tub for indoor CT)

Keeping the charge is a challenge…my goal this year is to try for everyday outdoor CT..still as I've said before, each winter changes things up and this winter is a vast improvement on last.

I agree Sue, that the stress vs positive thermal challenge is a big deal…..I'm experiencing only good stuff now whereas before I would get core temp. drops, reynauds and shivering that would last for hours. It was something I had to experiment with, but also served as very good markers for what was going on. It was still a necessary step in the right direction, but the feedback wasn't working as well.

I've stopped wearing a watch and taking the pond's temperature….I'm not much of a chart and graph gal like you two, so I just wing it and pay attention to sensation. Sometimes I count seconds to get a sense of time passing, but my internal clock tells me to get out at 5 minutes most days.

### Inger — 2014-12-31T21:08:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151258>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=151249)
>
> I read an online article ages ago about the adrenal glands, and it talked about adrenal related muscles. There are apparently 5 skeletal muscles related to adrenal gland function, (Leg muscles: sartorius, posterior tibialis, gastrocnemius, and soleus, inner thigh: gracilis). I’ve been wondering if having those muscles exposed for 7 minutes in the icy cold river would cause signals of thermal stress, whereas a minute or so in the rain barrel would be more of a positive thermal challenge signal. To get my torso under the water, my legs stick out of the short step bath I have, just the backs of my calves are dipping in the water, but I can only tolerate the legs and feet fully under very icy water for about 30 seconds which I do by sitting upright, putting my legs and feet under and counting, just before I get out. Its the most difficult bit of the session.

well... it definitely felt like thermal stress... in the river the water is flowing too so it makes it even colder.....
I am not cold after my minute dips in the icy barrel at all. Very comfortable. Funny that you feel it so much harder to keep feet and legs in! I use no socks or gloves or hat so far in my barrel this year but I think I will start using them and stay a little longer instead......
I am for sure no CT queen (but intend to ramp it up in the new year!) but still get shocked how cold most people seems to be in the night.... heating on... pajamas.. socks.... thick covers.....
oh man I get so warm in the night I often have to air my feet and shoulders and arms outside the covers and my sheet is light too.......
if I had a huge dinner/lots of food during the day I get super hot in the night BTW. Like all the food goes to heat. Uh! I have been doing BPC with lots of grassfed butter for breakfast and just a about 3-4 hours feeding window from 2-5 PM when I mostly eat around 500 g raw seafood and it feels great  way more fat and less protein lately. I do MSM too and my hair has stopped falling out like mad
I will continue this practice and see what happens.... + increased CT...... sleep was crap latest weeks as I was by the boyfriend and decided to stay up with him and man I felt like a alien... never knew sleep is so important to me........ but it is........ It was like I lost my mind and thoughts went crazy and oh man it was no fun to be honest. Little sleep, late nights + some alcohol.... they just kill my mood.

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-01T11:59:28+0200

My sense of time is off I think, I can sometimes be out in the tub and think that several minutes have passed and its only been a minute, others times I can look at the clock and find I've been in longer than I thought. It's almost as if my internal clock works at different speeds on different days, so it will be interesting to see if that improves.

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-02T10:28:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151361>

The cold snap has gone, the water was back up to 10.4c yesterday, but there was a strong wind and thick black clouds. Prefer the much colder water with no wind, clear blue sky and some winter sun. Maybe its the adrenal related lower leg muscles sticking out of the bath picking up the environmental signals from the weather that affects my tolerance of the cold.

I woke up 3 times in the night because I was too hot, just threw off the covers for a bit, drank some water and then settled straight back to sleep. I dont feel it disrupted my sleep much because each time I woke I remembered what I had been dreaming about. Thinking I just need to keep going during the transition of my internal thermostat resetting itself ..

### nicld — 2015-01-02T17:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151386>

Going to try for 2 sessions for the next 3 days. First ones at 2 hours at 45F (7.2C) and then a short one at a temp of 33F (.5 C) thanks to snow.

### nicld — 2015-01-05T01:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151633>

So we are having a bone chilling deep freeze going on. Windchills up to -30F tonight. I am ok with that but my fingers don't like it so much as I needed to do some shoveling and they got stiff and cold. But the good news is that they did not turn white like they have in the past. Other good news is that I will have snow to do short sessions of deep CT (34F water temp) bad news, is that my water gets all dirty almost like doing CT in a lake. Oh well,

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-05T20:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-151708>

Water was 7.1C this morning, but it was overcast with cold air, not in a nice way like when there is photons up for grabs ...... As I was about to go out this morning I saw a black cat across the field and thought it was our cat Fred. Had I stayed indoors the cat across the field would have been Fred in my reality ..... it was only by going outside to do CT did I see that Fred was sat on the outside table glaring at the imposter Fred ..... So I finish up sat in the cold water thinking about Schrodinger's cat, collapsing waveforms ...... and then it was about whether my reading about the sun, watching TV programs about it, thinking about it when I was doing CT meant I was becoming entangled with the sun's consciousness..... .

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-08T11:25:53+0200

Update: Inner thigh massively improved, legs look more normal now. Just need to keep going!  Under the belly where its usually warm because I'm curled up is definitely reducing. The girls aren't shrinking (is that because I'm not losing much heat from the armpit?) it seems to be shrinking down the sides, (side fat at waist almost gone).

I decided this morning that because it was pouring with rain I would just stand in the water up to the knees for a minute or so to "challenge" the lower leg "adrenal" muscles. I did that and then thought why not just CT as usual in the pouring rain ..... Really enjoyed it ...

### Butters — 2015-01-08T21:10:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-152142>

I also think that too much food goes into heat. I ramped up my food intake and my body answered with enormous heat production at night. And it happens instantanously after big meals. I have no cold tub anymore so far  thats not good. I have to do it with my CFB.
When its April I'll have a tub but than its getting warmer outside. Now temperatures in germany are between 0-10°C, so its very warm here  . No winter so far
I am excited about the next winter, because when I improve my heat production it will be freaky.

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=152142)
>
> I also think that too much food goes into heat. I ramped up my food intake and my body answered with enormous heat production at night. And it happens instantanously after big meals. I have no cold tub anymore so far  thats not good. I have to do it with my CFB.
> When its April I'll have a tub but than its getting warmer outside. Now temperatures in germany are between 0-10°C, so its very warm here  . No winter so far
> I am excited about the next winter, because when I improve my heat production it will be freaky.

For what it's worth, I'd take the heat dumping/production as a good sign.. to me it means that your body is under the impression that it is operating efficiently enough to not need to store, so just burns the extra.

### SeaHorse — 2015-01-11T13:08:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-152394>

What kind of sauna??

### nicld — 2015-01-11T18:22:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-152411>

> [David Limacher said:](/goto/post?id=152369)
>
> Which CT Tub to pick? Keep in mind the -20\*C temps...wondering if it'll freeze solid?
> [http://peaveymartonline.com/index.php?route=product/category&path=61\_92](http://peaveymartonline.com/index.php?route=product/category&path=61_92)
>
> Could I wrap heat tracing around it to keep from freezing? Or would that have an undesirable effect on the water?
>
> Or is this just something I would do seasonally. I like cold showers but I really want to just lay in there......for hours....

Yes, eventually a stock tank will freeze solid. You can get one of these to put in it to keep it from freezing but your electric bill will jump. [http://www.amazon.com/Allied-Precis...F8&qid=1420993130&sr=8-6&keywords=tank+heater](http://www.amazon.com/Allied-Precision-15N-1500-Watt-Sinking/dp/B000BD8JAE/ref=sr_1_6?ie=UTF8&qid=1420993130&sr=8-6&keywords=tank+heater)

When we had animal this is what we had in the tank. Now that we don't the electric bill has dropped greatly this winter but we had it running all of the time. I am sure that if you hook it up to a timer it would not be so bad.

### fitness@home — 2015-01-12T18:48:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-152557>

I was in the grocery store yesterday and saw an employee wheeling one these thru the store with it full of ice. Light bulb moment - instantly thought portable CT tub . They are available in insulated models.

[http://www.globalindustrial.com/p/storage/Display-Store-Fixtures/grocery-displays-accessories/gto-ice-bkice-tub-49-12l-x-25-12w-x-31-14h-abs-galvanized](http://www.globalindustrial.com/p/storage/Display-Store-Fixtures/grocery-displays-accessories/gto-ice-bkice-tub-49-12l-x-25-12w-x-31-14h-abs-galvanized)

**Galvanized Tubs Are Used For Storing And Displaying Grocery Produce As Well As Cold Beverages.**

Galvanized Tubs are made with a heavy duty rust-resistant ABS plastic liner for long lasting durability. Features a **built-in reservoir and drain system for ease of use**. Insulated liner reduces ice melt for increased reliability. Includes casters for easy mobility.

### nicld — 2015-01-18T02:20:46+0200

Ahhhh, 23 minutes of deep CT 34F and I look like a lobster, LOVE IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!! No shivering at all. I am pretty sure that the over head heater that I am using to get the fournier effect helps. That was on top of the 2+ hours at 47F earlier. I love the weekends.

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-18T11:11:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153236>

I did 10 mins at 35f yesterday. Cloudy so missed my overhead heater . A bit of warmth overhead makes such a difference....

Fell down a step carrying a piece of furniture later and twisted my ankle , not sure if i'll manage to get in the bath today, but hoping DH will help me give it a go as it should help .... He was at the auction yesterday buying my second outside CT bath . It's a walk in, seated fibreglass one, cost £10. Hoping that because its not metal, lined with fibreglass, like the one I've got, it won't retain heat so much in summer. Gotta decide now where to install it....

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-19T10:50:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153312>

Water was 38.7 f yesterday, did 30 minutes. Was helped in at 10 a.m and then he popped back every few minutes to see if I was ready to come out. Kept my feet out apart from regular 30 second immersions. The sun was lovely, makes such a difference to my cold water tolerance, and being out over 20 minutes seems to recharge my battery better.... Made a big difference to my foot, I could wiggle my toes more, and take my weight on my heel.

### Terminator — 2015-01-19T17:29:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153348>

Is it ok to CT in municipal water that has chlorine, fluoride in it ?

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-19T22:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153361>

Did 10 minutes at 37f this morning, was expecting a visitor so couldn't stay in longer, but at lunchtime DH offered to go back to work a bit later so I could squeeze another short session in while the sun was out. I think its surprised him how quickly my foot is healing thanks to the CT. . I managed to get in and out on my own, so I can go out solo again now  and I went up on tiptoes this evening without thinking about it to get a glass out of a cupboard, and no pain .... I'm about 90% there.

### SeaHorse — 2015-01-19T23:05:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153367>

> [Terminator said:](/goto/post?id=153348)
>
> Is it ok to CT in municipal water that has chlorine, fluoride in it ?

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-20T15:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153426>

Foot almost better, very slight twinge across the top if I point my toes, but I'm walking normally. I had the thought whilst in the tub this morning that the injury has shown me the healing potential of CT, but that without the injury other healing through CT would still be happening, it's just not as obvious. So I may not be losing body fat at the rate I want to, (hoping that will happen before I run out of winter....) but perhaps that's because more important stuff is going on first. Oh well, at least that thought kept me outside despite my least favourite environmental conditions, cold water, wind chill and heavy clouds.  Only did about 12 minutes, I'm getting to a stage where I can sense my muscles in their pre-shiver state IYKWIM. (Improving BAT activation?) I got out when I started to sense it, 3 hours later and the house is so cold I can still feel it. It feels ok though, not stressful.

### Sue-UK — 2015-01-26T14:02:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-153889>

A good few days of CT.... One morning the water was 0.5C so only did 10 minutes, this morning the water was 8.3C with fairly strong winds so did 32 minutes, lots of cloud watching today. The clouds appeared to be racing towards, and being swallowed by the sun. Then the sun shone in a clear blue sky, so I positioned myself in the bath for part of the time so that I was half submerged, half sunbathing.

I was out late on Saturday night, champagne, wine, eating late, dessert, wedding cake ..... in my defence I did have a seafood starter  What surprised me was that despite all that and not getting to bed until 2 a.m. I was up, breakfast and in the CT tub (3.6C) just a bit later than usual. A year ago the same situation would have triggered hunger, carb cravings, wiped me out of energy for a couple of days, and you wouldn't have found me anywhere near cold water ....  So I'm counting having a great night out and the realisation afterwards that things are moving in the right direction as a result

### QuantumRadical — 2015-02-05T20:43:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-154845>

> [Terminator said:](/goto/post?id=153348)
>
> Is it ok to CT in municipal water that has chlorine, fluoride in it ?

Very good question. What is the tradespace here? If you are already exposed to less than optimal water, is sitting in the same water more less doing damage versus the benefit of getting cold. A cold soak in the tub is very efficient from a CT perspective--covers a lot of skin all at once. What is the absorption penalty of sitting in this type of water versus the benefits of the cold. Countervailing? I wonder if anyone has any real scientific data on this versus this community making a declaration. I get in the cold tub and I add in Hydrogen peroxide, epsom salt, magnesium sulfate flakes, and baking soda. I have no scientific evidence if this is beneficial or not. But I do it. But I do know that the cold water is the bulk of the benefit, like on the order of 99.999%. In the summer, I use a small pump and aquarium cooler to cool the water. I am sure this isn't the best water, but the cold is still cold doing its thing.

If anybody has real research on this kind of stuff, I'd be eager to read it. We live in a very contaminated world already, so we need effective antidotes. I believe, and what Jack has said about CT, that cold trumps a lot of contamination. Just my thoughts.

### QuantumRadical — 2015-02-05T20:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-154846>

But I could be totally wrong. No hard evidence or data.

### nonchalant — 2015-02-05T22:51:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-154853>

I CT'd in fluoridated water for a long time. But once I got an outdoors tub with well water, I became reluctant to CT in city water. But I think CT in fluoridated water is beneficial.

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-06T16:23:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-154903>

I started going down with a cold on Monday afternoon, but decided to carry on CT as normal. Then I noticed that if I drank cold water the cold symptoms subsided, and with that and the CT I was comfortable and the cold didn't go to my head like it normally would. At one point I didn't bother with a glass, I just drank from the litre jug, the more I drank the better I felt. The problem was night, after 4 hours of not drinking because of sleep, I started to relapse, and it took a while of lost sleep to get back on track, probably because I was more concerned about not getting up to pee than how much I drank. On Tuesday night each time I woke up I drank a whole glass of water instead of the usual mouthful or two. Wednesday morning I easily did CT at 0.5c for 20 minutes after breaking the ice. Wednesday night when I woke up dry and coughing I drank even more water, and CT at 1.3c in the morning was fine. I kept it to 10 minutes as I was going to work for four hours in a basement at 9c. Last night I drank until the symptoms disappeared and then drank even more ... I got a sensation of my lips being plump and hydrated, when I next woke up the sensation had gone so I drank until the sensation came back. Went through 2 litres ... This morning did 15 minutes CT at 0.6c, again breaking the ice. Now concentrating on what my lips feel like to make sure I am better hydrated.

### nicld — 2015-02-09T18:27:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155142>

Managed to do 4 hours of regular CT this weekend (46-47F) and one 20 minute session of deep CT (34F). I think I rewarmed faster with the deep CT than the long ones. Something so invigorating about sitting in snow water and turning bright cherry pink. I have to admit though, I have not been wanting to do CT during the week. The HCG is making me a bit colder and as the evening goes on, I am naturally getting colder so wanting to take a cold bath before bed is not my idea of fun, but I need to start adding back at least one night a week. Our snowfall this year has been poor so I need to get as much deep CT as I can before I have no snow.

### nonchalant — 2015-02-10T04:07:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155177>
