I have been paying attention to my lips in the last few days now, and I see what you mean. I have spent most of my life, since I was quite young, just covering my chapped lips with balm. At one point I noticed that I was using lip gunk about once per hour! Now instead of reaching for the lipstick, I reach for the water. Thanks for the tip, Sue!

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-10T12:10:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155203>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=155142)
>
> Managed to do 4 hours of regular CT this weekend (46-47F) and one 20 minute session of deep CT (34F). I think I rewarmed faster with the deep CT than the long ones. Something so invigorating about sitting in snow water and turning bright cherry pink. I have to admit though, I have not been wanting to do CT during the week. The HCG is making me a bit colder and as the evening goes on, I am naturally getting colder so wanting to take a cold bath before bed is not my idea of fun, but I need to start adding back at least one night a week. Our snowfall this year has been poor so I need to get as much deep CT as I can before I have no snow.

Yesterday it was foggy first thing so I waited til lunchtime before CTing in glorious sunshine, blue skies. Couldn't measure the water temp as I've managed to break my thermometer changing the battery ... decided it was a sign to give that a rest and just go with my gut as to when it was enough. But I was sat there listening to the birds and getting some photons, and it suddenly struck me that spring is here .... and Sally's fab pic in the Cortisol thread shows me I haven't done enough to take full advantage of the winter cold lane .....  I don't count calories, or think about macronutrient ratios, or measure myself, get labs etc and I only weigh myself every three of 4 months, but don't record it, just watch the trend .... I have only had any interest in measuring the water temperature and timing a session, so as I'm left with timing the session I might just drop that for a few days too .....  Reread CT6 and " _This means that a cold environment (skin temps 50-55 degrees) selects for heat production no matter what the light cycles or the thyroid status says it is on any hormone panel"_ caught my attention. I've ordered a skin thermometer .... on days I am home I am going to do a bit of measuring after CT and for a while afterwards, and when the skin temp starts creeping up to 55F I'm going to either leap back into the tub for a few more minutes, or hit the cold shower. Time to see what temperature the skin receptors are observing that I can't sense...... and try keeping them in the cold lane all day

>
> Managed to do 4 hours of regular CT this weekend (46-47F) and one 20 minute session of deep CT (34F). I think I rewarmed faster with the deep CT than the long ones. Something so invigorating about sitting in snow water and turning bright cherry pink. I have to admit though, I have not been wanting to do CT during the week. The HCG is making me a bit colder and as the evening goes on, I am naturally getting colder so wanting to take a cold bath before bed is not my idea of fun, but I need to start adding back at least one night a week. Our snowfall this year has been poor so I need to get as much deep CT as I can before I have no snow.

^^^^
If I did this amount of time an teperature CT, I would be an olympic level athlete ! lol
Between being lean, slim naturally and being a puss, I don't see how it will happen but I wish I could do it !

### Terminator — 2015-02-10T18:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155248>

How much fat do you eat when you do long CT sessions ?

### nicld — 2015-02-10T19:30:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155255>

Right now, none as I am doing a round of HCG (no fat allowed). When I am having fat, I will have some salmon and BPC in the mornings but I specifically do not load up. I have no gall bladder so I have found that trying to eat all fat and trying to get into ketosis is not working for me right now. It has taken me quite a while to get to this length time and I do have an overhead heater that I use for the Fournier effect.

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-14T14:22:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155598>

There was high winds yesterday morning so I didn’t get out until later in the day. I was feeling a bit low, but 15 minutes turned that round. This morning the water was at 4.8C (new thermometer arrived) and I did 30 minutes with a couple of floating sessions for a couple of minutes to get some photons coming through the clouds. Took an hour to warm up afterwards.

In general I think I am getting pink quicker, as if my skin is responding to the temperature faster. To start with this was patchy, particularly on my legs, which when I dried off afterwards I would notice would look pink dappled with white patches. Now it is more consistent. Between CT’s my skin also seems a warmer colour, and softer, more fluid underneath, rather than hard and lumpy. Hunger definitely dropping.

I felt when I started CT that I would need to get rid of a lot of glycation before things would really have a chance to kick in (The _“What is biologic concrete?”_ blog was such an eye opener, and was one of the main reasons I haven’t got despondent about not losing weight faster). There’s been a lot of hard maple syrup for the environmental signals to get through, and for the cold to help clear out! It has taken months, but when I read back through the Biologic concrete blog this morning, I can see how far I’ve come, particularly as a post-menopausal female.
Happy CTing!

### fitness@home — 2015-02-17T23:30:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155934>

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-18T12:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155954>

Monday morning it was raining, but I just positioned myself with my feet and legs out and "rain bathed" there was still some photons to be had .... Yesterday morning the temperature was 3.1C but lovely blue skies and sunshine, so I positioned myself so that I could get some sun on the front of my body, but kept my spine under the water. Sometimes I just bobbed up and down as if I was floating in the sea, so that it was sun, cold water, sun, cold water .... just to give the receptors something to think about .....  Went back in just after lunch, water warmer (about 12C), almost went to get some ice, but decided to gather the photons again so just did the same as this morning, then spent the last few minutes sat with my feet under the water which I can't do at the colder temperatures for more than 30 seconds or so. Then I emptied the bath and refilled it from the well (its coming out at 8.4C at the moment). Walked barefoot round the field for a bit while it was filling, so probably spent one and a half hours outside yesterday grounding, getting some photons and CTing..... Went barefoot to get some well water and drank 2 large glasses straight away (hopefully whilst it was at its most energetic). I woke up at 2 am feeling very hot, not sweating though, threw off the covers for a few minutes, drank some water and then went back to sleep. This morning the water was 2.8C, colder wind, but blue skies again, so I did the same as yesterday. Hoping to get back in again later, keep the skin receptors on their toes

### SeaHorse — 2015-02-18T14:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155961>

We've been in a deep freeze mode here…sub-zero temps most days and two nights ago it was -27F and there were winds. In the midst of it all, one of my ewes had a little ewe lamb the day after Valentines (her name is Valentina). It was just sheer luck and instinct that brought me to the barn right as she was giving birth. With these temps. the race for the lamb to get dried off and nursing is always lost unless there is some help. They just can't get dry fast enough before the birth fluids freeze and they become chilled. But, I was there and the lamb and mom were able to bond while I started to dry her…even so, she began to get hypothermic and weak...then I had to milk some colostrum from the ewe, wrap the lamb in towels, put her inside my jacket and run up to the house. I tubed the milk into her, finished drying her and put her on my husband's lap for half and hour while I made her a little sweater from an old sweater sleeve.

Once she woke up and started bouncing, I rushed her back down to the barn and made sure she had some milk through the proper channels…we did rig up a heater because the barn was still around zero degrees. As a single she is getting the full benefit of her mother's attention and milk…she is doing wonderfully well.

All in all, a lesson in critical temperatures….given that my ram lives with the ewes and I don't try to time their breeding..,in this case it means little Valentina would not have made it outside a domestic situation. In most sheep raising areas of the world lambing is timed to come a month or two later and then things can proceed as nature intended.

Anyway….this has nothing much to do with CT except it is my reasons for not getting in the pond for the this week and being outside at all is pretty much of CT workout despite winter clothing And….
.[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/image-2-jpg.1424/) [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/image-3-jpg.1426/)

I wanted to post a picture….

### patgrif@hotmail.com — 2015-02-18T17:31:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-155964>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=155954)
>
> Monday morning it was raining, but I just positioned myself with my feet and legs out and "rain bathed" there was still some photons to be had .... Yesterday morning the temperature was 3.1C but lovely blue skies and sunshine, so I positioned myself so that I could get some sun on the front of my body, but kept my spine under the water. Sometimes I just bobbed up and down as if I was floating in the sea, so that it was sun, cold water, sun, cold water .... just to give the receptors something to think about .....  Went back in just after lunch, water warmer (about 12C), almost went to get some ice, but decided to gather the photons again so just did the same as this morning, then spent the last few minutes sat with my feet under the water which I can't do at the colder temperatures for more than 30 seconds or so. Then I emptied the bath and refilled it from the well (its coming out at 8.4C at the moment). Walked barefoot round the field for a bit while it was filling, so probably spent one and a half hours outside yesterday grounding, getting some photons and CTing..... Went barefoot to get some well water and drank 2 large glasses straight away (hopefully whilst it was at its most energetic). I woke up at 2 am feeling very hot, not sweating though, threw off the covers for a few minutes, drank some water and then went back to sleep. This morning the water was 2.8C, colder wind, but blue skies again, so I did the same as yesterday. Hoping to get back in again later, keep the skin receptors on their toes

Sue, the way you describe your 'version' of CT I can really relate to, I think I could say I very much approach it in a similar way. I like the "just to give the receptors something to think about ....."...............I find I can ONLY do CT with plenty of sunshine around! I live in LA so that's not an issue of course but I love playing off the contrasts of heat (sunshine) and cold. One seems to magnify the other, I like to 'get warm' plenty of sun and then go in the water and it's like I can FEEL everything then so intensely. I can play around like this for 4 or 5 hours sometimes. I just cant do CT if it is un-remitingly cold maybe one day if I become more 'cold adapted' but not now...............

I mentioned this once to Dr Kruse on a Q@A and he seemed to think that was fine even gave it a name "The Fournier Effect" which has something to do with the physics of heat and cold. The way I think of it is using the analogy of charging the battery or running current through the system, the cold reduces 'resistance' and the sun supplies the current or maybe better said 'voltage' so with a high current/voltage and the lower resistance you have the formula for very good current flow. Which is synonymous with being 'alive' good current flow means life.................

In that state I feel more 'alive' than anywhere/anytime else in my life right now I even use it to delve into my 'unconscious' sometimes I feel I have even stumbled on the 'royal road to the unconscious' to use Freud's phrase. He thought it was dreams by the way which though they can be very illuminating and interesting I prefer CT because to me I feel in a kind of 'dream like' state but the funny thing is I am wide AWAKE! Dreams in Freud's world are then 'interpreted' with CT I feel I don't need 'interpretation' I am figuring it all out for and by myself and it feels more real and authentic that running by some 'shrink'...............these days I think of the ocean as my shrink..............

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-20T16:21:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-156137>

> [patgrif@hotmail.com said:](/goto/post?id=155964)
>
> Sue, the way you describe your 'version' of CT I can really relate to, I think I could say I very much approach it in a similar way. I like the "just to give the receptors something to think about ....."...............I find I can ONLY do CT with plenty of sunshine around! I live in LA so that's not an issue of course but I love playing off the contrasts of heat (sunshine) and cold. One seems to magnify the other, I like to 'get warm' plenty of sun and then go in the water and it's like I can FEEL everything then so intensely. I can play around like this for 4 or 5 hours sometimes. I just cant do CT if it is un-remitingly cold maybe one day if I become more 'cold adapted' but not now...............
>
> I mentioned this once to Dr Kruse on a Q@A and he seemed to think that was fine even gave it a name "The Fournier Effect" which has something to do with the physics of hear and cold. The way I think of it is using the analogy of charging the battery or running current through the system, the cold reduces 'resistance' and the sun supplies the current or maybe better said 'voltage' so with a high current/voltage and the lower resistance you have the formula for very good current flow. Which is synonymous with being 'alive' good current flow means life......................

I don't think I "live in the cold lane" enough to say I'm properly cold adapted (other family members want the heating on etc ). I only do outside CT, and the British weather means I get an ever changing environment, so I am just working what I've got, i'm better adapted at getting into cold water, and certain weather, for varying periods of time dependent on the conditions. I've been intuitively drawn to other senses ([http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-senses.11361/)](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/the-senses.11361/), I think working with the environment I have is part of my "metabolic trap door" because timewise I can't manage long CT sessions, and I want my system trained to read and react to the environment second by second, as well as the other CT benefits.

This week I have had sessions in warmth, pouring rain, icy winds, under clear blue skies, thick grey clouds, with varying water temps. I went out a second time one day at lunchtime to CT, sunshine but cold wind, then sat and had my lunch outside. I was nude, cold from the CT, there was sun but it was still a cold wind, but I had hot soup ...... it was fine, a soup Fournier effect perhaps?  Went back out later that afternoon, the bath was now in the shadow of the building, warmer water but a colder wind so much tougher. I usually find later CTs more difficult to rewarm from but I was curious what a third session would be like with those conditions, and although I wouldn't have called it pleasurable at the time, the gratitude it had finished, and the realisation that I was fine afterwards were my reward. My mitochondria are responding faster ....

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-23T18:37:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-156395>

Yesterday the water was 2.2c, cloudy, with a biting cold wind, so I bobbed in and out of the water, only managed 11 minutes, really tough session. As soon as I came in and closed the door I felt great though. It was like the wind chill made it a combination CT and cryotherapy session, but the cryotherapy part was nowhere near as low a temperature as a cryotherapy chamber, so I wasn't at risk of frostbite. I dont like cold wind so it was more about it being a shock to the system, so today I've done two 15 minute CT sessions in the cold wind, stood in the cold wind to dry, and then spent time outside emptying and refilling the bath and getting my well water, barefoot and in a thin layer of clothes, cold packs on my stomach. Let's see what staying longer in the cold lane does for me

### Sue-UK — 2015-02-25T10:49:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-156565>

Wind still cold but did 20 minutes which was an improvement, then listened to the February webinar and Q&A. Realised from that I maybe shouldn't have skipped the face dunking part of CT, so decided to give it a go, filled the sink with cold water ..... And couldn't do it  My lips and the end of my nose got wet but I physically couldn't push my face any further in, it's the face first thing I think, having fallen face first into a lake as a child when I couldn't swim. I can splash my face with cold water, I can shower my face, if I was already in a swimming pool I would be able to go underwater (would hate it, but could do it), I have never dived into a pool, I always get in gradually, swimming isn't really my thing, but it could be a low hanging fruit if I can get past this ..... The face dunking challenge is on  maybe I'll try starting with warm water and work down the temperature, baby steps

### Sue-UK — 2015-03-02T12:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-157148>

My face dunking hasn't been going well  Got to a couple of seconds in warm water. On Saturday I was outside in the bath, and had pulled myself up tight to the end of the bath, legs out, trying to sit on the bottom of the bath rather than the step, it gets the water right up to just under my mouth. So then I thought I could probably keep like that but lean back and put the back of my head on the step, under the water, and just hold on to the sides. A sort of backward face dunk  Would have done it then except I'd had my hair done the night before So this morning I just went for it ..... Held my nose with one hand, held on to the side with the other .... Counted to ten (but on fast forward speed if I'm honest ) then came up .... It was definitely easier to do, less panic, but the pain .....The water was just too cold (4.3C) to do it like that without proper adaptation. It felt like my skull had shrunk, and I couldn't think straight. What I could do with is one of those hairdressing sinks that you lean back into to have your hair washed  but I will probably do a bit of indoor CT, lay back in the bath water to cover most of my scalp, using warmer temps to start.

### JMO — 2015-03-05T00:06:09+0200

### Sue-UK — 2015-03-07T15:59:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-157738>

Reading the ubiquination series …. And that led me back to CT. If CT or the dropping of temperature at night causes mitochondria to emit infra-red light, this would produce a bigger exclusion zone in the water surrounding the mitochondria....?. Thinking Gerald Pollack’s book, the EZ acts like a cohesive net, helping maintain structure and position, so I'm thinking that this "net" might also contribute to avoiding excessive ubiquination? (the mitochondria are more like submarines than boats, the exclusion zones will have the EZ net effects from all sides because of immersion?).

But I wondered if I CT when I’m not properly hydrated I won’t be getting the optimum effect of the EZ net? So last couple of days I have drunk 16oz cold water before getting into the (morning) bath and 16oz straight after, plus have upped my early morning consumption generally. I have already noticed that if I drink 2 liters of water over the course of the evening before going to bed, I don’t need to get up and pee in the night, even if I start getting the water down later than normal. With less water in the evening I would sometimes wake up and reach for the water, even if it didn’t take much to help me get back to sleep.

It feels like if I go to the watering hole  morning and evening and drink a lot, it works better than drinking the same volume spread out over the course of the day. Perhaps it’s about making sure that twice a day the cell tide is well in, rather than it being partially out a lot of the time. The experiment continues …..

### Inger — 2015-03-07T18:53:51+0200

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=157148)
>
> My face dunking hasn't been going well  Got to a couple of seconds in warm water. On Saturday I was outside in the bath, and had pulled myself up tight to the end of the bath, legs out, trying to sit on the bottom of the bath rather than the step, it gets the water right up to just under my mouth. So then I thought I could probably keep like that but lean back and put the back of my head on the step, under the water, and just hold on to the sides. A sort of backward face dunk  Would have done it then except I'd had my hair done the night before So this morning I just went for it ..... Held my nose with one hand, held on to the side with the other .... Counted to ten (but on fast forward speed if I'm honest ) then came up .... It was definitely easier to do, less panic, but the pain .....The water was just too cold (4.3C) to do it like that without proper adaptation. It felt like my skull had shrunk, and I couldn't think straight. What I could do with is one of those hairdressing sinks that you lean back into to have your hair washed  but I will probably do a bit of indoor CT, lay back in the bath water to cover most of my scalp, using warmer temps to start.

My water temps are the same like yours BTW

I am a wimp......  But the one- two minute makes me feel awesome too

### Sue-UK — 2015-03-10T16:23:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-158082>

Switching things around this week …… I’ve not used supplements for over a year, I haven’t worried about the slow pace as long as I was moving forward. I weigh the same as I did a year ago, (went up 5lb, then lost it slowly, and now fluctuate a pound either side of my starting weight). I rarely weigh myself, but in terms of glycation, body composition, energy levels etc I am pleased, but the trend is that the CT water is getting warmer…. And I’m gonna run out of winter before I lose the body fat I still need to lose.

The changes starting yesterday are that when I get up in the morning I take a glass of water with me and go outside and ground and get the natural sunlight for a few minutes., but I am also putting a dose of a liquid homeopathic remedy (for the hypothalamus) in the water, (seemed like the appropriate timing to take it.  I’ve ordered some better blue blocking glasses so I may add a second dose when they go on in the evening). I’m also using a homeopathic hydration remedy in all my water. I’ve resisted using them because it would be too easy take the remedies without doing the work IYKWIM (and my issues weren’t caused by lack of X or Y homeopathic remedies …..). So I’m intending to use them as an add on, not a replacement, now that I’ve made the lifestyle changes first.

I am going to continue to CT in the morning (starting anytime between 8 and 9.30) but now in a fasted state, and then not eat until lunch. (Thinking the ubiquination blogs, my CT is my exercise at the moment). I drink 16 oz. of water before CT and 16oz after. (I know it would probably be better to eat breakfast and do CT later in the day, but I don’t make the excuses not to CT if I do it early). Yesterday my CT tolerance was a bit less, (got out 5 minutes earlier than usual), but it was a cold windy day. I got out really thirsty, not hungry like I normally feel if I don’t eat breakfast first. I drank 6 liters of water over the course of the day, and still didn’t need to get up in the night to pee. Woke once, drank about 4 oz. of water and then went straight back to sleep. This morning managed the normal CT time comfortably, but there was no wind, and it was blue skies and photons, I am not as thirsty as yesterday, maybe a bit more than usual. Both days I’ve stayed colder for longer after the CT, but I’m not shivering, it’s manageable, and I’m not hungry. It’s almost as if I am staying in the cold lane until I eat my lunch. I am going to be doing 2 meals within a 6 hour window, with seafood at both, either as the main meal or as a starter before a meat meal (to keep me off the carbs).

That’s phase 1 plan for this week……. Going to see how it goes before adding in phase 2 ….

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

(canal d'ille et rance)

It's the first time I did that. And.. How come in 20+years here i've never done that?? dummy!!
It's one of those things you do it and then tell yourself you should have been doing it all your life.
While I was doing it time seems to have slowed down, it's crazy. I think I was doing things right. nice water(better than tap water for sure), grounded, the sun shining, cold. I've got a high with it.
It was colder than the tub I think, but for some reason it was much easier getting in, staying in.. more primal, full body and instead of shivering you swim and shiver from time to time.
I'll tell you.. it's much better than the bathtub and only a 5 min walk away.

My only potential concern is pathogens really. But I think it's an overblown fear spread by media and our current fear-based culture. The water is cold anyway, so less pathogens and a better immune system should compensate. Worst case.. that's the real purpose of antibiotics.

### Sue-UK — 2015-03-20T19:10:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-159061>

It was the solar eclipse this morning ……. Because of getting to work I opted to CT outside for 20 minutes before the eclipse was due, and at least spend 10 minutes during it. But when I went out it was thick grey slow moving cloud, there was just one tiny bit of clearer sky in the distance. I wasn’t going to see the eclipse, so I decided to use my other senses to “feel” it, and register when it got even darker than the cloud cover was already making it. I then decided that even if I wouldn’t see it, when it was time I would look up in the direction of where the moon would be crossing, and just “tune in” using other senses. ……

At the right time I looked up ….. and the one tiny clearer patch of sky was crossing over where the eclipse was ………. I had an amazing glimpse of the event, the moving cloud conditions and the eclipse position were really comfortable on my eyes, it was more like looking at an eclipse over a distant star in the night sky, or as if the remaining cloud was acting like blue blocking sunglasses….. It felt like such an unexpected gift from the sky, it took my breath away. The clearer patch moved on and there was just thick grey cloud, but every so often the grey seemed to thin just enough to reveal the eclipse. I got so cold I finally had to get out of the bath, and just stood there shivering, grounding and catching a few more glimpses. It felt like a dream state, I was out there so long DH even came out with a cup of tea, just in time to see the sky offer one last glimpse. Then the sky started to clear as the clouds moved across, I was late and it was no longer safe to look in the direction, the sky was taking its blue blockers off as the moon moved past. Far from spoiling it, the dark grey skies made it an amazing experience, my favourite CT yet.

### thomas — 2015-04-02T15:46:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-160131>

So I've been taking some body measurments with cold baths.. In celsius obviously..
Water temp: 12 C

Before the bath: 37.3 C
Few minutes after getting in: 37.7 C (wtf)
Around 10 minutes or so: 37.3 C (back to zero yeah).
About 20 minutes 37.0 C (around)

I usually have a hard time staying more than 20 minutes, as the shivering gets more intense and uncontrollable, and so does the cold sensation. I can probably stay longer if I put my all my focus into breathing deeply and relaxing all my muscles.

Few minutes after the bath: 35.1 C.

Why a sudden drop like that only after??
Dunno

If anyone's got data and explanation share..

Yesterday, I did my first ocean CT of the year. Still only about 45F here, so I kept it short at 10 min.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-12T22:14:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-167855>

I'm on a roll now...six days in a row. Much simpler now that I have a CT tank that is ready and chilled for me.

One thing I've noted is that CTing inside is much different than outside. When outside in sunshine I could easily submerge my body (and even my arms if I wear compression sleeves).

CT inside is harder. Getting in and submerging to my waist - - no problem. Lowering my upper body into the water is much _much_ harder. If it wasn't for the @#$!% mosquitoes the tank would be outside.

### fitness@home — 2015-06-14T00:49:45+0300

Much, much easier to settle down into the cold water. I think I'm on to something...

### jackware — 2015-07-22T19:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-172662>

For one week now I have only cold showered. On most days I'm using a 20 minute jog to warm my core and make the CT more of a refreshing experience than a torture. I can say I'm feeling a really clear and alive feeling in my mind over the last few days. My sleep has been very good as well, but it usually always is. Still eating one seafood meal a day, and portion sizes have been much easier to keep under control. I'm just not as ravenous for carbs as I used to be. Natural sunlight and grounding is rounding out my daily tasks. I'm also starting to feel that CT craving. I can't wait for it.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-16T06:00:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-179636>

Getting back into the CT swing. I've gone up and soaked in the river in the middle of the volcanic caldera several times in the past couple weeks. This past week: Monday, swimming in the lake for over an hour at about 55-degree F temps, plus sitting on rocks in the water while hanging out. Wednesday and today, sitting in my grounded CT tub in the morning for about 20 minutes each time. The tub is about 65 degrees at the moment as the temps have still been in the 70s during the day, but it's getting colder and I suspect soon it'll be frigid! As much as I prefer soaking in the natural cold spring water up in my favorite canyon, I'm still heebie-jeebied out by the damn water beetles that bite. I'm so stoked to have my own CT tub right out my bedroom door now! In the deep of winter I'll still make weekly treks up into the canyon because truly, nothing is as magical and spiritual as being in that canyon with eagles, owls and ravens as companions, the sun shining down to dry me off, and being totally grounded in the natural environment.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-10-18T04:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-179770>

Today's CT was bliss. The water was much colder today as temps are dipping. I sat in my CT tub with 50-degree temps for 30 minutes or so (didn't have a watch) reading a compelling book, which helps for sure. Then I went out on my back south-facing patio and sunned myself for an hour. Wow! Talk about morning spectabulousness. This was also combined with fresh salmon for breakfast. My cold tub in on the north side of my house, and I have ample access to sun on the south side. I now need to ensure that I work this routine in most days during the week.

Since my egg-related food sensitivity cataclysm, well, actually it was exactly 19 days into it that my ankles and feet swelled up like balloons...worse than anything I experienced in either pregnancy. CT, even when combined with serious grounding, didn't seem to be doing anything. Until today. Today, after the long soak and long sunlight harvesting, my right ankle is completely normal, and my left is nearly normal. For me, the edema seems to be somewhat hormonally related, but today, it actually felt like the week of cold and sun was what was making the difference.

I will definitely be working this into my daily routine.

### seanb4 — 2015-10-18T13:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-179777>

Been CTing using a bath for about 2 months now, daily 20-30 mins 10-12C. Recently temperature has dropped rapidly so just as I was starting to get used to it I have to re-adapt to colder temps.

Things I have noticed:
\-Baths way more effective than showers.
\-Tolerance depends on food intake. On days I haven't been able to take in much food I have a much harder time with CT...

### nicld — 2015-11-03T16:37:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-181067>

Did CT under the stars last night and it was awesome. I am SO glad that I had the yard light removed and could see that the sky was light up.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-08T03:37:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-181365>

Ice in the CT tub this morning after our first real snow on Wednesday and Thursday and then cold temps Friday. 1-inch thick on top. Brr! I've been doing a clay therapy treatment for the past several weeks, and today when I looked at my tub and the ice I almost couldn't muster up the courage. But I put on my mud treatment, went out in the morning sun for about 40 minutes, then soaked in the tank for about 30 minutes, and then back in the sun for another 45 minutes. My feet were SO freaking cold in the tank, (I don't have neoprene booties at the moment) but warming back up in the sun makes my feet instantly feel good. There isn't any issues like I've experienced when going from tank or springs to not sun. I like the sun warming process WAY more than anything. It's delicious. Like addictive delicious. I drained my tank today and I'm moving it to the south side of the house and yard as it was in my little north-facing courtyard. Because of the snow, it will get buried, so I'm moving it where I'll have unencumbered sun exposure for soaking through the winter.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-12T04:54:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-181729>

I moved my CT tank to the south side of the house on the patio, and got it all grounded with copper wire. Last night came in like a freaking ice dragon with snow and winds that were up to 60 mph gusts. I woke up this morning and saw the tank cover had blown away and went to retrieve it. It was crazy cold. The first real winter day of the season. But, by 10:30 am the winds had calmed, the sun was shining, and the CT tank was calling. I had overfilled the tank, so it was up almost to my shoulders. I leaned back, kickin' it in the sun, and it felt SO good. Ahhh...with nearly 3 full months of regular CT under my belt now, it's feeling so good. I'm loving the morning sun CT on bare skin with the post soak warm-up in the sun. Damn, it truly is amazing!

Cheers to more soaking!

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-12T04:55:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-181730>

Funny emoji for the 10:00 am! On 11:11 to be exact.

### Chrysanthemama — 2015-11-12T04:58:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-181731>

Oh! And the one thing I consciously did today that I'd never done before was make sure my wrists were in the water, even though I kept my hands out (usually do as I read a book), and I was astonished at the sensory input I was receiving with my wrists in the cold water. The sun was shining on them, too. Curious how that might relate to the Quantlet, but it felt intuitively, pretty good. I'll be more conscious of it in the future.

### thomas — 2015-11-21T19:38:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-182481>

> [Flawed said:](/goto/post?id=182467)
>
> Hey,
> It was a really cold november morning today and i went in the sea for 25 mins. When i came out my hands and feet were hurting. It went away after about 30mins but it wasn't fun. I feel great now due to the CT and the sun being out while i was doing it though.
>
> Would wearing thermal gloves/socks stop me from grounding while i CT?
> Any other methods for stopping hands/feet hurting?
>
> Thanks

I know that burning sensation.
For me: hands or feet getting numb = get out of the water.
You're likely staying too long. Shorter but more frequent cold exposures would be better.
Gloves and socks are a good idea yeah.(means you can stay longer..)
Sea water is a very good conductor so you're always grounding unless you put a rubber suit on... and even then it would have to be waterproof.

### lohd2015 — 2015-11-23T21:40:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-182542>

Hi All,
I am new here, and am really excited about all the knowledge Doc is so generously sharing.

Have a question: Does staying in 60-65 deg Fah room temperature all day (6 to 8 hours) in t-shirt and shorts do anything?

I have had very low redox potential all my life, just starting to improve in the past two years with ketogenic diet (mostly coconut oil, low protein, less than 20 g carbs from fruits/veggies) and Lugol's 2% iodine (about 16 drops, plus another 15-20 drops transdermal application with coconut oil).
Also started to sun 2 hrs in the morning (without clothes on for about 1 hour, temp about 50-55 without sun).
Thanks so much.

### freesia — 2015-11-25T22:09:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-182673>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=182671)
>
> Thank you seanb4 for your helpful input.
> I am reading about cold adaptation. I am very thin now, due to being on a ketogenic diet for over 2 years. Will retraining my body to be cold adapted make me even thinner? I am about 95 lbs at 5'4'', very small boned. I am hoping to swap some of my WAT with BAT, and not lose any more weight.

I didn't lose any weight but my body comp changed a bit.....developed muscles in my arms and lost cm's from the waist. Never did work out if the arm muscles might be due in part to hauling so much ice!
No one can predict exactly how you'll respond. The key points I think are to try it with a starting point that's going to challenge you but not crush you. then be consistent in moving forward with it even if you do so with the pace of a snail...

### lohd2015 — 2015-11-27T20:01:06+0200

Thank you freesia for the encouragement and the helpful info from your own experience. I am already feeling a big difference in how my body handles cold temperatures. I used to wake up feeling cold, now I actually feel some internal warmth in the morning. I am putting on less clothes when I go out. I also noticed I am more flexible and nimble. It could be the combo of sun and cold. It has been cloudy for the past two days, and I can feel a big difference. So just relying on the cold to keep me going.
Doc is truly God's gift to humanity, and I am in awe and totally grateful for being able to learn from him, and all of you on this forum.

### seanb4 — 2015-11-28T11:47:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-182765>

@[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/) @[freesia](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1614/)
I have gastroparesis so started at low body fat with poor caloric intake, didn't get any thinner and in fact gained weight. And freesia it's not from carrying the ice, I don't exercise AT ALL any more due to massive fatigue however my muscles have grown far bigger from CT in all areas of my body.

Started with 5 months daily shower and currently at 3 months almost daily baths. Baths are far more effective and I have noticed much greater adaption in this time.

Also since messing around with full spectrum and UV lights CT has again gotten far easier. I put this down to my body being able to create more of the hormones it needs thanks to UV and the overal increase energy / redox from IR and UV...

also I think I was massively missing out on IR so since adding that maybe it is stored in my cells and then used in CT. But I'm not sure on this.

### thomas — 2016-02-10T21:17:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-188711>

How I made CT'ing every day EASY...

I gotta admit these last few monthes I rarely ct'ed. I would do a brutal cold traumatising session and then not do it for a month...
So I did the opposite.
I used a temperature that's cool/cold but reasonably easy to get into and to stay. (18C)
I stay only for 10-15 minutes in the bath.(more would make me avoid it for the "I don't have time" bs).
Result: No problem doing it every day. I get all the usual mood/mental/sleep/libido boosts from CT. Doing that everyday is better than doing it once or twice a week longer and colder period.
The plan... To drop about 1degC a week, and stay a little longer maybe. Maybe in a year I'll do 5degC... who knows, Don' care. My goal is just to be addicted to CT then indulge in my addiction. And still do it in a 1000 days, 10000 days...

### Sascha Heid — 2016-02-23T16:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-189654>

Hello everybody!

I'm new here and it's only a week since i stopped thinking that Jack is batshit-crazy.
Anyways, i started to kinda do CT one week ago.
Since i run or cycle to work (it's only 3 miles) i simply switched to my sommer clothing. It's currently winter here in germany with temps between 2 and 5 degrees Celsius. In the beginning i used gloves and a cap but it was surprisingly not cold, so i ditched them. First time i was cold was when i ran 3 miles followed by 15 miles of cycling at 2 degrees Celsius, slight rain and lot's of wind.
But today i was honestly concerned.
I cycled to a friend. It was snowing at 1 degree celsius and so it was also wet. I had my lightest summer-clothes on with gloves and a cap. The gloves got wet, the shoes got wet, and because i lost my way it took me longer then expected, about 90 minutes.
When i arrived i was shivering uncontrollably, i could not stand, i was not able to talk, my heart raced and i breathed really quickly and strongly. I was totally f\*ed up. My Hands and feet were deep blue and 30 minutes into the hot bath i was still shivering strongly.
So i guess that was too much. Had it been only 45 minutes as it was planned i probably would have been fine, but this was something else.
Shit happens if you don't follow the protocol.

Not directly before...should I be? I get up, look at the sky, drink about 20 oz of iced RO lemon water, eat breakfast (usually seafood), have coffee (bulletproof or optimal) and then do my CT session. I like to CT early, gets it out of the way of the rest of my day.

### nonchalant — 2016-04-15T18:59:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-192767>

> [kathylu said:](/goto/post?id=192759)

> I like to CT early, gets it out of the way of the rest of my day.

Since CT increases alpha MSH, you are allowing the brain to control everything at the time. And since you are CTing early, that would mean your steroid hormones (which are created after the sunrise cortisol peak and before UV light starts) are being optimized. I think Jack mentioned that early morning is the best time for CT. Of course, CT later on would be awesome for dopamine and all the other solar hormones. And CT at night would be fabulous for leptin release/reception and melatonin creation from serotonin...

Great to hear from you, Kathylu!

### Optimalbound — 2016-05-18T19:50:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-195035>

Yup. Long overdue.
Today was overcast, rainy and really quite miserable. Not at all tempting for CT, so I had to use my old "baby steps" routine:

"Gee, today looks far too cold and miserable for CT, but I might as well go to the beach and have a look".
Drives to the beach and has a look.

"Ouch, that ocean really looks much too cold for me. And there's no sun, and that rain is really cold and pouring down. But now that I'm here, I might as well put my swimming trunks on".
Puts swimming trunks on.

"That sea still looks way too cold. But what did a wise man once tell me? 'When in doubt, CT.' Hmmm. Ah well, now that I'm here, I might as well wade along the beach for a few meters, at least."
Goes to the water and wades for a bit.

"That's really cold. But now that I'm here, I might as well try getting the water up to my thighs."
Walks a bit further out. And a bit further out.

"Well, both the sea and the rain feels really cold, but now that I have water up to my hips, I might as well try submerging up to my sternum."
Walks a bit further out.

"Well, now that I have water up to my chest, I might as well try submerging up to my neck."
Walks a bit further out.

"Well, now that I have water up to my neck, I might as well swim a little bit."
Swims a little bit.

"Hmmm. That wasn't so bad, actually. Well, now that I'm here, I might as well swim back and forth for a while."
Swims back and forth for 30 minutes.

At about the 25 minute mark, I activated my thermometer, and was a bit surprised to read 7 degrees C (45'F). Lower than I expected, although I did notice it in my fingers and toes towards the end. Being alone on the beach, I chose to end my swim after 30 minutes to be on the safe side.

### FNJORD — 2017-01-10T01:45:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-206470>

No shivering! I'm amazed actually. Saturday was my first day with the tub and after the same amount of time I was shivering on the couch for 3 hours.
Sunday was colder so I was not in it as long but went for walks after so no chance to shiver. The cold is getting easier VERY quickly, today I sunned my toes in the snow for 20 mins without a problem. I'm following keto BAB exactly, bought fish oils and taking vitamin D.

When I was a kid (prepubescent) I played in the snow a lot without wanting hat and mits and would always take off my coat, mum had to write a note to the teachers telling them I was allowed to take off my jacket if I wanted to. At 18 I worked for UPS in the middle of the night through the winter outside at the airport loading and unloading planes, after 3 months I could be out in -20, middle of the night with crazy winds in just a t-shirt. I'm looking forward to that robust feeling

I'm looking forward to that robust feeling

**bought fish oils and taking vitamin D**
\===================================

Definitely no fish or krill oils.
I prefer to first do fatty acids analysis and then decide on seafood consumption.

Vit D should be naturally made using sunlight.
Sun + food containing sulfur (garlic, onions, MSM)

We also need vit A
should be made naturally using sunlight and carotene (carrots, peppers)

Sunlight contains variety of frequencies, they depend on time of the day and latitude.
In average continental USA location.
UVa--vitA is available for most of the day
UVb--vitD is available for about two hours before solar noon and two hour afterward.

Currently, in winter, solar noon and clock noon are at about the same time.
best time for making vit D is 10AM-2PM
summer and fall comes daylight saving time, clock is pushed one hour ahead.
during daylight saving time the best time to make vit D is 11AM-3PM

\==================
If sun is not available, Red Sperti is good substitute.

Sun light have many frequencies that we utilize.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-176178)

### Inger — 2017-01-10T17:03:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-206511>

socks and a hat are just fine . I am not using them now as I always take a short minute dip in my barrel outside after my shower every morning. I go with the outside temps, now it is freezing temps or close to. I always dip and I let the temp be what it is. I am lazy..lol I stay naked and barefoot outside on the stone-terrace in the light for a minute or more always after my dip. When it is sunny I stay a bit longer and warm up naked in the winter sun. This is a very short procedure but I feel it gives me sooo much! As I do it every single day. I do not shiver at all, it is too short for that.
For me it is better to do something quick and easy regularly. That means I will always do it . I crave it. It is the most beautiful moment of the day, and it has no pain or suffering either included, only joy

### Inger — 2017-01-10T17:09:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-206512>

Yeah... working way late in fake light.. drinking way too much wine in the evening.. and even eating Christmas chocolate a few times... I never do this!  Still I survived and I am very fast back on track, non issues, and enjoying the sweet well-being feeling I get when I go for my walks and after/during my rain barrel dips. I did not get sick once either....so far this winter. And I had many 11 hour shifts with just one 15 minute break to eat. I can still do 3 pullups with zero exercise... For a woman I think this is quite good! Even men I know that do exercise cannot do any more than that.. so I guess I must do something right

I eat lots and lots of seafood...it is my staple food to be honest
and I always sleep with window wide open, also in freezing temps, and get all the fresh air on my face all night long... I am addicted to it

### nija — 2017-03-23T00:38:18+0200

I've been doing ice baths now for about 2 weeks. One week I did face dunk in ice water .While doing the CT , it is manageable for me .
Now I start shivering almost immediately afterwards for long time . I tried 60-65F and 30 min but my body remain too cold and shivering for long time . I switch on d heater and wear warm loth socks but still feel very cold afterwards . I drink ice water first and a fatty meal and I do you put clothes on afterwards.

Any ideas on what's going on?

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-23T09:43:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-210914>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=210908)
>
> I've been doing ice baths now for about 2 weeks. One week I did face dunk in ice water .While doing the CT , it is manageable for me .
> Now I start shivering almost immediately afterwards for long time . I tried 60-65F and 30 min but my body remain too cold and shivering for long time . I switch on d heater and wear warm loth socks but still feel very cold afterwards . I drink ice water first and a fatty meal and I do you put clothes on afterwards.
> Any ideas on what's going on?

I'm the woman who once had to get through an important phone meeting so cold I could barely think, and trying to stop my teeth chattering. Maybe my hindsight is better than my foresight ... but you might find something from my experience.
My body is working with the respiratory protein capacity it has now. After starting CT as I improved I found that I started generating heat earlier, but it took time. If what I was doing was making me feel miserable, in hindsight I'd try taking it back a notch. 15 minutes (or less) that stimulates what I want could be a better choice than 30 minutes. If that is comfortable add a couple of minutes, or take the temperature down a notch. It might also be that my body was struggling trying to raise the temperature of iced water, and deal with the stimulus on the skin at the same time. Maybe just try drinking the water cold, but not iced for a few days. If its a hassle with using blocks of ice to get to 60-65 degrees its tempting to stick it out to make the most of the effort, but 15 minutes in that and a couple of 10 minute dunks in the water when the ice has melted can still give a stimulus and take out some inflammation. I've also found that getting dressed straight away makes me stay colder far longer, so worth trying to stay naked afterwards for longer. Some days I cope with CT much better than others, what was easy yesterday might be beyond me today. Better to get out too soon than stay in too long. Listen to your body. A quick CT session and then naked in even a winter sun is amazing .... I find it much more powerful than freezing my arse off for longer in the dark or in dismal weather.

Have fun exploring your own CT N=1.

### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-24T06:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-214551>

Would like some clarification regarding certain aspects of the CT protocol

1\. Why is a compression shirt necessary? What roll does it play?
2\. Why do you have to have a fat containing meal before CT?
3\. What is the purpose of drinking ice cold water before CT?
4\. Is it necessary to submerge in a tub or pool when you only need spot treatment around your abdomen?

### drezy — 2017-05-24T19:29:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-214598>

> [Dennis Durost said:](/goto/post?id=214551)
>
> Would like some clarification regarding certain aspects of the CT protocol
>
> 1\. Why is a compression shirt necessary? What roll does it play?
> 2\. Why do you have to have a fat containing meal before CT?
> 3\. What is the purpose of drinking ice cold water before CT?
> 4\. Is it necessary to submerge in a tub or pool when you only need spot treatment around your abdomen?

1.) From [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-easy-start-guide/) : It's part of a slow cold adaption. Some people are in conditions that make them so un-adapted to cold that they get cold urticaria.

2.) I'll leave this to the pros since I regularly eat fatty meals and stopped thinking about it.

3.) When you get out of a CT bath you will have to urinate enough that race horses within earshot will start look for the gates. Also I suspect it's part of prepping your vagus nerve (like splashing the face with cold water). I couldn't come up with any good horse imagery on that one. Sorry.

4.) cautionary answer:
With all due respect beware the following statement "only need spot treatment around your abdomen". While that may be true, don't limit yourself before trying this out. The fat cells in your abdomen weren't sitting around in an otherwise optimized body and then decided they and they alone were just going to go wild without receiving any signaling.
I very much recommend building up to full CT to see if your N=1 receives any serendipitous (at least relative to your current perspective) benefits. I sure did. Like you I went in targeting just weight loss. I came out with perfect sleep, higher libido, knees that don't pop, better cognition, and increased sense of drive to name a few. All of that is secondary to the best benefit for me so far. My wife, long time insomnia suffer, now sleeps great when she keeps up with CT. Hell, it even gives me a marital shield. When she complains about sleep and is crabby I just drop the "When was your last CT?" bomb and it puts and end to it(at least me having to experience much) . That really was a game changer in my household. I can't tell you if just an abdomen pack would have had the same effects but will share my crude mental model.

Take a smartphone and put it into a 120 degree oven for 20 minutes. It won't be so "smart" anymore. Could a small icepack applied to just the right spot while it's in the oven make it work better? Yes. But whoever owned the smartphone would insist that I first take it out of the environment that is effecting it and then put it in a fridge or cool spot so it would regain it's functioning sooner. That, in a nutshell, is a significant part of the advice given here.

I think that if it weren't for the discomfort (due to non-cold adaption) or hassle of possibly having to buy ice if you live in a warm climate CT would be a very easy sell. The adaption came over time for us (Mom, dad, and son). The hassle of getting ice became a little bit of an obstruction that I've since worked around.

### Brent Patrick — 2017-06-03T09:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-215340>

Glad winter has arrived in Sydney Australia, I have been doing CT 6am each morning at around 9c and 8pm each night at average 11c ,,observations so far sleep has become deeper energy levels have increased libido threw the roof
skin has become more "youthful" friends have commented on this fairly often. But most of all I have noticed interactions with other people and individuals I normally wouldn't talk to have been very positive in nature (Magnetic Attraction ) and problem solving skills have increased from a new awareness .
Each CT presents itself with different challenges those are which are more related to mental state and overcoming the lead up to throwing oneself into a very cold situation ,but once Monkey Brain is diffused the overwhelming feeling of
accomplishment is very satisfying. I have used my CT experience so far as a metaphor for other areas of my life. Thanks Dr kruse

### Antonis — 2017-11-22T13:22:02+0200

>
> 4.) cautionary answer:
> With all due respect beware the following statement "only need spot treatment around your abdomen". While that may be true, don't limit yourself before trying this out. The fat cells in your abdomen weren't sitting around in an otherwise optimized body and then decided they and they alone were just going to go wild without receiving any signaling.
> I very much recommend building up to full CT to see if your N=1 receives any serendipitous (at least relative to your current perspective) benefits. I sure did. Like you I went in targeting just weight loss. I came out with perfect sleep, higher libido, knees that don't pop, better cognition, and increased sense of drive to name a few. All of that is secondary to the best benefit for me so far. My wife, long time insomnia suffer, now sleeps great when she keeps up with CT. Hell, it even gives me a marital shield. When she complains about sleep and is crabby I just drop the "When was your last CT?" bomb and it puts and end to it(at least me having to experience much) . That really was a game changer in my household. I can't tell you if just an abdomen pack would have had the same effects but will share my crude mental model.
>
> Take a smartphone and put it into a 120 degree oven for 20 minutes. It won't be so "smart" anymore. Could a small icepack applied to just the right spot while it's in the oven make it work better? Yes. But whoever owned the smartphone would insist that I first take it out of the environment that is effecting it and then put it in a fridge or cool spot so it would regain it's functioning sooner. That, in a nutshell, is a significant part of the advice given here.
>
> I think that if it weren't for the discomfort (due to non-cold adaption) or hassle of possibly having to buy ice if you live in a warm climate CT would be a very easy sell. The adaption came over time for us (Mom, dad, and son). The hassle of getting ice became a little bit of an obstruction that I've since worked around.

I personally do cold showers and swimming in the sea. I don't have any tub so I use only the nature to do it. Water is not too cold. It is about 18-19 °C. I feel bit cold when I shower and then I use to it. But to be honest am cold aswel when am with my shorts and t shirts but I resist. Environmental temperature is around 18°C but we have humidity which makes you feel the cold. It is the first time I do it for the winter. I also wear less clothes at the moment. I find it a bit hard but I keep pushing my brain to forget that is cold. December and January will be colder. In the night is colder and I find it hard to not cover myself when I sleep. What should I do. Do you have any recommendations?

### Michael Leger — 2017-12-10T18:05:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-229943>

I took a circuitous route to CT. I did the face dunking for a week or so. Added walking my dogs around sunrise in shorts, moccasins, gloves/hat as necessary. Morning temps were/are in the lower 20s to lower teens (F). Then added 15 minutes of tai chi in shorts with moccasins and gloves. All together about 45 minutes around sunrise. Then added partial cold shower, then full cold shower and today did cold tub for the first time. I was in the tub for 10mins, submerged up to the middle of my belly. Last few minutes put my torso under too. I haven't measured the exact water temp, but guessing it's between 55 and 60.

It was harder to be outside in bare feet (light snow on the ground now) w/o gloves than to be in the tub. I drank a cold smoothie (plant-based, will be the last one f0r awhile as I am starting the leptin reset) afterwards. Still shivering a bit. Some numbness in my fingertips.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-12-10T23:34:04+0200

Breakfast - 2 slices of seed bread with homemade sprouted almond butter, beef jerky, wakame/sauerkraut salad, gotu kola/ginkgo/milky oats tea (this is a brain/nervous system combo).

> I have a rainwater collection system so my filters are big, white, and float in the sky.

Are you using tarps or something similar? Alchemists are really big on collecting rainwater during a thunderstorm. It has special properties that I suspect align with what is being discussed here. "Properly collected water contains within it the universal flame of life." [http://www.levity.com/alchemy/reid2-8.html](http://www.levity.com/alchemy/reid2-8.html). "Rain water and snow have both been used by modern alchemists at Paracelsus College (Parachemy 1977, Essentia 1980 & 82). Rain water was collected during a thunderstorm in insulated containers before it contacted the earth in order to prevent the high electrical charge from being grounded out. Pure rain water has a higher nitrogen content during an electrical storm (Parachemy 1977)."

The problem in the early 21st century is rain's exposure to nnEMF on it's way back to Earth.

### Michael Leger — 2017-12-26T16:23:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-231094>

Air CT. Sunny, -6f. 20 mins of tai chi. Fingers were the limiting factor. With gloves, skin temps moving towards the low 40s got too painful. Very light winds. I'm sure that stronger winds will make below zero temps much harder to tolerate. Cold shower after air CT. Fingertips are still a bit numb.

### Janet Shold — 2018-01-14T07:29:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-232507>

I’m about a month into cold therapy and have graduated to the tub. Morning cold showers are an incredible pick-me up, and I still have a soak a couple of hours before bed. Had a nice soak in a tub this evening, and I feel amazing! It was 50 when I got ink and 57 when I got out, 25 minutes later.

It’s a trip getting in, and my family is amused by the noises I make...(scares the dogs), but after a few minutes it’s pretty relaxing and I get some reading done.

### Greenie — 2018-01-21T23:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-233089>

Per the daily morning ritual: Sat outside nude (~56F ), hands and feet on the ground, in the sun for 45 minutes; garden hose for 15 minutes starting with the feet before moving up. Rotate different body areas for about 60-90 seconds. At the moment, the garden hose is the best option. (The water is colder (55F to 60F) than the inside shower; plus, being in outside feels even more refreshing!)

While no shivers occur during the CT itself, it takes about 20-30 minutes before they stop thereafter. Does anyone else find shivering kicks in after CT?

I urinated a lot.........was 77 F and I got in cold water.

### Butters — 2019-01-22T21:37:59+0200

### OliverGruener2 — 2019-04-09T10:39:27+0300

I need to step it up now. Hesitate to fill up the bathtub for less than 10 mins though. Seems a waste of water until I can stay in it for longer (like a warm relaxing bath).

### Bryan Anderson — 2019-04-09T22:08:41+0300

Used these for spot CT in the sun for a while now, they work really well (no toxic gel and very cold). I'll cool exposed areas that get hot then put the pack back on brown fat patches (or at least where one is supposed to be, back of neck between shoulder blades is a good one for me). N=1, but always seem to get a better pop from sunlight this way. And make sure I'm grounded in a good place if possible. The spot CT has helped me adjust to full body CT in H2o as a nice bonus.

### Bryan Anderson — 2019-04-12T18:54:21+0300

### Lahelada — 2019-04-12T20:17:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-267932>

Redneck tub ! I have little outside space and kiddie pools would have been too shallow. Friends were doing some construction work and I swiped the bags the sand gets delivered in. I added impermeable lining and the bag handles are tied to fixed points on the patio to create height of the tub. I was looking for full immersion occupying little floor space. Everyone says I should find a prettier colour but you only get white green or blue. I did not think I want any of those. Gimme purple or red , then we are talking. The reflection you see in the water is the branches of the palo borracho or Chorisia, a kind of magnolia tree. Very relaxing CT watching sky and the pink blossoms. So far I am only doing ambient temp CT which is around 20 degrees. Next week the weather will turn, so it will get more interesting !

### drezy — 2019-04-16T15:43:51+0300

Everyone here my age or younger thinks it's "jury rigged". I don't bother with the history lesson...

All German made goods I've bought are rock solid perfect quality. One test and metrology company I worked for had a German assembly facility. I swear that I could take one of our measurement units, throw it out a 6 story window, kick it across a soccer field, and it would still be more accurate than our competitors units. Even not when necessary the products made there were so physically sturdy they always felt as if they were completely carved out of one piece of metal. Every new product offering was like Christmas when I opened the shipment for the first time.

### Marko Pollo — 2019-04-16T19:19:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268222>

I looked up jury rigged and it is thought it comes from jury mast, meaning temporary. So that would be the correct termfor a temp fix till returning to port or later time.

I think this is different than jerry-rigged, or any other racially derogatory name which implies inferiority with prejudice.

It seems that redneck engineering can be used both respectfully to imply intelligent use of limited resources or in a derogatory way to imply inferiority. Rednecks are always gonna make fun of lawyers and visa versa. As a redneck, I see it as being able to do stuff vs being able to hire people to do everything for you.

An alternative Ive read about( either alan savory or allan nation i think) and try to use is the New Zealand method.
When you have a problem, you first ask how can I solve this problem for free?if nothing pops up, then how can i solve it cheaply? If still you have no solution, ask is this really a problem i cant live with or not even a problem at all. In my life it had become easier to solve problems with my wallet. A credit card is like a gun. If you dont leave it in your pocket for an absolute emergency, youll prolly regret it. Rent a piece of heavy equipment to avoid labor, buy a tool when i could get by, hire someone to d o something i can do myself, purchase replacement instead of repair ect. This attitude also helps because when others have problems, they also think MY wallet is the solution. I am willing to roll upmy sleeves and help someone who is trying themselves. Usually this involves an explanation of why i dont solve problems with money and just showing them how quickley we can do shit ourselves. If money was the only solution this person was interested in, adios mf!

Respect to your cold tub! Lahelada

### Saichi — 2019-04-22T02:12:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268583>

Only my second day doing CT 41st latitude Pacific Ocean. My first day yesterday about 15 minutes, today 25 minutes. Once my hands' dexterity began reducing I got out although I felt great and wanted to stay in longer. My toe dexterity wasn't an issue since I was constantly fighting the waves by digging in the sand. I want to caution anyone doing this in Pacific Northwest should be careful of sneaker waves, couple times they took me out further than I wanted. This morning didn't think I was gonna go out since it's sunny but decided I could wear my straw African hat in the ocean to block the sun, worked perfectly and even prevented the waves from splashing my face as well. I dried off immediately then got in the car and turned the heat on full blast for about 15 minutes. I was shivering involuntarily for about 12 minutes, I'm sure once I become more cold adapted I can apply meditative breathing to calm the shivers.

Beside feeling the best I've felt in maybe a decade I noticed on the drive back my vision reverted back close to 20/20! I had the top lasik done in 2008, following decade ignorantly ruined my eyes with blue to about 20/30 for the past few years.

I'm really dreading going to NYC later this week because there's no way a cold shower can match the Pacific.

### Saichi — 2019-04-22T22:52:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268641>

Third day CT Pacific Ocean. Sunny and no geoengineering overhead. Six foot waves crashed over me half the time, other half I swam the waves before they broke. Asked someone the time when I jumped out thinking I was in for just 30 minutes, I was in for a full hour.

My four year caffeine habit of several cups green tea, gone. Depression, gone. Cold extremities at night or when tired, gone. Compromised vision, gone.

Deep appreciation for all life on par with Amazonian shamanic experience 100%, in fact I find it more effective.

Nature the real medicine.

Too much beauty.

### Saichi — 2019-04-23T01:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268651>

Looks like sunrise/sunset and cloudy days will be my only option for CT now. Pretty bad sunburn on my upper back, didn't even think to check first whether UVC penetrates seawater and of course it does. Was getting a good 5 microwatts/cm^2 btwn 246 and 262nm, which could be as high as 55 microwatts for whole UVC spectrum. And convert for human size that's anywhere from 50 to 550 milliwatts (not microwatts) per meter squared. Learned the hard way again. More than breaking up my skin DNA/RNA, who knows what the quantum topology effects are.

I do have a UV blocking umbrella I could take to do CT during a sunny day, assuming the waves don't destroy it, then the public will think I'm certifiable. Little do they know, what comedy reality is.

### Dani — 2019-04-25T00:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268857>

What's the temperature of the water at 41 latitude N?

### Saichi — 2019-04-25T01:09:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268859>

> [Dani said:](/goto/post?id=268857)
>
> What's the temperature of the water at 41 latitude N?

I haven't taken a thermometer but online says around 52 deg. I was in for an hour yesterday at sunset and would've stayed longer but was getting dark quickly. My post-CT shivering wasn't as intense, still strong, only lasted about 10 minutes. First time since I started CT I ate too much couple hours before so couple hours after CT I vomited a small amount. Felt fine since only slept five hours last night, ran a bunch of errands, cut down half a decent size tree with a telescoping chainsaw in the backyard which is harder than it sounds and looking forward to another sunset dip in four hours. Believe I'm cold adapting well, my mother's mother ate shit on a shingle most her life and lived close to 90.

I've had a bit of heartburn today which is different.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-04-25T20:07:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-268908>

I wish I could go for a swim in the ocean for my CT.

For now I just have a cold shower with an outside tap in the sunrise.

### Saichi — 2019-04-26T19:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-269018>

Did my first shower CT today for an hour. Glad I installed the Big Berkey shower filter first. My waterfall shower head doesn't tilt much so had to lean my head back a bit for the hour which isn't an ideal position. Only felt like 30 minutes again.

### Solidsilverteeth — 2019-04-26T19:34:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-269021>

An hour well done.

I don't have that much time, my entire morni routine involves doing upto 100 burpies and then laying down on the earth looking at the sunrise, then outside cold water shower while looking at the sun rising. It's all done in less than 39mins then I have to go to work.

It's no point me getting up earlier because the sun hasn't risen yet, but sunrise is getting earlier every day towards summer,Mao will aim for longer cold water showers

### Pablo — 2019-04-30T15:26:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-269339>

Take care

### Saichi — 2019-05-04T19:02:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-269676>

Missed CT for first time yesterday (big mistake for my mental health), today my back felt warm for most the 45 minute shower which is new for me but my front side still prickly icy hot. Good 15 minutes in the middle my breathing was completely relaxed and comfortable. Ate a few fresh dayboat flounder roe with local sorrel afterwards for a late breakfast. My appetite's so low compared to my pre-CT life. Been eating a dozen oysters almost every other day though, wild Peconics and Wellfleets. Can't find my favorite Bosoles anymore bit chuffed about that. Thought the Cape Cods were quite unpalatable.

Found out Jones Beach is 52 degrees right now so contemplating the 2 hr trek for ocean CT tomorrow.

### Elguava — 2019-06-22T22:35:29+0300

> The chiller works splendidly although it does take a bit longer to cool down the water than I would like. It reduces the water temp about 6 degrees per hour. Starting temp now is 77 (tap water temp) F. The pump model is:
> **Hydrofarm AAPW550 550-GPH Active Aqua Submersible Pump. ($39 US at Amazon)
> The chiller model is:
> **Hydroponic Chiller - Refrigeration Unit - 250-1200 Gal/Hr. - 1/2 HP - 4100 BTU - 360 Watt - 110-120 Volt - Use with 1/2 3/4 1 in. Tubing - Active Aqua AACH50 ($600 US at Amazon).
>
> The chiller gets the water quite cold, less than 45 degrees.
>
> Hope this helps.****

### MattD1995 — 2019-09-18T16:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-278159>

As with all things, I think there’s a natural progression with CT that should probably vary based on an individual’s level of redox. My redox is absolutely shot. I’d be willing to bet that gradual cooling might be better for me as I continue to consume DHA daily and add sunlight.

For now, I’m going to stop using the freezer for full submersion every day and focus on:

1) Extended soaks in my pool in the sunlight. My pool is 87 degrees. Jack has said that anything below body temperature can have a positive, cooling effect.

2) I’ll also be reverting back to step 1 and 2 of the CT protocol (face dunks and ice blocks on my bare skin). I’ve found ice blocks on my skin for 45 minutes to be much more tolerable in the past than even 20 minutes of full submersion in my freezer.

I tend to be an impatient guy. But with redox, I’m coming to realize that slow and steady is what will win the race. Continued daily DHA, water, and sunlight will hopefully allow me to be taking full submersion ice baths by winter.

I also am going to add back in a small amount of carbs temporarily while I continue to DHA load going into winter. My redox is just to trashed right now to operate on Keto.
