### Pablo — 2020-01-02T16:24:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-284145>

Just finished 37´beginning 10ºC (50f) and finishing at 13.8 (average 11.9), after devouring 0,5kg wild raw alaskan salmon (and 2nd day taking bitter melon powder). I think it is my new PR. My objective is to reach 45´at 10C/50F by february. As much as I hate to say it... I think the cold is being a game changer for me. More so than the sun. I ´m feeling better right now at the 43 latitude than during summer... Is it cause of I´ve just being more time in this quantum health path or cause I just do better in cold+ketosis? I need to meditate on that (and maybe get some 23andme testing for mitochondrial haplotype?)

By the way, do you find it is always better to use compression clothing while doing CT, or only in the beginning? I used it a while ago but I am pondering using it again.

I still fear the cold pretty much..damm! hehe. One thing I find really useful is listening some motivating music beforehand. Keep it up

### Saichi — 2020-01-02T17:32:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-284148>

I would very much fear CT if I did it in any environment over 50 micros, and it's probably why so many people have side effects and difficulty doing it.

### Pablo — 2020-01-12T16:40:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-284463>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=284148)
>
> I would very much fear CT if I did it in any environment over 50 micros, and it's probably why so many people have side effects and difficulty doing it.

With dirty electricity over 50 mV you mean? Yeah I need to get a meter for that. Most environemnts suck

Just finished a 52´begining at 9 celsius and finishing at 13. New PR for me. I had my feet and hands outside for most of the time (42´) and I´m feeling that it allows me to stay a bit longer.

Shit I feel pretty high!! I only fear the cold before getting inside. After the initial shock I feel in heaven in 5 seconds. 20 first minutes are a blessing. 10´are ok and then it gets slightly uncomfortable. But I´m feeling good benefits... energy and libido go pretty high for the day.

I want to keep consistent with this. I want to keep progressing though not sure what´s best. Options:
\-Doing every single day (I´m doing 4-5 a week average)
\-More time
\-Less temp
\-Getting feets and hands inside all time
\-Using compression clothing

I´m inclined to put in all of my body I can (tub is small so some acrobatics are needed), lower temps to 7-8 celsius (upper 40´s F) and maybe using some compression. Basically because of time constraints. Also planning to get into the river if it is not flooded. It should be freezing.

One question: What´s better, getting into 7celsius/ upper 40´s F river or 12-13 celsius ocean (55F). Probably it´s a tricky question and the answer is n=1... I´d say the first provided you are a bit adapted and can stay some decent time inside...

Lots of improvement and learning ahead

### Charlotte English — 2020-06-30T03:05:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-290638>

Can I ask for some help or advice?

I am very cold intolerant - eg. it's 20ºC in my house right now and I'm numb up to the mid-calf area and all the skin on my arms and legs is cold to the touch. Apparently fat isn't such a great insulator as all that...

With cold showers, I can only bear it at all when I start very hot, like after half hour naked in the hot sun. But after a month of trying it just isn't getting any easier. I go white, then start to go blue and find 2mins a struggle. My husband seems to only find the first minute or so horrible but he goes red. Even our 8 year old can do 10 mins no problem (and likes to whenever he needs to feel superior to us wimps). I tried plunging my face in very cold water but it makes me hyperventilate straight away and then I get a headache and feel sick, so I'm in a bit of despair about how to get better at this. But I really want to!

If anyone has any ideas, I'd really appreciate your help!

My mitochondrial haplotype is X2b but it was one that Jack didn't mention, so I'm not sure if I'm more of a light girl than a cold one and I should focus on that more or if I should try to work up heat tolerance instead. I'm not great with the heat either but I can relax into it more.

I'd love to know if anyone else has this X2b haplotype or any ideas of what the best thing to do to work up to cold.

Thank you everyone!

Earth is flat too.

Jack should remember growing up in NYC we used to have three to five feet of ice packs all around the city on sidewalks and parking spots that would last weeks to months at a time with regular sub 0 F temps often. Now we get maybe a few inches of slush that lasts a day or two once the entire winter. Even without CT adaptation people don't need thick long wool coats but maybe for less than a week now. When I first moved to coastal Pacific Northwest we had rain all year long besides the summer which was the norm for the Redwood rainforests but we've been in perennial drought for almost a decade now with just one season of condensed deluge couple years back.

How anyone can say we have a cooling planet with record global average temps increasing exponentially and arctic ice volumes decreasing likewise boggles the mind as much as the general public believing in covid.

We would've already had the blue ocean event if it weren't for geoengineering.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-351285>

Direct TV dish is only a receiver so it can't beam anything at you. Sounds like it's your own desktop or iPad that's blasting you, should be easy to see with the meter you have.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-25T23:04:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-351287>

No. I have everything off in the house and the levels are good or acceptable in the lower part of the house. When I go to the other side and up the signal jumps way up like I’m near a cell tower but only from the neighbors side who have the direct tv dishes. It could be strong WiFi coming from their house. I check outside too. Not sure why you would think RF is coming from an iPad connected to Ethernet anyway. It’s not from the iPad. The signal from this house is much stronger than all the other homes that border my property. So it’s not from a smart meter either. They do have solar panels but so far I have not detected high EMFs from other homes with solar panels. It could be a tower but then I would think that signal would be detected all over the property and not so directly from this one house and only when I point my meters towards their dishes.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-25T23:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-351288>

>
> Direct TV dish is only a receiver so it can't beam anything at you. Sounds like it's your own desktop or iPad that's blasting you, should be easy to see with the meter you have.

I get that the dish is a receiver but every dish I have measured gives off signal. In El Salvador at my airbnb the Claro dish was giving off a signal until I unplugged the WiFi router and then it stopped. Also, to receive, wouldn’t a satellite need to have gotten a signal from the location of the dish to know where to send it? I am no expert but I am just going by meter readings, observation, and common sense. Furthermore, to receive a signal means that signal is coming from the satellite above. So the dish is instigating signal to that location regardless of whether it’s sending or receiving. There is signal going to that location which happens to be in very close proximity to my house and is leaking over so it seems. If it’s coming from a cell tower it wouldn’t be so localized to that small area. It would be all over my yard and house.

### Saichi — 2025-04-25T23:45:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-351290>

Satellite frequencies are above what your meter can read and they're very targeted to the dish. The dish would be connected by coax to a receiver box that's transmitting with wifi which depending on how close your house is to their wall can easily be high to extreme levels. Any transmitter can also beam form greatly intensifying targeted exposure.

I also recall that combining heat and cold is good too. (Fournier effect?) Like when you are immersed in cold water but have the hot sun on your head or back. Snorkeling should be nice

Thanks for all your input Saichi! We asked the neighbors to power everything down and see if the readings changed, and I did not see any significant difference. The average readings are acceptable, but the “peak” or blip can get pretty high and I think it’s actually coming from an antenna or multiple ones in the neighborhood. I’ve been testing levels while out walking my dogs in the morning and found out that a large cross on top of the church down the corner is actually an antenna for 5G or something. Very high readings! I will post a pic of it later.

### Saichi — 2025-04-29T02:01:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-351455>

How far is the church's cell tower?

### Woodstock — 2025-04-29T16:31:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-was-your-ct-today.499/post-351477>

> [Saichi said:](/goto/post?id=351455)
>
> You sure your neighbor actually unplugged their router and turned off all devices?
>
> How far is the church's cell tower?

He cut off all power at his fuse box. I don’t have any way of verifying that but he is a really nice guy and was very willing to help out and said if we needed anything else to just ask.

The church tower is maybe 200 yards away. There are multiple houses and a lot of trees in between us and its not visible from my house, but as soon as you go to the end of my cul-de-sac, readings jump from 10 µw/m2 to 100 on my safe and sound classic 3. I did actually order the safe and sound pro 2 so I can get more accurate readings but it isn’t here yet. Ive been taking the meter with me on walks and it’s been very interesting. The High School and Middle School and Rec Center are also close by so the pulse Im seeing could also be from those, but the church is a little closer and it does seem to be directionally coming from the church as I don’t get the high readings pointed towards the direction of the schools. I feel sorry for the people with houses right across from the antennas as they are being blasted with 10,000-100,000 µw/m2. So i guess inverse square law is saving me a bit. But there is that pulse. The average readings are not high. It’s the pulsing that seems to vary quite a bit in frequency and not always the same strength.

Could the short pulse be from
\- radars
\- smart meters
\- solar panels / electric car loading stations
?
(A friend recently told me that the solar panels on his neighbours garden house send signals to the smartphone in the house. I also heard of loading station for electric cars sending wireless signals to the owner's smartphone app.)

With the sound on and with practice one can determine the source based on the type of sound.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-29T17:48:50+0300

Thank you! These are all good points. The neighbor does have solar panes. Not sure about the smart meter. But I figured he wouldn’t be able to turn these off or do anything about it if it was the source. It’s up really high. The signal is strongest from the highest point of my house. So I suppose it could be the solar panels, but also I thought it was the batteries that store the solar power that put off emfs and not the panels themselves? I am currently using the acoustimeter and a mini safe and sound classic 3 but the safe and sound pro 2 will be here soon so I can get more precise readings.

I suppose it could be smart meters but since the signal is stronger from high up, I think its more likely something further away with a stronger signal or I would be seeing these reading from my yard rather than higher up.

It could be solar panels but we had our smart phones turned off when we had the neighbor power down his fuse box. So I don’t think its solar panels sending a signal to our smart phones either.

I don’t know how to tell from the sound but I am going to get my building biologist over here to teach me more on how to identify. Thanks so much for your input.

here’s a picture of the “Jesus-Tenna” that gives off a very strong signal. So glad I don’t live right next to it but it could be the source of the pulse Im seeing.

# Thread: Hello from Barrie, Ontario :) A few issues need help with....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-barrie-ontario-a-few-issues-need-help-with.500/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4

### spinnsugar@hotmail.com — 2012-03-28T20:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-barrie-ontario-a-few-issues-need-help-with.500/post-7827>

I wanted to say a hello to everyone, and pick your brain.

I have been on leptin reset for 3 months and CT for 7 weeks on saturday.

Having some issues, just wondering if anyone had some input for me...

I am late on my TOTM, by 2 weeks. I am never ever late unless I am pregnant, I know the minute I am, and I know I am not. I mean I know 28 days down to a few hours when it will arrive.

As well in the past week I am getting(dark) facial hair, I mean what? I am so strict about LRx and I know my hormones should go wacky, they did week 2/3 of CT, pmssy and acne and greasy and all. But this is awful.

I am in Canada, and my Dr will not run tests, just had them and they were 'fine' and I just cant afford a naturopath and hope that they may run them. I am freaking out, more so about the hair!

Sorry this isnt really a positive post, but I am thinking this is a deal breaker for me I just dont know what to do...

Thanks

### nonchalant — 2012-04-05T23:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-barrie-ontario-a-few-issues-need-help-with.500/post-11907>

Hi Spinnsugar, hope you were able to work things out.

### cantweight — 2012-04-07T05:41:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-barrie-ontario-a-few-issues-need-help-with.500/post-12374>

I would see OB/GYN and rule out ovarian cysts. Also sounds like some high testosterone. Maybe get a good hormone work up and see where you land?

Not sure how any of this could spur that kind of reaction. Hang in there!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-08T03:37:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-barrie-ontario-a-few-issues-need-help-with.500/post-12565>

I think hormone issues and maybe even a leaky gut is present. You need to get some testing done. Welcome here. We will do what we can.

# Thread: What is more important? Sleep or Cold?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-more-important-sleep-or-cold.501/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-28T20:40:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-more-important-sleep-or-cold.501/post-7844>

I'm getting in late tonight and I won't have time to fit in a bath until I get home. However, if I do a CT bath at say 11 pm, I know I won't get any sleep. So am I better off skipping it and get back to schedule tomorrow? or I am better of getting less sleep?

### Jim — 2012-03-28T20:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-more-important-sleep-or-cold.501/post-7847>

I say sleep. You don't want to mess with your circadian rhythm. You can take a cold shower in the morning to get your CT in that way. I am also starting to sleep with fewer/thinner covers. Slowly working my way to nothing. That way I can sleep and CT at the same time.

### Clabbergirl — 2012-03-28T20:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-more-important-sleep-or-cold.501/post-7849>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;7886 wrote:** I'm getting in late tonight and I won't have time to fit in a bath until I get home. However, if I do a CT bath at say 11 pm, I know I won't get any sleep. So am I better off skipping it and get back to schedule tomorrow? or I am better of getting less sleep?

I asked this question in the comments of one of Dr. Kruse's blogs and he didn't really answer clearly - pretty much quality of sleep is more important than quantity, and CT is wonderful. I can't find his response now for some reason, my apologies. I battle with this all the time except that CT baths make me sleepy. Hopefully he will answer you here or someone with more knowledge.

# Thread: Two weeks into Leptin Rx: Relapsed, huge binge! Discouraged.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Maria Anna — 2012-03-29T01:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-7993>

Hi,

My name is Maria Anna, I'm 5,4 and 115lbs. Managed to come down from 145lbs. I kept on having cravings though, and usually wake up in te middle of the night, hungry and eat. I suffer from nocturnal sleep related eating disorder.

So I started the Leptin Reset two weeks ago. At first it felt fantastic! No hunger, no cravings whatsoever. But yesterday night, after going to bed I was so hungry and craving wheat and sugar that I binged on everything I could get my hands on.

I feel discouraged now. I don't want to give up this new way of eating but how do I stay on track?

It's also that I know that according to Kruse, a relapse puts you back two weeks in time.... So I basically have to start all over again....

Haven't I made any progress then?

### hellojtm — 2012-03-29T01:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8000>

Hi Maria,

In the words of Art DeVany, "there is no failure, only feedback". Just live in the moment and plan for the future. All you can do now is go back to what was working, i.e. the leptin reset was working for you. If you feel you need to tweak it then propose some tweaks on the forum that would make it more sustainable to you and see what others have tried.

I disagree with what Dr. Kruse wrote about two weeks. The reason is that you lost 30lbs!! Did you gain that back or even a fraction of it? Probably not. Maybe your metabolism will be slightly worse (insulin resistant) for a few days.. maybe not. We don't know.

You know what a cyclic ketogenic diet is? Lots of people lose tons of weight with that diet and it has a 1 or 2 day a week high carb day. This seems to work well for active people.

I know Dr. K's approach is deeper than this and to really get there you'd eventually need to be full keto and do cold therapy etc.. during winter. But you have 6+ months to get it dialed in... during summer I doubt you're going to pull off a 100% ketogenic diet, especially with sleep issues involved. (fix those and watch the carb cravings melt).

best of luck!

jesse

### Maria Anna — 2012-03-29T01:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8003>

Hi Jesse,

Thanks for replying so quickly. I just read what you wrote and I need some time to digest it - pardon the pun!

M.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T05:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8099>

Hi, Maria Ann. That is virtually the same time I had my first (and only) binge since starting about a month ago. Just get right back on track - you can do it. Stick with a monster breakfast, and fill up on healthy protein and fat.

I think Mark Cisson says that it takes the body 21 days to switch from burning sugar/carbs to burning fat for fuel. So if you can stick with it for a bit longer, I think those cravings will start to ease away.

Also, try looking at things as an experiment. Did you do anything that day differently? Can you experiment with different snacks when this happens to see if you can avoid a binge? So there is no failure; there is only results from your experiment. If you don't get the outcome you want, then you have to adjust the variables.

### Birdy — 2012-03-29T17:20:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-8267>

I did not find Dr. K's assessment of the amount of time to continue to be on-target in my case. In my experience, it really DOES get easier and easier with each restart. I am at the point where I can feel my body slipping back into sub-optimal. I don't test much, especially in frequency, but I seem to be able to tell when the rt3 is fluctuating. I start to get cold, hungrier, and have more cravings. I try to beat this back by really concentrate on sleep, stress and careful eating, and plenty of fat. You might try being very strict with your diet. I had to get rid of all dairy for a time, including whey, and sausage, bacon because they have additives and sugars. Green tea has really helped with mood and cravings, so you might try drinking some throughout your day.

### Maria Anna — 2012-03-31T12:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-9146>

Thanks so much for the input, everyone! I'm back on track again after a two day binge. Feels good to have my BAB down! Bit worried over a dinner party in a week from now. Doesn't start after 7pm, so one rule broken already then. But we'll see how it works out.....

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-03-31T16:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-9217>

Don't worry about that dinner party Maria Anna. I usually eat before I attempt this kind of event, and once there I have a salad. I think it's better to have veggies after 7:30 than have junk and alchool. It's not that bad to have food late at night if you have no choice, but you have to eat the good stuff. Focuss on the time being and on trying to make everyday a success, one meal at the time, and one night at the time.

I also have sleep problems, and its getting better with the reset. If I wake up in the night and can't go back to sleep, I usually eat, but most of my binges happen day time. With BAB (up tp 80-85 gram of protein!), I have no cravings at all and the hunger is more easy to manage at night. Do you always wake up at the same time? Like 2 or 3 o'clock? It's what I do, and it's a cortisol problem...

Everybody here with eating disorder lose it from time to time, but you haven't lose all progress you have done. This time will be easier, and it will probably happen again. This is just NORMAL!! Read this, it,s really interesting: [http://primitivestimulus.com/2011/04/moments-of-weakness/](http://http://primitivestimulus.com/2011/04/moments-of-weakness/)

Have a good day, and don't hesitate to come back if you need any support!

K

### Maria Anna — 2012-04-01T11:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/two-weeks-into-leptin-rx-relapsed-huge-binge-discouraged.504/post-9727>

Thanks K! It's so good to have all your support. I also assume that my waking up has something to do with cortisol. Doctor's gonna run a test. It's a blood test though - no saliva test. We'll see what comes out....

# Thread: Leptin Reset Protocol for Hashimoto's Thyroiditis

### Alimac — 2012-03-29T02:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8012>

Hi all,

I have Hashimoto's thyroiditis and am creator of the Hashitmoto's 411 group on facebook. I am an advocate for first and foremost addressing diet as a first step in treating autoimmune disease like Hashimoto's which in turn helps to address the underlying conditions that cause autoimmune responses. I've heard from a few of my members who've done this protocol with incredible results. I have been gluten free for about 2 years now and have done an elimination diet to determine other food sensitivities. That was lifechanging but occasionally I have setbacks mostly due to adrenal fatigue but i'm currently feeling like I need to get an ever tighter reign on my diet to address insulin resistance which is keeping some unwanted lbs on and creating symptoms again. I'd love to hear from any others with Hashimoto's or any other autoimmune disease who are on this protocol. I'm especially curious about the cold showers! With cold intolerance being a symptom I just can't imagine doing it but I'll give it a try. : )

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T02:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8014>

CT fixes cold intolerance. Seriously! Look around the forum, and hopefully some of those with Hashi's (not rare around here!) will share their experiences. (BTW, I strongly recommend cold baths over cold showers - you get used to it and it's quickly tolerable, instead of constantly being bombarded by new waves of cold water and warmer air.)

Be sure to read the first CT blog (something like "rewiring the leptin reset" or something), b/c it has the protocols for easing into the cold water.

### Slone07 — 2012-03-29T02:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8022>

Haven't done any CT yet, as I am so used to being an icicle that I have a pretty knee jerk reaction to the thought of purposely touching ANYthing cold...but we'll see. I've been reading about it and it sounds incredibly promising.

I'm eager to hear what others have to say...

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T02:59:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-8030>

Hi Alice -

I am a member of Hashimotos 411 and you are wonderful. I have done most everything you have listed above too. For me the Leptin reset was a great way to bring my "whole body" back to the focal point and work toward an optimal state. This is so much more than just the thyroid. However, within 1 month of being on the leptin reset I lowered my thyroid meds as well as went off my BP medicine.

About the col intolerance I would have said the same thing in January. But who would have thought that in February I would be jumping into a cold bath almost every day. My body warms up differently than it used to and blankets have come off the bed. I am up to about 45 min in 55 degree water but just recently added ice blankets to my chest and back. Brrrrr. But it took me almost six weeks to get to 45 min so go slow. Now it's much colder and I shiver a lot but I just listen to a podcast or read a book and time goes by fast. I'm rooting for ya!!

### Alimac — 2012-03-29T04:05:40+0300

### hazyjane — 2012-04-02T18:30:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-10346>

Hey Alice! It's neat to see you here! I'm in your 411 group and we're actually FB friends (I'm Erin F.)

I haven't posted in the 411 group in awhile (it gets old reading about so many people who just won't change their diet or med-centric views...)

I've been having some neat results with Leptin Rx and CT. I still have some adrenal fatigue- mostly just low morning cortisol, but I do feel a lot more energetic and motivated all around. My hair is getting thicker since CT, too (YAY!!!!!) My hair is one of my thyroid gauges.

Have you tried any of the Apex thyroid support supplements? Maybe you'd tolerate them better than meds and maybe they'd do more for you than glandulars... ### Tucker — 2012-04-03T16:10:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-protocol-for-hashimotos-thyroiditis.505/post-10751>

Hi Alice,

This is my first post on the forum. I feel the need to be a Starfish as Dr. Kruse encourages us to be.

I have Hashimotos as well. Been dealing with it since 2008. There were times when I felt like I couldn't muster up enough energy to take my dog for a walk. It was bad. Have done salivary testing and it showed I was in Adrenal fatigue.

Now since 2011 I started working with Dr. Datis Kharazian and have followed his protocol since Aug. 2011. No gluten, no dairy, no coffee and using the Apex supplements. ( Glutathione Recycler, X-FLM & X-Viromin, Nitric Balance) I take other supplements but those are the big hitters. Dr. Datis had me do the test to find out if I am TH1 or TH2 dominant regarding the autoimmunity. I showed no real bias to either one so I take both the X-FLM & X-Viromin. So this protocol helped me tremendously. It cleared the brain fog, I had more energy, my hair stopped falling out. But man the scales would not budge. I would hover around 193-198lbs. It was so frustrating that the weight wouldn't budge especially since I was being so good with my diet and the supplements. I did an elimination diet and everything.

OK, now I have been to the 411 forum and would see people talk about Dr. Kruse. So I checked him out and started reading his mammoth blog posts. I was floored. I really think this guy will get the Nobel prize one of these days. Everything I was reading just made sense to me. I also listened to all of his pod casts he had up on his site. I was PUMPED to try this protocol. It made sense that obesity is caused by brain inflammation. My brain is inflamed because of the autoimmunity and that is why the scales aren't budging. So I jumped right in, literally I went straight to the tub.

March12, 2012 I started the Leptin Reset protocol and Cold Therapy. The first day I did an 18 minute CT in the tub with 54 degree water. COLD!!!!!!!!! Now I do two CT sessions a day for 25-30 minutes. Within 5 days I broke through a plateau and hit 188lbs on the scale. I was amazed. Then the next 2 weeks I was feeling like a mess due to hormonal dumps from the CT. I got bummed out for a while and was wondering why I wasn't seeing any progress. But just a few days ago I think a switch turned on and the weight started dropping. This morning the scales said 185lbs. Now the day I started the scales were 190lbs so it's only 5lbs over 3 weeks. But you have got to remember that I had not seen anything below 190lbs for a couple of years.

I believe it is the CT that is giving me the biggest bang. CTing is challenging at times but I feel so much better afterward. The Kharrazian protocol had helped tremendously with reducing the brain fog but the CT has totally wiped out the brain fog. I also feel like the CT has improved my gut. I don't have the bloating that I had before. I feel it is helping my adrenals as well, since I have more energy since the CT implementation. This is what I think is going on, the CT is reducing inflammation through out the whole body but especially the brain. So now the hypothalamus can work correctly and function the way it was designed too. Also, it must be having an impact on the Vagal nerve, now my brain and gut can have a more clear conversation with each other. That is why the bloating has reduced and my digestion feels better. I could be completely wrong on all of this but all I know is I feel better than I did before the Hashimotos. In a way the Hashimotos has been a blessing because by it making me feel absolutely like shit for so long it forced me to go out and find answers. Pre-Hashimotos I thought I was healthy and eating correctly but I wasn't. Now I"m on my way to being Optimal and better than before. This is all very exciting and right now I feel I"m a success story in progress because I'm not done yet. I really think the CT can stop autoimmunity in its tracks. All I ever here is once you turn on an autoimmune gene you can't turn it off. I say bullshit, that is limited thinking. Thinking that maybe only applies to a warm adapted mammal. I'm turning into a cold adapted mammal and I will turn off the autoimmune gene. My thoughts are changing which in turn my DNA is changing. EPIGENETICS!!!!

So yes, I would highly recommend to anyone with Hashi's to give the Leptin Reset and Cold Therapy a try. They need to be patient and determined. Believe me I have always been cold in my life and embracing cold therapy did not give me a warm fuzzy feeling inside. But I love the thought of doing a bio hack on myself. The cool thing is it's working.

That is my very long story about all of this. I will have more breakthroughs to post in the future.

Hope this can give someone some encouragement to give this a try. When I was at my worst it was really scary to think is this how life is always going to be? If someone feels that way today let me tell you NO! If you put the effort into yourself you will get better and most likely feel the best you have ever felt.

JUST DO IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

### Tucker — 2012-04-03T16:18:13+0300

Prior to staring CT the previous 3 menstrual cycles I would have a hormonal migraine the first day of my cycle. My last menstrual cycle that started 4 days after my first CT session I didn't have a headache and had absolutely no menstrual symptoms at all.

# Thread: AAAH! Minimal weight loss after 4.5 weeks.  Disappointment and fury!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 67
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### optimus prime — 2012-03-29T02:26:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8018>

It seems like everyone is having hearty success with leptin rx, but I keep losing and gaining the same 3 pounds! With Leptin Rx I lost 5 lbs in the first two weeks but then it pretty much all came back when I got my period. I'm still trying to work it off 2 weeks later. I was really excited when I first began 4.5 weeks ago because I'm so accustomed to eating low cal, VLC, lowfat in order to see any results.

I'm 30, 5'4", 159-162 lbs - heaviest I've ever been. No amount of fasting, dieting or exercise has worked for me the past 3 years (since I stopped my birth control - while on BC I was 133-138). I've been 20-30 lbs up and down since I was 17, accompanied by depression, fatigue, hair loss and a host of other fun stuff. I never really put it all together until a year ago when my progesterone blood test was way below range (1.5, range 5-40). That's when I started looking into hormones, hypothyroidism, etc.

I've been doing strict paleo - no grains, no dairy, little nuts, almost no fruit, and only grass-fed pastured animal proteins. BAB of eggs, coconut oil (or pastured goat milk butter

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-29T02:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8021>

Ok. Go back to the basic principles of the LR. BAB >50g protein, lunch 4-5hrs later, dinner 4-5 hrs after lunch, but at least 3 hrs before bedtime

Next focus on your circadian rhythm & sleep. Get sun in your eyes as soon as you get up in the morning, don't wear sunglasses, keep in-natural light to a minimum at night and use blue light blocking glasses if you're going to watch tv, be on the Internet etc

Also consider implementing CT!

Fwiw when you gain weight on the LR you most likely have a hormonal imbalance I gained 10lbs When i started the LR

Lots of ppl have had success merging the LR & CT

### optimus prime — 2012-03-29T03:08:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8035>

Huck, thank you for putting that back into perspective. However, it seems most people do lose a good amount of weight in the process.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T03:29:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8042>

I didn't lose any weight in the first month or two, as I recall. It took me six months to lose ten pounds. It's absolutely not a weight loss "diet," it's a "health recovery diet" that sets you up to begin to lose weight (because weight is just a symptom of underlying problems; it is NOT the problem itself).

Some people (usually male) do lose from the beginning, but you are by no means alone in not doing so!

ETA just checked - I weighed the same after 3-4 months as I did at the start.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-29T03:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8045>

> **MamaGrok;8085 wrote:** I didn't lose any weight in the first month or two, as I recall. It took me six months to lose ten pounds. It's absolutely not a weight loss "diet," it's a "health recovery diet" that sets you up to begin to lose weight (because weight is just a symptom of underlying problems; it is NOT the problem itself).
>
>
>
> Some people (usually male) do lose from the beginning, but you are by no means alone in not doing so!
>
>
>
> ETA just checked - I weighed the same after 3-4 months as I did at the start.

Agree. I was one of the woman that did lose week by week, but I think my hormones were less screwed. You are going through recovery, and I also think, by the sounds, that you are carrying water-weight. It all sounds hormonal to me, and these things take time and testing to get it right.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-29T04:06:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8064>

I think if you want encouragement, you can also go back and re-read the Leptin series. You'll see all the ways to be leptin resistant (liver, muscles, etc) and how JK says several times that 1) women see signs of LS that typically do not include weight loss (so that implies it comes later to me) and that 2) many gain weight.

If you've been low fat and restricting calories for a long time, plus doing damage to your metabolism, hormones (and theoretically your gut), you've got to correct disease before you move forward. For me, part of embracing this way of living was to really get 'inside' the thinking and the framework. In other words, STOP counting calories and worrying about if you're exercising enough (these will be outcomes of getting inside the thinking, not ways to do so). This, for me, was essential because I have to believe in what I'm doing so that I can keep going even when I don't lose weight or I don't feel like getting into a freezing cold bath, etc. Also if you understand the principles, you won't worry about exercise because you'll know that until you are LS at the muscle level it won't help (and can hurt). And you'll eat more BAB and not worry.

It is a huge jump and it's really tricky to let go of the scales being what dominates. (I solve that problem by not having one!). I think it is especially hard for women, for whom there is so much body pressure, and yet things tend to be more complicated hormonally for us and the weight is slower to come off.

As many have said before, if you can't do testing, marry the Leptin Rx with the Leaky Gut Rx and do CT. And maybe up your B vitamins to help clear the toxins if you do start CT.

Good luck!

### optimus prime — 2012-03-29T04:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8078>

For me, part of embracing this way of living was to really get 'inside' the thinking and the framework. In other words, STOP counting calories and worrying about if you're exercising enough (these will be outcomes of getting inside the thinking, not ways to do so). This, for me, was essential because I have to believe in what I'm doing so that I can keep going even when I don't lose weight or I don't feel like getting into a freezing cold bath, etc. Also if you understand the principles, you won't worry about exercise because you'll know that until you are LS at the muscle level it won't help (and can hurt). And you'll eat more BAB and not worry.
>
>
>
> It is a huge jump and it's really tricky to let go of the scales being what dominates. (I solve that problem by not having one!). I think it is especially hard for women, for whom there is so much body pressure, and yet things tend to be more complicated hormonally for us and the weight is slower to come off.
>
>
>
> As many have said before, if you can't do testing, marry the Leptin Rx with the Leaky Gut Rx and do CT. And maybe up your B vitamins to help clear the toxins if you do start CT.
>
>
>
> Good luck!

Thank you for the great suggestions, KiwiLauren. Sounds like a good idea to go back and re-read. I haven't read the Leaky Gut Rx yet, but I know I need to implement something for digestion. All my hormones and nutrient levels were low the last time I tested which I think implies an absorption issue. I've done 3 rounds of CT in the tub, but it's a little tricky with the roommates and all, haha. I'll keep at it.

Also, I can afford some testing but not all the ones he suggests. I know I should definitely get a Female Hormonal Panel with Cortisol, vitamin D, B, Iron, Thyroid. What am I missing?

### ealachan — 2012-03-29T04:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8089>

> Also, regarding lunch, There is no way I can eat 3-4 hrs after BAB.

You don't HAVE to eat lunch, if you're not hungry. I've been skipping lunch since about my 3rd day in, because I truly wasn't hungry for it.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T04:58:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8095>

I wanted to throw in that I am obese, and I have only lost 5-7 pounds in nearly a month. BUT, I feel slimmer, I have more energy, I'm sleeping great, I'm rarely hungry between meals, aches and pains are going away, I routinely skip dinner (but will eat it when I'm hungry), and I'm just \*happy\*.

So please hang in there. Weight loss may not come right away, but it will come.

One other thing I might do is to cut out the goat's milk for now especially since you've experienced symptoms of dietary intolerances. I believe it has a similar sugar reaction in our bodies as cow's milk even though some can digest it easier. You can always add it back in later. I cannot tolerate goat's milk or cheese myself.

Also it seems to me like you're doing a LOT of exercising. I might guess that your cortisol isn't functioning well.

### optimus prime — 2012-03-29T05:44:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8120>

> **PaleoSue;8138 wrote:** One other thing I might do is to cut out the goat's milk for now especially since you've experienced symptoms of dietary intolerances. I believe it has a similar sugar reaction in our bodies as cow's milk even though some can digest it easier. You can always add it back in later. I cannot tolerate goat's milk or cheese myself.
>
>
>
> Also it seems to me like you're doing a LOT of exercising. I might guess that your cortisol isn't functioning well.

Hi Sue! The goats milk butter is just a trial with dairy. I thought it might be okay because 1) it's mostly fat, 2) it's goat milk, and 3) it's from pastured goats. It's the first stick I've bought and I am enjoying it sooo much, haha. Now that you mention it though, I've been really stopped up for three days - which is super unusual and I've been eating the goat butter for about 4 or 5 days. So, you might be onto something! Dammit. Regarding the exercise, I've been working out regularly for a good 5 years now and it's so hard for me to stop altogether. It really helps regulate my mood. Maybe I'll just limit it to easy stuff like walks, yoga and 1 day of strength training instead...

I was really happy at first too. Like, super elated. Now I'm a little calmer, but I definitely feel much better overall. And that absolutely makes every day life more tolerable. Just impatient about becoming fabulous again!

Thanks for the thoughts. Best to you!

### Snowyky — 2012-03-29T06:21:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8134>

> **MamaGrok;8085 wrote:** I didn't lose any weight in the first month or two, as I recall. It took me six months to lose ten pounds. It's absolutely not a weight loss "diet," it's a "health recovery diet" that sets you up to begin to lose weight (because weight is just a symptom of underlying problems; it is NOT the problem itself).
>
>
>
> Some people (usually male) do lose from the beginning, but you are by no means alone in not doing so!
>
>
>
> ETA just checked - I weighed the same after 3-4 months as I did at the start.

this has really put things into perspective for me, I need to remember that this is a 'health recovery diet' absolutely correct. I've been putting to much emphasis on losing the weight because I'm morbidly obese and I want this weight gone ... yesterday already. I need to look at the positives which are my clothes fit alot better, I do look thinner and have people comment on it. The other day my husband was watching me while I was making breakfast and he had a quizzical look on his face, he asked me to turn around and stand sideways, yes my legs are getting thinner and there is more definition to my waist, his hands waving through the air as he's describing what he sees, lol I love how descriptive he is.

I'm raising my cortisol because I'm stressing about what's wrong with me, finding a doctor I can trust and one that won't look at my paleo diet and CT as something out of this world but see it as the natural way to eat, closer to nature then all the processed food we keep getting thrown at us. Thank you Mama G for your wise words, you've helped me alot today.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-29T06:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8144>

> **optimus prime;8163 wrote:** Regarding the exercise, I've been working out regularly for a good 5 years now and it's so hard for me to stop altogether. It really helps regulate my mood. Maybe I'll just limit it to easy stuff like walks, yoga and 1 day of strength training instead...

If you have a look through info Dr Kruse writes about cortisol and exercise (especially cardio) you'll see that the 'mood elevation' is really not that at all, but is your body's response to a threat situation. Cortisol goes up, then you get the pain numbing opiods that kick in to help, and voila, elevated mood. But it isn't really. And what you've got underneath is chronic elevated cortisol.

If you're doing the Rx, you may want to start from scratch with it and ditch all exercise (except easy walking and easy yoga perhaps) until you start showing signs of leptin sensitivity. Then start with the HIIT/lifting and see how you go. This has really worked for me. And I loved that I could see slowing down with exercise as a way to be kind to myself and just nurture my body, instead of always having to push it to be trimmer. (So I would sit in the sun and read instead of exercise. That sort of thing).

### Petra — 2012-03-29T12:12:38+0300

Petra

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T14:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8205>

> **optimus prime;8163 wrote: Maybe I'll just limit it to easy stuff like walks, yoga and 1 day of strength training instead...
>
>
>
>
> Sounds great! IDK if this is an issue for you, either, but don't be afraid of losing physical shape when you stop. I was a chronic cardiohead for ten years, then was too fatigued to keep at it (even though I loved it). In the brief windows of good health I've had since then, I've found that just a week or two of some brief harder exercise (like hiking up a steep mountain or swimming hard) periodically will get my ability to do hard exercise (like a LONG hike up a steep mountain with a child on my back, or skiing for 8hrs straight down blacks and double-blacks) back - and sometimes not even that is necessary. It will also return my strength to where it always was (excepting the push-ups - used to do 60 full push-ups with no problems!).
>
>
>
> Even just the every day work of picking up babies and iron skillets has retained 90% of my strength. When the fat finally departed, I saw that my biceps, quads, calves, & abs were still defined under all that flab!
>
>
>

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-29T15:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8218>

> **optimus prime;8077 wrote:** Hey Shijin. Thanks for the encouragement. I too am assuming I have major imbalances. What was the turning point for you? It must have taken a lot for you to keep going while gaining. Also, regarding lunch, There is no way I can eat 3-4 hrs after BAB. I didn't even know about IF when I started skipping lunch. I just simply couldn't eat anymore. Think it's okay to eat a smaller breakfast?

One thing I forgot to mention...stop tracking calories - I tried it doing the LR and it stopped me from getting the LS signals. So don't worry ...just eat.

As for BLD - you can do 2 things. Stick w/the BAB you're eating, and skip lunch, and have dinner. Or back off BAB a tiny bit -so you can have a small lunch (tin of oysters, 5-10 slices of salami..etc)

Since I had adrenal issues I ate lunch b/c it helps speed along the LS signalling, and works to correct cortisol issues, and helps w/pregnenolone steal

Whats your goal? weight loss or optimal health? the LR isn't for weight loss its for righting the metabolic ship of the body... fix that...then the weight will come off...

my weight loss has been extremely slow - I took me 3 mths to loose 3lbs of fat from Dec - March... took me 4 months to loose 2lbs (Sep-Dec)... look at the LR as an opportunity for your body to heal... once its healed It will shed fat like nobodys business!

### texasmom4 — 2012-03-29T18:45:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8329>

This is the most encouraging thread I've read in a long time. Thanks to everyone that contributed!

I've gained weight following the reset, even while doing CT (immersion w/ 20 lbs of ice added) on an almost daily basis. I'm not eating any dairy or any nightshades. I'm doing everything the way Dr K has laid out for us, minus whatever Factor X is!  I'm positive that good things are happening in my body though - great sleep, great skin, great energy, to name a few. Horrible crankiness! My anger is on a hair-trigger switch right now - snapping at husband/snapping at kids. This is NOT normal for me, but I'm seeing it as a good thing. From what I've read, I think I'm doing some major detoxing and hormone shifting. I'll be getting all of my bloodwork and saliva testing results back next week, and then I'll be able to focus in on what exactly my body is needing. (Do we know when Dr K's paid site will open yet? How cool would that be to have HIM hack my labs and map out my path to optimal!)

Again, great thread!

Hey Shijin,

Thanks so much for posting this. I did read your story, but for some reason it didn't all sink in . . . must be this ridiculous brain fog that hangs over my head like a friggin' black cloud and refuses to leave!

Because I track what I eat in an app on my phone to make sure I'm getting the 50 g BAB I do notice calories and I'm still getting somewhat hung up on them. I know I shouldn't pay attention to them at all, but I'll have a day where I notice my calories are on the low side (for me anyway) and get all excited thinking it's going to lead to some weight loss. Problem is I then totally focus on that and stress out if I feel like I need to eat more the next day. Your point has been totally taken here. I am absolutely going to ignore the calories and just look at my fat/protein intake to know I'm doing good on my BAB.

I've also struggled with trying to decide whether or not to eat lunch. Some days I can go without it, but I've had this nagging feeling that it's just not good for my adrenals. So glad to have that confirmed by someone who knows what they're talking about!

If you stick w/the plan you get results. I remember when I was tracking and doing the LR - BAB came in at about 1100 cals, Lunch and dinner would put me anywhere btwn 1800-2800 depending on what I ate.

once I stopped I went by if I was still hungry or if I was full - Once I stopped caring things started happening... its timing, light, and sleep

if you have adrenal problems... 3meals/day is key - you have to do it... even if lunch/dinner are much smaller to fit them in...

today I'm eating lunch/dinner right now...b/c I have class tonight & don't want to eat after 7pm... so I'm having asparagus, mushrooms, roast beef and some salami.. this will hold me until HAB tomorrow morning!

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-30T17:54:57+0300

thanks so much for all of those with experience who chimed in on this thread. That is AWESOME!

Now I'm just not hungry and don't want to force anything down. Plus it would almost always be after 7 p.m. Other nights I'm ravenous, and then I do eat until I'm satisfied.

### thomasina838@gmail.com — 2012-03-30T18:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8812>

**optimus prime;8061 wrote:** It seems like everyone is having hearty success with leptin rx, but I keep losing and gaining the same 3 pounds! With Leptin Rx I lost 5 lbs in the first two weeks but then it pretty much all came back when I got my period. I'm still trying to work it off 2 weeks later. I was really excited when I first began 4.5 weeks ago because I'm so accustomed to eating low cal, VLC, lowfat in order to see any results.

I'm 30, 5'4", 159-162 lbs - heaviest I've ever been. No amount of fasting, dieting or exercise has worked for me the past 3 years (since I stopped my birth control - while on BC I was 133-138). I've been 20-30 lbs up and down since I was 17, accompanied by depression, fatigue, hair loss and a host of other fun stuff. I never really put it all together until a year ago when my progesterone blood test was way below range (1.5, range 5-40). That's when I started looking into hormones, hypothyroidism, etc.

I've been doing strict paleo - no grains, no dairy, little nuts, almost no fruit, and only grass-fed pastured animal proteins. BAB of eggs, coconut oil (or pastured goat milk butter

### BenG — 2012-03-30T18:31:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8824>

> **thomasina;8863 wrote:** your expectations seem unrealistic. i did the reset perfectly for 3 months and my weight did the same thing. down a few; up a few. then i found out that is is often a long time before women see weight loss. i asked Dr. K. about it and he told me to keep going; it would happen. but i got impatient and started JUDDD. still doing the reset. and i have been dropping slowly since then. i have also seen a lot of paleo women say it was 6 months before they saw weight loss.

I've been losing about 2.5-3lbs a week (although I had a stall a couple of weeks ago). Dr. Kruse said the rate is determined by Vitamin D and Testosterone. In my case, my T was clinically low starting out. The LRx + CT brought it up over 100 ng/dl in two months of LRx and 1 month of CT. Dr. Kruse says his went from the 300's to 800's in 3-6 months of CTing. The point is, the more messed up your hormones are, the longer it will take for LRx + CT to normalize/optimize your hormones to make losing weight easier/quicker.

I've heard stories of some women who couldn't lose weight forever and then the LRx+CT fixed their hormones, cortisol, DHEA, sex steroids, thyroid, etc. etc., and they could finally drop the weight they wanted. YMMV depending on where you are starting out from.

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-30T23:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8964>

Well, lucky me that I read all of ms shinjin's antics with trying to rewrite the protocol over on MDA before I started . Tough as it was, this Crossfitter switched to only doing Mobility WOD's from K Starr, housework and maybe walking with a friend. That's it for the 6 weeks. And considering I was a early morning workout type, you can imagine the huge difference on my cortisol and sleep. I ate the BAB and where I absolutely couldn't eat 50 I added the rest as fat grams with macadamias. I wasn't hungry between meals within the first few days - but I presume that was because I was already fairly paleo (- Christmas -oiy!) I am "hungry" around 1-2pm but it feels different than that "I need carbs NOW hunger". It feels different both in the head and the gut. And I eat a few ounces of meat/fish and some veggies and move on. Dinner is variable depending on how hungry I am, but usually bigger than lunch/smaller than breakfast. I am trying to switch now to the post reset protocol.

Yes, I've lost weight - 15lbs in about 6 weeks - BUT I was stringent with the protocol, and my issues are probably different than others. Given my age and previous wine habit - my liver is probably part of my issue. Ditto for the adrenals because I was at the point where my WODs were decreasing in performance, I had pain that persisted after workouts (more than DOMS). I have never shown a thyroid problem on a test - well at least not the stuff my PCP does... lol.

I also supplement - PQQ, CoQ10, NAC, ALA, B6 B12, Folic Acid, Krill oil, fish oil, Vit E, Vit D, magnesium. I went for the whole package reworking those mitochondria at both the muscle and the liver.

As I moved the diet to the post reset stage and adding some CT, I'm sticking with most of the supplements. Some like PQQ, CoQ10, krill because of my age (dr K's recomm). The ALA - simply because it cuts brain fog like a knife through butter for me. The NAC and the Bvits because I still need to rework the body comp and that means detoxing as the fat dies.

So key points

Follow the protocol - yes customize to your situation, but don't use that to justify something that is anathema to the protocol!

This is natural healing - it takes time.

Weight is symptom of other issues... address them not the weight.

And if all you can say is "I need to lose weight", well then it is time for testing.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-30T23:57:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-8995>

I've been thinking about the themes in this thread for a couple of days now and realised that what really hits home for me is the need to try and 'do everything right.' I feel so much pressure to understand it all, to get it right, to not fall prey to my neolithic thoughts, to succeed, to solve the problems, to figure it all out, etc etc. While I believe in this framework and that sustains the effort it takes, at the same time this is an 'old' habit of mine, trying to be 'perfect' (and failing!). I don't know if anyone else can relate to this, but this thread made me realise that I feel constantly under pressure. I try to relax and liberate myself, but I think that feeling that as hard as I'm trying I can never quite understand all the information can put me in a bad place. So it's no surprise to me that if my weight doesn't shift or I start feeling yuck or test results come up with less-than-optimal results, that I (we?) can feel pretty discouraged.

For example, I realised today that my husband (who is following along with keto paleo and CT, despite having read almost nothing on the site) is not only losing weight at high speed but is feeling little hunger and is totally cold adapted already. So I am cold all the time AND eating more than he does (I am 5'3", he is 6'1") and I had a bit of a freak out. I feel like I should be on the remedial bus to the School of JK.

there is no need to get it all right all at once. you dont have to understand it all to do any one thing correctly. if you did only the high protein breakfast every single day. 50 - 75 grms protein within 30 mins of arising then many things will just fall in place by themselves. like the disappearance of hunger or nibbling during the day. and i know that men often start losing weight right away but it takes a lot longer for women. i did the leptin reset for 3 months straight without really losing anything. i asked Dr. K. about it and he said keep going and it would happen. but i didnt want to wait so i incorporated JUDDD and did start dropping slowly. 6 weeks 10 lbs. still doing the reset. since them i found out that it is often 6 months before women start to see weight loss. and that being cold will respond to the cold therapy more than you think possible. but start slowly. with just the face in cold water according to the cold thermogenesis protocol. i am using reusable fake ice cubes in sheets in my bed over my clothes. having my feet warm while i do this made it possible to try. and wearing a sweatshirt helped too. a lot of women who say they didnt lose on the reset see that fhange when they try the CT.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T17:01:51+0300

So I am cold all the time AND eating more than he does (I am 5'3", he is 6'1") and I had a bit of a freak out. I feel like I should be on the remedial bus to the School of JK.

hang in there Kiwi, I also feel the same way you do, I feel I need to have everything perfect, constantly reading, trying to understand how it all fits together, letting my neolithic thoughts take over at times, also letting CW docs affect how I think, they are telling me what I'm doing is crazy and wrong and I'll never get better if I keep following these protocols. I know that this will work eventually, I know that things are not going to change overnight, I was driving myself crazy trying to find a doctor who would order the tests that I feel I need but the stress from constantly being told no even after I've brought in the printed out information from Dr K's site, has left me feeling lost and sick of it all.

I read something MamaGrok said awhile back about these protocol's are a "health recovery diet" and I realized I was focusing on the wrong things, I now have put the scale and tape measures away and am relying on how my clothes fit, how good I feel, am I sleeping better, am I satisfied eating this way, no more cravings, can I last between meals without feeling hungry, all those points I've answered yes. I know my body is re sculpturing and people around me are noticing it, I need to give myself a break and relax and know this is the best way to live for my health and the weight will come off.

It's tough because we have been brainwashed into thinking that the weight should just melt off if we believe what we see on the tv commericals for diet aids... for some it does, but for some of us it doesn't and we are the ones that Dr. K has done all this for, I for one am going to give him the benefit of the doubt and relax and believe it will come off, meanwhile I'm getting healthier by the day

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-04-02T14:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-10260>

Not my intention to stress Kiwi Lauren into "must do it right".... that raises cortisol! My intent is more to say that if you aren't having success, it may be that there are tweaks to be made to make sure you are following it better. And to echo what has been said many, many times... this is first about health, not weight. Longevity, not performance. IMHO, I would go get some testing done, and some good BMI measurements. And when you get LS, start recording your exercise in a log. You naturally want to see results. But that means picking some VALID metrics, collecting them and then following them. And there are alot of people in this forum - even those with SW's & GW's in their signature line that will tell you weight is NOT the most important metric. It is about as helpful at times as total cholesterol - but that's a whole other story

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-04T00:24:04+0300

### BenG — 2012-04-06T13:36:17+0300

I confirmed with him HS-CRP slows/stops weight loss (blocks leptin). When I was detoxing during CT, my weight loss stopped and my HSCRP shot up from 0.5 to 5.0. After the 5th week of CT, (he said it takes 4-6 weeks for liver to up regulate toxin clearance from CT), my symptoms died down, and now I lost 2lbs the 5th week and 4lbs the sixth week of CT (9th week of LRx).

### optimus prime — 2012-04-07T06:46:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-12389>

Woweee! I've been away and I've really enjoyed reading all of these posts today. I'm inspired by all the great information and encouraging words. I can't believe how supportive everyone is. You've certainly helped ease my frustration and remind me what's most important. Thank you! In fact, you've made me feel so comfortable that I'm just dying to divulge further intimate details about the inner workings of my body!

I just got some blood work done and I'd like some help decipher the results. I have no idea what many of these values mean and I want to be well prepared when I go back for my follow up. I haven't had any hormones tested yet. I'll be doing that next week. For now I just have the results of my "General Health Panel". Below are my thyroid, lipids and few other things. I posted the rest of the results to a google doc. It is set to accept edits, so you can type comments directly on the spreadsheet if you like. My questions are listed below the results on this post. Here is the link to my full lab report: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0AhpZKlPqqhpFdHBtTWVnZXp1Y2Q5OFA5dWJBOWVBRXc.

\-B12 - holy cow, I have never tested my B levels before, but I guess that's one thing that's responding to the supplements...Weird. Should I stop them?

\*Questions

\-I know it's hard to determine anything without my hormone levels, but I mainly just want to know about the cholesterol and inflammation relationship. Could this be an indication of something else going on?

\-Does anyone know what would cause a chronically low TSH with glaring hypothyroid symptoms?

\-Could low D be related to low TSH? What else can I do to get my Vitamin D up? I've been supplementing for at least a year and the level hasn't really budged. Also, not much sun in Chicago. I feel like there's something else causing this

\-Finally, I thought I might have absorption issues but the high vitamin B and improved Iron are making me second guess that. I'm also left thinking that something else is causing my D to be low....

Reminder: I'm 30, 5'4", 160 lbs (still no weight loss)

I hope you've all enjoyed the 2nd installment of my novella series. Thanks for reading!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-07T16:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-12441>

From my non-doctor POV, cholesterol, ferritin, & B12 are solid (might lay off the B12 until next time you check; your level is ideal). TPO and Antith-Ab indicate Hashi's is unlikely.

> **optimus prime;12458 wrote:**
>
>
>
> \-Been testing thyroid for 3 years now and TSH, T3 and T4 are always bottom of the range. Confusing because I have ALL of the hypothyroid symptoms.

Without a doubt, you're hypo. TSH is usually worthless. If free T3 is low in the range, and \*especially\* if you have symptoms, you need more T3, period. Get thee to stopthethyroidmadness.com, pronto! Check their info on how iron can affect thyroid, too. They also explain how hypo + low TSH usually indicates a pituitary problem, rather than no problem.

Could you have fat malabsorption, prohibiting the D from rising? Or perhaps the form of D you're taking isn't working for you?

possible it's due to 1 year of meat eating after 4 yrs of pescetarianism)

There are several reasons LDL can be quite high with good HDL & Trig's, especially if it's the good (fluffy) kind, which you HDL & Trigs indicate is likely the case for your LDL. Could be you're finally getting enough fat to increase LDL, but not yet able to make the proper hormones from it, b/c of too low DHEA, or pregnenolone steal from cortisol problems, for instance. Can't remember the others off the top ofmy head.

Oh, yeah, another would be fat loss, but that's not happening for you quite yet.

### BenG — 2012-04-08T10:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-12614>

Your VitaminD is too low for Dr. K. He likes it 70-100ng/dl range. Either get out in the sun or supp (or both). Your LDL is most likely pattern A (light fluffy = good) since your Trigs are low and your HDL is high. IF you're eating Keto, higher FBG is normal. CT can fix the Thyroid issues.

> **optimus prime;12458 wrote:** Woweee! I've been away and I've really enjoyed reading all of these posts today. I'm inspired by all the great information and encouraging words. I can't believe how supportive everyone is. You've certainly helped ease my frustration and remind me what's most important. Thank you! ### optimus prime — 2012-04-09T17:51:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-13003>

> **MamaGrok;12510 wrote:** From my non-doctor POV, cholesterol, ferritin, & B12 are solid (might lay off the B12 until next time you check; your level is ideal). TPO and Antith-Ab indicate Hashi's is unlikely.
>

> Without a doubt, you're hypo. TSH is usually worthless. If free T3 is low in the range, and \*especially\* if you have symptoms, you need more T3, period. Get thee to stopthethyroidmadness.com, pronto! Check their info on how iron can affect thyroid, too. They also explain how hypo + low TSH usually indicates a pituitary problem, rather than no problem.
>
>
>
> Could you have fat malabsorption, prohibiting the D from rising? Or perhaps the form of D you're taking isn't working for you?

That's a great site, and one that I've spend a good amount of time reading through. I actually came to the same conclusion as you did, narrowing things down to the pituitary. So I went to see a pituitary endocrinologist. Big mistake. After an hour of discussing my symptoms and a brief exam, he said there is absolutely nothing wrong with me. I even have a hard, peanut-like bump in my neck in the thyroid region. He couldn't find it so he just dismissed it as a lymph node! He didn't even feel it! RAR. Anyway, I'm hoping my hormone labs will reveal something that I can work on.

Never thought about fat malabsorption because I have difficulty losing weight... How can I test for fat malabsorption? Should I test for other fat soluble vitamins A and E?

Thanks Mama!

PS - I didn't realize there ia a whole forum for labs, so I re-posted over there. In case this doesn't really belong here.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-04-12T03:40:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-14044>

> **Shijin13;8474 wrote: If you stick w/the plan you get results. I remember when I was tracking and doing the LR - BAB came in at about 1100 cals, Lunch and dinner would put me anywhere btwn 1800-2800 depending on what I ate.
>
> once I stopped I went by if I was still hungry or if I was full - Once I stopped caring things started happening... its timing, light, and sleep
>
>
>
> if you have adrenal problems... 3meals/day is key - you have to do it... even if lunch/dinner are much smaller to fit them in...
>
>
>
> today I'm eating lunch/dinner right now...b/c I have class tonight & don't want to eat after 7pm... so I'm having asparagus, mushrooms, roast beef and some salami.. this will hold me until HAB tomorrow morning!
> I SO needed to read this...I can freak out over how many calories my BAB is and how much I eat in a day. Seems like a lot some day (like today)! And I sometimes stress myself over "geez, I still NEED lunch." But I am working on rehabbing my adrenals, so I feel better knowing it's common to need lunch. Thanks!
>
>
> > **cgk;9016 wrote:**
> >
> > So key points
> >
> > Follow the protocol - yes customize to your situation, but don't use that to justify something that is anathema to the protocol!
> >
> > This is natural healing - it takes time.
> >
> > Weight is symptom of other issues... address them not the weight.
> >
> > And if all you can say is "I need to lose weight", well then it is time for testing.
>
> good points,thanks!
>
> KiwiLauren;9047 wrote:** I've been thinking about the themes in this thread for a couple of days now and realised that what really hits home for me is the need to try and 'do everything right.' I feel so much pressure to understand it all, to get it right, to not fall prey to my neolithic thoughts, to succeed, to solve the problems, to figure it all out, etc etc. While I believe in this framework and that sustains the effort it takes, at the same time this is an 'old' habit of mine, trying to be 'perfect' (and failing!). I don't know if anyone else can relate to this, but this thread made me realise that I feel constantly under pressure. I try to relax and liberate myself, but I think that feeling that as hard as I'm trying I can never quite understand all the information can put me in a bad place. So it's no surprise to me that if my weight doesn't shift or I start feeling yuck or test results come up with less-than-optimal results, that I (we?) can feel pretty discouraged.
>
>
>
> For example, I realised today that my husband (who is following along with keto paleo and CT, despite having read almost nothing on the site) is not only losing weight at high speed but is feeling little hunger and is totally cold adapted already. So I am cold all the time AND eating more than he does (I am 5'3", he is 6'1") and I had a bit of a freak out. I feel like I should be on the remedial bus to the School of JK.

I am the same way - either I am playing the role of the "A" student or I feeling like I'm flunking out! Why do we put so much pressure on ourselves?! I keep acting like it's a big deal if I pick the wrong supplement for gosh sake. Lately I tell myself that you have to use trial and error to see what works and what doesn't!

Thanks this whole thread was super helpful!

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-18T10:24:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-45507>

Thank goodness I found this thread, I am fairly typical, worrying about the amount of food I am eating (hard to lose CW on that )and really frustrated that I am gaining rather than losing. Of course, the reason I am doing this is to repair my system. Thanks so much for pointing the blindingly obvious out to me !!

I am so looking forward to losing some weight though.

### Destiny — 2012-08-18T15:54:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-45525>

> **somewhatchubby;45880 wrote:** Thank goodness I found this thread, I am fairly typical, worrying about the amount of food I am eating (hard to lose CW on that )and really frustrated that I am gaining rather than losing. Of course, the reason I am doing this is to repair my system. Thanks so much for pointing the blindingly obvious out to me !!
>
> I am so looking forward to losing some weight though.

Be ready that it might take some time. I have been doing this for 10 months and lost only 7 lbs., my husband lost 43 lbs. during the same period of time. So it is individual depending on many other things.

In my case, my hormones and thyroid are a mess so my journey is not straightforward but I am moving forward!

### somewhatchubby — 2012-08-27T00:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aaah-minimal-weight-loss-after-4-5-weeks-disappointment-and-fury.506/post-47361>

hi destiny, my DH today complained that he had only lost 3lbs in two weeks. I am 2lbs heavier.However I know that I will keep up the effort longer than he will, he is already focussing on what he cant have rather than what he can.

I had a couple of sleep blips, not sure why, first night I just couldnt switch off and didnt get to sleep until midnight, then the next night I was so stuffed ,despite not eating that much that at bedtime I was still stuffed and had some indegestion which is very unusual for me. I took a smaller brekky today and was careful to eat modestly tonight, so I hope for a good night's sleep.My appetite is definitely getting smaller.
