# Thread: Is there a supplement to heal a twitch?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bontask — 2012-03-29T02:51:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8027>

Since beginning LR about 4 weeks ago, my fibromyalgia (which only rarely flared up in past few years) has returned big time. Also suffering from an aggravating twitch in an eyelid. I am considering using CORVALEN again ([http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b](http://http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b) but I'm not sure if that is a good move or not. Years ago, the corvalen was an effective tool for me to end the fibromyalgia symptoms.

I barely have a grasp on the science of LR (I am studying!!!) and would sincerely appreciate any insight, suggestions about why the onset of the fibromyalgia again, is the twich related? and is corvalen a mistake???

Many thanks in advance.

Barbara

### L8F — 2012-03-29T03:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8059>

Often twitches are mag deficiency--which is really really common.

### ealachan — 2012-03-29T04:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8080>

I second the magnesium suggestion. It has REALLY helped my restless leg issues. It also helps me drop off to sleep more easily. I take 500mg in the evening about an hour before bed.

### shilohman — 2012-03-29T04:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8084>

> **bontask;8070 wrote:** Since beginning LR about 4 weeks ago, my fibromyalgia (which only rarely flared up in past few years) has returned big time. Also suffering from an aggravating twitch in an eyelid. I am considering using CORVALEN again ([http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b](http://http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b) but I'm not sure if that is a good move or not. Years ago, the corvalen was an effective tool for me to end the fibromyalgia symptoms.
>
>
>
> I barely have a grasp on the science of LR (I am studying!!!) and would sincerely appreciate any insight, suggestions about why the onset of the fibromyalgia again, is the twich related? and is corvalen a mistake???
>
>
>
> Many thanks in advance.
>
>
>
> Barbara

Have you been tested for celiac disease or leaky gut? Do you eat any diary?

### bontask — 2012-03-29T19:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8352>

> **shilohman;8127 wrote:** Have you been tested for celiac disease or leaky gut? Do you eat any diary?

I'm on Armour Thyroid, I've been tested for celiac, it was negative but except for an occasional lapse don't eat grains at all. I don't know about the leaky gut and I do eat dairy - goat milk yogurt every once in a while and cream - I try to avoid milk. I have a healthy diet - prepare meals from scratch. Trying to get my head around the science of this so that I can properly select the right supplements - it would be lovely to have a collected list somewhere - I'm parsing through posts to try to do a better job with that!

It sounds like corvalen would be a good idea then (Each serving contains 5 grams ribose, 40mg elemental magnesium (in the form of 800mg magnesium gluconate) and 240mg malate).

Thank you all.

Barbara

### REN2BRAINS — 2012-03-29T19:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-8361>

I had eyelid twitches when my thyroid was out of whack with Hashimoto's. Started on Armour and have never had them again.

### thomasina838@gmail.com — 2012-04-01T18:09:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-9800>

> **bontask;8070 wrote:** Since beginning LR about 4 weeks ago, my fibromyalgia (which only rarely flared up in past few years) has returned big time. Also suffering from an aggravating twitch in an eyelid. I am considering using CORVALEN again ([http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b](http://http://www.bioenergy.com/store/Main.aspx?html=ds&topic=b) but I'm not sure if that is a good move or not. Years ago, the corvalen was an effective tool for me to end the fibromyalgia symptoms.
>
>
>
> I barely have a grasp on the science of LR (I am studying!!!) and would sincerely appreciate any insight, suggestions about why the onset of the fibromyalgia again, is the twich related? and is corvalen a mistake???
>
>
>
> Many thanks in advance.
>
>
>
> Barbara

there is one other thing to consider for nerve involvement. that is lecithin. you can get granules flavored with pineapple/coconut by kirkman labs. doesnt taste bad. you can mix it in liquids or just eat it off the spoon. the covrerings of the nerves are 90% lecithin. so not unreasonable to try to add this to your supplement intake.

### Optigal — 2012-04-05T15:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-11687>

I would also suggest you check out your b vitamins especially 6 & 12 & folate, hope it improves for you

### selma@decna.no — 2012-04-13T14:56:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-supplement-to-heal-a-twitch.507/post-14658>

Magnesium helped me. I'm on Erfa for hashimoto's and hashimoto's patients often have chronic magnesium loss and celiac (or gluten intolerance) which depletes the body of minerals ... And loose stools will also rid the body of magnesium. (My toe-cramps come back each time I go through that.)

Just check which magnesium you try if you decide to try. The bioavailability is very different between the types.

# Thread: Love-story J. Kruse-style
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-story-j-kruse-style.508/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glamorama — 2012-03-29T03:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/love-story-j-kruse-style.508/post-8043>

When I first met my husband, 7 years ago, I was 25 and quite fit. I wore these tight skinny jeans on the night we first met, and because they were so expensive, I kept those jeans over the years even though they didn't fit me very well as I kept putting on weight (sounds familiar, anyone?).

I have a photo taken of me on my 25th birthday, wearing those jeans, and I always use to marvel at how tiny my bum looked in that photo. I had no muffin-top, no spillage...! I use to think "_Those were the days, I'll never wear them again, but I have the memories". Anyway, I put them on today, and they fit perfectly without_ a muffin-top. I can only assume that it means my bum look tiny again. And I can marvel at the fact that these are the jeans I wore when I first met The One. Thats my love-story Jack Kruse-style! You got me back into those wicked jeans! My husband thinks its brilliant- told you I got a good bum in those jeans, haha! LOL

The moral of the story is: I've lost so much weight but my brain hasn't caught up. I don't look in the mirror and see and slender person. But I KNOW that those jeans only fit when I was fit and slim. So the fact that they fit again now pulls my brain into the process: "Yes, you have lost a lot of weight!!! By eating fat!"(my favourite part).

By the way you look beautiful in your avatar!

# Thread: Children  CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T04:40:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-8086>

This is the thread where I want to hear how you're implementing CT with your kids, how you might plan to introduce it in the future, what you said that interested them, what was successful, what wasn't, anything to do with kids & the cold!

I just took my nice, cold bath, and to my surprise, my two year old hopped right in! I thought she would recoil at the cold, but she loved it (it came up to her bottom, but not tummy). She played in it for at least 15 minutes, only getting out when I got out.

To my further surprise, when she got out, she wasn't cold. Normally, when she gets out of the bath, she is freezing and can't wait to get warm clothes on.

My seven year old son kept asking to put his legs in, too, and did (this is why I wear a bathing suit while CT'ing, lol).**

They are already TOTALLY CT-adapted just from being born!** (and probably, from having eaten well since birth for the baby and for the last 4-5 years for the older one, and b/c I've never been one to insist on coats or shoes if they don't feel the need for them).

What are you doing with your babies (young or old)?

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-29T15:26:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-8221>

When I run the kids bath I've been making it a bit colder... just introducing it slowly... now if they want to wear shorts/short sleeves I let them... and I \* foreget\* their coats

I figure they'll start to jump in when they see mama doing a cold bath on the weekends...

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-29T19:04:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-8342>

The last week we've started to use our pool which has been ranging from 52-56 degrees. Its been so nice not to bother drawing a bath. Anyhow, our 7 year old jumps right in and it doesn't seem to bother him at all. He recoup's faster than we do. My 3 year old wants nothing to do with the cold water. He stood next to the pool crying the other day because I wouldn't go in the house and get him "snow gloves." The two babies (10 months) I've been bring outside without bundling them up.

I've never been big on insisting on coats, probably because I've always found them to be more of a nuisance and don't usually wear them. Now that I know about CT I will recommend them even less.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-30T06:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-8638>

Love this thread, hope more chime in.

My almost 6 yr old has seen me doing it, and is kinda making his own version. He has said everyday since he started "I need to do my cold therapy now!", to him mostly that is standing in the cold water longer than the last time. We use a big stop watch on the ipad and he digs it. He also sits down and times that, but only wants to do that every couple of days.

One of my major queries is if someone is in good shape, like a paleo raised/seasonal child who isn't really screwed up - do they need to go keto other than the dead of winter?

### MJ* — 2012-03-30T23:52:42+0300

Her diet is improving, but she is a picky eater so no fish or seafood of any kind (which is why it makes sense to me that her o3s would be low)...lots of organic eggs and meat (I have not found a great place to get grass fed beef yet that isn't a fortune, love some ideas on that) I get what I can from Trader Joes. She loves berries and most fruit and eats a lot of salads, and likes cucumbers, spinach, peppers, broccoli, and sweet potatoes, and we are working on eliminating wheat but I'm not sure she'll do it completely being a teenager - pizza is huge when they go out...I am having a hard time taking milk from her diet, because that was one of the components that her doctor has her on for her growth hormone deficiency, and it actually helped a lot, she grew with dairy even before the therapy began, and the combination of both have been like a blessing. She was always very thin and organic whole milk, cream, butter I get the Kerrygold, cheese and ice cream have been helping her put on the weight she needed for growth - which is 5 lbs. for every inch of growth (that's what all children need). She stopped the ice cream awhile ago though. She's almost completely given up sugar which was really hard, as it's so abundant with friends at school.

I agree those were pricey, but if she eats them I'm all in...I will check out those other brands...we have a really great vitamin shop near here I'll check with them. Thanks for the suggestion!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T00:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9009>

> **MJ\*;8881 wrote:** .she was icing herself like crazy and then developed red itchy bumps on her arm. I had her stop the CT for a bit, because I was concerned that it was a detox and wasn't sure if it was ok for her to continue...is this normal, and should she CT through it or let it heal first?

She wasn't icing herself in the spot where the bumps came up, right? Yes, children need to detox, too. The GAPShelp yahoo list is full of parents trying to help their kids detox crap gently. As long as it isn't bothering her enough to make her quit, I'd just do it. Detox can be quick and more intense, or slower and less intense. You get to pick! Bumps are pretty easy to deal with!

Fermented CLO isn't in any store I've heard of, unfortunately. (The founder said Whole Foods approached him, but he said no, can't remember why.) Best prices are at Green Pastures' web site if you can order it in bulk. Easy for my family!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T00:50:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9011>

BTW, if milk helps her grow (as it did my son), I bet she needs K2 (has to be animal sources to help with bones). Best sources are fCLO (surprise!  ), pastured liver, pastured egg yolks, pastured butter, and fish roe. Easiest sources are yolks & butter, obviously!

My kids get two eggs/day, all the Kerrygold they want, and 1/4tsp fCLO per day. (Notice the really small serving size - it has turned out to be cheaper than any other CLO option that I've found because of this.) The fCLO/BO combo is really helpful for those who need big bone work.

You could also supplement MK4 K2, but I'm still wary, b/c my understanding is that all MK4 is synthetic, and I have a phobia of synthetic fat soluble vitamins, lol.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-31T03:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9057>

How is the flavor of the fCLO? I'm always wondering if my kids will take it well. Though, they are the ones that love seafood and I have a hard time not gagging on it when I eat it because I'm so repulsed by it. How do you determine how much your child needs? What are the benefits of it over regular CLO?

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T04:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9076>

Definitely look through the last eek of the "monster thread" here on Optimal Living, b/c we answered those questions and more! If you can't dig it up (try going back a week, then hitting control-F on every page and searching on clo or "liver oil"), I can answer them here. Basically, if you love seafood, it's not bad at all; if you hate it (like me), just hold your breath and take the liquid, b/c caps give burps later, and "regular" CLO is actually modern processed CLO, while fCLO is the real thing, the way it was done for millennia until factories came around and changed it. You also use way less than most use of CLO.

### trishalinn@gmail.com — 2012-03-31T04:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9079>

Basically, if you love seafood, it's not bad at all; if you hate it (like me), just hold your breath and take the liquid, b/c caps give burps later, and "regular" CLO is actually modern processed CLO, while fCLO is the real thing, the way it was done for millennia until factories came around and changed it. You also use way less than most use of CLO.

Thanks MamaGrok for the info! I will also look on the monster thread and see what was said. I know this is off topic, but as a seafood hater have you been getting much/any down since following Dr Kruse's protocol? I've been trying, but it just about makes me sick. I used to not mind shrimp and real mild fish, but since my pregnancy with my first son just the smell of it nauseates me!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T04:41:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9086>

I've been eating it on Fridays because it's Lent, and decided I will continue eating it on Fridays throughout the year, for multiple purposes! I \*have\* to eat wild-caught fish. The quality difference is HUGE, like the difference between meh & pastured lamb. Sockeye salmon is very tolerable, most white fishes. I had deep-fried red snapper in Puerto Rico and it was fabulous!

I even got a whole can of smoked oysters, another of kippered herring, and another of sardines down this morning (in a broth, covered in cinnamon, coriander, and cumin) - definite progress! I never, ever, ever could tolerate fish before.

BTW, quality, fresh fish shouldn't smell fishy at all.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T05:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-9109>

Nah ... 15 years ago I was eating Froot Loops for breakfast and bagels for (a lowfat!) lunch, sugar yogurt all afternoon, and frozen lowfat meals for dinner.

I do think finding exactly the waya given person likes eggs is very helpful. Once I found my sweet spot, I \*never\* get tired of eggs. Love them as much now as when I started them 2-3 years ago, every single day. Lightly scrambled in CO or butter, with salt & pepper. One child likes them over medium, another with cheese, another omelets every day. Doesn't bother me, just as easy to cook them all, and they keep eating them!

So my son (almost 6) has been hopping in my baths after me. Usually water around 65 degrees. He is timing and trying to increase his times. Just does it as he feels it.

One interesting thing, may or may not be related - but before going all paleo with him, he had some lower arm rashyness and he had a bout of athletes foot when he was 4ish.

On Friday his arm rash showed up out of nowhere, as did the athletes foot irritated skin. I wonder if he was storing something that the CT released.

### BeingVenus — 2012-05-06T15:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/children-ct.509/post-21758>

Though you guys might enjoy some more family CTing from Moscow. [http://www.morz.h12.ru/club/kid-ice-swimming-picture-eng.html](http://http://www.morz.h12.ru/club/kid-ice-swimming-picture-eng.html)

I love that they refer to ice swimming as "Hardening" [http://www.morz.h12.ru/club/polar-bear-picture-eng.html](http://http://www.morz.h12.ru/club/polar-bear-picture-eng.html)

# Thread: Exercise and Hcg and CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2012-03-29T04:47:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-8090>

I will be starting a round early April. I've been CTing for a few weeks, going slow, had hives.

I want to do this round continuing my normal exercising, walking/running once or twice per week, yoga/pilates, some strength training body exercises like pushups, planks, lunges, and squats using body weight.

I know, I know...people say not to, but I don't want to lose muscle and I'm not doing something new just what I"ve been doing. Last round I did my body became worse proportioned and I never did lose the inches with the pounds everyone was talking about. I'd like to exercise when I feel up to it and I won't if I'm too tired or don't feel good.

Any thoughts? I've actually had to exercise less when I started CT because I felt tired and weak from the CT so I took it easy a lot of days. But I'm feeling ok this week and exercising normally again.

### indigogirl — 2012-03-29T05:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-8100>

Hi Claudia,

When I did my first round of HCG last year, through an MD, I was told to continue whatever exercise I was already doing.

Who says not to exercise?

I know on the LR you are not supposed to exercise until you are LS.

### colleencoble — 2012-03-31T04:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-9091>

you can do whatever you're used to. There's no ban on exercise.  What form of hcg did you use and dose?

### Claudia — 2012-04-03T04:22:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-10652>

I have done Rx sublingual and take .5 ml twice per day. I'm still waiting for my hcg to arrive. I tried JUDDD for 5 weeks but lost nothing and decided to order the hcg. I've been doing CT for several weeks, too. I should have written down when I started but I didn't so I just know it's been several weeks, maybe 4-5 weeks. I took a CT break because I got a bad cold and I'm back to CT today hoping it will help with the cold. I just couldn't bear to get into the cold bath feeling so crummy. I feel better so it wasn't as hard to do it today and I did it mid-day instead of at night, easier to do I think.

### colleencoble — 2012-04-03T04:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/exercise-and-hcg-and-ct.510/post-10656>

Just make sure the exercise you do falls in with Dr. Kruse's guidelines too. That's the only thing.

# Thread: Super Slow is working Super Awesome !!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaleoMom — 2012-03-29T04:48:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8091>

Unlike the many hard core CT's on this forum, I'm a wuss when it come to cold water. Funny since I keep the house cooler than most, my bedroom is about 50 and I don't overdress outside, but water? I'm a wuss.

Soooo, I've started a super slow approach that is working quite nicely. I did face dunks and tons of ice packs with no problem, but trying the baths and showers was brutal. I decided that in only 1 month I could lower the water temp by a single degree daily and reach my goal. Day 1- 80 degrees, day 2- 79 deg. etc..... Today was 72 deg. (1 hour baths)

What I've noticed. First day I was shivering like mad in 80 deg water (sad, I know) but by even the second day I couldn't believe how much more comfortable it was. Each day I go down a degree and yet it always feels warmer than the day before! Amazing!

I'm often doing more spot CT in bed after the bath too for another hour. I'm feeling warmer during the day, burning up at night and often sleeping with no sheet or just a sheet at night even in our cold bedroom.

Just thought I'd share to help out anyone else like me who wants to do this but just can't jump right into 55 or lower degree water. I'm still getting the signs of getting cold adapted, and like I said earlier, it will only take a month to get painlessly down to 50 deg.

The only negative I've noticed is daytime fatigue, needing more sleep and not feeling rested on waking (all things that were awesome for me before CT)

### Adria — 2012-03-29T16:41:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8251>

Great to hear, Paleomom! I'm also going my own slow route with CT. I'm up to 20min in the cold bath 55 degrees at CT wk#5. But seeing benefits already !!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T17:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8260>

How are you decreasing and measuring the water? My amazon.com thermogun seems to be measuring the tub, not the water, so I can only go with the measurement after sitting in the water a long while, when the tub has cooled down to match the water.

### differentstory — 2012-03-29T18:40:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8327>

> **PaleoMom;8134 wrote:** Unlike the many hard core CT's on this forum, I'm a wuss when it come to cold water. Funny since I keep the house cooler than most, my bedroom is about 50 and I don't overdress outside, but water? I'm a wuss.
>
>
>
> Soooo, I've started a super slow approach that is working quite nicely. I did face dunks and tons of ice packs with no problem, but trying the baths and showers was brutal. I decided that in only 1 month I could lower the water temp by a single degree daily and reach my goal. Day 1- 80 degrees, day 2- 79 deg. etc..... Today was 72 deg. (1 hour baths)
>
>
>
> What I've noticed. First day I was shivering like mad in 80 deg water (sad, I know) but by even the second day I couldn't believe how much more comfortable it was. Each day I go down a degree and yet it always feels warmer than the day before! Amazing!
>
>
>
> I'm often doing more spot CT in bed after the bath too for another hour. I'm feeling warmer during the day, burning up at night and often sleeping with no sheet or just a sheet at night even in our cold bedroom.
>
>
>
> Just thought I'd share to help out anyone else like me who wants to do this but just can't jump right into 55 or lower degree water. I'm still getting the signs of getting cold adapted, and like I said earlier, it will only take a month to get painlessly down to 50 deg.
>
>
>
> The only negative I've noticed is daytime fatigue, needing more sleep and not feeling rested on waking (all things that were awesome for me before CT)

This WAS going to be my plan too, but my opinion is that it was so much easier to just go whole hog and drop the temp down. I started at 80 and it was brutal. Did that a few times. Went to 60 and it was brutal. 3 days of that and I was fine. Now the water fluctuates anywhere between 50 and 60 and I don't really notice. Just my opinion.

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-29T19:51:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8370>

Keep it up PaleoMom; whatever works, works. I am transitioning too and it gets easier. You can get there but I am finding it takes patience and persistence. I listen to my body more these days and that helps with more successes than losses.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-29T20:42:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8392>

> **MamaGrok;8306 wrote:** How are you decreasing and measuring the water? My amazon.com thermogun seems to be measuring the tub, not the water, so I can only go with the measurement after sitting in the water a long while, when the tub has cooled down to match the water.

I'm using a thermometer made for putting in the fridge and freezer. I wasn't sure it was even water safe, but seems to be doing fine. It is slow to adjust though, so that can be challenging. It works fine though for me and it won't be long before I just go full cold and don't think about it.

### PaleoMom — 2012-03-29T20:44:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8394>

I'm just such a comfort person and non adrenaline junkie! It's great to find an easy way to do this without feeling like I'm torturing myself. I have patience and I've got plenty of time to cold adapt before the local lake is ready for swimming (they raise the water level in May)

### primat — 2012-03-29T20:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/super-slow-is-working-super-awesome.511/post-8399>

# Thread: I'm getting stronger!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kiera2 — 2012-03-29T05:55:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8125>

I am just blown away with how quickly I'm making progress. Only a few weeks ago, I couldn't lift anything due to degenerative disc disease. I couldn't hold 2Kg at arms length. Then, after 2 weeks of CT, I could hold out 4Kg, with no pain, and no following migraine. First time since 2003!

Now, I can raise 5Kg _in each hand_ at arms length! Both out in front, and at each side. Yep, 10kg! Unbelievable!

It's a little shakey, and I couldn't lift even a few grams more; and I'm so pleased!

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-29T06:20:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8132>

That's great coriander. I'm getting stronger too. One thing I've noticed is that I don't get sore after a workout anymore. It used to be, "no pain, no gain" but I'm liking the switch to: "no pain, ... good!"

I'm working on trying to do a pull up someday,

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-29T06:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8135>

Congrats Coriander! I love to hear these stories of such success. I look forward to great leaps in strength for me too!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-29T07:09:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8150>

Yay! Thats wodnerful!!

### Petra — 2012-03-29T12:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8178>

It confirms what I see in my body.

Thank you for posting this!

Glamorama,I'm sure you also made progress in the heavy lifting division, right?

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-29T13:40:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8187>

Awesome! That is an amazing story.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T07:48:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8652>

> **Petra;8222 wrote:** It confirms what I see in my body.

> Thank you for posting this!
>
>
>
> Glamorama,I'm sure you also made progress in the heavy lifting division, right?

Yes. Insane increases, but I didn't start out like Coriander, who had disc-issues. When I started, my squat was 15 kilos. 5 weeks later its 40 kilos.

### Grottenolm — 2012-03-30T08:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-getting-stronger.514/post-8659>

# Thread: The un-official Telomere before and after thread
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 83
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-29T08:06:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-8163>

Hi All,

I know some of you can afford, and have ordered, telomere length testing to prove out what you are doing. It would be a huge benefit to the rest of us if you could share that info, as much of it as you feel comfortable sharing. It seems like some info would be ideal to have, and others could help clarify this list (hint, dr. k)

\- real age (or use a variable)

\- telomere age (or just use your variable and a +/- should be good enough)

\- before body composition and diet as of initial telomere testing

\- lifestyle before (light exposure and sleep, exercise, work)

\- diet during (especially if you are consuming any non-paleo foods or foods not matched to the canonical season)

\- lifestyle during

\- ending body composition

\- ending telomere length (again with the variable or a real value)

If I were to do the testing I would be very strict on the protocol and test again in 1-3 months. However, it would be great if people that are doing before and after are a range of strictness. Especially if some are just unable to follow the protocol or the diet or get optimal sleep and they still publish their results. In fact, that will be the most valuable info.

So please, help the community. I can't spend the ~$600 on before/after testing. If there is a group of people all willing to pitch in $600/n I will do a strict program and document everything for the greater good. I'm good at tracking stuff and I'm also vigilant about my lifestyle and diet factors so I could even try 2-3 protocols over a 4-6 month period and have multiple telomere tests, based on the consensus of the payees. I actually may be a good test case because I'm already lean from over a year of good seasonal diet and light exposure so the CT is almost the only variable in here (i.e. I think weight loss can be a confounder in a lot of tests). Yay crowd sourcing...

jesse

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T07:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-8650>

Nowhere close to doing an actual test but my biological age was 48 (Sep 2011) a few months ago... now its 30. I am 31.

### Owl — 2012-03-30T07:54:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-8654>

Jesse, If I remember correctly, Spectacell's telomere testing costs $290 through their site:

[http://info.spectracell.com/bid/63271/SpectraCell-s-Telomere-Testing-Goes-Mainstream](http://http://info.spectracell.com/bid/63271/SpectraCell-s-Telomere-Testing-Goes-Mainstream)

### hellojtm — 2012-04-03T00:23:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-10519>

> **Glamorama;8700 wrote:** Nowhere close to doing an actual test but my biological age was 48 (Sep 2011) a few months ago... now its 30. I am 31.

Hi Glamorama,

Sorry, you lost me. If you didn't do an actual test where did you get your biological age from? Thanks for the response!

### Destiny — 2012-04-04T13:51:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-11207>

It appears to be a state specific. I just found on their website that labs in my part of NY don't perform this test.

SHIEL LABS - Nanuet

Address: 36 College Avenue Nanuet, NY 10954

Phone: 845-624-1809

**Only LPP testing available at this site; patients must provide test kit and requisition.

In NJ, you can have it but you do need a script.

ACCURATE DIAGNOSTIC LABS - Saddle River Drawing Station

Address: 82 East Allendale Road Saddle River, NJ 07458

Phone: 908-333-3082

Specimen Kits available at draw site. Call ahead for availability. Test requisitions must be completed and signed by physician.**

Just an update on my visit to the LabCorp to get the telomere test. The LabCorp phlebotomist/receptionist said she needed a docs name for the paperwork. I told here Spectracell said none was necessary (I made that up since I wanted the test and Spectracell sent me the kit). She called her supervisor (she was very nice throughout the whole process) and after much discussion she went ahead and drew the blood and there was no problem. I also got the micronutrient test done at the same time. Now can't wait to get the results!

### Glamazon — 2012-04-17T04:28:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-15805>

I finally got my test back. I am floored...in a good way. I had the test drawn on 2/29 and had started the reset on 2/21.

\- real age: 54

\- telomere age: 25!!!!! I said you are effing kidding me. This is a mistake. They said no it is not. I am calling the company this week just to check. I am in the 89 percentile of my age which means I am in the top 11%. According to the results I have an above average result. My telomere score is 8.71. The higher the score the "younger" the cells. Honestly, I thought I would be in the age 70s range.

\- before body composition and diet as of initial telomere testing: 1 week into Leptin reset. Before reset Primalish diet, estrogenic, apple body (need to loose about 100 pounds)

\- lifestyle before (light exposure and sleep, exercise, work): night owl, fair to poor sleep, rare exerciser, had just started to cross fit, work a desk job as a RN but...I do meditate and do chi gong and some martial arts (not the real strenuous type). Been cleaning up my diet for the last ten years as in moving to more organic, cleaner foods but not paleo or primal except recently.

)

\- diet during (especially if you are consuming any non-paleo foods or foods not matched to the canonical season): ketogenic paleo, occasionally fall off the wagon but mostly eat paleo/primal when I fall off. Usually it is just more carbs than normal.

\- lifestyle during

\- ending body composition

\- ending telomere length (again with the variable or a real value)

I wonder if I am an outlier. I do come from hardworking Northern European peasant stock. Long lived and healthy on the paternal side. Less so on maternal side.

Maybe one drink every couple of years, and I never smoked. I don't care for veggies or aerobic exercise. I have always been a hard core carnivore since childhood with a taste for marbled meat.

It is time for more lab tests from my primary care doc.

That score must be wrong.

### Kpcst — 2012-04-19T00:08:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-16355>

Got my test back today.

Hmmm... not what I expected or hoped for.

I'm 32. Telomere age was closer to 40.

It doesn't actually say, just has a graph. Think they were 7.29 or so in length.

Of course I have been a workout person since high school. Played sports throughout HS and into College. Have never had a real weight problem so I've eaten whatever I wanted. I partied hard until I was 28 or so. Just stayed out late and had lots of tequila. No crack or drugs... SO I guess that can equal a 29th percentile telomere result. I guess what really matters is from here on out. Now that I have a benchmark, I will just retest later and see if I'm improving or atleast slowing down the deterioration.

### Glamazon — 2012-04-19T05:29:52+0300

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-01T04:23:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-20275>

That brings up a good question I've wondered about.

In a healthy person, do they shorten as you approach (a rapid, fairly painless) death at the end of a long (& healthy & independent till close to the end) life? Do they shorten rapidly then? Or do they stay long forever, and one day you just die?

Can we know, since there aren't exactly a lot of people eating traditional, unprocessed diets out there to test? Or have they tested the 122yo's of the world, or even the healthy-till-94-then-drop-dead-while-mowing-the-lawn old ladies?

### Souldanzer — 2012-05-01T04:44:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-20281>

### MamaGrok — 2012-05-01T05:04:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-20285>

Exactly - does telomere shortening = bad health? Or does it equal aging?

My grandfather on one side and great-grandmother on the other side both died at 94. They both lived independently and worked physical jobs until (respectively) a few months and a few years before death. (All their descendants, btw, all raised on the SAD, all have chronic diseases, so far as I know, no matter whether 70 or 3.)

So did their telomeres stay long and strong till just a few months/years before death? (They didn't suddenly die in their sleep; they did decline in the last months/years, but I suspect that had a LOT to do with being drugged for conditions against their will.) Or did they stay strong even till the last breath? Or did they stay strong till maybe their 70s and then start a long, gradual shortening? What's most likely?

### neaderthal — 2012-05-17T23:52:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-25009>

> **Patty Cakes;25038 wrote:** I'm still trying to understand my results of 10.23 telomere length. It looks like I'm around 7 yrs old (really 53) and maybe younger as the graph kinda stops at around 7 but my marker is a little higher. I am in the 98% "Percentile relative to patient age and population". Does anyone know how to figure this out beyond the graph?
>

>
> Here is the info that came with the test results that appear to answer some of the other questions on this thread.
>

Regarding what Spectracell has listed under the 'Reporting' paragraph, I have 2 questions:

1\. From what source do they derive the 'population of people' with similar chronological age and how many 'people' are in this population to which your results are being compared?

2\. How many genes are used for the comparison? I'm not sure how to interpret their methods of analysis without knowing more details.

Having done no research on this myself, but having some academic knowledge regarding biochemical techniques used in genetic research, I would assume each laboratory uses proprietary methods and 'populations' for comparison; therefore, I would assume your results can differ depending on the company. It may be helpful if anyone has any knowledge and can share it with us. If my assumptions are correct, perhaps as pointed out in an earlier comment, the best use of this testing would be with initial testing as a baseline & testing again later for comparison, but then that would need to be with the same company and compared against the same 'population' as in the original test. Thoughts?

### NeilBB — 2013-08-29T16:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-94166>

I wonder how accurate the Spectracell telomere test actually is. It has been mentioned by some that a random WBC telomere length may have little correlation with the telomere lengths of more important cells such as stem cells. Some of the testimonials here I have seen seem to support that idea as the results seem very out of the blue for many, in either direction. I'm not sure that this assay is a true reflection of the underlying basic science.

I got mine and kinda wish I hadn't now. They were horrible. I'm 45, feel much younger and healthier than that, harcore epi-paleo/CT, EMF reduction, rockstar sleep, etc. And the test says I have like 80 year old telomeres. My labs, hormones, HS-CRP, body comp, and other indices are all really good and have been for at least 3-4 years, and still getting better. (Before that, yes, I was fat and did have metabolic syndrome picture.)

Feel like I paid a bunch to get kicked in gut. Considering repeating to see if it was error, but not anxious to waste more money if this test is not ready for prime time. And if it is accurate, it seems all my aggressive lifestyle changes and interventions over several years have not helped in lengthening these WBC telomeres at all. I kinda wish I had stuck to monitoring hormones and inflammatory markers and working the prescriptions.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-29T17:18:20+0300

### NeilBB — 2013-08-29T17:39:14+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-29T17:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-un-official-telomere-before-and-after-thread.518/post-94180>

EMF directly affects chromosomes so you know telomeres are affected. Resurecting an old thread, but [here's a comment by Jack](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/telomeric-stress-causes-wide-spread-dna-damage.10327/#post-118007) on this topic: "_81 to 55 in WBC telomere change. 51 to 29 was the best in an obesity case_."

@[NeilBB](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6879/), did you ever re-test your telomeres or consider trying to lengthen them? I recently tested mine and found I have ~85yo telomeres and I'm only 37. Typically I've always looked younger than my age...people typically guess I look 5-10 years younger than I am. I still get ID'd at the liquor store...

After reading up on telomeres, it's unclear whether short telomeres is a symptom of aging or a disease process, or the cause. If they're just a symptom, I wonder if lengthening them should even be a priority.

# Thread: No veggies... At all.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rhoswen — 2012-03-29T09:21:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8167>

So I have a problem that's been worrying me since I started my Primal/Leptin journey... And it's the fact I hate all vegetables. Every single one of them with ONE exception. They all taste bitter whether roasted, cooked in butter, coconut oil, steamed, sauted, raw... I just really really hate vegetables. Only exceptions are sweet potatoes, and it's pretty obvious why I like those! Haha!

I actually forced myself for a month to eat lots of vegetables, thinking that my tastes would change, thinking that I just had to get into a habit of it... I made it a whole month and at the very end, I nearly threw my plate of broccoli, califlower, and carrots across the room in disgust and frustration.

I'm worried I may be harming my health by not eating any vegetables... My diet consists of beef (beef gelatin too), bison, chicken, bacon, turkey, sardines, salmon, eggs, butter, coconut oil/manna, and... Well that's about it I think. I take Vitamin D, C, Cod Liver Oil, and L-glutamine.

Actually, I did realize something... I absolutely love fermented pickles. They're soooo tasty and way better than regular old cucumbers. However, I'm pretty sure I can't get the best nutrition from my only vegetables being fermented pickles!

Anyone got any advice? I know this sounds like a really bizarre problem (also embarrassing for an adult to be so picky) and I've NEVER seen any other primal/paleo folk talk about this kind of stuff unless it involved their kids being picky eaters.

### AdrianaDr. Radler-Pohl — 2012-03-29T09:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8168>

I was thinking about that... I've seen people pickle/ferment carrots before. I'm actually able to buy fermented pickles and sauerkraut from my local market so I haven't done it myself.  Maybe I should look into a fermentation pot for my home. Maybe do some more research on other good veggies to ferment.

### janagram — 2012-03-29T11:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8174>

sometimes I hear people say they hate vegies and then I find out they DON'T put tons of butter and salt on them!!!! (of course you wouldn't like them then) but with butter and salt they're goooood.

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-29T11:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8175>

> **janagram;8218 wrote:** sometimes I hear people say they hate vegies and then I find out they DON'T put tons of butter and salt on them!!!! (of course you wouldn't like them then) but with butter and salt they're goooood.

That's how I usually would eat them and I still couldn't stand them. Booo.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-29T20:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8384>

Is it for the micronutrient content that you are wanting them? You can try other means for that. For example, things like barley grass powder (a heaping teaspoon dissolved in water and drank) is full of intense quantities of micronutrients and you can drink it in 30 seconds. [http://www.livestrong.com/article/356106-nutrition-in-barley-grass/](http://http://www.livestrong.com/article/356106-nutrition-in-barley-grass/) (apparently also totally gluten free.) Also, while I do eat greens (and mushrooms - not sure if they are technically a veggie, but they're great), I also make tinctures of nettle tea daily. Buy dried nettles, steep them in a jar of boiling water with a lid overnight, put in the fridge in the morning and drink cold all day. I add sliced ginger or a peppermint tea bag to improve the taste. ([http://www.marksdailyapple.com/stinging-nettle-pesto/#axzz1qWm2VZrs](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/stinging-nettle-pesto/#axzz1qWm2VZrs) (this isn't about tea, but another nettle idea; nettles are anti inflammatory, rich in vitamins and minerals [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stinging\_nettle](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stinging_nettle)) Tea is zero carbs but, again, full of nutrients. Just a few ideas.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T20:54:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8400>

R, don't feel bad about not liking veggies! After all, we are supposed to be sticking to keto-paleo anyway. My diet looks much like yours. The only veggie I have is shredded coconut, coconut manna or mushrooms, on occasion. I will eat broccoli or cauliflower once in a blue moon, but otherwise I stick to coconut oil and meats: shrimp, salmon, crappie, side pork, ground beef, venison, steaks, roasts, goose, pheasant, etc.)

If you love pickles, how about olives? I bet your body is wanting some salt and that's why you like the pickles so much. Olives are also high in sodium and might fit the bill.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-03-29T22:00:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8444>

> **Rhoswen;8211 wrote:** So I have a problem that's been worrying me since I started my Primal/Leptin journey... And it's the fact I hate all vegetables. Every single one of them with ONE exception. They all taste bitter whether roasted, cooked in butter, coconut oil, steamed, sauted, raw... I just really really hate vegetables. Only exceptions are sweet potatoes, and it's pretty obvious why I like those! Haha!
>
>
>
> I actually forced myself for a month to eat lots of vegetables, thinking that my tastes would change, thinking that I just had to get into a habit of it... I made it a whole month and at the very end, I nearly threw my plate of broccoli, califlower, and carrots across the room in disgust and frustration.
>
>
>
> I'm worried I may be harming my health by not eating any vegetables... My diet consists of beef (beef gelatin too), bison, chicken, bacon, turkey, sardines, salmon, eggs, butter, coconut oil/manna, and... Well that's about it I think. I take Vitamin D, C, Cod Liver Oil, and L-glutamine.
>
>
>
> Actually, I did realize something... I absolutely love fermented pickles. They're soooo tasty and way better than regular old cucumbers. However, I'm pretty sure I can't get the best nutrition from my only vegetables being fermented pickles!
>
>
>
> Anyone got any advice? I know this sounds like a really bizarre problem (also embarrassing for an adult to be so picky) and I've NEVER seen any other primal/paleo folk talk about this kind of stuff unless it involved their kids being picky eaters.

Veggies do provide something (without looking it up, it's some specific carb/fiber) they feed the good bacteria in your intestines. Fermented veggies - the pickles! do this as well. So veggies help keep our guts balanced, but I don't think there is any good advice out there on how much is needed and/or if a few probiotic veggies work just as well. Interesting that you sort of crave the pickles. I bet it's for the bacteria. I say keep on keeping on, don't force the veggies if you are eating the fermented things. Perhaps branch out to different fermented veggies.

if you are eating "Hoof to horn" then animals can provide everything. . .but who does that? I'd say make sure you keep up on your bone broth. Interestingly, when I'm trying to stay keto - I start not to like the veggies too. I end up doing just about what you are. . . my carbs come mostly from 99% dark chocolate plus a little tomato used to make the meats. I try to eat probiotic veggies a couple times a week to feed the intestines.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-29T22:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8458>

Yes, if you're eating nose-to-tail, including all the organs, you are probably getting everything. Otherwise, you do need some plant matter. Fermented veggies are a great way to work on that, although it's hard to eat as many of them as you likely would need without organ meat. You can ferment most anything and it's EASY.

### Rhoswen — 2012-03-30T07:20:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8644>

Thank you guys!  I was mostly worried that I wasn't getting as many nutrients as I should without some low-carb veggies in my diet since I don't like organ meats. I actually had a really bad experience when I tried chicken livers which involved nearly throwing up into the plate that held them after one bite... Erk.

I'll probably stick to the fermented veggies I like and not worry too much about it.  The tea ideas sound interesting too!

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-30T15:44:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8713>

Most organ meats gave me that reaction the first time. It's a matter of trying different ways until I found something that worked for me, and holding my breath while eating until I did. I consider it the least I can do to make up for the abuse I gave my body all those years.

Lamb liver is the most delicate I've found (pastured), but I haven't tried calf liver (which they sell at Whole Foods), which is also supposed to be very delicate. Marinate it in lemon juice, remove the membrane, slice super super thin, sprinkle with salt/pepper/garlicifyouwant/maybe coconut flour, fry in bacon grease, top with hot sauce if you need to.

I don't think you're going to get enough nutrients from fermented cucumbers & muscle meat. I just don't. Long term there is going to be a deficit that really matters. Lots and lots of fat will also be needed to make up for lack of offal.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-30T17:44:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-8799>

I'm not currently eating a lot of veggies because I have a messed up GI tract from years of food abuse. Veggies make me quite sick. You \*can\* live on meat/fat alone (how else would we survive winter/ice ages?). I would encourage you this summer to experiment, though. You may find your tastes changes the longer you are off sugar and processed foods.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-30T21:37:30+0300

The bone broth is another way to get your veggies in .... cook them in the bone broth. Then season and puree.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-31T14:27:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-9176>

> **Rhoswen;9185 wrote:** I'd love to order from US Wellness meats but they've got that 75$ and 7lb rule for ordering from them. I'm also nervous about how I'd receive my order since I live in an apartment building. I've ordered from them a few times. I love their pemmican - makes a great snack or on-the-go meal. They've always had everythingg packed very well in a styrofoam cooler w/ ice blocks. The reason they have the weight minimum is to make sure things stay frozen. I've been at work before when they delivered and the package sat on my porch all day and was still solid when I got home.

### David — 2012-03-31T15:22:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-9187>

Marinate it in lemon juice, remove the membrane, slice super super thin, sprinkle with salt/pepper/garlicifyouwant/maybe coconut flour, fry in bacon grease, top with hot sauce if you need to ...

Excellent preparation method, although I omit any flour, rinse off the lemon after marinating and I always add onions; I can _never_ omit the onions to prepare tasty calf's liver. Chicken liver seems to be okay with only bacon fat.

### MamaGrok — 2012-03-31T17:08:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-9229>

>
>
> Our order came deeply frozen in styrofoam containers. There was no ice, but it was so tightly packed that there was no need - all the other meat was the ice. It sat around in our house most of the day int he coolers, then we packed it into our coolers with some dry ice and went on a three week trip. The meat stayed frozen in the cooler with dry ice (we didn't open it more than once or twice) for 9 days.
>
>
>
>
> > **Jack Kruse;9232 wrote:** This is my 11 yr old daughter too.......so she is a carnivore of fat and protein. 100%.

>
>
> David;9248 wrote:** Excellent preparation method, although I omit any flour, rinse off the lemon after marinating and I always add onions; I can _never_ omit the onions to prepare tasty calf's liver. Chicken liver seems to be okay with only bacon fat.

I still don't really like it, but I can eat it without holding my breath this way. And strangely, onions are the only vegetable I still don't like, after all the years of recovering from "my only vegetables are pickles & corn" youth.

### IceBarbie — 2012-04-07T09:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-veggies-at-all.519/post-12416>

I just finished some of the Wellness Meats Liverwurst the other day. Very good. I also like the Braunschweiger. These both contain honey but not much as far as I can tell. I do like their Sugar-free Beef Hot Dogs too. And they just changed their "White Sugar-Free Bacon" from containing Honey Powder to having none at all and it's just as good as the previous recipe with honey powder. It tastes like regular bacon and I don't miss the sugars at all. I buy the frozen Pet Food for my dogs which is beef and liver but not intended for human consumption. I swear it looks better than the local grass-fed beef I buy locally for my family! I tried their Beef Snack Sticks this time and they were good too. And their large raw Wild Caught Shrimp is excellent. I sautee it in homemade chicken broth with lots of organic garlic salt. My meat arrives frozen solid each time I get it though it arrives in the morning and there's always someone here to open it right away. One time the cooler contained some dry ice, the other time it didn't cuz the cooler was fuller.

# Thread: Pork Belly Suggestions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2012-03-29T14:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8195>

Just picked up a pork belly at my local farm because it just sounded good . . . now I have no idea what to do with it! Suggestions? Recipes? Thanks!

### Martin — 2012-03-29T15:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8208>

Hmmm, pork belly.

Such a grose sounding food item...... until one has a bite. Simply one of the best things I've ever eaten. But it was at a fancy restaurant so I'm afraid I can't offer much other then drool.

Martin

### David — 2012-03-29T16:05:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8238>

Try this, it looks really good!

[http://momofukufor2.com/2010/01/roasted-pork-belly/](http://http://momofukufor2.com/2010/01/roasted-pork-belly/)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-03-29T16:13:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-8240>

This is my FAVE cut of pork besides ribs! I slice it and treat it just like bacon. My favorite thing to do is bake it with sea salt and cinnamon. Yum!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-01T02:58:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9608>

Hi BJK77,

I have just the thing for you. I used to make my own smoked bacon but I got to the point where it became too time consuming. (I would rather be spending my time making sausages!) Here is a simple recipe for salted pork belly. It is a lot like bacon without the nasty chemicals:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/salted-pork-belly/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/salted-pork-belly/)

If you like that recipe, I have just completed an index of **eatkamloops.org** recipes. Here is over three years of recipes. Most of the recipes will be good for someone on the modified paleo diet:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/)

### BJK77 — 2012-04-01T04:06:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9637>

Thanks so much for all the suggestions. DH and I are finally, finally going to get a date night next weekend so I think I'll pick one of these recipes for a special meal!

### Baba — 2012-04-01T18:17:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9803>

The secret for a crispy skin is a good soak. So cook it in the oven skin down in liquid (beer, wine, broth, water...) for about two hours. Low heat. Then take out, cool and dry the skin. Score the skin with a craft knife and sprinke with salt. Bake in a hot oven skin side up and watch the crackling almost explode. I'd say half an hour or so. Drool. Eat. Get someone else to clean the oven.

### Birdy — 2012-04-01T19:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-9856>

wow that looks good....

### Sheddie — 2018-03-16T01:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237266>

> [Jan Christer said:](/goto/post?id=237259)
>
> Hi Caroline,

Jan -- Hello!
Here's my favorite pork belly recipe (and a good read) posted by our very own Master Chef Drezy, on p4 in Thread: How To Make Deuterium Depleted Water, along with some discussion posts.
Coincidentally, this very moment, I just pulled out of the oven my third time making it using this recipe. Best luck finding your fave recipe!
[https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169](https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169)

PS drezy -- I lower my oven down to 300degF the last 20 minutes -- because I like the dark brown color at that point. Then, baked it to the end of the full recipe time. Forever in your debt!

### Jan Christer — 2018-03-16T02:34:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237270>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=237266)
>
> Jan -- Hello!
> Here's my favorite pork belly recipe (and a good read) posted by our very own Master Chef Drezy, on p4 in Thread: How To Make Deuterium Depleted Water, along with some discussion posts.
> Coincidentally, this very moment, I just pulled out of the oven my third time making it using this recipe. Best luck finding your fave recipe!
> [https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169](https://deadspin.com/how-to-cook-pork-belly-which-thoroughly-kicks-bacons-a-1619788169)
>
> PS drezy -- I lower my oven down to 300degF the last 20 minutes -- because I like the dark brown color at that point. Then, baked it to the end of the full recipe time. Forever in your debt!

Thanks much Sheddie!

### Earth Monkey — 2018-03-17T07:57:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237351>

These instructions rom my local gourmet butcher (one of the top 3 in my city) produce excellent results: score the skin of the pork belly with a sharp knife in a criss-cross pattern, sprinkle liberally with salt and rub in. Roast in an open tray in the oven for 1hr @ 160deg celcius, then turn up to 220C for 20mins

### John Saf — 2018-03-17T18:23:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237367>

I make my pork belly low and slow: rub with salt (and black pepper if it is okay)
Owen for 72'C for 8-12 hours. In water bath half way up. If you have local onions and herbs now it gives a great taste if you put it it in the water.
Roasting it at high temps is not optimal even if the taste is great. Low/slow makes it melt in the mouth, hope it is aprowed method.

### drezy — 2018-03-17T19:15:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237370>

> [John Saf said:](/goto/post?id=237367)
>
> I make my pork belly low and slow: rub with salt (and black pepper if it is okay)
> Owen for 72'C for 8-12 hours. In water bath half way up. If you have local onions and herbs now it gives a great taste if you put it it in the water.
> Roasting it at high temps is not optimal even if the taste is great. Low/slow makes it melt in the mouth, hope it is aprowed method.

I'll have to give that one a shot. I found a good, but less slow recipe and got comments from others here:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/quick-mods-to-a-great-pork-belly-recipe.20730/)

That is only 2.5 hours so I bet you end up with fall apart pork at the end. I like your slower method more in principal already. I'll chime back in once I test drive it. Living where I do I'll have to drop some hot peppers into the water as well to infuse spiciness.

Just to share some of my kitchen thinking, if you add black pepper just go ahead and add turmeric and vice versa. They work together in more than just taste:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-help-quantum-diseases-linked-to-light.18468/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/why-turmeric-curcumin-help-quantum-diseases-linked-to-light.18468/)

### John Saf — 2018-03-18T16:11:04+0200

Jack kruse:
the nitrate thing is a bunch of shit......what is not is cooking temps of your meat.......that is a big deal

### drezy — 2018-03-18T18:57:57+0200

### Sheddie — 2018-03-18T21:29:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237452>

> Owen for 72'C for 8-12 hours. In water bath half way up. If you have local onions and herbs now it gives a great taste if you put it it in the water.
> Roasting it at high temps is not optimal even if the taste is great. Low/slow makes it melt in the mouth, hope it is aprowed method.

Is that a method with a 'sous vide' appliance? Sounds delicious.

### drezy — 2018-03-18T23:43:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237462>

I have a vacuum sealer and a cast iron pot that I call my redneck sous vide setup for now. I think a crockpot could work well too.

### John Saf — 2018-03-19T00:00:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237468>

> [Sheddie said:](/goto/post?id=237452)
>
> Is that a method with a 'sous vide' appliance? Sounds delicious.

No sous vide. I cook in a heatresistant glass pan or stainless caseroll without lid. I dont want to do plastics/(heat)/food in my house. slowcocked fatty pork cuts in the owen with water kicks sousvides ass

### freesia — 2018-03-19T01:41:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237475>

John - so no water in with the pork....just in the bath around the pan the pork is sitting in?
I have been stockpiling pork belly strips in the freezer trying to figure out how I'm going to cook them well without too much heat. Might try your method but in my slowcooker.

Dr Seignalet, a French immunologist, wrote quite a lot about avoiding cooking (proteins, fats and carbohydrates) at temps above 110 degrees c. He warned about acrylamide - which as far as I can tell is why coffee (roasted) has fairly recently been classified carcinogenic.

### freesia — 2018-03-21T06:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-237640>

Ha well I did it.
Laid pork belly strips in a (lidded) ovenproof glass pan, rubbed in salt, pepper and a bit of cayenne, ginger juice and lime zest. Propped the pan up an inch in the slow cooker so water would surround it from underneath. Filled with hot water to halfway up the pork pan and cooked on "low" setting for 5 hours...water was barely at a simmer. So easy to eat!

I opted for a lidded pan because the nature of cooking in a slow cooker is that condensation collects on the inside of the lid and I didn't know whether I wanted water dripping into my pork. It did cross my mind though - that water \*might\* be DDW...hence upgrade my pork further

### Sheddie — 2019-04-02T02:37:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pork-belly-suggestions.520/post-267075>

### drezy — 2019-04-02T03:57:47+0300

# Thread: random rashes - cold urticaria or... ?... and what do do
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-29T15:01:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8207>

So after following Dr. K's CT protocol and getting to the icy tub stage a few times, I developed itchy/scabby areas on my head which I thought were contact dermatitis from my neoprene cpap headgear. So... I switched to another mask/headgear type, eased off the CT, and after 2 weeks of worsening, finally resorted to a weak cortisone lotion. That seemed to help a lot, so earlier this week, I started up with a couple of tubbings, alternating days with icepacks and....the scalp issues are BACK  I'm upping my fish oil and krill oil and started using the cortisone (.1%) lotion again. Should I quit the CT for awhile?? I was fine during the pre-tubbing coldpack stage, no problems at all. If it's cold urticaria, why my HEAD?? And if it's not, what is it and should I "stay the course" or...?

\*sigh\* So glad for this forum and all you folks!!

### shilohman — 2012-03-29T18:18:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8323>

> **meyoolia;8252 wrote:** So after following Dr. K's CT protocol and getting to the icy tub stage a few times, I developed itchy/scabby areas on my head which I thought were contact dermatitis from my neoprene cpap headgear. So... I switched to another mask/headgear type, eased off the CT, and after 2 weeks of worsening, finally resorted to a weak cortisone lotion. That seemed to help a lot, so earlier this week, I started up with a couple of tubbings, alternating days with icepacks and....the scalp issues are BACK  I'm upping my fish oil and krill oil and started using the cortisone (.1%) lotion again. Should I quit the CT for awhile?? I was fine during the pre-tubbing coldpack stage, no problems at all. If it's cold urticaria, why my HEAD?? And if it's not, what is it and should I "stay the course" or...?
>
>
>
> \*sigh\* So glad for this forum and all you folks!!

Get your omega6/3 tested along with HS-CRP. Sounds like you have a lot of inflammation in your body. You might try rubbing some CO or olive oil on your head once the rash is gone. I would stop CT until the rash is gone or go back to the ice packs until the rash is gone.

### shilohman — 2012-03-29T18:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8330>

Oops, forgot, you can't rub any oil on your head if you have hair. I don't , so ignore that part.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-29T20:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8380>

I did just that a few weeks ago. 1.1 mg/L, with under 1.0 being "low risk" (for heart attack I guess), and 1-3 "average". yeeshk, i do hate the thought of having to reacclimate, but I'll back off some, back to the coldpack stage and up my krill oil til i can get my 6/3's tested.

And re the CO, heh heh... sure I can.... it just wouldn't be pretty! I might just give it a try, messier but less pharmaceutical than cortisone  And with this whole "mom's gone nuts" paleo/CT thing with the family these days, i think i could grease my head up good and stick twigs in and they'd simply shake their heads. rofl....

\*still smirking\* Good ideas, thanks

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-30T22:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8952>

Ok, so I've picked up some homeopathic apis...gonna try that for a day or so, then it's on to your suggestion, Shilohman, and the coconut oil shmear (I've also picked up a fabric "bonnet" that should keep the worst of the oil off my pillow) - somehow with coconut oil being such a grand thing for intestinal nasties, it seems to ring right that it'd be good for skin nasties, too  ....then comes, maybe, aloe..... and somewhere in there - \*sigh\* - stopping even the icepacking awhile, if nothing else works.

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-03-30T23:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-8988>

> **meyoolia;9004 wrote:** the coconut oil shmear

The ladies from India that I have worked with swear by combing CO through the hair in the evening and then washing it out in the morning. "Best conditioner ever" is what I've been told. So it's not just a paleo crazy thing.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-31T21:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-9338>

> **WeAreDevo;9040 wrote:** The ladies from India that I have worked with swear by combing CO through the hair in the evening and then washing it out in the morning. "Best conditioner ever" is what I've been told. So it's not just a paleo crazy thing.

Upside: They're right! my hair's great this morning after a night "soaking" in it.

Downside: Rash is worse  doubt it's due to the CO tho.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-31T22:30:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-9358>

When I started LC three years ago, my persistent scabby scalp cleared up. Then, when I began the reset with my husband at the beginning of February, it started popping up again. This was before I even began the CT protocol. I have been doing the CT for awhile now, and it seems to be easing up. So, I am not so sure that it is the CT causing the scabby scalp at all. I am trying to see it, perhaps, it might be one of the supplements that I am now taking. Since I don't think that it is the food that I have taken away, and I really haven't added any new foods, I am checking out the ingredients on the supplements.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-04-02T04:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/random-rashes-cold-urticaria-or-and-what-do-do.521/post-10109>

A possible lead.... "Localized cold reflex urticaria leads to symptoms of hives and swelling away from the area of direct exposure to cold. An ice-cube test on the arm may cause hives to form a few inches away from the site of ice-cube application, for example".

Has anybody experienced this?? Benadryl is helping a bit, but not much

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-29T15:29:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quotes.522/post-8224>

> **Jack Kruse;8256 wrote:** I think you should link Dr. Kruse's daily morning quotes and his Sunrise pictures to the this site for all people to see........( my daughter, Kali, came up with this one)

your daughter is freaking Brilliant!

### Vicster — 2012-03-29T15:32:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quotes.522/post-8225>

Yes, that apple did not fall far from the tree.

### Guest — 2012-04-05T15:58:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quotes.522/post-11708>

> **Jack Kruse;8256 wrote:** I think you should link Dr. Kruse's daily morning quotes and his Sunrise pictures to the this site for all people to see........( my daughter, Kali, came up with this one)

I don't think cross-posting the quotations and pictures is a bad idea. I would, however, be careful to cite your sources with the quotes, as it seems that some people think they're your own words. Today's quote, for example, cobbled together four quotes by other people that are easily found through a Google search. The last thing you want to be accused of is plagiarizing MLK.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-05T21:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quotes.522/post-11833>

I think we should all chip in and buy Dr. K a typing course. I know it's a bother to put in the citations, but it is a legit gripe some have.

Nothing wrong with using other's genius for inspiration, and I know better than to think he's trying to get others to think it's his own wholly original genius. It's silly for some to think he is actually trying to plagiarize (rather than just lazy). He knows we have Google.

Still, it wouldn't be so much to take the seconds to give a shout-out to the authors from whom the inspiration for the final product originated.
