### LisaAPB — 2012-03-30T16:45:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-8764>

The Top Twenty List:

1\. What in the H.. is Factor X? \[LisaAPB\]

2\. How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes. At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] +1 +1 & Combine the following: How important is CRON (Calorie Restricion) in CT? (formerly Q20)

3\. Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] +1 How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted? +1

4\. Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488)

At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\] +1

5\. After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\] +1 +1

6\. Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] +1 CT & Age

7\. How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] +1

8\. Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy? +1

9\. How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\] +1

10\. Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] +1

11\. What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama} +1 +1

12\. I would like to know the seasonal distinctions..If we are eating carbs (seasonal allowed fruits/veggies) in summer do we still CT? +1

Also.. The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

13\. What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] +1

14.Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits acrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\] +1

15\. How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg). Additionally when should you LHT/HIIT? Before or after CT? +1

16\. Immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women (donkjellberg). + 1

17\. Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad) How does Vitamin D help CT leverage the ancient pathways?+1

18\. Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)? How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT? +1 (formerly Q19)

19\. Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis

20\. What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation?

combined Q19 with Q18

I would think Dr. K might have already seen clinical impact on thyroid labs with CT, so the seems like a useful question over some of the more "theoretical" questions. I've also seen a lot of post on the forum about thyroid, but that's just my 2 cents.

### MJ* — 2012-03-30T17:57:10+0300

Dr. Kruse I totally appreciate your work, and passion for helping humanity. At the same time, I agree with these statements, unless we can do our blood work through you somehow, this is going to be difficult to explain to a doctor, because we do need them to order the tests for insurance purposes. I would love it if we could get it all through you...I freakin' hate most doctors (except for you LOL) and haven't been to one in many years...but I know from taking my kids that you only get so much time, how do we easily recruit their help? Their careers are on the line, and the litigious society that we've become makes doctors even more cautious. They, like you once were, are products of a thinking that is narrow when it comes to disease prevention. We will need to educate them...so if we can't somehow do the testing through you, a one page bullet point document that easily explains the testing needed would be really helpful. Then we can point them in your direction if they, and hopefully they will, want to learn more.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-30T18:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-8813>

For those of you trying to figure out what tests you want to run - do this...

tell your doc you want the following tests run, and you want to leverage health insurance as much as you can, all he/she has to do is sign the chit, and provide dX codes.

I emailed UHC and gave them the lists of tests I wanted to run and verified which would be covered/not covered - they actually gave me my DX codes -

my Chiropractor will run what ever tests I want - and all I do is use the same DX codes... my new PCP who I found on Paeo Physicains network - will also run any test. She's very progressive in her testing & she's figured out how to get insurance to cover most if not all tests

You're paying your doctor - you've enter a contract w/each other; ask for what you want - when the balk - ask them - "What harm does drawing and extra vial or two do? Does it hurt you? Does it hurt me? I'm interested in seeing what these labs look like and I'd like to see if insurance will cover them. - if they don't I'm willing to pay for them - but I'd rather go through you vs going out on my own... I'm trying to take responsibility for my health - I thought as a doctor you'd appreciate a patient who's a proactive participant in their care. I guess if you're not interested in that, I can always find another doctor"

While threatening to leave their practice may not scare them today - but as more and more ppl take responsibility for their own care - the doctors who thrive in mediocrity will find themselves w/o patients eventually. It won't happen over night but it will happen...

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-03-30T19:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-8849>

The Top Twenty List:

1\. What in the H.. is Factor X? \[LisaAPB\]

2\. How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes. At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] +1 +1 & Combine the following: How important is CRON (Calorie Restricion) in CT? (formerly Q20)

3\. Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] +1 How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted? +1

4\. Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488)

At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\] +1

5\. After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\] +1 +1

6\. Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] +1 CT & Age

7\. How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] +1

8\. Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy? +1

9\. How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\] +1

10\. Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] +1

11\. What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama} +1 +1

12\. I would like to know the seasonal distinctions..If we are eating carbs (seasonal allowed fruits/veggies) in summer do we still CT? +1

Also.. The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

13\. What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] +1

14.Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits acrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\] +1

15\. How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg). Additionally when should you LHT/HIIT? Before or after CT? +1

16\. Immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women (donkjellberg). + 1

17\. Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad) How does Vitamin D help CT leverage the ancient pathways?+1

18\. Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)? How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT? +1 (formerly Q19)

19\. Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis

20\. What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation?

My question:

21\. How to safely use CT for children? Age ranges connected to safe temperature and time ranges would be helpful.

22\. What and how should children eat w/ CT and how can they do the Leptin Reset if needed?

### donkjellberg — 2012-03-30T20:05:40+0300

The only reason I was asking about it was because you had mentioned that tests can be a good predictive measurement. In order to get insurance to pay for these tests I have to go to my doctor.

I stopped seeking advice from my doctor a year ago when I came in for labs after losing 130 pounds: he wanted to put me on statins :/

I also changed dentists to get my mercury fillings removed. I don't need standard preventive healthcare anymore, especially with the formation of obamacare (scary). Healthcare is invaluable when it comes to high levels of acuity of course.

I'm going with my gut these days. Being a nurse who likes reading research with a perspective on prevention helps.

We will probably have to give our docs the book when it comes out. Then, decide whether they are hopeless or not.

1\. **+7 SEASONAL EATING:

How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes? At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? 2\. **+6 CLIMATE CHANGE:

Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted?

3\. **+6 Best CT Practices:

Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488) At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\]

4\. **+6 Frequency:

After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\]

5\. **+4 Maintenance:

Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] CT & Age

6\. **+3 Inflammation:

How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\]

7\. **+5 HOT WATER AND AIR:**

Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy?

8\. +5 TELOMERES:**

How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\]

9\. +5 SUPPLEMENTAION:**

Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad) How does Vitamin D help CT leverage the ancient pathways?

10\. +7 DETOX:**

What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama}

11\. +5 CT + HCG HOW TO:**

The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

12\. +7 LABS and RANGES SEX and AGE:**

What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] What are the minimum most beneficial tests that you would recommend particularly for most of us who will be dealing with skeptical reluctant doctors?

13\. +6 CT INTENSITY:**

Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits accrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\]
br />

14\. **+5 EXERCISE:

How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg). Additionally when should you LHT/HIIT? Before or after CT? Is it ok to help our body warm up afterwards (putting clothes on, exercising, etc) or is it best to just let your body heat back up on its own? (BJK&&)

15\. **+6 RESULTS &EFFECTS OF CT:

What are the immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women? (donkjellberg).

16\. **+4 BAT Vs. WAT:

Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT?

17\. +3 SHIVER VS. NON-SHIVER:**

Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis

18\. +4 HYPOTHERMIA & FROST BITE:**

What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation? What are the signs of frost bite?

19\. +2 CT and Children:**

How do you safely use CT for children? Please discuss Age ranges connected to safe temperature and time ranges. What and how should children eat w/ CT and how can they do the Leptin Reset if needed?

_\*Combined the ones that were redundant, (supplementation and seasonal eating) and added in the questions on children & CT combined._ Great list!

_\*Combined the ones that were redundant, (supplementation and seasonal eating) and added in the questions on children & CT combined._ Great list!

To go with both #6 and #3... there seems to me still some unanswered questions about O6:O3 ratios in preparation for and adaption to CT. I realise the suggested ratio is 4:1. However, if you compare the way Dr Kane (suggested by Dr Kruse) is calculating the 4:1 ratio it is NOT the same as others are calculating it. For example, someone can have a ratio of 3:1 when they look at total O6 to total O3, but when using the exact same labs and looking at LA to ALA (which is the way Dr Kane suggests calculating it - using the parent fatty acids in each category) you get a 1:63 ratio. This is extremely confusing to me and I have not seen resolution of this discrepancy anywhere (and I've read and re-read the blogs and comments). It is further confusing because Dr Kane recommends a supplement of decidedly non-paleo oils (flax and sunflower) with diet and possibly fish oil supplementation. If anyone can provide clarification of this, then feel free to take it off the list. Otherwise, this seems a really important issue yet it remains unresolved. (At least for me... apologies if I'm the only bear-of-little-brain on this.)

### Shinegirl — 2012-03-30T22:09:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-8934>

Question # 11 I'm one of those hcgers. Although I'm pretty darn healthy to my knowledge and have only 10-15 lbs to lose, I think this is the future of our health and I would not waste my time on this diet without incorporating CT without coconut fat. My last 2 tries at this were failures before I realized what I really wanted which is to be "optimal". I'm hoping this will step up my game.

### QuantumRadical — 2012-03-30T23:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-8982>

**MJ\*;8954 wrote: 1. **+7 SEASONAL EATING:

How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes? At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] Combine the following: How important is CRON (Calorie Restricion) in CT? (formerly Q20)

2\. **+6 CLIMATE CHANGE:

Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted?

3\. **+6 Best CT Practices:

Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488) At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\]

4\. **+6 Frequency:

After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\]

5\. **+4 Maintenance:

Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] CT & Age

6\. **+3 Inflammation:

How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] U

7\. +5 HOT WATER AND AIR:**

Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy?

8\. +5 TELOMERES:**

How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\]

9\. +5 SUPPLEMENTAION:**

Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad) How does Vitamin D help CT leverage the ancient pathways?

10\. +7 DETOX:**

What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama}

11\. +5 CT + HCG HOW TO:**

The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

12\. +7 LABS and RANGES SEX and AGE:**

What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] What are the minimum most beneficial tests that you would recommend particularly for most of us who will be dealing with skeptical reluctant doctors?

13\. +6 CT INTENSITY:**

Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits accrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\]

14\. **+5 EXERCISE:

How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg). Additionally when should you LHT/HIIT? Before or after CT? Is it ok to help our body warm up afterwards (putting clothes on, exercising, etc) or is it best to just let your body heat back up on its own? (BJK&&)

15\. **+6 RESULTS &EFFECTS OF CT:

What are the immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women? (donkjellberg).

16\. **+4 BAT Vs. WAT:

Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT?

17\. +3 SHIVER VS. NON-SHIVER:**

Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis

18\. +4 HYPOTHERMIA & FROST BITE:**

What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation? What are the signs of frost bite?

19\. +2 CT and Children:**

How do you safely use CT for children? Please discuss Age ranges connected to safe temperature and time ranges. What and how should children eat w/ CT and how can they do the Leptin Reset if needed?

_\*Combined the ones that were redundant, (supplementation and seasonal eating) and added in the questions on children & CT combined._ Great list!

MJ, you are awesome! Thanks for consolidating.

### Birdy — 2012-03-30T23:51:23+0300

### hellojtm — 2012-03-31T00:19:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9003>

Started from MJ's compilation, added Birdy's #20 request to the #1 request and put my vote behind it.

1\. +8 SEASONAL EATING:

How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes? At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] Combine the following: How important is CRON (Calorie Restricion) in CT? (formerly Q20). What is more important? Seasonal adjustments based on local light levels, local temperatures, or availability and quantity of local food? _(the goal of course is to eliminate mismatches, but also address prolonged CT as a means of reversing vs. a sustained seasonal practice)_

2\. +6 CLIMATE CHANGE:

Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted?

3\. +6 Best CT Practices:

Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488) At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\]

4\. +6 Frequency:

After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\]

5\. +4 Maintenance:

Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] CT & Age

6\. +3 Inflammation:

How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] U

7\. +5 HOT WATER AND AIR:

Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy?

8\. +5 TELOMERES:

How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\]

9\. +5 SUPPLEMENTAION:

Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad) How does Vitamin D help CT leverage the ancient pathways?

10\. +7 DETOX:

What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama}

11\. +5 CT + HCG HOW TO:

The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

12\. +7 LABS and RANGES SEX and AGE:

What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] What are the minimum most beneficial tests that you would recommend particularly for most of us who will be dealing with skeptical reluctant doctors?

13\. +6 CT INTENSITY:

Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits accrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\]

14\. +5 EXERCISE:

How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg). Additionally when should you LHT/HIIT? Before or after CT? Is it ok to help our body warm up afterwards (putting clothes on, exercising, etc) or is it best to just let your body heat back up on its own? (BJK&&)

15\. +6 RESULTS &EFFECTS OF CT:

What are the immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women? (donkjellberg).

16\. +4 BAT Vs. WAT:

Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT?

17\. +3 SHIVER VS. NON-SHIVER:

Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis

18\. +4 HYPOTHERMIA & FROST BITE:

What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation? What are the signs of frost bite?

19\. +2 CT and Children:

How do you safely use CT for children? Please discuss Age ranges connected to safe temperature and time ranges. What and how should children eat w/ CT and how can they do the Leptin Reset if needed?

### MJ* — 2012-03-31T00:21:19+0300

### FF_BC — 2012-03-31T00:35:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9006>

> **Honey Crisp;8774 wrote:** How about meal timing?

As an Intermittent Fasting-person, I say: Eat before CT. Did CT fasted 27h - it was a lot harder without food than with. Also if I eat too soon (around 1h) upon doing CT I will suffer, 2-4h is ideal for me at least. But I eat about 1500-2000kcal in one sitting, might be different if eating less. I don't know if this is optimal for _cold adaptation_, but it is better for not shivering as much and makes the CT more comfortable

Also doing CT after a meal should limit the fat storing consequences of a large meal, the same as when doing physical exercise after a meal.

### Sueville — 2012-03-31T00:38:40+0300

> **ChimpChick;8637 wrote:** Here is +3 on this one!

Ditto on these questions.

In addition you said no CO in winter unless you are obese. How do you define obese? For example, my weight is within normal BMI range, however, my BF% is 40% as measured by a BOD POD machine.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-31T00:48:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9010>

hmmm. this brings up a good point. When we toss around words like 'lean' and 'obese' and 'overweight' people may be inadvertently miscategorizing themselves and those categories may not really be that meaningful. I know that I've been carrying a bit of a filter in my head on a lot of the recommendations because of the fact that they are not one size fits all. For example, how much CHO will take one person out of ketosis vs. another, how much PRO did the eskimo's eat to stay out of ketosis, actually pretty high according to the studies I read. So if some recommendation was , e.g. , if you're overweight stick to a ketogenic diet of

### Glamazon — 2012-03-31T02:12:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9031>

> **Cody;8960 wrote:** I believe question 19 is unneeded. I'm pretty sure I was a children once. And I just naturally loved the snow.

when my nephew was 3 he loved to throw himself into the waves on the beach the gulf of mexico in florida in January. that would be in the low 60s, high 50s. We had to keep running after him to pull him out because we thought he would get too cold. We had to drag him away from the water. I loved the snow and cold when I was little too. When I was in my teens my body ran so hot I could get away from wearing a coat and people would stand near me to keep warm. I think I would just present cold water to a kid and let them lead.

During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\]

4\. +6 Frequency:

After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\]

5\. +4 Maintenance:

Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] CT & Age

6\. +3 Inflammation:

How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] U

7\. +5 HOT WATER AND AIR:

Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy?

8\. +5 TELOMERES:

How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\]

9\. +5 SUPPLEMENTAION:

Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad) How does Vitamin D help CT leverage the ancient pathways?

10\. +7 DETOX:

What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama}

11\. +5 CT + HCG HOW TO:

The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

12\. +7 LABS and RANGES SEX and AGE:

What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] What are the minimum most beneficial tests that you would recommend particularly for most of us who will be dealing with skeptical reluctant doctors?

13\. +6 CT INTENSITY:

Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits accrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\]

14\. +5 EXERCISE:

How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg). Additionally when should you LHT/HIIT? Before or after CT? Is it ok to help our body warm up afterwards (putting clothes on, exercising, etc) or is it best to just let your body heat back up on its own? (BJK&&)

15\. +6 RESULTS &EFFECTS OF CT:

What are the immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women? (donkjellberg).

16\. +4 BAT Vs. WAT:

Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT?

17\. +3 SHIVER VS. NON-SHIVER:

Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis

18\. +4 HYPOTHERMIA & FROST BITE:

What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation? What are the signs of frost bite?

19\. +2 CT and Children:

How do you safely use CT for children? Please discuss Age ranges connected to safe temperature and time ranges. What and how should children eat w/ CT and how can they do the Leptin Reset if needed?

20\. What is more important? Seasonal adjustments based on local light levels, local temperatures, or availability and quantity of local food?
Adding Birdy's question #20

> **KiwiLauren;8974 wrote: To go with both #6 and #3... there seems to me still some unanswered questions about O6:O3 ratios in preparation for and adaption to CT. I realise the suggested ratio is 4:1. However, if you compare the way Dr Kane (suggested by Dr Kruse) is calculating the 4:1 ratio it is NOT the same as others are calculating it. For example, someone can have a ratio of 3:1 when they look at total O6 to total O3, but when using the exact same labs and looking at LA to ALA (which is the way Dr Kane suggests calculating it - using the parent fatty acids in each category) you get a 1:63 ratio. This is extremely confusing to me and I have not seen resolution of this discrepancy anywhere (and I've read and re-read the blogs and comments). It is further confusing because Dr Kane recommends a supplement of decidedly non-paleo oils (flax and sunflower) with diet and possibly fish oil supplementation. If anyone can provide clarification of this, then feel free to take it off the list. Otherwise, this seems a really important issue yet it remains unresolved. ### josephd — 2012-03-31T07:35:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9122>

Are there other options than the face dunking, to get started on the CT therapy? And is it necessary to wait on this, or can it happen at the same time as the initial LR? I found your work via Dr. Andrew Colyer, as I was already noticing, that icing my body with 4 really excellent ice packs at a time, was making a profound difference in my ability to sleep well at night. I am now in the early first days of getting set up for the Leptin Reset, etc. Changing my diet to more protein at breakfast this week (shooting for 50g at this point, sometimes I hit 70g), and eating only 3 times a day, with very low carbs, has already meant I have lost four pounds this week! I was already getting such result with icing myself throughout the day (and sometimes, I use packs over night), that I haven't felt right stopping that (tried for one day, and I felt awful in comparison). This is a bit rambly -- but the deal is, I live in a very small apartment, the bathroom sink is too tiny for one's face, AND I honestly for whatever reason, feel really anxious / stressed to try it anyways -- I suppose my kitchen sink might work, but, well, I am resisting. I am happy with the ice packs, and would consider switching to just the Ice Vest you have listed, next, and using packs on my face (as I have been doing). I do also have a bathtub -- so, perhaps once I can order the reusable ice chunks, that might be a good option -- But, since the ice packs work so well, and I really want an ice vest soon -- Is there a big problem with this solution? I really don't mean to sound horribly wimpy, I just am pretty happy as it stands, and it feels like it's taking me a couple weeks to really take in what you are writing on your website, and switch around with food, sleep habits, etc, etc.

Also -- is there a problem with me wanting to sleep on ice packs at night?

I experimented with this, and skipped three nights, and each night I didn't have the ice packs in bed with me, I slept worse, and woke up later. When the ice packs are in bed (on my skin, just with their cloth covering on them), I have happier dreams than I have had in over 3 decades ( I am 45 yo), and I wake up early, pretty ready to go for the day (sometimes at 4.30a! which is shocking to me!!)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-31T13:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9157>

> **MJ\*;8858 wrote:** Dr. Kruse I totally appreciate your work, and passion for helping humanity. At the same time, I agree with these statements, unless we can do our blood work through you somehow, this is going to be difficult to explain to a doctor, because we do need them to order the tests for insurance purposes. I would love it if we could get it all through you...I freakin' hate most doctors (except for you LOL) and haven't been to one in many years...but I know from taking my kids that you only get so much time, how do we easily recruit their help? Their careers are on the line, and the litigious society that we've become makes doctors even more cautious. They, like you once were, are products of a thinking that is narrow when it comes to disease prevention. We will need to educate them...so if we can't somehow do the testing through you, a one page bullet point document that easily explains the testing needed would be really helpful. Then we can point them in your direction if they, and hopefully they will, want to learn more.

Doctors wont respond to this mode.......they only respond when they know you know something they dont. Your results will capture their fancy more than anything else. The primal Rx for your health is you learning this for yourself applying it and showing them.........it is precisely how I fixed myself.......now they all want to know what I did and how I learned it. You can all order labs without a doc.........today. If you try to get somebody to pay for it you have to have skills like Dex and figure it out for yourself........Our job is not to figure out how to make the system fit your current condition.......that is your job as the patient. My job as the doc is to keep you well and keep you free of disease...........if we are both successful we will not need each other much........The knowledge is what you need.........in the beginning this will cost you because the system is not set up to keep you well it is set up to only to help you after your get sick..........to avoid this system of wallet biopsies and illness you will need to do things that you currently are not doing or do not want to do.........That I cant solve. I can only facilitate the path to wellness.........I did it for myself as a patient and it works..........it is harder for you the patient but it works........the current system is easy for you to use and access but it is not successful for most patient in getting better........it is great for just becoming a medical annuity. I think you have to decide how you want to roll in this time where the system does not meet the road to optimal.........I am building the road ot optimal based upon how I got me and my family better.......this is the road I traveled to get it done. I am not going to create alternate roads for everyone's particular needs........that is their job to figure out. Patients rely far too much on the system and they need their own skin in this game........that is a huge problem with out current system.

### Kiera2 — 2012-03-31T13:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9161>

> **Suhenna;9211 wrote:** Are you not the one who says we need to get tested? Now I am confused.

It is confusing. When we were first introduced to the Leptin Rx, whenever someone bumped up against suboptimal results, they were told to test, test, test. Don't fly blind! Dr Kruse has even written a blog on Optimizing Labs.

But then came Cold Thermogenesis. With this amazing process, we can detox, heal hormonal imbalances, heal physical degeneration (thankyou!), live longer, stay younger, the sky's the limit!

It would seem that CT also renders blood tests useless. If levels are screwed up in the beginning, it doesn't matter, because they'll be fixed. Eventually. And test results will be screwed up if taken when you start CT anyway; all that detox and hormonal upheaval.

We know that if we eat as we evolved to eat, and get ourselved cold adapted, and let sunshine be our only light, all our neolithic disease will be addressed. So why test?

The only time I think it would be useful, is if you tested _before_ making any changes. Then you'd have something to compare to after six months of living the protocol.

Have I got that almost right Dr Kruse?

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-31T14:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9174>

The IT side of me would love in general to see more diagrams/charts to support all the great writing in your blog and help people answer questions for themselves.

**What to eat, When to eat it table

For example, if someone wanted to eat sweet potatoes being able to look up in a chart that you should eat no more than x grams per kg of lean body mass, and only when the total avail daylight ( nautical sunrise to nautical sunset??) is > Y hours. Yes I picked one of those controversial safe starches on purpose. What comes to mind is the type of charts the coop extension services publish on things like "when to prune deciduous shrubs/trees" or "when to plant "

**Symptom/Possible Cause/Possible Solution Diagram**

a chart on symptoms - whether they are pre existing or the result of LRx or CT - potential causes that merit investigation

For example - nausea - postprandial to the BAB - consider x. post CT - consider y. Having a list of potential natural helps to side effects would also be valuable.

Protocol Decision Tree.** Decision diagram to help figure out which protocols to use and when. Do you fail the mirror test? Yes, then Leptin Rx and then continue on to Leaky gut questions. If No to the mirror test, then ask Vit D/ HSCRP /Reverse T3 parameter questions. Something similar to the popular "Is it Paleo" decision tree.

If I had to prioritize these, it would be the What Protocol, What to Eat/When, Symptom Chart. And as I finish this post, I realize that it makes absolutely no sense to put any of these in a FAQ blog post, but as potential charts in your book.

> **Jack Kruse;9208 wrote:** This line of thinking is a huge problem......modern parenting is a neolithic thought that subjugates your children's paleo genes.........we need to stop making life easy for them and begin to make it what it is supposed to be. Little mammals like the cold and snow and hate vegetables for a reason..........we force things on them because of cultural and social beliefs........parents must begin to challenge everything they believe too.......In my house.......that also has changed and it was not easy to re train my kids of wife.......it took some very dastardly moves on my part. But it is now done. I took no quarter doing them.

o.m.g...this is so obvious now. I hated veggies growing up unless it was something that I tore out of the ground and ate. My fav veggie meals were anything that involved bacon...like bacon salad dressing and green beans and bacon...heavy emphasis on the bacon.

I still have to fight the neolithic conditioning at age 54 to not feel guilty because I am not eating veggies at every meal. I have always craved meat and fat since I was a kid but my parents, schools, and neolithic acculturation were horrified. My mother cringes even now when I tell her how fat heavy my diet is. I am losing weight, I feel better and eat my pork belly for breakfast without guilt. Even now I work for a huge HMO, Kaiser, as an RN and it would be heresy and career suicide to talk about a paleo ketogenic diet when the staff nutritionists/diabetes certified are promoting the ADA diet.

### TheKid — 2012-03-31T16:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9210>

> **Glamazon;9261 wrote:** o.m.g...this is so obvious now. I hated veggies growing up unless it was something that I tore out of the ground and ate. My fav veggie meals were anything that involved bacon...like bacon salad dressing and green beans and bacon...heavy emphasis on the bacon.
>
>
>
> I still have to fight the neolithic conditioning at age 54 to not feel guilty because I am not eating veggies at every meal. I have always craved meat and fat since I was a kid but my parents, schools, and neolithic acculturation were horrified. My mother cringes even now when I tell her how fat heavy my diet is. I am losing weight, I feel better and eat my pork belly for breakfast without guilt. Even now I work for a huge HMO, Kaiser, as an RN and it would be heresy and career suicide to talk about a paleo ketogenic diet when the staff nutritionists/diabetes certified are promoting the ADA diet.

This brings up the VERY important point of what to do with kids. It is something I have commented on before, and I am still not entirely settled on the issue. I know Jack would probably say I have some neolithic thoughts holding me back, but I think that the issue of "kids" is one of those "where the rubber hits the road" type issues that Jack will need to answer sometime in the future, either here on his site or in response to his critics.

For me personally, I know that I am facing several modern disease states for which Jack's protocols seem to show evidence of having efficacy. But scientifically speaking, I do have concerns when it comes to children, especially babies and toddlers. I therefore would love to see in a future blog, more on Jack's rationale behind the protocols and how they apply to various demographics, age ranges and between the genders.

While the "this is how we are supposed to live" message is powerful and transformative, Jack is not the first (and likely will not be the last) to make such assertions. There is a lot of debate and testing that is going to go into making an airtight case for what he proposes. And when it comes to kids and babies, parents are going to be very discerning.

For instance, as I know, babies are limited in their sense of taste, with sweet being the most developed at birth. I also came across a couple articles such as the one in the link below that discuss a waning preference for sweet as a child develops and how that may correlate with bone growth.

[http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/7955837.stm](http://http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/7955837.stm)

Such studies obviously need to be critiqued, but I bring it up because there is a lot of information out there, conventional or otherwise.

The question of kids was one of the things that quickly became an issue in other Paleo forums, which is why Robb Wolf has addressed it directly and why books like Everyday Paleo garner attention.

I am particularly concerned about Jack's "no safe starches" assertion. I get it that, for adults, starches are OK if you are LS, cold adapted and if the date is June 21. But does that apply to all children as well? What are the implications of restricting generally Paleo-approved starches from kids at other times of the year? Is it valid to use evidence of eating patterns in traditional Inuit children and to extrapolate that to all human children, or all humans for that matter?

Matt LaLonde is particularly skilled at investigating such questions and looking at the thought process all the way from premsie to conclusion. I would love see that type of explicit analysis in a new blog and I would love to see Jack specifically address children and any other age ranges where variation in recommendations might be needed.

The Kid, I honestly do not understand your concerns really... Dr Kruse is telling us to eat seasonally. What could be better and safer? Give your kids as much wild food as you can, and as seasonal as you can. It could not get easier! And babies, only mothers milk as long as they want. ### LisaAPB — 2012-03-31T17:37:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9239>

Mothers milk is sweet and fat laden, it looks like butter. I can't tell you how many children I've had to chase away from dipping into the butter dish on the counter. The most persistent child, the one who hit that butter dish fast and often, is my grand niece, who is a bit autistic. I think she instinctively knew what was best for her, choosing eggs and bacon over french toast or pancakes any day of the week. I had the extreme good fortune to lose my job when my grandson was getting ready to try adult foods, so I babysat him for a number of months. I gave him tidbits of all kinds of things, and followed his lead on what he wanted/needed. Some days he was pure carnivore, other days he preferred bits of fruit. And all of the kids loved playing in the pool, sprinkler, or with tubs of cool water regardless of how cold it was. They'd get cold, shiver, stop, repeat... but during naptime or night time, they heat up like little furnaces, sweat and throw off the blankets.

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-31T18:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9275>

Abnormal fat is WAT, not BAT. WAT is storage fat, not energy producing brown fat. Hcg effects progesterone levels, which somewhat mimic the first trimester of pregnancy (when cells are growing/forming/dividing). The Hcg diet is calorie restrictive, somewhat ketogenic, protein rich and is often proceeded with a carb load. In Nature carb loading was found seasonally and preceding the onset of winter cold. Carb loading preps the body in anticipation of cold weather, limited calories, and metabolic changes to survive the harsh months. If one never brings on the cold, the carb loading SAD way leads to more WAT.

WAT produces metabolic hormones that can throw the body out of whack if the cold isn't introduced seasonally to reset the balance. Both WAT and BAT are types of fat cells, created by Nature for a reason. The WAT ideally would store enough energy to supply the BAT to get one through a cold harsh period of deprivation. Cold induces the growth and activation of BAT, and it takes it's conversion from WAT. Some BAT is converted into muscles tissue. All of this conversion also effects the different gas levels in the tissues too, such as oxygen.

Our cells have memory, and developed adaptations to grow or survive under certain environments. Since hCG functions in our earliest environment prior to birth, and it was not disadvantageous to the growing fetus, it served a purpose. The adaptive changes induced by cold also served a purpose. We have thwarted that purpose by overfeeding our WAT and underfeeding our BAT.

In the general context of health if my statement regarded that, then I could see why you'd make this statement. However, in the context of this thread, and my statement, I respectfully disagree with your assumptions. This thread was started to find out what our FAQs are for CT to help others who might eventually have the same questions and concerns that we do. As a parent it is my job to be concerned about all the decisions I make for my children. I support the questions about CT and children for the FAQs because there are things that are not so clear about whether or not the procedures for children should be the same or different. If some of us have questions, others will too. If the procedures are the same, then saying so will make the questions go away. If the approach should be different, then outlining how is a good idea. I am trying to do what's best and safest for my children, and those to come along in this journey...the ability to do that comes from knowledge - knowledge is power, and context is everything.

> **Shijin13;8864 wrote:** For those of you trying to figure out what tests you want to run - do this...
>
> tell your doc you want the following tests run, and you want to leverage health insurance as much as you can, all he/she has to do is sign the chit, and provide dX codes.
>
>
>
> I emailed UHC and gave them the lists of tests I wanted to run and verified which would be covered/not covered - they actually gave me my DX codes -
>
>
>
> my Chiropractor will run what ever tests I want - and all I do is use the same DX codes... my new PCP who I found on Paeo Physicains network - will also run any test. She's very progressive in her testing & she's figured out how to get insurance to cover most if not all tests
>
>
>
> You're paying your doctor - you've enter a contract w/each other; ask for what you want - when the balk - ask them - "What harm does drawing and extra vial or two do? Does it hurt you? Does it hurt me? I'm interested in seeing what these labs look like and I'd like to see if insurance will cover them. - if they don't I'm willing to pay for them - but I'd rather go through you vs going out on my own... I'm trying to take responsibility for my health - I thought as a doctor you'd appreciate a patient who's a proactive participant in their care. I guess if you're not interested in that, I can always find another doctor"
>
>
>
> While threatening to leave their practice may not scare them today - but as more and more ppl take responsibility for their own care - the doctors who thrive in mediocrity will find themselves w/o patients eventually. It won't happen over night but it will happen...

I just went to a doc I found on paleo physicians' network and brought my list of tests I wanted. She was able to get me many of them, but not the sex hormones to test if I still have estrogen dominance or not. She said I had to go to the obgyn for that or to an alternative doc (who won't be covered by insurance). I did get all of the thyroid panel, TSH, t4, t3, reverse t3, and antibodies. She said I had to have reasons so I said hair loss, fatigue, weight gain. And she said that would work.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-01T00:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9413>

Added #11 and put my vote back on #2 and 4/5

The Top Twenty List:

1\. What in the H.. is Factor X? \[LisaAPB\]

2\. How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes. At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] +2

3\. Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] +1 How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted? +1

4\. Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488)

At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\] +2

5\. After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\] +1 Claudia +1

6\. Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] +1 CT & Age +1 Claudia

7\. How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] +1

8\. Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy? +1

9\. How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\] +1

10\. Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] +1

11\. What is the role of quantum mechanics in the ancient pathway? \[hellojtm\]

12.

13.

14.

15.

16.

17.

18.

19.

20.

### hellojtm — 2012-04-01T00:12:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9419>

GREAT POST THIS. BOLDED some of my favorite parts.

> **Jack Kruse;9213 wrote: Doctors wont respond to this mode.......they only respond when they know you know something they dont. Your results will capture their fancy more than anything else. The primal Rx for your health is you learning this for yourself applying it and showing them.........it is precisely how I fixed myself.......now they all want to know what I did and how I learned it. You can all order labs without a doc.........today. If you try to get somebody to pay for it you have to have skills like Dex and figure it out for yourself........Our job is not to figure out how to make the system fit your current condition.......that is your job as the patient. My job as the doc is to keep you well and keep you free of disease...........**if we are both successful we will not need each other much........The knowledge is what you need.........in the beginning this will cost you because the system is not set up to keep you well it is set up to only to help you after your get sick..........to avoid this system of wallet biopsies and illness you will need to do things that you currently are not doing or do not want to do.........That I cant solve. I can only facilitate the path to wellness.........I did it for myself as a patient and it works..........it is harder for you the patient but it works........**the current system is easy for you to use and access but it is not successful for most patient in getting better**........it is great for just becoming a medical annuity. I think you have to decide how you want to roll in this time where the system does not meet the road to optimal.........I am building the road ot optimal based upon how I got me and my family better.......this is the road I traveled to get it done. I am not going to create alternate roads for everyone's particular needs........that is their job to figure out.** Patients rely far too much on the system and they need their own skin in this game........that is a huge problem with out current system**.

AMEN Dr. Kruse.

Sorry for all the caps, but damn. Great post.

### Tizawee — 2012-04-01T14:44:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9737>

The Top Twenty List:

1\. What in the H.. is Factor X? \[LisaAPB\]

2\. How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes. At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] +1

3\. Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] +1 How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that Iâ€™m cold adapted? +1

4\. Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488)

At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\] +1

5\. After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it?\[ Lexi\] +1

6\. Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst\] +1 CT & Age

7\. How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] +1

8\. Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy? +1

9\. How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\] +1

10\. Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] +1

11\. What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama} +1

12\. I would like to know the seasonal distinctions..If we are eating carbs (seasonal allowed fruits/veggies) in summer do we still CT?

Also.. The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG...

\-If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

13\. What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) \[Garfield\] +1

14.Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits acrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\]

15\. How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg).

16\. Immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women (donkjellberg).

17\. Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad)

18\. Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

19\. How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT?

20\. How important is calorie restriction in CT?

21\. Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis.

22\. What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation?

My input:

+1 to all the above.

23\. If we're tracking our progress through LR + CT, which metrics would be useful? There could be as many versions of a journal as there are people keeping one. Off the top of my head I can think of weekly weight, measurements, and photos. Which other metrics would be useful for someone studying our demographics? (Because we're the first sizable group doing this, it's likely going to happen at some point.)

\- Things commonly asked by people working through the process, but to which we already have answers

\- Things that may or may not be commonly asked, but to which we do not already have answers

I don't know if Dr. Kruse is trying to create a FAQ, or answer questions we on the forum currently have. If the former, we should ask things like, "Should I hold off on labs till detox is done?" (we just learned that answer), but if the latter, we should ask things like, ... well heck, I suddenly can't think of anything I want to know that hasn't already been answered. Brain fog. Okay, like, "should children do the LR as-is, or modified in any way, and based on what variables (height, weight, age, pre-/post-puberty)?"

Most of the questions I've seen in these lists onthis thread so far have been answered. If we just want the answers, some of us can take the time to answer those and cite the forum or blog or talk where Dr. Kruse has answered it. If we want those questions and answers available on the blog (which I think is a good goal), then those questions should stay.

### Tizawee — 2012-04-01T16:41:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9763>

> **Destiny;9271 wrote:** One would naively think that HMO would be open to ways to decrease their expenses.

I work for UnitedHealthcare. We do have an initiative in place to reward us for proactively taking preventive measures. However, they take their cues from the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force, a government agency. The government is still listening to conventional (erroneous) wisdom about low fat diets. For example, their solution for me, with FBG 112, is to eat more complex carbohydrates. Ugh!

Atkins had been in print for about 20 years before low-carb really took off. And the government has only begun to realize, in this century, that there may be something to it. Until there is enough irrefutable empirical evidence that high fat/protein/low carb is doing more than just helping people lose weight, they will not change their approach.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-04-01T16:49:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9765>

Speaking about Children:

Funny enough I added that question even though I have no children.. because I have friends I'm trying to help who do all have children.

Maybe "over-thinking" is involved, but that's just how parents seem to roll. LOL. I still think there are plenty of issues about children doing ct and/or the reset that should be addressed.

Here are multiple examples..

1\. You have a kid who is 8 yrs old and is overweight or obese. You want them to do the Leptin Rx.. Now should a child really eat 50g of protein in the morning.. or should that be a smaller amount? What is the best amount of protein for children of different ages?

2\. You want your kid to do CT. All of the pools around you are heated. How cold should you make their water? Is it the same for kids as for adults? Is it the same time length?

I'm 28.. and growing up I loved the cold and cold showers. BUT, everyone else I knew took hot showers and hated the cold. Yes, we played in the snow, but some people don't live where it snowed. Even that long ago almost all of the pools around us were heated and only open in the summer. There was one unheated pool and all of us hated it and usually went to the heated ones. Being in the cold and eating a bunch of meat and fat isn't necessarily the "natural" inclinations of every child. Food is still mainly decided by upbringing and what is socially acceptable. In the US at least offal and insects (which are quite healthy) are not something most people eat. Therefore their children think it's gross as well. And we all know that just eating muscle meat and fat isn't going to cut it. We need seafood and offal if we aren't going to eat any veggies.. and they are better than veggies anyways.

All of my friends with kids are/will be skeptical of ct for their children. People are worried about kids out in the cold air without gobs of coats and stuff on. I can tell them what's best, but they would be more convinced if I could point them to an actual post by a Dr. AKA Dr. Kruse that explained how things should work for children. I don't know any parent that would just take me on my word that they should put there 3 yr old in a cold bath instead of a warm one.. and that being out in the cold without a jacket/hat/gloves is actually healthy and they won't get sick. I'm not a doctor and I'm not a parent. They will still just do what they think is best.

I'm lucky in that I have so much free time to read all of these awesome posts and learn how to be healthy. My friends with kids can't find the time to read a short post on anything. So, for me to be of any help I need to have answers to their many questions about their children. So, this may be "over-thinking", but I want my friends kids to be healthy and this is what it takes.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-01T18:38:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-9814>

I have made a rule for my kids that they all have to have protein that is at least 1/6 of their body weight (I started this rule when I did Radiant Recovery) for each meal. Sounds picky, but what it really means is the little ones get an egg (or the equivalent in meat) for breakfast and at least an ounce of meat for each other meal (usually they eat much more), and the big ones get two eggs (since none of them are over 70lb yet) and at least two ouncesof meat for each other meal (they ALWAYS eat much more).

What I've seen is similar to what I saw in me adding the BAB - the little ones who were melting down each afternoon stopped as soon as I required them to have an egg each morning. Just like that. Cravings started to diminish.

When I started the LR, I upped their protein to at least 14g for everybody, and things got even better.

They aren't keto, but have no grains, and starches are rare. 1/2 - 1-1/2 servings fruit per day, depending on time of year ... working on making that even more seasonal.

> What I've seen is similar to what I saw in me adding the BAB - the little ones who were melting down each afternoon stopped as soon as I required them to have an egg each morning. Just like that. Cravings started to diminish.
>
>
>
> When I started the LR, I upped their protein to at least 14g for everybody, and things got even better.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> They aren't keto, but have no grains, and starches are rare. 1/2 - 1-1/2 servings fruit per day, depending on time of year ... working on making that even more seasonal.

Mamagrok, could you post this on the Kids thread? This info would be so helpful to parents, etc. Thanks!

### hellojtm — 2012-04-02T18:51:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-10360>

This thread has slowed down. Time for a recap, plus I had another question to add that will help people and I don't believe are answered on the blog. I propose that for the ones that have been answered on the blog that people start putting the responses in this thread.... I will answer a couple below:

1\. What in the H.. is Factor X? \[LisaAPB\]

2\. How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes. At one extreme, I understand winter should be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto. What about summer and diet, icy tubbing, etc? Can/should we ease up on the keto and cold? THANKS \[Meyoolia\] +1

3\. Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer? \[healthnut\] +1 How will the sunny weather effect me biologically now that I’m cold adapted? +1

4\. Please talk about how to maximize CT.... is there something to a compression garment or not? would we have to be moving, like in Vasper, to get this lactic acid/HGH benefit?

[http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488](http://http://www.smartplanet.com/video/new...of-hgh/6246488)

At NASA Ames Research Park, scientist Peter Wasowski has developed an alternative exercise machine called Vasper. The user wears compression cuffs on the arms and legs and core-cooling panels on the chest and head. During exercise, the machine traps large amounts of lactic acid in the body, stimulating the pituitary gland and producing more HGH or human growth hormone. \[Lexi\] +1

5\. After some point does the law of diminishing returns set in? How much CT would one need a week to maintain what they got if they are happy with it? Will I have to do CT daily for the rest of my life to maintain the pathway? Or once I'm adapted is there a different protocol?\[Kpcst/Lexi\] +1 CT & Age

6\. How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation? \[Shijin13\] +1

7\. Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy? +1

8\. How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened? {Shijin13\] +1

9\. Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose? \[Marsha\] +1

10\. What are good methods of speeding detox? \[Glamorama} +1

11\. The big HCG connection.. I feel like 3 of us get it and the rest are trapped by their fear of fat plus HCG... If CT plus HCG will light the world on fire -And CT needs fat -Wouldn't HCG + CT require fat in the absence of the protocol apples(ChimpChick)

12\. What are the ranges you look for in the basic lab tests? (Please include differences for gender/age/menopause differences.) If we are tracking progress what other metrics would be useful? \[Nonchalant/Garfield\] +1

13.Dr. Kruse. Related to this, you have mentioned in your blogs that additional benefits acrue from " steepening the gradient." could you describe some of these benefits along with the specifics of what you mean by "steepening"? Longer? Colder? More often? Your CT protocol mentioned that it was focused mainly on weight loss. What are the other protocols for other benefits (please describe them)? Thanks! \[randychaps\]

14\. How you should modify exercise while doing CT (donkjellberg).

15\. Immediate perceived side effects of CT for both men and women (donkjellberg).

16\. Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season.(localad)

17\. Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

18\. How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT?

19\. How important is calorie restriction in CT?

20\. Is CT enhanced if done at temperatures that DON'T result in shivering? ie. Non-shivering Thermogenesis.

21\. What are the signs/indications of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too far on the protocol, would mild hypothermia have any effect on the progress of cold adaptation?

22\. Where does the 50-55degree skin temp specification come from? Is this a specific trigger that has been shown or is it a rule of thumb? Is the energy throughput more important or the skin temperature. It would seem that the body would respond to the energy demand of the pathway as well. If 50-55 degrees is a trigger then we should trigger it many times a day, but possibly would not need to be for the 45-60 minute durations of the ice baths, which could provide a strong energy demand of the pathway at lesser temperatures. Please expand.... \[hellojtm\]

5 & 6 merged

11 & 2 merged, retained hog part of 11

12 & 22 merged

added 22

ANSWERS:

1\. buy the book

2\. more carbs and fruit in summer unless you are still trying to lose weight or trying to recover a lot of telomere length

3\. no, being cold adapted makes you more tolerant of temperature in general

5\. there is a different protocol depending on if you are trying to lose weight, if not you can reduce to 2-5x @ 10-20mins/week

17\. it's an important part but only the tip of the iceberg

18\. assume that you don't until you are cold adapted (definition to follow?)

19\. it's not important, but it's easy

### donkjellberg — 2012-04-18T20:27:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-16281>

I cleaned up the questions and made them look more like general FAQs. The HCG concerns are removed per Dr. K's suggestion.

1\. What in the heck is Factor X? How does it relate to the triad of strick paleo, CT, and sleep?

2\. How should diet and CT be adjusted to adapt to seasonal changes? Should it be dark, COLD, and hardcore keto all year or should diet and CT be adjusted in the summer?

3\. Does being cold adapted make one more intolerant of a hot climate in summer and should we be concerned with being cold in winter?

4\. Are there methods to optimize CT such as use of compression garments, exercise like in Vasper, for lactic acid/HGH benefit?

5\. Does the law of diminishing returns set in during CT? How often would one need to use CT to maintain optimal benefits?

6\. How does CT reduce/reverse inflammation?

7\. Are saunas, hot tubs and sunbathing unhealthy?

8\. How does CT reverse telomeres that are shortened?

9\. Why is it important to take Bitter Melon before CT and what is the recommended dose?

10\. What are some methods to help with the detox related to CT?

11\. What are the ranges you look for in lab tests, i.e., variations for gender, age, and hormone changes?

12\. If we are tracking lab progress what should be considered?

13.What is "steepening the gradient" or deep CT?

14\. Your CT protocol focused mainly on weight loss. What are other benefits?

15\. How you should modify exercise while doing CT?

16\. What are some of the immediate perceived side effects of CT for men and women?

17\. Should Vit D supplementation level track the CT season?

18\. Isn't CT mainly about activating, recruiting, and using brown adipose tissue (BAT)?

19\. How do we know if we have adequate BAT to properly use CT or do we even need it?

20\. How important is calorie restriction in CT?

21\. How is CT affected by using temperatures that DON'T result in shivering?

22\. What are the signs and symptoms of hypothermia? If someone pushes themselves too what are the dangers and pitfalls?

23\. Where does the 50-55 degree skin temp recommendations come from?

23\. Since CT is supposed to help those of us become leptin sensitive, what time frame or indications should we looking for?
