### Snowyky — 2012-05-02T02:39:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-working-on-a-new-blog-and-i-want-your-input-ct-faqs.534/post-20568>

> **FF\_BC;9058 wrote:** Also if I eat too soon (around 1h) upon doing CT I will suffer, 2-4h is ideal for me at least. But I eat about 1500-2000kcal in one sitting, might be different if eating less. I don't know if this is optimal for _cold adaptation_, but it is better for not shivering as much and makes the CT more comfortable

I take the 2 capsules of bitter melon right before I get ready to set up CT, read somewhere you should take it at least 2 hours before, but it works better for me to take it just before, this is what Dr K is talking about, we need to test these things ourselves, it isn't a one size fits all kind of thing. I don't shiver but then I don't sit in ice either, the water is between 50 and 55 degrees, if it's warmer I don't sweat it, I just get my husband to get more frozen bottles out of the freezer until the temp goes down. Sometimes I just do what I can. I did try to make this whole protocol too complicated when I first started, if I went up a lb I stressed about it, I finally put the scale away, quit using fitaday and am eating as clean as I can. The weight is coming off, maybe not as fast as I'd like but it's always on the downward stretch. I'll eat this way the rest of my life, and will CT as well since I feel better when I do. Remember that Dr K said this is all new, he's not going to have every answer for every complaint because it hasn't been documented before.

# Thread: Can sciatica be nutrition related?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthnut — 2012-03-30T03:31:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-8540>

The past two weeks I've been experiencing sciatica.... sleep is impossible. I am doing all the stretches and exercises, but it has not gone away yet.

I was wondering if there might be specific depleted nutients in those who experience this.....or is it more of a mechanical problem?

Maybe there any supplements that might target nerve pain? The CT seems to help somewhat, but the pain is most pronounced at night....runs into the glutes, legs and the back of my hips....torture!

I had read a while back that sciatica may present as a type of neuropathy that is seen in diabetics? I take large doses of ALA & benfotiamine for peripheral neuropahty and that has kept me symptom free. Which leads me to believe the sciatica is more of a structural issue.

### tweety — 2012-03-31T01:53:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9024>

Interesting. I got this a few weeks ago. I was trying to decide if I was healing old injuries (retracing) or if I had actually injured it again while walking in bad shoes. Well, it went away on its own. It started when I really cleaned up my diet and did CT, so I am leaning towards it being a healing reaction.

With everyone really cleaning up their diets here, I would guess there is a lot of healing going on.

### healthnut — 2012-03-31T03:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9052>

I started CT a few weeks ago....maybe that's the deal. I didn't make the connection, thanks! The retracing theory is interesting. I bulged some discs about 5 years ago, so maybe the formerly injured areas are getting some new healing and nerve stimulation.

### shilohman — 2012-03-31T03:30:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9061>

according to Dr K it most definitely is. Can't remember which blog where he states that. Just search for sciatica, you should find it.

### Melinda — 2012-03-31T03:31:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-sciatica-be-nutrition-related.535/post-9062>

Me too! I've had sciatica off and on over the years and two days after my first tub soak, wham, I bent over the dishwasher and all the muscles in my lower back pulled at once. The sciatica developed over a few days and I'm still holding myself carefully. I feel great when I'm in the swimming pool and I think I'm healing quickly. I wondered what got it started?

# Thread: Strength in muscles (and other places!)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Cody — 2012-03-30T03:39:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-8549>

OK. So I've been icing for about a month now. Sleeping with my windows open, and taking varying degrees of cold showers.

I've had my share of negative side effects. Lots of light headedness, dizziness, freezing my butt off, etc. etc. Lots of hunger, tiredness, wakefulness, moodiness, etc.

All attributable to toxins and estrogen getting released from my rapidly emptying fat cells.

On top of the above, my excrement now smells awful. Chemicaly. Sorry if this is TMI, but am I the only one having this issue? It smells like poison or plastic or something. Really bad.

Anyway. So I did experience one benefit the very first week of CT (and in my case I went from a 1000 calorie per day PSMF to Keto eating with no limit on calories, and very high fat). I got stronger and my muscles got noticably larger.

I was unable to do a full pushup to the ground before, and a month later, I can do ten with perfect form. And keep in mind that I have been strength training for years AND I haven't lost any scale weight.

I actually did push ups with my arms on one platform and my feet on another, so that I was basically suspended in mid air, and was able to do five going much lower (because of my belly not hitting the ground).

I'm extremely impressed with the strength and muscle gains here!

On another, probably TMI note, my libido is through the roof the past few days. Good stuff, Maynard!

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T05:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-8612>

### thyme — 2012-03-30T15:44:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-8714>

> **agatha;8736 wrote:** I am hoping for some strength gains - do you find you have to do the CT justt prior to exercising to get the benefits or are you still stronger even if you haven't CTd that day?
>
> I have never been able to do a press up in my life - it would be nice to be able to do even just one...

I've found a general strength build-up regardless of CTing on any given day, but I haven't looked closely enough to note whether that increase is even greater on CT days. Right out of the bath, I'm too bloody cold to do much, but a few hours later-watch out!

off-topic: The press-up thing is near and dear to my heart because I was exactly the same way. A year ago, I decided enough was enough and I was tired of being a weakling. I followed Mark Sisson's

[push-up progression](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/how-to-proper-pushup-technique/#axzz1qWGhN8bW) and it worked beautifully. Watch the video on that page and download the free e-book--he has you start with wall push-ups, and then bench push-ups, building strength as you go. Follow those progressions, and you'll be doing press-ups much faster than you think!!

### texasmom4 — 2012-03-30T15:48:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-8719>

I'm doing the reset right now, and staying away from LHT per protocol for the reset. I have noticed the past couple weeks that my biceps are popping out though, and when I "make a muscle" it's actually pretty decent and impressive. It would have to be from CT since I'm not doing anything else. So amazing to me. Dr Kruse is a genius!

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-30T16:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-8732>

I'm doing the reset and CT (for over 3 weeks now). I'm not lean, but I also don't have lots to lose. I also haven't been working out AT ALL while doing this (except for a daily walk during lunch break).

Just out of curiousity, I tested a couple of things to see if strength spontaneously improved, and it did. I went from being able to do 3 normal push ups to being able to do 8. I went from never doing a chin up in my life, to doing one, with just a little help from my feet against the frame of the door (I was using the iron gym bar that hand on the door frame), and I can hold myself up there for a long duration, which was also something I couldn't do before starting these protocols. Gonna test these both again next week to see if they have improved.

And, and heck yes to the rise in libido. If my libido wasn't so nuts, I'd actually CT more, but I find I'm spending every other night playing in bed instead of playing in the tub.

Cody - I'm not sure my poop smells as you described, but it's noticeably worse-smelling than it was before CT.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-30T16:24:20+0300

my strength gains on massive CT days is nothing short of astounding.....

### Cody — 2012-03-30T18:19:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-8820>

I do CT in the morning and also right after I workout. From what I've read, muscle growth and strength are limited by the inflammatory response after a workout. Lowering inflammation will speed up the process. I'm able to heal much more quickly from a workout.

Brad Pilon has a great article about why testosterone is a natural anti-inlammatory and that is why raising testosterone increases fat burning and muscle growth.

### Tizawee — 2012-04-06T12:52:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strength-in-muscles-and-other-places.536/post-12081>

> **Cody;8598 wrote:** On top of the above, my excrement now smells awful. Chemicaly. Sorry if this is TMI, but am I the only one having this issue? It smells like poison or plastic or something. Really bad.
>
>
>
> Good stuff, Maynard!

Cody -

My Mom used to say she'd left a "monster" in the bathroom. For some reason it made an impression on me, and I began paying attention to my own bathroom monsters. I have always been able to tell if I've over-caffeinated or overdosed my vitamins by the smell of my urine, or if I've had corn-fed beef or too much dairy by my stool. And Day-Quil capsules make my stool smell like crude oil. Ugh! Never take \*that\* anymore.

I discovered all kinds of food allergies when I went on Atkins 2 years ago and have had a really clean diet for the past 18 months... not paleo because I hadn't heard of it yet, but close. These days, IF I leave a monster in the bathroom it's because I've had an offending food.

It'll pass once you dump all the toxins. (pun intended )

Geesh! I didn't think anyone remembered Maynard anymore!

Elin

# Thread: Intermittent fasting and CT - Swedish Style!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### FF_BC — 2012-03-30T05:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-8621>

I copy paste my contribution in the meet and greet section of the forum (posted 2012-03-27):

> Hello everyone!
>
>
>
> I'm a young man from Sweden, almost 22 years old
>
> Height: 190cm
>
> Starting weight (summer 2011): 109 kg
>
> Present weight: 97 kg
>
> Goal weight: Do not know
>
>
>
> I've been low carb since mid-summer 2011, went from 109 kg to 102 kg in 3 months. Since about Januari I've been IF:ing (intermittent fasting) leangainsstyle. While eating paleo/low carb I lost about another 7 kg in 3 months. The last month or so I've actually gained 1-2 kg partly because eating like a moron, though I suspect some muscle gain from squating and deadlifting.
>
>
>
> Came across Dr Jack Kruse and CT while reading some blogs from paleopeople for about two weeks ago, read some of Dr JK:s blogpost and just could not stop thinking about CT for about a week. Ended up taking colder showers and dunking my face in cold water for a couple of days then thought: "wtf I'm Swedish, cold shouldn't be a proplem for a Viking like myself!". Did my first cold bath (not using ice, just the water from the tap at coldest setting) three days ago, the first 5 minutes I shivered like crazy and then it became oddly nice Sat for 15 minutes my first session, 30 minutes the second and 35 minutes yesterday (third session).

So now I've been travelling for a couple of days, not having access to a bathtub. Tried a cold shower, but I rather try waterboarding, showers are like torture!

I'm usually fasting for 16-23h a day, just drinking coffee and water until breaking the fast.

My goal with this journal is to write down my heavy lifting and CT-sessions.

Today it was Squat day!

\* Squat 3 set x 5 reps at 100 kg

\* 5\*5 pullups - **_I've been noticing a improvment since starting with CT, don't know if it is due to loosing weight or getting stronger_

I just want to add that I'm a rookie at lifting weights, until 2 months ago I never lifted weights. Right now I'm focusing on technique rather than lifting the heaviest weights.

I rate todays workout 8/10!**

### FF_BC — 2012-03-31T01:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-9021>

No training today, rest day. Working the nightshift now and I'm suspecting CT has influenced my hormone balance. First night yesterday I was so tired I could barely keep awake betweem 5-7 am. This despite having slept more than I usually do before the first night...

Now I'm preparing myself for deadlifts tomorrow - RPT style!

### FF_BC — 2012-04-02T01:52:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-10034>

So I didn't do deads yesterday, I decided it was better to take another day of rather than pushing my body too far with nightwork and all.

Did deads today instead. Fasted 22h. Tried 130 kg but wasn't able to lift it of the ground  Lowered to 120kg and made two reps, lost my grip and as I set the barbell to the floor, tried to switch grip but lost momentum and had to quit. Then lowered to 110 kg and did 5 reps.

Deads

120kg \*2

110kg\*5

Chins (palm towards face)

3\*5

Really need to buy a belt, so I can add weight to my chins/pullups.

Last nightshift, only 8h left!!

### AmyJ — 2012-04-02T03:47:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-10096>

"Last nightshift, only 8h left!! "

Yay! here's to sleeping when it's dark!

I'm so happy for all the people who have found Dr. K while they are young!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-02T04:08:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-10118>

i agree. If I can save one young kid its worth it......they could be the starfish

### FF_BC — 2012-04-03T13:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-10718>

Sleeping when it's dark is awesome! Dr J.K I plan on living for as long as I can, figure if I start as soon as possible my chances increases...

So I'm done abusing my body for at least a couple of weeks. Stayed awake from 18:45 pm sunday until 20:10 pm monday, 25h. Slept 13h, woke up around 9am (swedish time). So I've been abusing my circadian rythm quite hard.

Watched the lecture from Cambridge on circadian receptors yesterday, in the lecture prof Foster said that there was circadian receptors many places in the body, heart, eye, gut, etc. So i thought: "How do I tell my body it's time to be awake and reset my circadian rythm?" so with that in my mind I decided to eat breakfast today. Ate a high protein breakfast, with lots of coffee. Actually that did no good to my "nightshift hangover", it might have made things worse even... The next time I'm working nightshift I will stick to IF when "turning the clock", that works better for some reason.

I grew more frustrated at the abuse of my body...

So I did what every screwed up human (hormone wise) should do, I poured myself a tub of cold water and sat there for 20 minutes.

Due to the abuse of my body the 20 minute session was more uncomfortable than the 40 minute session I did almost a week ago. Got out of the tub and went to the balcony (in shorts) and dried in the sun  The sun and the CT did wonders to my nightshift hangover! Dried in the sun for about 10 minutes, it is 6 degrees Celsius (42 fahrenheit) outside and a little windy so after 10 minutes I went inside to type this and put some clothes on. Now I'm going to pour myself another cup of coffee and read a book in the sun

### FF_BC — 2012-04-04T13:39:07+0300

Dips, BW

5set\*5rep**

### FF_BC — 2012-04-05T12:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-11662>

I **did 15 minutes of CT yesterday evening (120404), I was pretty sore after walking quite far and wearing boots all day. Usually I'm sore the day after walking that far, but today I feel nothing! I have no soreness in my legs or feet today despite of squating yesterday, though I have soreness in my shoulders after the dips, but my shoulders did not get any CT yesterday

It seems that CT really boosts recovery after workouts!**

### FF_BC — 2012-04-12T12:27:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-14160>

So I've been away for a while, visiting family and friends. Yesterday (120410) was squat day. When I'm forced to take multiple rest days, I often start with Squats after the break, since the squat is the most important exercise.

**

Squat

4\*105kg

5\*95kg

6\*90kg

Weighted pullups

BW+15kg\*4

BW+10kg\*5

BW\*6**

Best workout in a long long time!!

### FF_BC — 2012-04-13T13:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/intermittent-fasting-and-ct-swedish-style.537/post-14647>

After reading this thread: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?704-Ice-Cold-post-exercise-Blunts-Adaptations](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?704-Ice-Cold-post-exercise-Blunts-Adaptations) I have decided to change my CT timing. It seems my experience that CT boosts recovery are false, it _limits_ recovery somewhat by decreasing the acute inflammation post exercise - hence no muscle soreness...

With that in mind I will now do CT in the evenings on rest days (after breaking the fast), this will be my "real CT" session were I focus on cold adaptation and CT endurance.

On workout days I will do a shorter session of CT in a fasted state in the mornings. This CT session will be a lot harder to endure and will not bring the same cold adaptation effect as CT with food in my stomach, I know. However the morning pre training CT session will increase my energy expenditure therefore possibly letting me be "more fasted"

Also I'm getting CT abstinence... Being at work and travelling not having a access to a tub  But I hope cold showers and sleeping with the window open (0-7 degrees celsius outside) will maintain the small cold adaptation I've been getting so far...

### FF_BC — 2012-04-14T15:19:22+0300

120kg\*4

120kg\*5** Last time I had a really hard time with 120kg, this time it went up like it was nothing!  It felt so easy i didn't bother lowering the weight for the next set.

Did 5 reps pullups 2 set, then 1 set of chins. A nerv in my elbow seems to be a bit sensitive so I switched to chins but that didn't help... So I tried some pushups, did 20 of them then felt the nerv again... Not painful or anything, but you got to listen to your body!

Another thing that happened yesterday: I had a debate with a person on Facebook, this person was telling me that we are adapted by evolution to eat carbohydrates and that you're supposed to eat every third hour to stoke the metabolic fire

### LisaAPB — 2012-03-30T06:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-8640>

Two weeks ago, mine was at a 12 yr low, measured at 80. I did the Leptin Rx last autumn, went to post Leptin Rx and CT late winter. Why aren't all diabetics doing this?

If you were/are diabetic or pre-diabetic, please share.

### primat — 2012-03-30T12:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-8683>

Good going

Well, "most" people think the whole hearthealthy grain dogma is what's keeping them from developing more full blown neolithic disease. We are just a bunch of paranoid freaks wearing batik sarongs colored with organic tea-leaves while we're discussing gouvernment corruption.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-03-30T20:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-8866>

One of today's patients: A1c went from 13.4 to 8.3 in the last 3 months. Honestly, I was shocked. I asked her how she's been doing on the diet, "I cut out all sugar and grain and vegetable oils." She's been eating more nuts, eggs, meats, fish, shrimp, veggies, and occasional fruit.

Eating food your genes want works pretty well apparently.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-30T20:23:07+0300

### OptimalChick — 2012-05-15T00:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-24035>

I have a different problem. My glucose was always between 89-99 not matter what. Morning prior to breakfast, after eating, hour after eating and hours after eating 89-99. Now, it can be 74 and then 119 1 hour after eating a lamb burger (plain without bun of course) and greens. What gives? I been mostly paleo (some cheese and yogurt here and their, leptin reset since Feb, Ct since Ct I)... I don't get it. Mu husband thinks I screwed something up with my system doing these "crazy things" lately.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-05-15T00:49:20+0300

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-05-16T00:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-24411>

When I started the leptin Rx last October my fasting blood glucose was in the low 100s (and 125 lbs overweight). I immediately saw improvement and by the end of the year it was down in the 80s. Then, for a couple months I would see it up in the 90s again at times (I was testing 2-3 times a week). For the past two months it has finally become consistent at 80-84 (I'm still 60lbs overweight).

Interestingly, my husband (who is thin and struggles to keep weight on) started at the blood glucose level and cut sugar and grains when I did, followed an almost identical pattern (despite eating WAY more carbs than I do) and is now consistently in the low 80s too.

### OptimalChick — 2012-06-15T16:38:56+0300

Sometimes I am in the low 80's but the last 2 morning that I have checked is has been 105 and 115!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-15T17:59:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-31347>

### OptimalChick — 2012-06-15T21:43:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-31398>

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-20T16:13:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32394>

Ok... since my FBG, HbA1C, and Insulin is high... I've decided to start testing before and after each meal

today my FBG was 86mg/dl w/in 30mins of waking and right before having bkfst.

1hr after eating it was 84mg/dl

breakfast was 3 eggs, 1 cup of berries and 1/4c cream, and BPC

I figured I'd see what the berries did - I figured I'd spike big time.... but to my surprise I didn't.

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-20T21:06:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32471>

Interesting... for lunch I had zuchinni Lasagana. BG pre-meal was 88, 20min after meal it was 110, 45min it was 117. (got to run out and pick up more strips) so I can test in another 30mins!

Its either the cheese or the tomatoes...

in my previous bg testing dairy didn't spike my bg, nor did the tomatoes....things are changing

Eggs seem to be ok...but dairy/tomatoes spiking BG indicate I SHOULD RUN, not walk to the AI Paleo template.....

### Ray — 2012-06-20T22:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32493>

117 doesn't count as a spike!

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-20T22:11:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32494>

> **Ray;32609 wrote:** 117 doesn't count as a spike!

for me it does... my bg doesn't move much when I eat meat/veggies. 10-20points at most. so to go from 88 to 117, that's almost a 30pt spike for me... 2& 1/2hrs later its still above 100. I'm guessing this is one of the reasons my FBG has shot up, and why my Fasting Insulin, and HbA1C are a bit high...

### Ray — 2012-06-21T01:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32537>

> **Shijin13;32610 wrote:** for me it does.... 2& 1/2hrs later its still above 100. I'm guessing this is one of the reasons my FBG has shot up, and why my Fasting Insulin, and HbA1C are a bit high...

Well, certainly the lower the better (helps keep the protein glycation rate as low as possible). A figure I've seen batted around as the threshold for cell toxicity is around 140, although I don't have any direct knowledge of that. It does sound like your return to baseline after a meal is a little slow.

### Destiny — 2012-06-21T01:31:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32540>

I did not test my sugar before starting paleo/reset and CT. All I know that it never was an issue, just for insurance purposes I had my FBG at 79 two years ago.

When I started low carb last fall, I got a BG monitor. At that time my FBG was anywhere from high 90's to 110's. Once I went on BHRT and started CT, my FBG went down to 80's. I was so happy! Then, after a month it started to climb back into 90's and this week it was between 103-106.

I just ate a few slices of meat, cod liver, a few cherries and berries with heavy cream and CO. My sugar jumped to 150 after 1/2 hour. I am lost on what is going on.

When I get hungry or eat only protein, I feel like everything is pumping in me. Everything is working too hard in me. Any ideas?

### Ray — 2012-06-21T01:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32545>

> **Destiny;32657 wrote:** ...
>
> I just ate a few slices of meat, cod liver, a few cherries and berries with heavy cream and CO. My sugar jumped to 150 after 1/2 hour. I am lost on what is going on.
>
> ... Any ideas?

A similar thing happened to me too having been low carb for many months and then splurging on some starchy carbs (except I hit 170). Take a look here:

[http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2007/10/physiological-insulin-resistance.html](http://http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2007/10/physiological-insulin-resistance.html)

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T02:04:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32547>

Nature doesn't suddenly splurge on starchy carbs.... they come about slowly and the body has time to reverse the insulin resistance. But I'm not sure that the induced insulin resistance is something that should happen if you're optimal even in the winter.

### Destiny — 2012-06-21T02:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32549>

> **Ray;32663 wrote:** A similar thing happened to me too having been low carb for many months and then splurging on some starchy carbs (except I hit 170). Take a look here:
>
>
>
> [http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2007/10/physiological-insulin-resistance.html](http://http://high-fat-nutrition.blogspot.com/2007/10/physiological-insulin-resistance.html)

Thanks, Ray! My HbA1c was 5.8 in April, so nowhere close to 4.4 as in the blog. I was concentrated on fasting values and I really have to look into the ones after meals as well.

### Destiny — 2012-06-21T02:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32550>

> **Souldanzer;32665 wrote:** Nature doesn't suddenly splurge on starchy carbs.... they come about slowly and the body has time to reverse the insulin resistance. But I'm not sure that the induced insulin resistance is something that should happen if you're optimal even in the winter.

My diet have always had a few carbs. Otherwise, I feel very sluggish and as I mentioned I feel like my cortisol is pumping in overdrive. Also my heart rate goes from 85 to over 95. It feels like my gut and heart is very stressed. It is hard to explain but I feel the physiological impact.

### Ray — 2012-06-21T02:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32552>

> **Souldanzer;32665 wrote:** Nature doesn't suddenly splurge on starchy carbs....

??? Without manipulation, the fruiting of most plants is tied to seasonal changes, with each species maturing at the same point in the yearly cycle. For consumers of each plant crop, it would either be feast or famine. In my case, even if I rationalize real hard, I can't splice what I did into the paleo model. (unless rice crackers, cheese, and beer is now paleo. I'll have to go check on MDA since he is now running a series on "is it paleo")

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T02:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32555>

not true

leafies come first, then early season watery veggies/fruits, then sweet fruit, then starchy carbs..... at least that's what my garden does every year

not getting your second part?

### Ray — 2012-06-21T02:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-32562>

> **Souldanzer;32673 wrote:** ...
>
> leafies come first, then early season watery veggies/fruits, then sweet fruit, then starchy carbs..... at least that's what my garden does every year
>
> ...

we are saying the same thing with regard to the seasonality of crops. It is feast or famine for each crop in turn. The second part is a joke (sort of). The definition of paleo is a moving target. Mark at "Marks Daily Apple" is running a blog series where he tells you whether a particular food is paleo or not; however, FOR SURE cheese, crackers and beer is not paleo, and for the record, I shouldn't have done it.

As far as whether physiological insulin resistance is a natural response of a well adapted human on a ketogenic paleo diet, nobody really knows yet. When some of you all get optimum, you can run the experiment. On the other hand there is a strong argument that alternate biochemical pathways are activated in an individual in ketosis, and it is a good possibility.

FBG 89, Post meal at 30min 89, 1hr 100. I'll be checking in 30min to see where I'm at.

### Kathy1967 — 2016-06-01T17:28:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-195809>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=31347)
>
> Checked my BG this AM after doing 28 degrees for 22 minutes.......62.

Hi Jack - I know this post is old but hopefully you'll see this question of mine! I've been tracking my blood glucose and ketones (got a glucometer that a very passionate lady named Stephanie "Keto" Person recommends in her numerous youtube videos) and my glucose is usually low-ish - 60's to mid 80's. Sometimes it goes into the 90's and it even went up to 105 which I THINK coincides with when I have a lot of protein - say 150 to 200 grams daily. I've been trying to keep the protein to around 60 grams daily to keep my sugar down but I wonder if only 60 grams is too low. My blood ketones are usually between 2.0 and 4.0 mmol/L which I believe means I'm in ketosis. Interesting that I've noticed if I do a 30 minute cold bath my blood glucose is lower than what I would expect even if I've had tons of protein. By the way, I keep my net carbs to probably around 15 grams and its from fibrous veggies like broccoli or brussel sprouts usually. My question is - do I have to worry if my blood sugar goes over 80 sometimes? I don't want to limit my protein - currently the protein sources are eggs from my chickens, shrimp, oysters, and probably too much grass fed beef! Also - if I understand things I've read in your forum it seems that in the winter blood glucose can be higher because it acts like an anti-freeze. Thanks for all of your freely given advice Jack!!!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-01T19:02:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blood-glucose.538/post-195815>

The glucose response can changed by the sun skin and retina signal......that is my N=1. In winter it always raises it......it used in autumn in nashville but it does not in New Orleans......I've learned this over the last 3 years.

### nate.reik@yahoo.com — 2012-08-06T17:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-mercola-notices-cooling-as-well.539/post-43221>

> **Raiken3712;43496 wrote:** [http://products.mercola.com/vision-program/?e\_cid=20120806\_DNL\_YRP\_3&source=nl](http://http://products.mercola.com/vision-program/?e_cid=20120806_DNL_YRP_3&source=nl)
>
>
>
> This one ...I would love to work.....really skeptical on this but its sort of hard to test without a large time commitment without wearing glasses. Natural vision correction...if only. I saw this somewhere else before Dr. Mercola had it on his website.

It can and does happen. Look into Vision Therapy for children w/ binocular vision problems. (And adults!) Part of the initial process is to get your eyes to essentially work better individually, before they can be trained to work better together. I myself have never needed glasses, but have binocular vision problems, double vision from my brain not merging images completely because my eyes are pointed slightly differently. I'm in vision therapy now (@ age 27) and have seen an improvement in the ability for the individual eye to focus. Exercises w/ lenses, etc. Sue Barry has written a book about her experiences in vision therapy, and gaining stereo vision at nearly age 50.

My point is that you can improve how the 2 eyes work together, and in my experience improvement of each eyes' sight has also been noted.

Whether the Bates method works like they say I have no exeperience in, but the fact that your sight changes throughout the day is true, and eye exercises/work/etc has improved my vision.

# Thread: HELP: Total Cholesterol Help (and fatty liver/thyroid?)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lennyaus — 2012-03-30T11:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8676>

I'll give some background:

Overweight, trying leptin rest, cold baths on an almost daily basis for 20 minutes however I am in a rut with life. Work and play etc. Fiancee not liking this suggested anti-depressants (She is on them). I say no, but reached a compromise that I would see the GP. Well, we know how this is going to turn out right?

I spoke to my GP. He is very friendly and a great communicator. He doesn't feel that I need medication for my moods (yet) and likes my suggestion of a blood test as maybe something is out of whack. So I get a fasted one taken.

I get it back and my total cholesterol is high. According to the sheet I asked for it is : 6.8 mmol/L. GP says that it is close for prescribing medication for. Look, I am never going to go on that nonsense. However, I have a fiancÃ©e who is now concerned about my health and I can't change her ways because I am still overweight and depressed.

**Questions:**

-   Does anyone know if Total Cholesterol lowers with adherence to a ketogenic diet?
-   Do I stand my ground?
    Do I perhaps limit fat/meat intake a bit to keep the peace? For example, replace meat with more fish?

I looked at the low-cholesterol diet that I was given and want to spew! It sucks. I got my blood pressure measured and it was 120/70. So that's cool.

Also with the test two other things came up. Liver and Thyroid. Apparently my thyroid is on the over-active side? Well slightly. Liver reading is not good but that was expected. Perhaps Liver is why it is hard for me to lose weight? I did abuse alcohol for a period of years.

Here is the list of "chemistry bloods" from the blood test I got:

NAME UNITS RANGE:

S SODIUM:141 mmol/L (135-145)

S POTASSIUM: 4.6 mmol/L (3.5-5.5)

S CHLORIDE:103 mmol/L (95-110)

S BICARB: 25 mmol/L (20-32)

S UREA: 5.9 mmol/L (3.0-7.5)

S CREAT: 92 umol/L (60-110)

S eGFR: 84

ANION GAP: 18 mmol/L (10-20)

T-BILLI: 29 H (I assume H means high?) umol/L (4-20)

S ALP: 81 U/L (35-110)

S GGT: 24 U/L (5-50)

S ALT: 80 H (This is liver I think) U/L (5-40)

S AST: 24 U/L (10-40)

T PROTEIN: 74 g/l (66-83)

S ALBUMIN: 49 g/l (39-50)

S GLOBULIN: 25 g/l (23-39)

S CHOLESTEROL: 6.8 H mmol/L (3.5-5.5)

S TRIG: 1.5 mmol/L (0.5-2.0)

S HDL-CHOL: 1.12 mmol/L (>1.00)

S LDL-CHOL: 5.0 H mmol/L (>3.5)

S LDLC/HDLC: 4.5 H (>4.0)

S Chol/HDLC: 6.1 H (>4.5)

S IRON: 14 umol/L (5-30)

S TRF: 2.7 g/l (2.0-3.2)

S TRF.SAT.: 21 % (10-45)

S FERRITIN: 241 ng/ml (30-500)

GLU (FAST): 5.1 mmol/L (3.6-6.0)

I thought the doc asked for testosterone too, but I don't see it on this list.

Basically if anyone who has any understanding of the readings above could provide me some advice as to what is good/bad/ugly and how to combat it, it would be greatly appreciated.

I do not eat 100% optimal, I will say that now. That is defined by Dr.Kruses blogs. I am weak and have sugar free energy drinks in the morning. The most ridiculous thing about this is I know it is bad. I only drink them at work though. Coping habit? (I know this sounds pathetic, but no avoiding the facts). I eat a heavy meat diet with occasional white rice. Actually if someone who sounds like they know what they are talking about tells me sugar free energy drinks are the nutritional hybrid of Hitler/Kony it would be welcome.

Anyway sorry, this became a bit of a ramble/rant. I basically got home from the doctors, google'd some of the stuff listed above to try and decipher and started writing this post. Any input would be greatly appreciated. I've told my fiancee I'll cut down on the meat a bit. I don't want too, but maybe my quantities are too much. Thanks for reading.

### Glamorama — 2012-03-30T11:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8680>

My total cholestrol was 7.2 last month. Thats after 9 months of Paleo eating. Doc wasn't worried, neither am I. My ratio between LDL and HDL is very good, indicating that the bad cholestrol is low why the good is high. You can never win if your doc only looks at the total, a Paleo diet will always give a high total reading, but that number is not relevant. Whats relevant is the ratio and if your LDLs are fluffy or dense.

I had liver-issues at my last blood-test. Suspected fatty liver. Def a reason behind me not burning off fat as fast as I should be. I'm seeing a naturopath an taking some herbal supplements to deal with that, my liver-reading was far better a month in that initially. I suggest you read some of the paleo bibles like Robb Wolff's books (he covers cholestrol in depth) and start taking what the doc says with a massive grain of salt. A naturopath might be more helpful for you.

And quit the energy drinks. They sure as hell are not helping your liver.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-30T19:49:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8856>

I'd suggest reading this post by Griff on MDA re: cholesterol. An awesome read and super informative. This helped me tremendously when I freaked about my high cholesterol numbers. [http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread4723.html](http://http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread4723.html)

Like Glam said above, it's about the ratio - not the individual #'s.

Oh - and definitely ditch the energy drinks. There's no room for that junk on a path to optimal.

### PaleoSue — 2012-03-30T21:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8901>

I've suffered from bad depression most of my life, and I am amazed at the change since starting the leptin reset. I had already done a lot of natural emotional healing (pm me if you want more info) but the mood change since following the diet has been outstanding.

I'm happy. I mean truly incredibly happy. I hope things stay like this!

I won't lie; the first 2 weeks on a pure leptin reset plan were tough. Lots of withdrawal and cravings. But now it is astoundIng. And getting sugar free drinks out was important.

### Chrisgraff — 2012-03-30T21:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-total-cholesterol-help-and-fatty-liver-thyroid.540/post-8924>

Your ALT is the same as mine. That's an Indication of fatty liver. I've been taking milk thistle for it; from what I've read it will take 3-4 months to heal.

I'd fix your sleep first. Bed by 10...no TV/computer at night. REM sleep is essential. If you're not dreamIng, you're not healing!

# Thread: Let's talk sex drive

**Posts:** 6

### BJK77 — 2012-03-30T14:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-sex-drive.542/post-8700>

I've seen so many posts where people excitedly reveal they are getting their sex drive back and I know Dr K says this will happen both with the leptin rx and with CT, so for those that have been lucky enough to have a comeback:

\- How long did it take you w/ diet and/or CT for your drive to return?

\- How long has it been since you lost your drive?

\- Have you done any hormone testing? Taken BHRT to help?

This is one of the main things I'm hoping to recover, but I've been on the reset for 9 weeks now and CTing for 4 and only seeing very little hints of my drive returning - nothing substantial at all. I do know my hormones are whacked though,

### TheKid — 2012-03-30T15:28:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-sex-drive.542/post-8706>

> **BJK77;8750 wrote:** I've seen so many posts where people excitedly reveal they are getting their sex drive back and I know Dr K says this will happen both with the leptin rx and with CT, so for those that have been lucky enough to have a comeback:

>

I am 8 weeks on the Leptin Rx and 4 weeks doing CT. I have had intermittent improvement with sex drive. But it has not been consistent. It continues to come and go seemingly randomly and often it does not stick around for long. I have not had my hormones tested, and I guess that is the next step.

My question would be, what constitutes "whacked" hormones and why? Are there combinations of test results that lead to certain problems?

### Evo Mama — 2012-03-30T16:15:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-sex-drive.542/post-8741>

\- How long did it take you w/ diet and/or CT for your drive to return? 2 weeks or so

\- How long has it been since you lost your drive? Since pregnancy with my son, who is now 2 years old. To clarify, I never had no sex drive, but I didn't have the drive to have sex like I do now, KWIM?

\- Have you done any hormone testing? Taken BHRT to help? No.

I've been on the reset and CT for over 3 weeks, and DH and I went from lucky to get intimate 1x/week to every other night, and sometimes more often.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-03-30T16:19:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-sex-drive.542/post-8743>

For me.....this this happened over 3-5 months.....and the result for the last 5 year is stunning.

### Shijin13 — 2012-03-30T17:23:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-sex-drive.542/post-8783>

Sex drive returned about 3-4wks into LR; CT amplified it - then my BHRT program came in (taking BHRT to address migraines, PCOS and Low P) - and following the program I decreased my Pregnenolone and 7Keto Dhea supplementation, even though I knew I should be taking the levels I was taking, and it took a nose dive...

7 weeks into CT... and changes in my BHRT I'm starting to see an upswing - though no where near what it was when I first got it back... Think it might have to do w/all the E2 I'm dumping out of the fat cells...what w/my PCOS and all.

I anticipate that the combo will address everything appropriately and things will go back to following my cycle - and it wont' be hard to get in the mood...

# Thread: Keto paleo and hypothyroid/adrenal issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pardus — 2012-03-30T20:31:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-8877>

Hi all,

I know there is a lot of dogma going around the paleosphere about keto diets causing hypothyroid and adrenal fatigue issues (cold hands and feet, bloating, constipation, brain fog, etc) to which the solution is always to add more "safe starches".

Unfortunately, my hands are still cold, only warming up for about an hour after I eat a meal. Been doing CT cold showers and cold walks for over a month now.

What is your view on the situation of vlc/keto causing hypothyroid and adrenal fatigue?

Did anyone with cold hands/hypothyroid, leaky gut, adrenal fatigue, etc notice any improvement with CT? If so, what was your experience and how long did it take?

### CheshireCat57 — 2012-03-31T16:53:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9220>

Hi Pardus, I qualify with the problems but have not been doing things long enough to become Cold Adapted. I don't have cold hands normally though so cannot offer anything for you on that. I do know that jack says all over his blog and in this forum that you should not be eating carbs in winter. I don't know when the cut over is but in the Northern hemisphere we are not in the season for harvesting (March 2012) many vegetables so I would think that carbs are still inappropriate.

One thread I read in the forum talked about not covering up when you stop the CT as the covers (shirts, Pj's etc) seem to trap the cold in. I did this last night and it worked for me...and the wife enjoyed it too  Jack speaks about keeping hands and feet warm during CT...

Hope this helps.

### Inger — 2012-03-31T16:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9221>

Do you get organs and fishheads to eat? Plain meat only will not do it longterm.

I believe fishheads espesially are great for healing. Try fishbroth or eat whole small fish with head and all. I occasionally make a smoothie with mackerelheads and water and drink it. Tastes awful but makes me feel great... I know it sounds crazy. Fishbroth with heads from wild fish are also real good.

### Baba — 2012-03-31T17:05:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9225>

My adrenals have recovered on a low-carb diet. I took Hydrocortisone for two years (to give them a break) and have recently weaned. The fact that I don't need antihistamines for hayfever (yet) this season is a sign that my adrenals are doing their job again. Although I weaned off HC before starting CT, I believe it helps a lot.

My thyroid doesn't show any signs of recovering, but I have Hashimoto's which may be a special situation.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-03-31T20:46:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9317>

Hi Friends,

I was really worried about this too. I have been doing the SCD/GAPS/modified paleo diet for over six years. I found I did better on a low carbohydrate version of the diet though I have never tried to be ketogenic. I have also played around with seasonal eating. I have cured myself of asthma, allergies, chronic sinus infections, yeast infections, osteoarthritis and epilepsy.

BUT I do have a low body temperature. I measure my morning temperature before rising and have done so for over 35 cycles. My charts would say I am estrogen dominant and I have a standard infertility pattern. (I am 46.) My charts indicate that I have an adrenal problem not a thyroid problem due to my extreme daily fluctuations in my morning temperatures.

There are two main views out there about low body temperature. One says its a sign of endocrine dysfunction. This appears to be the dominant view in the paleo community at this time with all the talk about safe starches. The other view would say, I am in calorie restriction, and that lower body temperature is great because it will increase my longevity.

I have been trying to figure out which of these two opinions might be happening for me. I have been experimenting with over-eating, safe starches, adrenal extracts, herbals, etc from about 2009. None of these experiments have increased my body temperature for any length of time. I was starting to think that maybe there was nothing wrong with a low body temperature since I appear healthy in other ways.

It was late last year that I learned about Dr Krase's work but I really didn't understand his earlier posts. It has been just recently that I have come back to his website to find the cold thermogenesis massives. Wow, what can I say. His material blew my mind. If he is right, and he has done six years of clinic work, it doesn't matter if my adrenals are burnt-out or my thyroid is under-functioning. Cold thermogenesis, a seasonal ketogenic diet and balancing circadian cycles will open a pathway in the brain that will overrun any glandular endocrine dysfunction.

By the way, I asked Dr Kruse about his opinion on low body temperature. He thinks it's better but he didn't give a reason. Maybe he will expand on the reason why here.

I have gone moderately ketogenic. I am turning off the lights at sunset and I am in the first stages of cold thermogensis. I will keep you posted as to how it goes and what it does to my system. I like the term, biohack!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-03-31T20:54:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9322>

> **Inger;9283 wrote:** Do you get organs and fishheads to eat? Plain meat only will not do it longterm.
>
> I believe fishheads espesially are great for healing. Try fishbroth or eat whole small fish with head and all. I occasionally make a smoothie with mackerelheads and water and drink it. Tastes awful but makes me feel great... I know it sounds crazy. Fishbroth with heads from wild fish are also real good.

Hi Inger,

Fish broths are very healing. The adrenal gland in fish is in the head, so you are eating fish adrenals. Eating the area behind the eyes is extremely high in vitamin C. Fish eggs are a superfood. Eating raw fish is very nourishing. I am impressed that you can eat the whole fish in a smoothie. You are a real trooper!

### Claudia — 2012-03-31T21:41:08+0300

What do you mean? Not covering up after CT? Do you mean when I come out of my cold bath, I should not get comfy and put on my PJs and robe? I am cold for about 3 hours and I do like to warm up by putting on clothes.

I have a question about ketosis. Has anyone here found eating hard cheese takes you out of Ketosis? I have never used Ketostix before last week. I started doing so and I find the day after I eat hard cheese, with everything else being the same, I will move from "moderate" ketosis to "trace" or even "negative". Any ideas?

### hazyjane — 2012-04-01T23:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-9962>

I've def. noticed a difference in body temps (I have Hashimoto's). I'm doing other things besides just leptin Rx/CT, though. I'm on an immune modulation protocol and I'm also on pregenolone and adrenal herbs for low cortisol and high-dose sublingual methylcobalamin (nearly everyone with autoimmune disease is too low in B12 and the US lab range is way too low).

### Pardus — 2012-04-02T05:56:12+0300

### hazyjane — 2012-04-02T06:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-10179>

### PaleoSue — 2012-04-03T15:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-10738>

I'm currently weaning off of it and seeing what happens. I plan to repeat a salivary cortisol then. I'm doing very well and don't think I need it any more.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-03T15:54:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/keto-paleo-and-hypothyroid-adrenal-issues.544/post-10746>

back in august I had a backward circadian cycle - Cortisol was HIGH At night, and LOW in the morning - influenced by years of shift work, and stress.

I continued w/the LR - eating BAB w/in first 30mins of waking, lunch 4-5hrs later, dinner 4-5hrs later, b/w3hr prior to bed. I took the following: Maca, Ltheanine, Rodihola, high dose VitC, high dose B-complex, and Apex Energetics Adaptocrine. I also stopped wearing sunglasses during the day, and made sure I got plenty of sun on my eyes throughout the day, kept lights low or off at night... saw a reversal to normal 6mths later.

When I started CT - I discovered quite quickly night CTing was not for me... I usually CT in the AM... but now 8wks into CTing I can CT in the afternoon and not impact sleep...

CTing w/the LR, following the circadian cycles, and seasonal eating will right the adrenals - and turn back on the HPA axis - as well as the Pituary -Thyroid axis... but you have to be patient...

# Thread: Hack my auto-immunity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-auto-immunity.545/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pardus — 2012-03-30T20:34:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-auto-immunity.545/post-8880>

Hi all,

Being very suspicious of this safe starch cure-all solution, I've stuck with strict keto paleo (autoimmune version) for about 18 months now. As on now, I've been doing CT every night as daily cold walks and cold showers for about a month. However, as of yet I have noticed no improvement in my seborrheic dermatitis, brain fog, chronic bloating, dry skin and eczema (had these conditions since birth). I also have allergies to mould, dust, pollen, etc. My hands have always been very cold, and this has either remained the same or gotten worse on keto paleo. I suspect I am hypothyroid, but official testing will say for sure. I am still very tired in the morning, yet have trouble falling asleep in the evening, and also get worried and stressed easily, which leads me to think I have adrenal fatigue.

I've read the leptin rx and leaky gut rx and have been following the protocols for about 3 months, no noticeable results so far. I'm 21 years old, 5'8" and about 158lbs. I dont think I'm overweight by a lot but chronic bloating + water retention makes my belly stick out.

Current supplements:

iodine

selenium

magnesium

zinc

MSM

coQ10

PQQ

K2 MK-4

D3

B complex

Quercetin

Curcumin

Betaine HCL

Diet:

Fish

Shellfish

Grassfed beef + lamb

green vegetables

grassfed beef liver

No eggs, dairy, nuts, grains, legumes of any kind (no green beans or peas either), no fruits, no starches.

Experimented with real kefir and probiotics with no results.

2 meals per day. Breakfast at 6:00am, Dinner around 4:00-5:00 pm.

Lifestyle:

HIT 15 mins once per week, in the afternoon (Body By Science, Doug McGuff)

CT during day: cold walk

CT at night, 2 hrs before bed: cold showers

Blueblocking glasses go on at sunset. In bed by 9:30pm, asleep by around 10:00pm, and up by 6:00am

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-30T21:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-auto-immunity.545/post-8914>

It sounds like there maybe a thyroid issue but the biggest thing I believe is the adrenal issues. You probably should get the full thyroid panel of tests including the rT3 as well as a salivary cortisol test. It may also be some hormonal imbalances that are not showing up without hormonal labs.

Another thought is a shellfish allergy? It may be way off base but it just came to me so I'm throwing it out there.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: persistent numb toes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T20:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8882>

OK. For the second time after CT i've had numb toes. I saw some other folks mention it and I searched the blogs. Dr. K. says something about the n3/n6 balance again and cell signaling for the nervous system. I believe it is a real phenomenon that we should start to iron out, i..e everyone share their experience with it so we can get a common thread and then let's understand the mechanism if possible. I think it will help us all, especially people that join in late and experience it, if we put our heads together and build our collective knowledge.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T20:43:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8887>

specifically my 2nd and 3rd toes on my right foot (if the big toe is the first toe). It'd be fun to get an acupuncturist or reflexologist to pontificate on it... I'm now going on 1.5 hours after getting out of the bath and they are starting to wake up. definitely a nerve thing as they feel asleep, possibly not getting any blood?

### Patty Cakes — 2012-03-30T21:03:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8895>

Are the toes white? Maybe it's Reynauds syndrome (an autoimmune disease).

Yesterday I had 3 toes on each foot turn white AFTER I got out of the bath. I don't even put my feet in the water and I've been CTing for six weeks! I took the dog for a walk yesterday right after the bath and it took 35 min for the numbness to go away.

In my case it's Reynauds. I know Dr. K had mentioned in the comments of one of the CT blogs that it is caused by too high 06s. I just had mine tested and I'm at 2.96:1. So I'm not really sure what is going on. I hope someone has some insights and look forward to the responses. I don't want to just say "Oh, I have this infliction". I want to not have this (Reynauds) at all.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-03-30T21:19:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8905>

After pushing the immersions from 20 minutes to 35 minutes, i get numbness intermittently in either my right small toe or my left small toe, and/or my small pinky finger. It isn't always my right or left side - however, if the toe and the finger go numb, it is always on the same side. example, my left pink and my left toe. my right pinky, my right toe. Sometimes it is just the toe too.

It usually goes away in 30 minutes, and they aren't turning white. I'm assuming it is just a side-effect of the vasoconstriction process.

### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T21:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8915>

Just to capture some more variables... getting my torso into my tub ends up being like holding a yoga pose for 30 minutes. I can't rule out some nerve impingement. My hips get sore and are complaining big time but there is not much room to shift around.

### BJK77 — 2012-03-30T22:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8944>

I have this happen as well - sometimes toes on both feet, but usually more isolated to my left side. At its worst, the numbness has lasted for over an hour. I also keep my feet out of the water and it doesn't happen until 20 minutes or so after I get out of the tub. I have yet to test my O6/O3 so not sure if that's the culprit in my case or not. I did develop a bit of Raynaud's this past winter for the first time, so I assume that's what it is.

### meyoolia@gmail.com — 2012-03-30T22:31:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8947>

ditto here, too, re numbness and not having feet in the water. Mostly on the "inside" half (if you cut it in half vertically, it's on the half that'd touch the other big toe  ) of my left big toe, and more recently similarly on the right... with a little tingliness on the 2nd left toe as well. It would take so long to clear I didn't even notice how long it took. I haven't had my o6/o3's checked either, but didn't develop contact cold urticaria when icepacking or tubbing. Weirdness!

### hellojtm — 2012-03-30T22:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-8950>

Could be Raynaud's for me too. Interesting, just reading the mayo clinic web page about it.

### Susan M. — 2012-03-31T01:26:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9019>

Today was my 15th 40 minute soak, and I am able to keep my feet in the water now for the entire time (at least today I could), and as usual, I have two of the smaller toes on my right foot go numb after I have been out of the tub for about ten minutes. I do wear neoprene booties. I try to keep my socks and shoes off until they are mostly back to normal. My heels are always much colder than my toes, but they don't seem to go numb. The biggest challenge I have with the toes being numb, is that it messes with my balance for awhile. Otherwise, things get back to normal fairly soon. I wonder if my toes are going to go numb every time regardless of how often, or who cold, I go?

### janagram — 2012-03-31T03:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9071>

How common is reynaud's?

theres 3 of you on this thread who think you have it.....you're scaring me...

### MonteD — 2012-03-31T07:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9120>

> **janagram;9123 wrote:** How common is reynaud's?
>
> theres 3 of you on this thread who think you have it.....you're scaring me...

Ditto.....

### KetoWarrior — 2012-03-31T18:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-9269>

From [Wikipedia](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raynaud's_phenomenon):

> People in their thirties are most likely to develop Raynaud's, and the phenomenon is more common in women than in men, with women three times more likely to be diagnosed than men. The Framingham Study showed that Raynaud phenomenon affects 5% of the population.

I got it a lot in my hands last winter. None this winter. I got in two toes during my first CT bath but not since and have experienced an overall improvement in my circulation to my hands and feet with CT just using skin color as an indicator.

### angieonthegogo — 2012-04-02T01:19:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10018>

I had toe numbness for the 1st time last night but it didn't start until after I was out of the bath for 15 mins or so. I CTd for an hour but I also was contorting myself for quite some time in order to stay as submerged as possible. I'm wondering if that was it or I just pushed it in the water too cold for too long and since the last time I ate.

### Gonzo — 2012-04-02T02:35:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10052>

I have Raynaud's. I soak in a tub at 50 degrees, but only put hands and feet in for about 15 minutes currently, during a 40-45 minute body soak. Fingers and toes usually do blanch white and go numb after I get out of the tub, just like typical Raynaud's episodes. My omega-6mega-3 just came back at 1.9:1, so I am not sure why the Raynaud's is still an issue. I plan to increase omega-6 foods just a bit to try to get to 4:1 or so, but don't know if it will worsen the Raynaud's. Of note, as I increase the time my hands and feet are in the water, it is less uncomfortable each time, and now I am comfortable within just 2-3 minutes. It is after the soak that I get numbness. I am hoping it will gradually improve with the CT.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-02T03:08:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10074>

My heels and toes go a little numb once out of the tub and I chaulk it up to the body trying to warm everything up and the feet are last on the list. I have not been concerned at all with my feet getting this way. I leave them like this for a bit and then put them in luke warm water before leaving for work if they are still pretty bad where it might affect my walk.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-04-02T16:30:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/persistent-numb-toes.546/post-10284>

If I take long cold showers, I get nothing. But if I do an immersion, I tend to have a random toe, or finger go numb. The affected digit does not turn white - but starts getting numb about 15-30 minutes after I get out of the tub and am completely dry. Also, I do not submerge my hands or feet when soaking - i'm chalking this up to vasoconstriction that hopefully goes away----numb fingers crossed.

# Thread: Low Thyroid, Gall Bladder Removed and Roux en Y due to botched surgery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jocelyn7777 — 2012-03-30T22:11:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-8935>

Dr. Kruse,

I want to start the Leptin Rx because my doctor already tested my Leptin and said I'm leptin resistant. Now, the only problem is that ever since I had my gallbladder removed and had to have repair surgery I'm hungry 24/7.

At first the doctors all told me that it was just heartburn, but I've done rounds of Prilosec and Zegerid. During the two weeks of medication, the hunger subsides a tiny bit. As soon as the round is through, I'm back to starving all the time.

I'm concerned that even doing the Leptin Rx, even eating something like 75 grams of protein, I'm scared I'll feel hungry just a few hours later.

I feel like I could constantly be a binge eater, but without the purging. It has been very disturbing to someone that was never food dependant. It was just something that you had to do, not something that I loved to do.

Now, I don't love it, but I crave food all the time. Even if I eat a huge steak for dinner like 12 oz with a large salad, I can be hungry about 15 min later.

Will doing the leptin reset fix this? And, if it works, will I be starving the first few weeks?

Thanks,

Jocelyn

### janagram — 2012-03-31T04:19:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-9081>

is it hunger... or the need for betaine hcl and enzymes (due tp no gallbladder)?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-03-31T05:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-9094>

I'd re-read the Leptin Rx protocol and the FAQ post again. It's not just protein (though you need that 50g + for the BAB) but also fat you need. The fat is definitely part of what satiates the hunger. Protein alone won't do it. Most of us don't find that we're starving at all, but the cravings remain and can be heightened in that first few weeks, especially if you've been eating carbs. You'll need to concentrate on getting those good fats in (the tallow, ghee and coconut oil of course) in good amounts.

### jocelyn7777 — 2012-04-01T02:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-9545>

I forgot to mention the Roux En Y was not for bariatric surgery. It was connected to a hepaticojejunostomy.

I wish I had issues with needing bile salts, but if anything mine overproduces bile (and sends me rushing to the bathroom).

I'll re-read the Leptin Rx, but I've been adding a heaping tablespoon of coconut oil to the eggs that I cook in the morning. Do you think I need to add more fat than that?

Maybe I need to add some of the supplements, like magnesium, etc?

It isn't carbs that I'm craving... It is any kind of food. I could eat bacon, meat, salad, anything... I could eat carbs because they are there, but the hunger isn't for carbs specifically.

I've wondered if maybe the liver bile is backing up into my stomach or something and causing me to feel hungry.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-01T16:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-9751>

Yes, you need more fat than that, most likeyl. CO, butter, tallow, ,lard, etc. I'd strongly recommended trying betaine HCl (and staying FAR away from anything that aims to reduce your stomach acid, as you almost certainly have too little). If you can take a capsule and not feel stomach discomfort or burns or runs, etc., you need it. I need a LOT. I maxed out at one point and now need a lot again after some time off it.

Anyway, digestive enzymes are also a good path to go down.

But yes, the type of hunger you're talking about, many people, including me, have had success in killing. I started the LR with 50g ofprotein, and it helped a lot with cravings, etc., but I still felt hungry after eating it.. I went to 65g and still felt every morning like I could eat the whole thing over again, but didn't. After a month at that level, though (and that was the level for me, your mileage may vary), I started feeling satisfied after breakfast. After another month, I couldn't eat lunch anymore, and haven't needed lunch more than once in nearly fivev months.

It works. Somehow it tells the body it is being nourished, and \*will continue to be\* nourished, and shuts off the desperate hunger from a body that fears malnourishment.

### edance83@yahoo.com — 2012-04-01T16:11:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-9755>

> **jocelyn7777;9607 wrote:** I forgot to mention the Roux En Y was not for bariatric surgery. It was connected to a hepaticojejunostomy.
>
>
>
> I wish I had issues with needing bile salts, but if anything mine overproduces bile (and sends me rushing to the bathroom).
>
>
>
> I'll re-read the Leptin Rx, but I've been adding a heaping tablespoon of coconut oil to the eggs that I cook in the morning. Do you think I need to add more fat than that?
>
>
>
> Maybe I need to add some of the supplements, like magnesium, etc?
>
>
>
> It isn't carbs that I'm craving... It is any kind of food. I could eat bacon, meat, salad, anything... I could eat carbs because they are there, but the hunger isn't for carbs specifically.
>
>
>
> I've wondered if maybe the liver bile is backing up into my stomach or something and causing me to feel hungry.

No, you def need digestive enzymes! When you have your gallbladder removed you can no longer digest fats. That is why you are "running to the bathroom". When you can't digest fats they are just shooting through your system without being broken down and assimilated. Also heartburn is a sign of low stomach acid.. if you actually have heartburn. I think you should get digestive enzymes.. making sure it has the stuff that digests fat.. should be easy. After a couple days I bet you stop feeling so hungry. I think you're feeling so hungry because you are unable to digest so much of the food you are eating you are literally starving yourself even though you are eating.

### jocelyn7777 — 2012-04-01T23:13:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-9947>

Thanks everyone... Any specific suggestions on the brands or amounts. I'm one of those that doesn't do so well when faced with too many options. i stand around and look dumb trying to figure out what to buy!

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-02T02:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-10056>

Me, too. I use Digest Gold for enzymes & Nutrivene or Twin Labs for HCl. I'd try Nutrivene first, b/c their caps are smaller, so you can start low and work up. But when you're at the top, it's helpful not to have to take so many, so I like Twin Labs then.

With HCl, my understanding is that you want to take it at the beginning, or partly through, each meal. Start with one before a meal. If no discomfort, do one the next meal. Then after a few days, two at one meal, then two at the other after a few days. Then three. Etc. The protocol I saw said to stop at three, but I don't know if that's universally recommended. At whatever point you start feeling discomfort (it varies for everyone; for me it was burning runs & nausea that showed up 4-5hrs after meals), start backing down slowly, continuing to back down each time you get to your personal max again. (It's not nearly so bad as I'm describing it, lol.)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-04-02T07:54:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-10221>

I think you should probably just give it a go. You started by saying, "**I want to start the Leptin Rx** because my doctor already tested my Leptin and said I'm leptin resistant. Now, the only problem is that ever since I had my gallbladder removed and had to have repair surgery I'm hungry 24/7." So give it a try and see how you fare. My guess is you'll be great!

### Casey — 2012-04-02T08:14:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-thyroid-gall-bladder-removed-and-roux-en-y-due-to-botched-surgery.547/post-10224>

Adding coconut oil to Green tea is a way to up your fat intake.

I would say to make sure you use gelatanized (cooked) maca rather than raw. It's never traditionally consumed raw. Whole World Botanicals and Maca Magic are both good brands.

Maca is made out of a combination of alkaloids that stimulate certain parts of the brain (such as the hypophysis and the hypothalamus) to produce different kinds of hormones (testosterone) that improve the sex drive and the libido.

# Thread: Cook Book - post recipe experiences here
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cook-book-post-recipe-experiences-here.552/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-31T03:06:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cook-book-post-recipe-experiences-here.552/post-9050>

Just thought it might me good to have a thread where people post their results/questions/culinary disasters trying to cook from the Optimal Book. Share your knowledge!

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-03-31T03:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cook-book-post-recipe-experiences-here.552/post-9054>

The Chokin On Shrimp

Made it tonight as it was a Friday during Lent - yay yummy meatless meal.

I used frozen shrimp that had all the work already done for me - was feeling lazy.

The recipe has two cans of artichokes listed, but I figured that was a typo.

As luck would have it I had shrimp stock in the freezer and used it instead of chicken stock (no meat on Friday...)

The hubby absolutely refuses anything with coconut, so I made the sauce with olive oil/butter (yes, I'm of French & Italian stock)

I served it with braised Lacinato kale on the side. The non paleo fam members ate some Jasmine rice with theirs.

I added sea salt and pepper to my sauce.

It was very, very tasty and I will definitely make it again, taking the time with fresh shrimp - so much better flavor. And I would be inclined to saute them vice boil them. Again more work, but that will yield even more flavor.

The dh ate it and when I asked what adjustment he would make... he said he would top it all with crumbled bacon or a toasted nut to add some crunch.

Definitely a winner!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-04-01T02:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cook-book-post-recipe-experiences-here.552/post-9597>

Hi Friends,

I have been doing a low carbohydrate version of the SCD/GAPS/modified paleo diet for over six years. I have found and developed many recipes that would be good for anyone on the modified paleo diet. Of course, not all food agrees with all people so use you good judgement with my recipes. Here is a link an index of all the recipes on the **eatkamloops.org** website. eatkamloops.org also has a listing of over 150 local farms and ranches in Kamloops, BC, Canada:

[http://www.eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/](http://http://www.eatkamloops.org/specific-carbohydrate-diet-scd-recipes/)

### ChristineKleiber — 2012-04-15T03:55:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cook-book-post-recipe-experiences-here.552/post-15198>

So I made the Picasso Shrimp and it was a disaster. The breading got too wet just like the recipe warned. Good news is that with the egg mixture it made wonderful "hush puppies". I also made the recipe again in a smaller quantity as breading for some baked haddock that was quite yummy.
